paper_title: Benchmarking Visual LLMs Resilience to Unanswerable Questions on Visually Rich Documents
authors: Davide Napolitano, Luca Cagliero, Fabrizio Battiloro

task_types: multi-hop QA, evidence aggregation, grounded reasoning
description: Benchmark for multi-hop reasoning over long scientific papers requiring evidence integration across text, tables, and figures. Provides explicit multi-hop reasoning annotations for step-level evaluation beyond answer accuracy.
paper_title: BRIDGE: Benchmark for multi-hop Reasoning In long multimodal Documents with Grounded Evidence
authors: Biao Xiang, Soyeon Caren Han, Yihao Ding

task_types: text-only retrieval, multimodal retrieval, document understanding
description: First large-scale public benchmark for Korean visual document retrieval using real-world official publications. Features complex visual elements and dual-task evaluation revealing cross-modal reasoning challenges.
paper_title: SDS KoPub VDR: A Benchmark Dataset for Visual Document Retrieval in Korean Public Documents
authors: Jaehoon Lee, Sohyun Kim, Wanggeun Park, Geon Lee, Seungkyung Kim

task_types: document-level error detection, document-level error-type classification, element-level error-type classification
description: LED is a benchmark that evaluates structural reasoning in Document Layout Analysis beyond surface-level accuracy. It defines eight standardized error types and provides quantitative rules for realistic error simulation, enabling fine-grained assessment of structural understanding through three evaluation tasks.
paper_title: LED: A Benchmark for Evaluating Layout Error Detection in Document Analysis
authors: Inbum Heo, Taewook Hwang, Jeesu Jung, Sangkeun Jung

description: DuwatBench is a benchmark for Arabic calligraphy recognition featuring 1,272 curated samples across six classical and modern calligraphic styles with sentence-level detection annotations. It addresses the gap in Arabic script processing, especially artistic and stylized calligraphic forms that challenge standard text recognition systems.
paper_title: DuwatBench: Bridging Language and Visual Heritage through an Arabic Calligraphy Benchmark for Multimodal Understanding
authors: Shubham Patle, Sara Ghaboura, Hania Tariq, Mohammad Usman Khan, Omkar Thawakar

task_types: document understanding, complex reasoning, question answering
description: MMESGBench is the first benchmark for multimodal understanding and complex reasoning across ESG documents, comprising 933 validated QA pairs from 45 ESG documents. It addresses the challenge of document-level reasoning in lengthy, structurally diverse, and multimodal ESG reports through human-AI collaborative construction with expert validation.
paper_title: MMESGBench: Pioneering Multimodal Understanding and Complex Reasoning Benchmark for ESG Tasks
authors: Lei Zhang, Xin Zhou, Chaoyue He, Di Wang, Yi Wu

task_types: review comment generation, outcome formulation, human preference alignment, adversarial robustness
description: First multimodal benchmark for LLM-based peer review automation spanning 17 research domains across 4 major academic disciplines. Evaluates review generation, outcome formulation, alignment with human preferences, and robustness to adversarial manipulation.
paper_title: MMReview: A Multidisciplinary and Multimodal Benchmark for LLM-Based Peer Review Automation
authors: Xian Gao, Jiacheng Ruan, Zongyun Zhang, Jingsheng Gao, Ting Liu

task_types: document boundary identification, document type classification, page ordering
description: First comprehensive benchmark for document packet splitting comprising five datasets of varying complexity. Addresses real-world challenges including out-of-order pages, interleaved documents, and documents lacking clear demarcations.
paper_title: DocSplit: A Comprehensive Benchmark Dataset and Evaluation Approach for Document Packet Recognition and Splitting
authors: Md Mofijul Islam, Md Sirajus Salekin, Nivedha Balakrishnan, Vincil C. Bishop, Niharika Jain

task_types: question answering, document reasoning, cross-modal comprehension
description: SciMDR introduces a large-scale training dataset and evaluation benchmark (SciMDR-Eval) for scientific multimodal document reasoning. It uniquely uses a synthesize-and-reground framework to generate faithful QA pairs with explicit reasoning chains across 20K scientific papers, focusing on cross-modal comprehension within full-length scientific workflows.
paper_title: SciMDR: Benchmarking and Advancing Scientific Multimodal Document Reasoning
authors: Ziyu Chen, Yilun Zhao, Chengye Wang, Rilyn Han, Manasi Patwardhan

task_types: error existence detection, error type classification, element-wise error type classification
description: Introduces a novel benchmark to evaluate structural robustness of document layout predictions by defining eight standardized error types and three tasks: error existence detection, error type classification, and element-wise error type classification. Creates LED-Dataset with synthetic structural errors based on empirical distributions from DLA models to test structural understanding capabilities.
paper_title: LED Benchmark: Diagnosing Structural Layout Errors for Document Layout Analysis
authors: Inbum Heo, Taewook Hwang, Jeesu Jung, Sangkeun Jung

description: Introduces a benchmark of 851 samples for evaluating large multimodal models on multimodal super-long document understanding with hundreds of pages. Requires open-ended solutions rather than extractive answers and includes a retrieval-aware tuning framework for efficient document reading.
paper_title: M-Longdoc: A Benchmark For Multimodal Super-Long Document Understanding And A Retrieval-Aware Tuning Framework
authors: Yew Ken Chia, Liying Cheng, Hou Pong Chan, Chaoqun Liu, Maojia Song

task_types: element extraction, perturbation robustness, narrative comprehension
description: VLM-SlideEval evaluates VLMs on structured comprehension of presentation slides through element-level extraction, robustness to geometric/stylistic perturbations, and higher-level narrative understanding. It uses standardized ground-truth from PowerPoint XML and live renderings for comprehensive slide analysis.
paper_title: VLM-SlideEval: Evaluating VLMs on Structured Comprehension and Perturbation Sensitivity in PPT
authors: Hyeonsu Kang, Emily Bao, Anjan Goswami

task_types: optical character recognition, document question answering
description: DISCO evaluates OCR pipelines and VLMs separately on parsing and question answering across diverse document types including handwritten text, multilingual scripts, medical forms, and infographics. It provides complexity-aware evaluation to guide optimal approach selection based on document characteristics.
paper_title: DISCO: Document Intelligence Suite for COmparative Evaluation
authors: Kenza Benkirane, Dan Goldwater, Martin Asenov, Aneiss Ghodsi

task_types: text extraction, table comprehension, chart interpretation, multi-turn reasoning
description: First multimodal benchmark for evaluating French financial document understanding. Contains 1,204 expert-validated questions spanning text extraction, table comprehension, chart interpretation, and multi-turn conversational reasoning from real investment prospectuses, KIDs, and PRIIPs.
paper_title: When Tables Go Crazy: Evaluating Multimodal Models on French Financial Documents
authors: Virginie Mouilleron, Théo Lasnier, Anna Mosolova, Djamé Seddah

task_types: multi-tabular reasoning, visual question answering, multi-hop reasoning
description: Novel benchmark specifically designed for multi-tabular visual question answering. Comprises 3,745 complex question-answer pairs that necessitate multi-hop reasoning across several visually rendered table images, addressing gap in evaluating diverse table image parsing and cross-table correlation.
paper_title: MTabVQA: Evaluating Multi-Tabular Reasoning of Language Models in Visual Space
authors: Anshul Singh, Chris Biemann, Jan Strich

task_types: error detection, quality assessment, document parsing evaluation
description: Provides fine-grained error detection and analysis for document parsing quality assessment using VLM-as-a-Judge. Identifies 28 predefined error types with hierarchical Chain-of-Checklist reasoning paradigm for comprehensive document parsing evaluation.
paper_title: DOCR-Inspector: Fine-Grained and Automated Evaluation of Document Parsing with VLM
authors: Qintong Zhang, Junyuan Zhang, Zhifei Ren, Linke Ouyang, Zichen Wen

task_types: code generation evaluation, content coverage assessment, hallucination detection
description: Reference-free evaluation framework for flowchart image-to-code generation using OCR-based content coverage and Visual Entailment for hallucination detection. Enables quality monitoring in production settings without ground-truth references.
paper_title: An Online Reference-Free Evaluation Framework for Flowchart Image-to-Code Generation
authors: Giang Son Nguyen, Zi Pong Lim, Sarthak Ketanbhai Modi, Yon Shin Teo, Wenya Wang

description: This work creates synthetic and real-world evaluation datasets specifically for vertically written Japanese text, addressing a significant gap in MLLM evaluation. It uniquely focuses on vertical writing support in Japanese documents, which is essential for comprehensive document understanding in Japanese.
paper_title: Evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models on Vertically Written Japanese Text
authors: Keito Sasagawa, Shuhei Kurita, Daisuke Kawahara

description: Comprehensive multimodal benchmark for evaluating LLMs on PowerPoint-related tasks, leveraging 958 PPTX files across four categories: Detection, Understanding, Modification, and Generation. Focuses on layout-centric challenges central to real-world slide creation and editing, revealing gaps between semantic understanding and visual-layout reasoning.
paper_title: PPTBench: Towards Holistic Evaluation of Large Language Models for PowerPoint Layout and Design Understanding
authors: Zheng Huang, Xukai Liu, Tianyu Hu, Kai Zhang, Ye Liu

description: A dataset of authentic university-level STEM student handwritten solutions with expert-verified transcriptions and grading reports. It simultaneously evaluates MLLMs' upstream recognition fidelity and downstream auto-grading performance on real handwritten mathematical content.
paper_title: EDU-CIRCUIT-HW: Evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models on Real-World University-Level STEM Student Handwritten Solutions
authors: Weiyu Sun, Liangliang Chen, Yongnuo Cai, Huiru Xie, Yi Zeng

description: Designed for comprehensive evaluation of video text understanding with multilingual content. Features hierarchical evaluation framework with video-level, clip-level, and instance-level tasks, plus paired perception reasoning tasks between textual and visual information.
paper_title: VidText: Towards Comprehensive Evaluation for Video Text Understanding
authors: Zhoufaran Yang, Yan Shu, Jing Wang, Zhifei Yang, Yan Zhang

description: Investigates LLM-based OCR capabilities for low-resource scripts including Urdu, Albanian, and Tajik. The study uses a meticulously curated dataset with controlled variations to simulate real-world challenges and highlights the need for annotated datasets and fine-tuned models for underserved languages.
paper_title: Deciphering the Underserved: Benchmarking LLM OCR for Low-Resource Scripts
authors: Muhammad Abdullah Sohail, Salaar Masood, Hamza Iqbal

task_types: multi-scene text reading, multilingual text reading, document parsing, key information extraction
description: Introduces CC-OCR, a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating LMMs in literacy with rich structure and fine-grained visual challenges. The benchmark comprises four OCR-centric tracks across 39 subsets, with 41% sourced from real applications for the first time.
paper_title: CC-OCR: A Comprehensive and Challenging OCR Benchmark for Evaluating Large Multimodal Models in Literacy
authors: Zhibo Yang, Jun Tang, Zhaohai Li, Pengfei Wang, Jianqiang Wan

description: Introduces PsOCR, a synthetic Pashto OCR dataset for evaluating LMMs on low-resource language OCR. The benchmark covers 1,000 unique font families with variations in colors, image sizes, and layouts, establishing a foundation for research in Pashto and similar scripts.
paper_title: PsOCR: Benchmarking Large Multimodal Models for Optical Character Recognition in Low-resource Pashto Language
authors: Ijazul Haq, Yingjie Zhang, Irfan Ali Khan

description: Introduces IRPAPERS, a visual document benchmark comparing image-based and text-based retrieval and QA systems on scientific papers. The benchmark reveals complementary failures between modalities and shows that multimodal hybrid search outperforms either modality alone.
paper_title: IRPAPERS: A Visual Document Benchmark for Scientific Retrieval and Question Answering
authors: Connor Shorten, Augustas Skaburskas, Daniel M. Jones, Charles Pierse, Roberto Esposito

num_samples: 10,000 human-verified QA pairs, 1,500 manually annotated test images
task_types: text recognition, text localization, handwritten content extraction, logical reasoning
description: Introduces OCRBench v2, a large-scale bilingual text-centric benchmark with 4x more tasks than previous benchmarks and the widest coverage of scenarios (31 diverse scenarios). The benchmark focuses on challenging tasks like text localization, handwritten content extraction, and logical reasoning.
paper_title: OCRBench v2: An Improved Benchmark for Evaluating Large Multimodal Models on Visual Text Localization and Reasoning
authors: Ling Fu, Zhebin Kuang, Jiajun Song, Mingxin Huang, Biao Yang

task_types: document classification, visual grounding, document layout detection, document transformation, QA
description: DocGenome provides comprehensive multi-modal scientific document understanding with 13 layout attributes and LaTeX source codes, featuring logical relationships between entities and diverse document tasks. It addresses multi-page scientific document extraction and within-document format processing like charts and equations.
paper_title: DocGenome: An Open Large-scale Scientific Document Benchmark for Training and Testing Multi-modal Large Language Models
authors: Renqiu Xia, Song Mao, Xiangchao Yan, Hongbin Zhou, Bo Zhang

task_types: document-to-table extraction, structured data extraction, reasoning
description: Synthetic benchmark for document-to-table extraction using reverse Table2Doc paradigm with multi-agent synthesis workflow. Adopts two-level taxonomy covering 5 major categories and 13 subcategories of Doc2Table capabilities, providing comprehensive testbed for evaluating reasoning, faithfulness, and conflict resolution in structured table extraction.
paper_title: DTBench: A Synthetic Benchmark for Document-to-Table Extraction
authors: Yuxiang Guo, Zhuoran Du, Nan Tang, Kezheng Tang, Congcong Ge

task_types: document layout ordering, reading order recovery
description: A benchmark for document reading order recovery that addresses the lack of robust evaluation benchmarks for complex layouts. Specifically designed to test layout ordering methods on both simple and complex document structures, serving as a foundation for RAG and LLM preprocessing evaluation.
paper_title: XY-Cut++: Advanced Layout Ordering via Hierarchical Mask Mechanism on a Novel Benchmark
authors: Shuai Liu, Youmeng Li, Jizeng Wei

description: Combines 55K high-fidelity synthetic scanned document parsing data with expert-filtered real-world documents for end-to-end reinforcement learning framework. Focuses on layout-aware document parsing with OCR, table and formula extraction, and reading order detection.
paper_title: Infinity Parser: Layout Aware Reinforcement Learning for Scanned Document Parsing
authors: Baode Wang, Biao Wu, Weizhen Li, Meng Fang, Zuming Huang

description: Consists of 324 full-length research articles and 1,688 complex structured JSON files for multimodal information extraction from scientific literature. Focuses on materials science with data extracted from text, tables, and figures, including curve similarity evaluation.
paper_title: MatViX: Multimodal Information Extraction from Visually Rich Articles
authors: Ghazal Khalighinejad, Sharon Scott, Ollie Liu, Kelly L. Anderson, Rickard Stureborg

description: Specialized evaluation framework for menu OCR and translation emphasizing cross-cultural communication. Features Chinese and English menus with intricate layouts, variety of fonts, and culturally specific elements, requiring accurate recognition and translation of dishes, prices, and units.
paper_title: Evaluating Menu OCR and Translation: A Benchmark for Aligning Human and Automated Evaluations in Large Vision-Language Models
authors: Zhanglin Wu, Tengfei Song, Ning Xie, Mengli Zhu, Weidong Zhang

task_types: visualized text understanding, text-image comparison, multimodal reasoning
description: A systematic benchmark evaluating visualized text understanding by contrasting pure-text and visualized-text questions under controlled rendering conditions. Spans multimodal perception, reasoning, and unimodal understanding domains to assess modality gaps in VLMs.
paper_title: VISTA-Bench: Do Vision-Language Models Really Understand Visualized Text as Well as Pure Text?
authors: Qing'an Liu, Juntong Feng, Yuhao Wang, Xinzhe Han, Yujie Cheng

description: This benchmark specifically evaluates VLMs' ability to understand flowcharts by testing directional arrows and graph topology interpretation. It consists of 90 questions from 30 annotated flowcharts, focusing on next-step queries, branch-result questions, and before-step questions with explicit arrow direction encoding.
paper_title: Arrow-Guided VLM: Enhancing Flowchart Understanding via Arrow Direction Encoding
authors: Takamitsu Omasa, Ryo Koshihara, Masumi Morishige

description: ReadBench is a multimodal benchmark specifically designed to evaluate reading comprehension capabilities of VLMs on text-rich images. It transposes contexts from established text-only benchmarks into images of text while keeping prompts intact, revealing sharp performance decline for longer, multi-page contexts despite minimal degradation on short inputs.
paper_title: ReadBench: Measuring the Dense Text Visual Reading Ability of Vision-Language Models
authors: Benjamin Clavié, Florian Brand

task_types: OCR quality assessment, document quality evaluation
description: OCR-Quality is a comprehensive human-annotated dataset of 1,000 PDF pages converted to PNG images at 300 DPI, sampled from diverse real-world scenarios including academic papers, textbooks, e-books, and multilingual documents. Each document is manually annotated with quality scores using a 4-level scoring system (1: Excellent, 2: Good, 3: Fair, 4: Poor) to evaluate and develop OCR quality assessment methods.
paper_title: OCR-Quality: A Human-Annotated Dataset for OCR Quality Assessment
authors: Yulong Zhang

task_types: visual table understanding, table question answering
description: Introduces a large-scale visual table understanding dataset with 4 million examples across 21 tasks, grounded in 2 million unique tables where 88% preserve original visualizations. Also introduces VisualTableQA benchmark requiring both visual perception and table understanding, addressing the lack of real-world complexity in existing synthetic table benchmarks.
paper_title: TABLET: A Large-Scale Dataset for Robust Visual Table Understanding
authors: Iñigo Alonso, Imanol Miranda, Eneko Agirre, Mirella Lapata

description: A large-scale cross-domain multimodal benchmark for document-level retrieval that unifies diverse document formats including Wikipedia articles, scientific papers, and presentation slides. The benchmark systematically integrates textual and visual information for comprehensive document retrieval evaluation.
paper_title: DocMMIR: A Framework for Document Multi-modal Information Retrieval
authors: Zirui Li, Siwei Wu, Yizhi Li, Xingyu Wang, Yi Zhou

description: Benchmark comprising scanned historical reaction diagrams with real-world artifacts to rigorously assess model robustness and out-of-distribution ability for chemical reaction diagram parsing. Tests VLMs on extracting chemical synthesis information from literature.
paper_title: Molecular Identifier Visual Prompt and Verifiable Reinforcement Learning for Chemical Reaction Diagram Parsing
authors: Jiahe Song, Chuang Wang, Yinfan Wang, Hao Zheng, Rui Nie

description: Introduces TextAnchor-Bench for evaluating fine-grained text-region grounding and TextAnchor-26M, a large-scale dataset of 26M image-text pairs with fine-grained masks. Proposes Q-Mask framework with causal query-driven mask decoder.
paper_title: Q-Mask: Query-driven Causal Masks for Text Anchoring in OCR-Oriented Vision-Language Models
authors: Longwei Xu, Feng Feng, Shaojie Zhang, Xin Chen, Hang Li

description: CalliBench is the first benchmark for full-page calligraphic contextualization using structured-report protocol based on (Location, Field, Value) triples derived from real clinical reports. It addresses fragmented context, shallow reasoning, and hallucination issues in Chinese calligraphy recognition and interpretation.
paper_title: CalliReader: Contextualizing Chinese Calligraphy via an Embedding-Aligned Vision-Language Model
authors: Yuxuan Luo, Jiaqi Tang, Chenyi Huang, Feiyang Hao, Zhouhui Lian

description: A high-quality, expert-verified benchmark designed for rigorous evaluation of Arabic OCR systems. Specifically targets Arabic document understanding challenges including cursive script and right-to-left orientation.
paper_title: Baseer: A Vision-Language Model for Arabic Document-to-Markdown OCR
authors: Khalil Hennara, Muhammad Hreden, Mohamed Motasim Hamed, Ahmad Bastati, Zeina Aldallal

description: SciEGQA is a scientific document question answering and reasoning dataset with semantic evidence grounding, where supporting evidence is represented as semantically coherent document regions annotated with bounding boxes. It consists of 1,623 human-annotated high-quality QA pairs and over 30K automatically constructed training pairs for scientific document understanding.
paper_title: SciEGQA: A Dataset for Scientific Evidence-Grounded Question Answering and Reasoning
authors: Wenhan Yu, Zhaoxi Zhang, Wang Chen, Guanqiang Qi, Weikang Li

description: A massive synthetic Arabic OCR dataset with 843,622 document images and 690 million words designed to simulate book-style documents. Uses ten distinct Arabic fonts and provides clean, controlled environment for training OCR models.
paper_title: SARD: A Large-Scale Synthetic Arabic OCR Dataset for Book-Style Text Recognition
authors: Omer Nacar, Yasser Al-Habashi, Serry Sibaee, Adel Ammar, Wadii Boulila

description: A historic dataset of handwritten forms from post-WWII Care and Maintenance program designed to evaluate few-shot information extraction capabilities of Large Vision Language Models. Establishes benchmarks for extracting name and birthdate information with different training set sizes.
paper_title: CM1 -- A Dataset for Evaluating Few-Shot Information Extraction with Large Vision Language Models
authors: Fabian Wolf, Oliver Tüselmann, Arthur Matei, Lukas Hennies, Christoph Rass

task_types: table extraction, multi-page table recognition, table structure recognition
description: A new large-scale dataset for full-page and multi-page table extraction supporting challenging table extraction tasks. Notably the first large-scale benchmark for multi-page table structure recognition, addressing a key gap in current vision-language models' capabilities.
paper_title: PubTables-v2: A new large-scale dataset for full-page and multi-page table extraction
authors: Brandon Smock, Valerie Faucon-Morin, Max Sokolov, Libin Liang, Tayyibah Khanam

description: Evaluates LVLMs on reading values from Digital Measurement Devices under challenging real-world conditions like clutter, occlusions, and motion blur. Includes both synthetic training data generation tool and real-world validation benchmark.
paper_title: CAD2DMD-SET: Synthetic Generation Tool of Digital Measurement Device CAD Model Datasets for fine-tuning Large Vision-Language Models
authors: João Valente, Atabak Dehban, Rodrigo Ventura

task_types: entity recognition, entity chain extraction, relation extraction, visual grounding
description: M³D introduces document-level video-based multimodal information extraction with multilingual support (English and Chinese) and fine-grained visual grounding. It uniquely combines paired document-level text and video data for entity recognition, entity chain extraction, relation extraction, and visual grounding in the biography domain.
paper_title: M$^{3}$D: A Multimodal, Multilingual and Multitask Dataset for Grounded Document-level Information Extraction
authors: Jiang Liu, Bobo Li, Xinran Yang, Na Yang, Hao Fei

description: MERIT provides a multimodal dataset of school reports with text, image, and layout information for Visually-rich Document Understanding tasks. It offers a controlled environment for benchmarking biases in Language Models and poses significant challenges for document understanding models.
paper_title: The MERIT Dataset: Modelling and Efficiently Rendering Interpretable Transcripts
authors: I. de Rodrigo, A. Sanchez-Cuadrado, J. Boal, A. J. Lopez-Lopez

task_types: document reasoning, code generation, GUI reasoning
description: BigDocs provides a large-scale open-access dataset for document understanding and code generation tasks, with BigDocs-Bench featuring 10 novel tasks involving reasoning over GUIs and code generation from images. It emphasizes real-world use cases and structured output tasks.
paper_title: BigDocs: An Open Dataset for Training Multimodal Models on Document and Code Tasks
authors: Juan Rodriguez, Xiangru Jian, Siba Smarak Panigrahi, Tianyu Zhang, Aarash Feizi

description: A benchmark pairing GPT-4o-generated receipts with authentic ones to study AI-generated document forensics, revealing that humans are better at seeing AI artifacts but worse at detecting AI documents due to arithmetic errors being invisible to visual inspection but verifiable by LLMs.
paper_title: GPT4o-Receipt: A Dataset and Human Study for AI-Generated Document Forensics
authors: Yan Zhang, Simiao Ren, Ankit Raj, En Wei, Dennis Ng

task_types: text detection, text recognition, information extraction
description: EPHOIE presents the first Chinese benchmark for visual information extraction containing 1,494 images of examination paper heads with complex layouts. It includes 15,771 Chinese handwritten or printed text instances, enabling simultaneous evaluation of text detection, recognition, and information extraction in a unified end-to-end framework.
paper_title: Towards Robust Visual Information Extraction in Real World: New Dataset and Novel Solution
authors: Jiapeng Wang, Chongyu Liu, Lianwen Jin, Guozhi Tang, Jiaxin Zhang

description: EGONORMIA evaluates normative reasoning in VLMs using egocentric videos across seven norm categories including safety, privacy, proxemics, and politeness. It uniquely focuses on physically- and socially-grounded norms in human interactions through a novel pipeline for generating grounded MCQs.
paper_title: EgoNormia: Benchmarking Physical Social Norm Understanding
authors: MohammadHossein Rezaei, Yicheng Fu, Phil Cuvin, Caleb Ziems, Yanzhe Zhang

description: ESPIRE is a diagnostic benchmark for embodied spatial reasoning that offers a simulated world for physically grounding VLMs and evaluating them on spatial-reasoning-centric robotic tasks. It uniquely decomposes tasks into localization and execution as generative problems rather than discriminative evaluations.
paper_title: ESPIRE: A Diagnostic Benchmark for Embodied Spatial Reasoning of Vision-Language Models
authors: Yanpeng Zhao, Wentao Ding, Hongtao Li, Baoxiong Jia, Zilong Zheng

description: CapNav is a capability-conditioned navigation benchmark that evaluates how well VLMs can navigate indoor spaces given specific physical and operational constraints of different agents. It defines five representative human and robot agents with different mobility capabilities and tests navigation performance under varying mobility constraints across 45 real-world indoor scenes.
paper_title: CapNav: Benchmarking Vision Language Models on Capability-conditioned Indoor Navigation
authors: Xia Su, Ruiqi Chen, Benlin Liu, Jingwei Ma, Zonglin Di

modalities: vision, audio, digital context, longitudinal data
description: WAGIBench is a benchmark for measuring progress in egocentric multimodal goal inference for assistive wearable agents. It features 29 hours of multimodal data from 348 participants with ground-truth goals alongside visual, audio, digital, and longitudinal contextual observations, testing the ability to infer user goals from contextual observations without explicit interaction.
paper_title: Benchmarking Egocentric Multimodal Goal Inference for Assistive Wearable Agents
authors: Vijay Veerabadran, Fanyi Xiao, Nitin Kamra, Pedro Matias, Joy Chen

task_types: manipulation reasoning, object interaction understanding, deformable object manipulation
description: ManipBench is a novel benchmark designed to evaluate the low-level robot manipulation reasoning capabilities of VLMs across various dimensions, including understanding object-object interactions and deformable object manipulation. It fills a gap in evaluating VLMs for precise robot movement decisions rather than just high-level planning, testing 33 representative VLMs across 10 model families.
paper_title: ManipBench: Benchmarking Vision-Language Models for Low-Level Robot Manipulation
authors: Enyu Zhao, Vedant Raval, Hejia Zhang, Jiageng Mao, Zeyu Shangguan

description: RoboCerebra evaluates high-level reasoning in long-horizon robotic manipulation with extended task horizons and diverse subtask sequences. It targets System 2 capabilities through deliberative, goal-directed thinking and structured System 1-System 2 interaction evaluation.
paper_title: RoboCerebra: A Large-scale Benchmark for Long-horizon Robotic Manipulation Evaluation
authors: Songhao Han, Boxiang Qiu, Yue Liao, Siyuan Huang, Chen Gao

task_types: visual question answering, spatial reasoning, spatiotemporal reasoning, social reasoning
description: SocialNav-SUB is a VQA dataset designed to evaluate VLMs' scene understanding capabilities for social robot navigation. It provides a unified framework for assessing spatial, spatiotemporal, and social reasoning in real-world social robot navigation scenarios.
paper_title: SocialNav-SUB: Benchmarking VLMs for Scene Understanding in Social Robot Navigation
authors: Michael J. Munje, Chen Tang, Shuijing Liu, Zichao Hu, Yifeng Zhu

task_types: visual question answering, non-verbal cue understanding, verbal instruction understanding, human-robot object relationship understanding, social navigation, person identification
description: HRIBench evaluates VLMs across diverse human perceptual tasks critical for human-robot interaction, covering non-verbal cues, verbal instructions, object relationships, social navigation, and person identification. It specifically addresses real-time human perception capabilities needed for effective HRI.
paper_title: HRIBench: Benchmarking Vision-Language Models for Real-Time Human Perception in Human-Robot Interaction
authors: Zhonghao Shi, Enyu Zhao, Nathaniel Dennler, Jingzhen Wang, Xinyang Xu

description: MV-RoboBench specifically evaluates multi-view spatial reasoning capabilities of VLMs in robotic manipulation contexts. It uniquely addresses the underexplored area of multi-camera setup integration for robotic reasoning, divided into spatial understanding and robotic execution categories.
paper_title: Seeing Across Views: Benchmarking Spatial Reasoning of Vision-Language Models in Robotic Scenes
authors: Zhiyuan Feng, Zhaolu Kang, Qijie Wang, Zhiying Du, Jiongrui Yan

task_types: embodied question answering, low-light perception, robustness evaluation
description: First benchmark evaluating embodied question answering under multi-level low-light conditions with physics-based visual degradations. Uses RAW space modeling and ISP-inspired rendering pipeline to simulate realistic illumination drops and sensor noise for robust 24/7 operation assessment.
paper_title: DarkEQA: Benchmarking Vision-Language Models for Embodied Question Answering in Low-Light Indoor Environments
authors: Yohan Park, Hyunwoo Ha, Wonjun Jo, Tae-Hyun Oh

task_types: safety inspection, hazard detection, embodied navigation
description: HomeSafeBench evaluates embodied VLMs in free-exploration home safety inspection tasks using dynamic first-person perspective images from simulated environments. It covers five common home safety hazards and enables evaluation of VLM capabilities for thorough safety inspection through multiple dynamic perspectives.
paper_title: HomeSafeBench: A Benchmark for Embodied Vision-Language Models in Free-Exploration Home Safety Inspection
authors: Siyuan Gao, Jiashu Yao, Haoyu Wen, Yuhang Guo, Zeming Liu

description: A visual reasoning benchmark designed for robotic environments with human-robot interaction. It provides structured, step-by-step reasoning annotations and adaptive query generation for customizable difficulty levels in human-crowded environments.
paper_title: JRDB-Reasoning: A Difficulty-Graded Benchmark for Visual Reasoning in Robotics
authors: Simindokht Jahangard, Mehrzad Mohammadi, Yi Shen, Zhixi Cai, Hamid Rezatofighi

description: A multi-modal planning benchmark with 1,187 instances across 153 household activities, featuring counterfactual planning evaluation. Each instance includes natural language task descriptions and multiple environment images from iGibson2 simulator.
paper_title: ActPlan-1K: Benchmarking the Procedural Planning Ability of Visual Language Models in Household Activities
authors: Ying Su, Zhan Ling, Haochen Shi, Jiayang Cheng, Yauwai Yim

description: A unified hierarchical closed-loop evaluation framework for Vision-Language Models in autonomous driving. Enables real-time, interactive assessment across both simulation and physical platforms with self-reflective scenario generation for safety-critical testing.
paper_title: Bench2ADVLM: A Closed-Loop Benchmark for Vision-language Models in Autonomous Driving
authors: Tianyuan Zhang, Ting Jin, Lu Wang, Jiangfan Liu, Siyuan Liang

task_types: long-horizon control, memory-based tasks, navigation, object manipulation
description: FindingDory evaluates long-term memory capabilities in embodied agents across 60 tasks in Habitat simulator. It specifically targets scenarios where memory is crucial for success, addressing limitations of existing benchmarks that overlook embodied challenges like object manipulation and navigation requiring sustained engagement.
paper_title: FindingDory: A Benchmark to Evaluate Memory in Embodied Agents
authors: Karmesh Yadav, Yusuf Ali, Gunshi Gupta, Yarin Gal, Zsolt Kira

task_types: visual question answering, object detection, external knowledge retrieval
description: SUPERGLASSES is the first comprehensive VQA benchmark built entirely on real-world data collected by smart glasses devices. It comprises 2,422 egocentric image-question pairs spanning 14 domains and 8 query categories, with full search trajectories and reasoning annotations for AI smart glasses applications.
paper_title: SUPERGLASSES: Benchmarking Vision Language Models as Intelligent Agents for AI Smart Glasses
authors: Zhuohang Jiang, Xu Yuan, Haohao Qu, Shanru Lin, Kanglong Liu

task_types: perception, motion control, task planning, action generation
description: BEDI introduces a systematic benchmark for UAV-Embodied Agents using a Dynamic Chain-of-Embodied-Task paradigm. It evaluates six core sub-skills (semantic perception, spatial perception, motion control, tool utilization, task planning, action generation) through both virtual and real-world scenarios with open interfaces.
paper_title: BEDI: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Evaluating Embodied Agents on UAVs
authors: Mingning Guo, Mengwei Wu, Jiarun He, Shaoxian Li, Haifeng Li

task_types: human recognition, symbol understanding, reasoning, action generation
description: CognitiveDroneBench is the first dedicated benchmark for assessing cognitive tasks in drone operations, trained on 8,000+ simulated flight trajectories across Human Recognition, Symbol Understanding, and Reasoning categories. It evaluates real-time 4D action command generation for complex UAV tasks requiring advanced cognitive abilities.
paper_title: CognitiveDrone: A VLA Model and Evaluation Benchmark for Real-Time Cognitive Task Solving and Reasoning in UAVs
authors: Artem Lykov, Valerii Serpiva, Muhammad Haris Khan, Oleg Sautenkov, Artyom Myshlyaev

task_types: temporal progress prediction, task completion estimation
description: A comprehensive benchmark for estimating task progress across diverse manipulation tasks involving both robotic and human embodiments, building upon Generative Value Learning approach. It uniquely evaluates temporal task completion prediction capabilities and serves as a practical tool for automated data curation and filtering.
paper_title: OpenGVL -- Benchmarking Visual Temporal Progress for Data Curation
authors: Paweł Budzianowski, Emilia Wiśnios, Michał Tyrolski, Gracjan Góral, Igor Kulakov

task_types: work zone detection, sign reading, navigation planning
description: A dataset and benchmark for learning to recognize, observe, analyze, and drive through work zones, addressing this long-tailed and underexplored scenario. It uniquely evaluates perception and navigation in work zones with specific focus on work zone sign reading, detection, and autonomous navigation through construction areas.
paper_title: ROADWork: A Dataset and Benchmark for Learning to Recognize, Observe, Analyze and Drive Through Work Zones
authors: Anurag Ghosh, Shen Zheng, Robert Tamburo, Khiem Vuong, Juan Alvarez-Padilla

task_types: noisy question detection, question correction, embodied question answering
description: A benchmark designed to evaluate agents' ability to recognize and correct noisy questions in Embodied Question Answering, introducing four types of noise: Latent Hallucination, Memory, Perception, and Semantic Noise. It uniquely addresses real-world scenarios where human-posed questions contain noise that interferes with agent exploration.
paper_title: NoisyEQA: Benchmarking Embodied Question Answering Against Noisy Queries
authors: Tao Wu, Chuhao Zhou, Yen Heng Wong, Lin Gu, Jianfei Yang

description: DriveAction is the first action-driven benchmark for VLA models comprising 16,185 QA pairs from 2,610 driving scenarios. It leverages real-world driving data with high-level discrete action labels from actual driver operations and implements an action-rooted tree-structured evaluation framework.
paper_title: DriveAction: A Benchmark for Exploring Human-like Driving Decisions in VLA Models
authors: Yuhan Hao, Zhengning Li, Lei Sun, Weilong Wang, Naixin Yi

description: ManiTaskGen automatically generates comprehensive, diverse mobile manipulation tasks for any given scene, creating both process-based and outcome-based instructions. It provides universal task generation for arbitrary scenes, facilitating benchmarking of embodied decision-making capabilities.
paper_title: ManiTaskGen: A Comprehensive Task Generator for Benchmarking and Improving Vision-Language Agents on Embodied Decision-Making
authors: Liu Dai, Haina Wang, Weikang Wan, Hao Su

task_types: robot policy evaluation, manipulation tasks, preference comparison
description: RobotArena∞ is a benchmarking framework that converts video demonstrations from robot datasets into simulated counterparts for scalable VLA policy evaluation. It uses automated vision-language-model-guided scoring and human preference judgments with systematic environment perturbations for robustness testing.
paper_title: RobotArena $\infty$: Scalable Robot Benchmarking via Real-to-Sim Translation
authors: Yash Jangir, Yidi Zhang, Pang-Chi Lo, Kashu Yamazaki, Chenyu Zhang

task_types: scene understanding, spatial perception, motion planning, risk assessment
description: ScenePilot-4K is a large-scale first-person dataset with 3,847 hours of video and 27.7M frames from 63 countries/regions for safety-aware vision-language learning in autonomous driving. ScenePilot-Bench evaluates models on scene understanding, spatial perception, motion planning, and semantic alignment.
paper_title: ScenePilot-4K: A Large-Scale First-Person Dataset and Benchmark for Vision-Language Models in Autonomous Driving
authors: Yujin Wang, Yutong Zheng, Wenxian Fan, Tianyi Wang, Hongqing Chu

task_types: episodic memory, interactive games, multi-hop reasoning
description: EMemBench evaluates long-term episodic memory of VLM agents through interactive games, generating questions from each agent's trajectory covering both text and visual environments. It tests single/multi-hop recall, induction, temporal, spatial, logical, and adversarial memory skills.
paper_title: EMemBench: Interactive Benchmarking of Episodic Memory for VLM Agents
authors: Xinze Li, Ziyue Zhu, Siyuan Liu, Yubo Ma, Yuhang Zang

description: A benchmark for evaluating embodied spatial understanding of LVLMs automatically derived from embodied scenes. It covers 6 spatial relationships from an egocentric perspective to assess critical spatial understanding skills for embodied tasks.
paper_title: EmbSpatial-Bench: Benchmarking Spatial Understanding for Embodied Tasks with Large Vision-Language Models
authors: Mengfei Du, Binhao Wu, Zejun Li, Xuanjing Huang, Zhongyu Wei

task_types: social group detection, interpersonal interaction recognition
description: A first-person view dataset for social group detection spanning 65 countries with diverse crowd settings and weather/time conditions. It captures social dynamics in real-world cultural contexts with dense annotations for persons and social groups plus rich geographic metadata.
paper_title: EgoGroups: A Benchmark For Detecting Social Groups of People in the Wild
authors: Jeffri Murrugarra-Llerena, Pranav Chitale, Zicheng Liu, Kai Ao, Yujin Ham

description: Comprehensive benchmark for evaluating vision-language-action models' zero-shot generalization in procedurally out-of-distribution environments from Procgen. Tests VLAs' performance on diverse procedural tasks with focus on action representation and task complexity sensitivity.
paper_title: Benchmarking Vision, Language, & Action Models in Procedurally Generated, Open Ended Action Environments
authors: Pranav Guruprasad, Yangyue Wang, Sudipta Chowdhury, Harshvardhan Sikka, Paul Pu Liang

description: Largest dataset specifically curated for off-road autonomous driving covering diverse terrains and environmental variations. Establishes comprehensive benchmarks spanning 2D free-space detection, 3D occupancy prediction, GPS-guided path planning, vision-language driving, and world modeling for off-road environments.
paper_title: Advancing Off-Road Autonomous Driving: The Large-Scale ORAD-3D Dataset and Comprehensive Benchmarks
authors: Chen Min, Jilin Mei, Heng Zhai, Shuai Wang, Tong Sun

task_types: intention recognition, embodied interaction, long-horizon planning
description: CookBench introduces a long-horizon embodied planning benchmark for complex cooking scenarios with two-stage tasks: Intention Recognition and Embodied Interaction. It features fine-grained spatial action granularity built on Unity engine, providing both macro-level operations and detailed embodied actions for comprehensive evaluation of planning and decision-making capabilities.
paper_title: CookBench: A Long-Horizon Embodied Planning Benchmark for Complex Cooking Scenarios
authors: Muzhen Cai, Xiubo Chen, Yining An, Jiaxin Zhang, Xuesong Wang

task_types: embodied cognition, egocentric video understanding, self-cognition evaluation
description: ECBench systematically evaluates embodied cognitive abilities of LVLMs using egocentric videos across 30 dimensions of embodied cognition. It addresses critical issues like robotic self-cognition, dynamic scene perception, and hallucination with class-independent human annotation and multi-round question screening, accompanied by ECEval comprehensive evaluation system.
paper_title: ECBench: Can Multi-modal Foundation Models Understand the Egocentric World? A Holistic Embodied Cognition Benchmark
authors: Ronghao Dang, Yuqian Yuan, Wenqi Zhang, Yifei Xin, Boqiang Zhang

task_types: theory of mind, goal inference, belief state inference, action prediction
description: EgoToM extends Theory-of-Mind evaluation to egocentric domains using a causal ToM model to generate multi-choice video QA instances for Ego4D dataset. It benchmarks the ability to predict camera wearer's goals, beliefs, and next actions.
paper_title: EgoToM: Benchmarking Theory of Mind Reasoning from Egocentric Videos
authors: Yuxuan Li, Vijay Veerabadran, Michael L. Iuzzolino, Brett D. Roads, Asli Celikyilmaz

description: BEAR comprehensively evaluates MLLMs on atomic embodied capabilities with 4,469 interleaved image-video-text entries across 14 domains in 6 categories. It covers tasks from low-level pointing and trajectory understanding to high-level planning in embodied AI contexts.
paper_title: BEAR: Benchmarking and Enhancing Multimodal Language Models for Atomic Embodied Capabilities
authors: Yu Qi, Haibo Zhao, Ziyu Guo, Siyuan Ma, Ziyan Chen

description: EgoPlan-Bench evaluates planning abilities of MLLMs in real-world scenarios from an egocentric perspective, mirroring human perception. It features realistic tasks, diverse action plans, and intricate visual observations to assess human-level task planning capabilities.
paper_title: EgoPlan-Bench: Benchmarking Multimodal Large Language Models for Human-Level Planning
authors: Yi Chen, Yuying Ge, Yixiao Ge, Mingyu Ding, Bohao Li

task_types: scene understanding, object understanding, perception assessment, collaborative decision
description: Introduces the first comprehensive benchmark for evaluating MLLMs in embodied aerial collaborative perception under challenging conditions. Contains 14.6k+ questions from simulator and real-world data across four task dimensions and 14 task types, focusing on multi-drone collaborative scenarios.
paper_title: AirCopBench: A Benchmark for Multi-drone Collaborative Embodied Perception and Reasoning
authors: Jirong Zha, Yuxuan Fan, Tianyu Zhang, Geng Chen, Yingfeng Chen

description: An egocentric gaze-guided video question answering benchmark that leverages gaze information to improve understanding of longer daily-life videos. Unique for incorporating gaze as an indicator of user intent in egocentric video understanding and enabling more personalized AI assistance.
paper_title: In the Eye of MLLM: Benchmarking Egocentric Video Intent Understanding with Gaze-Guided Prompting
authors: Taiying Peng, Jiacheng Hua, Miao Liu, Feng Lu

task_types: spatial reasoning, action planning, end-effector control
description: A hierarchical benchmark evaluating MLLMs across three tiers for bimanual coordination: fundamental spatial reasoning, high-level action planning, and low-level end-effector control. Unique for isolating bimanual challenges like arm reachability and kinematic constraints, distinguishing perceptual hallucinations from planning failures.
paper_title: BiManiBench: A Hierarchical Benchmark for Evaluating Bimanual Coordination of Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Xin Wu, Zhixuan Liang, Yue Ma, Mengkang Hu, Zhiyuan Qin

task_types: physical interaction, temporal-causal reasoning, intentional understanding, evaluative judgment
description: CFG-Bench consists of 1,368 curated videos paired with 19,562 question-answer pairs spanning three evaluation paradigms targeting four cognitive abilities: Physical Interaction, Temporal-Causal Relation, Intentional Understanding, and Evaluative Judgment. It systematically evaluates fine-grained action intelligence for embodied agents.
paper_title: Beyond Description: Cognitively Benchmarking Fine-Grained Action for Embodied Agents
authors: Dayong Liu, Chao Xu, Weihong Chen, Suyu Zhang, Juncheng Wang

task_types: human-AI collaboration, egocentric perception, task-oriented assistance
description: LifeEval is designed to evaluate real-time, task-oriented human-AI collaboration in daily life from an egocentric perspective. It comprises 4,075 high-quality question-answer pairs across 6 core capability dimensions, emphasizing task-oriented evaluation, egocentric real-time perception, and human-assistant collaborative interaction.
paper_title: LifeEval: A Multimodal Benchmark for Assistive AI in Egocentric Daily Life Tasks
authors: Hengjian Gao, Kaiwei Zhang, Shibo Wang, Mingjie Chen, Qihang Cao

task_types: semantic alignment, viewpoint association, temporal reasoning
description: First benchmark for egocentric-exocentric video understanding and reasoning. Comprises eleven sub-tasks organized into three core challenges: semantic alignment, viewpoint association, and temporal reasoning, enabling evaluation of cross-view intelligence.
paper_title: EgoExoBench: A Benchmark for First- and Third-person View Video Understanding in MLLMs
authors: Yuping He, Yifei Huang, Guo Chen, Baoqi Pei, Jilan Xu

description: Rigorous benchmark assessing planning capabilities of MLLMs across real-world scenarios spanning 4 major domains and 24 detailed scenarios. Constructed through egocentric videos with first-person perspective mirroring human problem-solving approaches.
paper_title: EgoPlan-Bench2: A Benchmark for Multimodal Large Language Model Planning in Real-World Scenarios
authors: Lu Qiu, Yi Chen, Yuying Ge, Yixiao Ge, Ying Shan

description: Comprehensive benchmark evaluating cross-domain generalization of MLLMs in egocentric video QA. Covers four diverse domains (surgery, industry, extreme sports, animal perspective) representing realistic high-impact scenarios beyond daily activities.
paper_title: EgoCross: Benchmarking Multimodal Large Language Models for Cross-Domain Egocentric Video Question Answering
authors: Yanjun Li, Yuqian Fu, Tianwen Qian, Qi'ao Xu, Silong Dai

description: An egocentric video dataset of face-to-face instructional interactions with ground-truth annotations for procedural step segmentation and conversation-state classification. Addresses the systematic study gap in computer vision for analyzing instructor-learner interactions in physical spaces.
paper_title: EgoInstruct: An Egocentric Video Dataset of Face-to-face Instructional Interactions with Multi-modal LLM Benchmarking
authors: Yuki Sakai, Ryosuke Furuta, Juichun Yen, Yoichi Sato

task_types: reward modeling, agent evaluation, perception, planning, safety
description: First benchmark specifically designed to evaluate reward modeling ability in MLLMs for real-world multimodal agents across perception, planning, and safety dimensions. Features step-level reward evaluation across 7 scenarios, providing granular assessment of agent capabilities during the planning process with difficulty control and high-quality manual verification.
paper_title: Agent-RewardBench: Towards a Unified Benchmark for Reward Modeling across Perception, Planning, and Safety in Real-World Multimodal Agents
authors: Tianyi Men, Zhuoran Jin, Pengfei Cao, Yubo Chen, Kang Liu

task_types: instruction comprehension, perception reasoning, planning, affordance prediction, failure analysis
description: Comprehensive benchmark systematically evaluating MLLMs as embodied brains across five dimensions: instruction comprehension, perception reasoning, generalized planning, affordance prediction, and failure analysis. Spans 14 capabilities and 25 tasks with datasets from diverse embodiments and multi-view scenes, introducing MLLM-as-world-simulator evaluation framework for planning tasks.
paper_title: Robobench: A Comprehensive Evaluation Benchmark for Multimodal Large Language Models as Embodied Brain
authors: Yulin Luo, Chun-Kai Fan, Menghang Dong, Jiayu Shi, Mengdi Zhao

task_types: intent recognition, next-step prediction, action understanding
description: EgoIntent provides step-level intent understanding for egocentric videos across three dimensions: local intent (What), global intent (Why), and next-step planning (Next). It uniquely truncates clips before key outcomes occur to prevent future-frame leakage, enabling clean evaluation of anticipatory understanding.
paper_title: EgoIntent: An Egocentric Step-level Benchmark for Understanding What, Why, and Next
authors: Ye Pan, Chi Kit Wong, Yuanhuiyi Lyu, Hanqian Li, Jiahao Huo

task_types: farming, crafting, exploration, combat, social interactions
description: StarDojo is a novel benchmark based on Stardew Valley that evaluates AI agents in open-ended production-living simulations, combining essential livelihood activities like farming and crafting with social interactions. It uniquely assesses both production skills and social competencies simultaneously in a unified environment.
paper_title: StarDojo: Benchmarking Open-Ended Behaviors of Agentic Multimodal LLMs in Production-Living Simulations with Stardew Valley
authors: Weihao Tan, Changjiu Jiang, Yu Duan, Mingcong Lei, Jiageng Li

task_types: visual question answering, day-night correspondence retrieval, egocentric depth estimation
description: The first comprehensive benchmark for nighttime egocentric vision understanding featuring day-night aligned videos and VQA tasks. Uses synthetic and real-world recordings to explore performance gaps between lighting conditions and includes auxiliary tasks like day-night correspondence retrieval and depth estimation.
paper_title: EgoNight: Towards Egocentric Vision Understanding at Night with a Challenging Benchmark
authors: Deheng Zhang, Yuqian Fu, Runyi Yang, Yang Miao, Tianwen Qian

task_types: visual question answering, road environment perception, spatial relations recognition, ego-centric reasoning
description: NuPlanQA-Eval is a multi-view, multi-modal evaluation benchmark for driving scene understanding with 1M real-world VQA pairs. It categorizes tasks into nine subtasks across three core skills: Road Environment Perception, Spatial Relations Recognition, and Ego-Centric Reasoning.
paper_title: NuPlanQA: A Large-Scale Dataset and Benchmark for Multi-View Driving Scene Understanding in Multi-Modal Large Language Models
authors: Sung-Yeon Park, Can Cui, Yunsheng Ma, Ahmadreza Moradipari, Rohit Gupta

task_types: embodied reasoning, navigation, manipulation, commonsense reasoning, spatial awareness, long-term planning
description: EmbodiedBench is an extensive benchmark with 1,128 testing tasks across four environments for evaluating vision-driven embodied agents. It features six meticulously curated subsets evaluating essential agent capabilities like commonsense reasoning, spatial awareness, visual perception, and long-term planning from high-level semantic to low-level atomic actions.
paper_title: EmbodiedBench: Comprehensive Benchmarking Multi-modal Large Language Models for Vision-Driven Embodied Agents
authors: Rui Yang, Hanyang Chen, Junyu Zhang, Mark Zhao, Cheng Qian

description: First comprehensive benchmark for ocean underwater embodied agents featuring eight realistic task domains in extreme underwater conditions. Tests perception, memory, and sequential decision-making under low visibility and dynamic ocean currents using optical and sonar data.
paper_title: OceanGym: A Benchmark Environment for Underwater Embodied Agents
authors: Yida Xue, Mingjun Mao, Xiangyuan Ru, Yuqi Zhu, Baochang Ren

task_types: navigation, memory-based question answering, embodied exploration
description: LMEE-Bench is designed for Long-term Memory Embodied Exploration, incorporating multi-goal navigation and memory-based question answering to comprehensively evaluate both the process and outcome of embodied exploration. It uniquely focuses on lifelong learning capabilities and leveraging long-term episodic memory for decision-making in embodied agents.
paper_title: Explore with Long-term Memory: A Benchmark and Multimodal LLM-based Reinforcement Learning Framework for Embodied Exploration
authors: Sen Wang, Bangwei Liu, Zhenkun Gao, Lizhuang Ma, Xuhong Wang

task_types: field work monitoring, safety incident reporting, real-world task execution
description: FieldWorkArena is a benchmark for agentic AI targeting real-world field work environments, focusing on monitoring and reporting safety, health, and manufacturing incidents. It uniquely consists of videos captured on-site and documents from actual factories and warehouses, with tasks created based on interviews with workers and managers.
paper_title: FieldWorkArena: Agentic AI Benchmark for Real Field Work Tasks
authors: Atsunori Moteki, Shoichi Masui, Fan Yang, Yueqi Song, Yonatan Bisk

description: EgoTempo addresses limitations in current egocentric video QA datasets where questions can be answered using few frames or commonsense reasoning. It specifically evaluates temporal understanding in the egocentric domain, emphasizing tasks that require integrating information across entire videos rather than relying on static cues.
paper_title: Omnia de EgoTempo: Benchmarking Temporal Understanding of Multi-Modal LLMs in Egocentric Videos
authors: Chiara Plizzari, Alessio Tonioni, Yongqin Xian, Achin Kulshrestha, Federico Tombari

task_types: egocentric video QA, scene graph reasoning
description: EASG-Bench creates question-answering pairs from spatio-temporally grounded dynamic scene graphs capturing intricate relationships among actors, actions, and objects in egocentric videos. It provides systematic evaluation framework identifying research gaps in long-context video understanding, particularly temporal ordering tasks.
paper_title: EASG-Bench: Video Q&A Benchmark with Egocentric Action Scene Graphs
authors: Ivan Rodin, Tz-Ying Wu, Kyle Min, Sharath Nittur Sridhar, Antonino Furnari

description: UrbanVideo-Bench evaluates Video-LLMs on embodied intelligence in urban spaces using first-person drone-collected video data. It tests recall, perception, reasoning, and navigation capabilities in real-world and simulated urban environments with multiple-choice questions.
paper_title: UrbanVideo-Bench: Benchmarking Vision-Language Models on Embodied Intelligence with Video Data in Urban Spaces
authors: Baining Zhao, Jianjie Fang, Zichao Dai, Ziyou Wang, Jirong Zha

description: EgoCVR introduces fine-grained Composed Video Retrieval using egocentric video datasets, where models must retrieve videos based on a reference video and textual modification. It focuses on high-quality temporal video understanding for composed retrieval tasks in egocentric settings.
paper_title: EgoCVR: An Egocentric Benchmark for Fine-Grained Composed Video Retrieval
authors: Thomas Hummel, Shyamgopal Karthik, Mariana-Iuliana Georgescu, Zeynep Akata

task_types: web agent execution, egocentric visual understanding
description: The first benchmark designed to bridge egocentric video perception and web agent execution, pairing real-world first-person video recordings with web tasks that require visual understanding, web task planning, and online interaction. Addresses the critical limitation of existing web-agent benchmarks that lack grounding in users' real-world physical surroundings.
paper_title: Ego2Web: A Web Agent Benchmark Grounded in Egocentric Videos
authors: Shoubin Yu, Lei Shu, Antoine Yang, Yao Fu, Srinivas Sunkara

description: LaMPilot-Bench is part of a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating instruction-following and reasoning abilities of LLM4AD systems, including CARLA Leaderboard 1.0 simulation and NuPlanQA for multi-view visual question answering. It specifically targets autonomous driving applications with real-world experiments on vehicle platforms.
paper_title: LLM4AD: Large Language Models for Autonomous Driving -- Concept, Review, Benchmark, Experiments, and Future Trends
authors: Can Cui, Yunsheng Ma, Sung-Yeon Park, Zichong Yang, Yupeng Zhou

description: WearVQA is the first benchmark specifically designed for VQA on wearable devices like smart glasses, featuring ego-centric imagery with realistic quality issues (occlusion, poor lighting, blur). It spans 7 image domains, 10 cognitive task types, and 6 common wearables-specific image quality problems.
paper_title: WearVQA: A Visual Question Answering Benchmark for Wearables in Egocentric Authentic Real-world scenarios
authors: Eun Chang, Zhuangqun Huang, Yiwei Liao, Sagar Ravi Bhavsar, Amogh Param

task_types: cooperative multi-agent learning, heterogeneous robot collaboration
description: Multimodal benchmark for cooperative heterogeneous multi-agent reinforcement learning with simulated robots in home environment. Combines vision and language modalities for collaborative task evaluation.
paper_title: CH-MARL: A Multimodal Benchmark for Cooperative, Heterogeneous Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
authors: Vasu Sharma, Prasoon Goyal, Kaixiang Lin, Govind Thattai, Qiaozi Gao

task_types: trajectory prediction, scenario-based evaluation, pedestrian behavior prediction
description: This paper presents a new benchmarking paradigm for egocentric pedestrian trajectory prediction that extracts driving scenarios based on contextual information for systematic evaluation. It uniquely proposes scenario-based analysis with new metrics to identify specific challenges for prediction models and expose model strengths/shortcomings.
paper_title: A Novel Benchmarking Paradigm and a Scale- and Motion-Aware Model for Egocentric Pedestrian Trajectory Prediction
authors: Amir Rasouli

task_types: robotic manipulation, protocol execution, laboratory automation
description: AutoBio evaluates robotic automation in biology laboratory environments, combining structured protocols with demanding precision and multimodal interaction. It uniquely extends simulation capabilities with digitized real-world laboratory instruments, specialized physics plugins, and biologically grounded tasks spanning three difficulty levels for language-guided robotic manipulation.
paper_title: AutoBio: A Simulation and Benchmark for Robotic Automation in Digital Biology Laboratory
authors: Zhiqian Lan, Yuxuan Jiang, Ruiqi Wang, Xuanbing Xie, Rongkui Zhang

description: EgoThink evaluates VLMs' first-person perspective thinking capabilities across six core capabilities with twelve detailed dimensions. It uniquely assesses models' ability to reason from an egocentric viewpoint using manually annotated question-answer pairs from egocentric videos.
paper_title: EgoThink: Evaluating First-Person Perspective Thinking Capability of Vision-Language Models
authors: Sijie Cheng, Zhicheng Guo, Jingwen Wu, Kechen Fang, Peng Li

description: A plug-and-play benchmark for evaluating embodied intelligence using static scene representations instead of interactive simulations. Covers 42 diverse scenarios and 8 core dimensions, providing scalable assessment through a unified interface.
paper_title: Static and Plugged: Make Embodied Evaluation Simple
authors: Jiahao Xiao, Jianbo Zhang, BoWen Yan, Shengyu Guo, Tongrui Ye

description: Curated collection of challenging driving scenarios where context is critical for correct judgment, annotated with pairwise human preferences. Tests context-aware evaluation of autonomous driving planners using Vision-Language Models.
paper_title: DriveCritic: Towards Context-Aware, Human-Aligned Evaluation for Autonomous Driving with Vision-Language Models
authors: Jingyu Song, Zhenxin Li, Shiyi Lan, Xinglong Sun, Nadine Chang

task_types: world modeling, sequence reordering, embodied reasoning
description: ENACT evaluates embodied cognition through world modeling from egocentric interaction using two sequence reordering tasks: forward and inverse world modeling. It uniquely tests affordance recognition, action-effect reasoning, and embodied awareness using robotics simulation data formatted as VQA.
paper_title: ENACT: Evaluating Embodied Cognition with World Modeling of Egocentric Interaction
authors: Qineng Wang, Wenlong Huang, Yu Zhou, Hang Yin, Tianwei Bao

task_types: visual question answering, navigation planning, trace generation
description: Visual Question Answering benchmark where models output 2D navigation traces in image space given instructions and embodiment types. Tests spatial grounding and goal localization across different robot embodiments with semantic-aware trace scoring.
paper_title: NaviTrace: Evaluating Embodied Navigation of Vision-Language Models
authors: Tim Windecker, Manthan Patel, Moritz Reuss, Richard Schwarzkopf, Cesar Cadena

task_types: navigation, object interaction, social interaction, attribute question answering, spatial question answering
description: Comprehensive interactive evaluation benchmark for MLLMs with embodied tasks in 125 varied 3D scenes. Tests navigation, object interaction, social interaction, and spatial reasoning capabilities within unified simulation framework.
paper_title: EmbodiedEval: Evaluate Multimodal LLMs as Embodied Agents
authors: Zhili Cheng, Yuge Tu, Ran Li, Shiqi Dai, Jinyi Hu

task_types: temporal scene understanding, visual explanation, question answering
description: PDB-Eval provides a benchmark for understanding Personalized Driver Behavior through two components: PDB-X for evaluating temporal driving scene understanding and PDB-QA for visual explanation question-answering. It focuses on finding valid visual evidence from external view to explain driver behavior from internal view, bridging MLLMs with driving comprehension.
paper_title: PDB-Eval: An Evaluation of Large Multimodal Models for Description and Explanation of Personalized Driving Behavior
authors: Junda Wu, Jessica Echterhoff, Kyungtae Han, Amr Abdelraouf, Rohit Gupta

task_types: action recognition, temporal detection, captioning, question answering
description: EPIC-KITCHENS-100-MQA reformulates the EPIC-KITCHENS-100 egocentric action recognition dataset into an MLLM benchmark for fine-grained action understanding. It uses difficult answers from specialist models as distractors and includes tasks for action recognition, temporal detection, captioning, and free-form question answering to improve diverse action understanding capabilities.
paper_title: LLaVAction: evaluating and training multi-modal large language models for action understanding
authors: Haozhe Qi, Shaokai Ye, Alexander Mathis, Mackenzie W. Mathis

description: Proposes a language-driven testing and evaluation platform for Vision-Language-Action models in robotic manipulation that automatically generates simulation environments from natural language inputs. Features paraphrase mechanisms for diverse task instructions and batch-style evaluation methods.
paper_title: LADEV: A Language-Driven Testing and Evaluation Platform for Vision-Language-Action Models in Robotic Manipulation
authors: Zhijie Wang, Zhehua Zhou, Jiayang Song, Yuheng Huang, Zhan Shu

task_types: decision making, embodied reasoning, perception-cognition-action integration
description: Presents a multimodal decision-making benchmark with three complex scenarios (autonomous driving, domestic robotics, open-world games) that evaluates integrated Perception-Cognition-Action chain capabilities. Features error localization capabilities and introduces PCA-Eval automatic evaluation protocol.
paper_title: PCA-Bench: Evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models in Perception-Cognition-Action Chain
authors: Liang Chen, Yichi Zhang, Shuhuai Ren, Haozhe Zhao, Zefan Cai

task_types: object understanding, spatial intelligence, social activity
description: Set of composite tasks inspired by common daily activities in early childhood development within a dynamic simulated home environment. Spans three core domains: object understanding, spatial intelligence, and social activity to evaluate general capabilities of embodied agents powered by MLLMs.
paper_title: Evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models with Daily Composite Tasks in Home Environments
authors: Zhenliang Zhang, Yuxi Wang, Hongzhao Xie, Shiyun Zhao, Mingyuan Liu

task_types: spatial reasoning, object permanence, embodied reasoning
description: Evaluates embodied spatial reasoning through multi-frame trajectories in controllable 3D environments, testing object permanence, spatial relationships, and numerical tracking across dynamic viewpoints. Addresses a critical limitation in MLLMs for embodied applications requiring viewpoint-independent cognitive mapping.
paper_title: REM: Evaluating LLM Embodied Spatial Reasoning through Multi-Frame Trajectories
authors: Jacob Thompson, Emiliano Garcia-Lopez, Yonatan Bisk

task_types: video editing evaluation, instruction faithfulness, hand preservation, temporal stability
description: Introduces EgoEditBench, an evaluation suite for instruction-guided editing of egocentric videos, targeting instruction faithfulness, hand and interaction preservation, and temporal stability under egomotion. The benchmark addresses unique challenges in egocentric video editing including rapid egomotion and frequent hand-object interactions.
paper_title: EgoEdit: Dataset, Real-Time Streaming Model, and Benchmark for Egocentric Video Editing
authors: Runjia Li, Moayed Haji-Ali, Ashkan Mirzaei, Chaoyang Wang, Arpit Sahni

num_samples: 272K manual semantic masks, 9.9M interpolated dense masks, 67K hand-object relations
task_types: video object segmentation, interaction understanding, long-term reasoning
description: Introduces VISOR, a dataset for segmenting hands and active objects in egocentric video with pixel-level annotations. The benchmark covers 36 hours of 179 untrimmed videos with three challenges in video object segmentation, interaction understanding and long-term reasoning.
paper_title: EPIC-KITCHENS VISOR Benchmark: VIdeo Segmentations and Object Relations
authors: Ahmad Darkhalil, Dandan Shan, Bin Zhu, Jian Ma, Amlan Kar

num_samples: over 30 km of recorded trajectories, 40,000 m² coverage
description: Introduces Oxford Day-and-Night, a large-scale egocentric dataset for novel view synthesis and visual relocalisation under challenging lighting conditions. The dataset addresses gaps in existing datasets by providing ground-truth 3D geometry, wide-ranging lighting variation, and full 6DoF motion.
paper_title: Seeing in the Dark: Benchmarking Egocentric 3D Vision with the Oxford Day-and-Night Dataset
authors: Zirui Wang, Wenjing Bian, Xinghui Li, Yifu Tao, Jianeng Wang

num_samples: over 14 hours per participant, 3,291 human-verified QA items
description: Introduces TeleEgo, a long-duration streaming omni-modal benchmark for evaluating egocentric AI assistants. The benchmark features synchronized egocentric video, audio, and text across four domains with 12 diagnostic subtasks testing memory, understanding, and cross-memory reasoning capabilities.
paper_title: TeleEgo: Benchmarking Egocentric AI Assistants in the Wild
authors: Jiaqi Yan, Ruilong Ren, Jingren Liu, Shuning Xu, Ling Wang

task_types: embodied question answering, urban navigation, spatial reasoning
description: CityEQA-EC is the first benchmark dataset for Embodied Question Answering in urban environments, featuring 1,412 human-annotated tasks across six categories in a realistic 3D urban simulator. It addresses the gap in EQA research by focusing on dynamic city spaces rather than indoor environments.
paper_title: CityEQA: A Hierarchical LLM Agent on Embodied Question Answering Benchmark in City Space
authors: Yong Zhao, Kai Xu, Zhengqiu Zhu, Yue Hu, Zhiheng Zheng

description: An open world survival game with visual inputs that evaluates a wide range of general abilities within a single environment. Agents are evaluated by semantically meaningful achievements that require strong generalization, deep exploration, and long-term reasoning.
paper_title: Benchmarking the Spectrum of Agent Capabilities
authors: Danijar Hafner

task_types: seat arrangement, social reasoning, preference acquisition, multi-objective optimization
description: Evaluates embodied social intelligence through a novel seat-ordering task requiring agents to arrange seating in 3D environments for LLM-driven NPCs with diverse identities and relationships. Integrates both social norms and physical constraints within realistic embodied contexts.
paper_title: S$^3$IT: A Benchmark for Spatially Situated Social Intelligence Test
authors: Zhe Sun, Xueyuan Yang, Yujie Lu, Zhenliang Zhang

task_types: embodied question answering, exploration, visual reasoning
description: The largest dataset designed specifically to evaluate both exploration and reasoning capabilities in embodied question answering. Features 777 exploration trajectories and 2,044 question-trajectory pairs with novel EAC metric measuring exploration-answer consistency.
paper_title: Beyond the Destination: A Novel Benchmark for Exploration-Aware Embodied Question Answering
authors: Kaixuan Jiang, Yang Liu, Weixing Chen, Jingzhou Luo, Ziliang Chen

description: Evaluates embodied agents in realistic indoor cleaning scenarios using mobile service robots with dual-mode capabilities (sweeping and grasping). Built on NVIDIA Isaac Sim with comprehensive evaluation covering task completion, spatial efficiency, and motion quality.
paper_title: CleanUpBench: Embodied Sweeping and Grasping Benchmark
authors: Wenbo Li, Guanting Chen, Tao Zhao, Jiyao Wang, Tianxin Hu

task_types: collaborative navigation, intention reasoning, human activity perception
description: A collaborative navigation benchmark where agents must reason about human intention by observing activities and navigate to intended destinations in advance. Features novel LLM-based humanoid animation generation framework for realistic human activities in 3D environments.
paper_title: CoNav: A Benchmark for Human-Centered Collaborative Navigation
authors: Changhao Li, Xinyu Sun, Peihao Chen, Jugang Fan, Zixu Wang

task_types: personalized object navigation, object localization, user-specific reasoning
description: A comprehensive benchmark for personalized embodied agents comprising over 2,000 episodes across 30+ photorealistic homes. Agents must identify, retrieve, and navigate to objects associated with specific users, requiring reasoning over user-specific semantics.
paper_title: PersONAL: Towards a Comprehensive Benchmark for Personalized Embodied Agents
authors: Filippo Ziliotto, Jelin Raphael Akkara, Alessandro Daniele, Lamberto Ballan, Luciano Serafini

description: CityAVOS is the first benchmark dataset for autonomous UAV visual object search in urban environments. It provides 2,420 tasks across six object categories with varying difficulty levels, enabling evaluation of UAV agents' capabilities to search for and identify target objects using visual and textual cues without external guidance.
paper_title: Towards Autonomous UAV Visual Object Search in City Space: Benchmark and Agentic Methodology
authors: Yatai Ji, Zhengqiu Zhu, Yong Zhao, Beidan Liu, Chen Gao

task_types: risk-aware manipulation, safety reasoning, robotic planning
description: ResponsibleRobotBench is a systematic benchmark designed to evaluate responsible robotic manipulation from simulation to real world. It consists of 23 multi-stage tasks spanning diverse risk types (electrical, chemical, human-related hazards) requiring agents to detect and mitigate risks, reason about safety, and engage human assistance when necessary.
paper_title: ResponsibleRobotBench: Benchmarking Responsible Robot Manipulation using Multi-modal Large Language Models
authors: Lei Zhang, Ju Dong, Kaixin Bai, Minheng Ni, Zoltan-Csaba Marton

description: HOTD-Bench evaluates Human-centric Open-future Task Discovery, focusing on identifying tasks that reduce human effort across plausible futures. It features over 2K real-world videos with a semi-automated annotation pipeline and simulation-based protocol for open-set future evaluation.
paper_title: Human-Centric Open-Future Task Discovery: Formulation, Benchmark, and Scalable Tree-Based Search
authors: Zijian Song, Xiaoxin Lin, Tao Pu, Zhenlong Yuan, Guangrun Wang

description: UAV-ON is a benchmark for large-scale Object Goal Navigation by aerial agents in open-world environments. It comprises 14 high-fidelity Unreal Engine environments with 1270 annotated target objects characterized by instance-level instructions that introduce realistic ambiguity and complex reasoning challenges.
paper_title: UAV-ON: A Benchmark for Open-World Object Goal Navigation with Aerial Agents
authors: Jianqiang Xiao, Yuexuan Sun, Yixin Shao, Boxi Gan, Rongqiang Liu

task_types: robot manipulation, multi-step reasoning, physical measurement
description: Novel simulation environment (MuBlE) built on MuJoCo and Blender for long-horizon robot manipulation tasks with accurate physics. SHOP-VRB2 benchmark comprises 10 classes of multi-step reasoning scenarios requiring simultaneous visual and physical measurements.
paper_title: MuBlE: MuJoCo and Blender simulation Environment and Benchmark for Task Planning in Robot Manipulation
authors: Michal Nazarczuk, Karla Stepanova, Jan Kristof Behrens, Matej Hoffmann, Krystian Mikolajczyk

task_types: code generation, robot manipulation, perception, control
description: A framework for evaluating Code-as-Policy agents in robot manipulation through CaP-Gym interactive environment and CaP-Bench evaluation suite. Tests agents across varying levels of abstraction, interaction, and perceptual grounding for embodied coding tasks.
paper_title: CaP-X: A Framework for Benchmarking and Improving Coding Agents for Robot Manipulation
authors: Max Fu, Justin Yu, Karim El-Refai, Ethan Kou, Haoru Xue

task_types: robotic manipulation, long-horizon planning, language conditioning
description: Long-horizon language-conditioned benchmark for robotic tabletop manipulation covering color, size, space, arithmetic and reference reasoning. Tests long-horizon reasoning capabilities without expensive annotated demonstrations in simulation environment.
paper_title: LoHoRavens: A Long-Horizon Language-Conditioned Benchmark for Robotic Tabletop Manipulation
authors: Shengqiang Zhang, Philipp Wicke, Lütfi Kerem Şenel, Luis Figueredo, Abdeldjallil Naceri

task_types: object arrangement, long-horizon planning, spatial reasoning, manipulation
description: First benchmark specifically designed for complex object arrangement scenarios in robotic manipulation. Tests agents' abilities to perform long-horizon planning for spatial reasoning and predicting chain reactions under ambiguous instructions, including target retrieval, obstacle clearance, and robot manipulation in realistic physical simulation environments.
paper_title: RoboCAS: A Benchmark for Robotic Manipulation in Complex Object Arrangement Scenarios
authors: Liming Zheng, Feng Yan, Fanfan Liu, Chengjian Feng, Zhuoliang Kang

num_samples: 30 distinct tasks with more than 200 scene and instrument assets
task_types: scientific manipulation, long-horizon planning, atomic actions, mobile manipulation
description: Hierarchical benchmark spanning five levels of complexity from atomic actions to long-horizon mobile manipulation in laboratory settings. Tests scientific embodied agents in high-fidelity simulation supporting multi-physics and chemically meaningful interactions, addressing significantly higher demands for perception of physical-chemical transformations.
paper_title: LabUtopia: High-Fidelity Simulation and Hierarchical Benchmark for Scientific Embodied Agents
authors: Rui Li, Zixuan Hu, Wenxi Qu, Jinouwen Zhang, Zhenfei Yin
