task_types: VQA, UI grounding, action generation, state prediction, result verification
description: Embodied, task-driven benchmark evaluating five complementary abilities: task-aware VQA, semantic UI grounding, action generation, state transition prediction, and result verification. Tests interaction with tangible control interfaces under ego-centric RGB video input across diverse real-world devices and appliances.
paper_title: SWITCH: Benchmarking Modeling and Handling of Tangible Interfaces in Long-horizon Embodied Scenarios
authors: Jieru Lin, Zhiwei Yu, Börje F. Karlsson

num_samples: 100,000 natural language tasks spanning 60 houses and 5,819 unique objects
task_types: human-robot collaboration, household activities, planning, reasoning
description: Largest benchmark for planning and reasoning in human-robot collaboration for household activities. Uses semi-automated task generation pipeline with LLMs incorporating simulation for grounding and verification, exhibiting spatial, temporal, and heterogeneous agent capability constraints characteristic of everyday tasks.
paper_title: PARTNR: A Benchmark for Planning and Reasoning in Embodied Multi-agent Tasks
authors: Matthew Chang, Gunjan Chhablani, Alexander Clegg, Mikael Dallaire Cote, Ruta Desai

description: QAsk-Nav is the first reproducible benchmark for Collaborative Instance Object Navigation (CoIN) that enables separate assessment of embodied navigation and collaborative question asking. It provides a lightweight question-asking protocol scored independently of navigation and an enhanced navigation protocol with realistic target descriptions for evaluating human-agent dialogue in navigation tasks.
paper_title: Benchmarking Interaction, Beyond Policy: a Reproducible Benchmark for Collaborative Instance Object Navigation
authors: Edoardo Zorzi, Francesco Taioli, Yiming Wang, Marco Cristani, Alessandro Farinelli

description: IndoorUAV introduces vision-language navigation for indoor UAVs with over 1,000 diverse 3D indoor scenes and realistic UAV flight dynamics. It yields over 16,000 high-quality trajectories with two subsets: IndoorUAV-VLN for long-horizon navigation and IndoorUAV-VLA for short-horizon planning.
paper_title: IndoorUAV: Benchmarking Vision-Language UAV Navigation in Continuous Indoor Environments
authors: Xu Liu, Yu Liu, Hanshuo Qiu, Yang Qirong, Zhouhui Lian

description: AbstainEQA focuses on abstention in Embodied Question Answering with 1,636 annotated abstention cases across five representative categories requiring agents to know when to withhold answers. It addresses the fundamental prerequisite of knowing when insufficient information is available for reliable interaction.
paper_title: When Robots Should Say "I Don't Know": Benchmarking Abstention in Embodied Question Answering
authors: Tao Wu, Chuhao Zhou, Guangyu Zhao, Haozhi Cao, Yewen Pu

task_types: scene awareness, spatial reasoning, navigation, task planning, motion decision
description: AeroVerse constructs the first large-scale real-world image-text pre-training dataset for urban drones from first-person perspective, defining 5 downstream tasks for aerospace embodied intelligence. It integrates over 10 visual-language models, datasets, evaluation metrics, and a simulator for UAV-agent evaluation.
paper_title: AeroVerse: UAV-Agent Benchmark Suite for Simulating, Pre-training, Finetuning, and Evaluating Aerospace Embodied World Models
authors: Fanglong Yao, Yuanchang Yue, Youzhi Liu, Xian Sun, Kun Fu

description: EmbodiedCity constructs a highly realistic 3D simulation environment based on real city elements with historically collected data and simulation algorithms for pedestrian/vehicle flows. It expands embodied intelligence evaluation from bounded indoor environments to open-world outdoor city scenarios.
paper_title: EmbodiedCity: A Benchmark Platform for Embodied Agent in Real-world City Environment
authors: Chen Gao, Baining Zhao, Weichen Zhang, Jinzhu Mao, Jun Zhang

description: VLABench provides 100 carefully designed categories of language-conditioned robotics manipulation tasks with 2000+ objects, featuring natural language instructions with implicit intentions, long-horizon tasks requiring multi-step reasoning, and evaluation of both action policies and language capabilities. It emphasizes world knowledge transfer and common sense reasoning in robotics contexts.
paper_title: VLABench: A Large-Scale Benchmark for Language-Conditioned Robotics Manipulation with Long-Horizon Reasoning Tasks
authors: Shiduo Zhang, Zhe Xu, Peiju Liu, Xiaopeng Yu, Yuan Li

task_types: physical manipulation, embodied reasoning, multimodal grounding
description: ManipulationNet is a global infrastructure hosting real-world benchmark tasks for robotic manipulation with standardized hardware kits and unified software clients. It organizes tasks into Physical Skills Track (low-level interaction) and Embodied Reasoning Track (high-level multimodal reasoning) to systematically evaluate manipulation capabilities.
paper_title: ManipulationNet: An Infrastructure for Benchmarking Real-World Robot Manipulation with Physical Skill Challenges and Embodied Multimodal Reasoning
authors: Yiting Chen, Kenneth Kimble, Edward H. Adelson, Tamim Asfour, Podshara Chanrungmaneekul

description: M3Bench is a benchmark for whole-body motion generation in mobile manipulation tasks featuring 30,000 object rearrangement tasks across 119 diverse scenes. It evaluates embodied agents' ability to coordinate base-arm motion while adhering to environmental and task-specific constraints in 3D scene contexts.
paper_title: M3Bench: Benchmarking Whole-body Motion Generation for Mobile Manipulation in 3D Scenes
authors: Zeyu Zhang, Sixu Yan, Muzhi Han, Zaijin Wang, Xinggang Wang

description: EcoG-Bench is an evaluation-only bilingual diagnostic benchmark of 811 egocentric clips with dense spatial annotations and millisecond-level stroke supervision for Egocentric Co-Speech Grounding. It tests whether agents can jointly predict What, Where, and When in situated collaboration scenarios requiring audio-visual alignment of deictic commands.
paper_title: Listening with the Eyes: Benchmarking Egocentric Co-Speech Grounding across Space and Time
authors: Weijie Zhou, Xuantang Xiong, Zhenlin Hu, Xiaomeng Zhu, Chaoyang Zhao

description: WorldArena is a unified benchmark designed to systematically evaluate embodied world models across both perceptual and functional dimensions. It assesses models through video perception quality, embodied task functionality as data engines/policy evaluators/action planners, and introduces EWMScore as a holistic metric integrating multi-dimensional performance.
paper_title: WorldArena: A Unified Benchmark for Evaluating Perception and Functional Utility of Embodied World Models
authors: Yu Shang, Zhuohang Li, Yiding Ma, Weikang Su, Xin Jin

description: A commercial scenario simulation for embodied delivery in multi-level connected building spaces modeled after polar research stations. Features autonomous human characters, robots with grasping/mobility capabilities, and 13k language instructions for service delivery tasks.
paper_title: Human-centered In-building Embodied Delivery Benchmark
authors: Zhuoqun Xu, Yang Liu, Xiaoqi Li, Jiyao Zhang, Hao Dong

benchmark_name: Ecologically Valid Benchmarks for Situated Collaboration
description: An interactive system-driven approach where questions are generated by users during real interactions with situated AI systems, rather than post-hoc generation. Focuses on ecologically valid assessment of situated collaboration capabilities.
paper_title: "Is This It?": Towards Ecologically Valid Benchmarks for Situated Collaboration
authors: Dan Bohus, Sean Andrist, Yuwei Bao, Eric Horvitz, Ann Paradiso

description: A large-scale multimodal dataset for egocentric robot perception in crowded/unstructured environments with 133K synchronized data, 1.4M 3D bounding boxes across full 360° view, and novel near-field 3D perception metrics. Supports 6 popular tasks.
paper_title: RoboSense: Large-scale Dataset and Benchmark for Egocentric Robot Perception and Navigation in Crowded and Unstructured Environments
authors: Haisheng Su, Feixiang Song, Cong Ma, Wei Wu, Junchi Yan

description: A scalable framework for automated large-scale generation of diverse bimanual manipulation data with structured domain randomization. Features RoboTwin-OD object library with 731 instances across 147 categories and spans 50 dual-arm tasks.
paper_title: RoboTwin 2.0: A Scalable Data Generator and Benchmark with Strong Domain Randomization for Robust Bimanual Robotic Manipulation
authors: Tianxing Chen, Zanxin Chen, Baijun Chen, Zijian Cai, Yibin Liu

description: A benchmark to assess generalization capabilities of vision-language robotic manipulation policies with seven action primitives and four generalization levels spanning novel placements, objects, and long-horizon tasks.
paper_title: Towards Generalizable Vision-Language Robotic Manipulation: A Benchmark and LLM-guided 3D Policy
authors: Ricardo Garcia, Shizhe Chen, Cordelia Schmid

description: A benchmark for High-Level UAV Vision-Language-Action tasks testing concise language interpretation and complex process-oriented trajectories with safety awareness. Built on 3D Gaussian Splatting-Mesh representation with collision-aware evaluation.
paper_title: HUGE-Bench: A Benchmark for High-Level UAV Vision-Language-Action Tasks
authors: Jingyu Guo, Ziye Chen, Ziwen Li, Zhengqing Gao, Jiaxin Huang

description: A unified benchmark for language-guided household tidying using 500 photorealistic 3D Gaussian Splatting scenes with 500 objects/containers, providing 6.4k manipulation and 1.5k navigation trajectories. Supports both VLA and VLN training.
paper_title: RoboTidy : A 3D Gaussian Splatting Household Tidying Benchmark for Embodied Navigation and Action
authors: Xiaoquan Sun, Ruijian Zhang, Kang Pang, Bingchen Miao, Yuxiang Tan

description: A physical benchmark for systematic evaluation of VLAs' affordance generalization using modular components (switches, sliders, buttons, etc.) that can be swapped and rotated to create variants with same affordances but different appearances.
paper_title: Benchmarking Affordance Generalization with BusyBox
authors: Dean Fortier, Timothy Adamson, Tess Hellebrekers, Teresa LaScala, Kofi Ennin

task_types: bias measurement, robotic manipulation, visual perception
description: First benchmark specifically designed to systematically quantify visual bias in robotic manipulation following factor isolation principle. Creates task instances to measure biases from individual visual factors and their interactions in embodied agents.
paper_title: RoboView-Bias: Benchmarking Visual Bias in Embodied Agents for Robotic Manipulation
authors: Enguang Liu, Siyuan Liang, Liming Lu, Xiyu Zeng, Xiaochun Cao

task_types: driving simulation, trajectory prediction, world modeling
description: DrivingGen is the first comprehensive benchmark for generative driving world models that combines diverse evaluation datasets from driving datasets and internet-scale video sources. It introduces new metrics to jointly assess visual realism, trajectory plausibility, temporal coherence, and controllability in autonomous driving scenarios.
paper_title: DrivingGen: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Generative Video World Models in Autonomous Driving
authors: Yang Zhou, Hao Shao, Letian Wang, Zhuofan Zong, Hongsheng Li

description: Bench2Drive-R is a generative framework that enables reactive closed-loop evaluation for end-to-end autonomous driving. Unlike existing methods, it decouples sensor rendering and behavior rollout by applying a separate behavioral controller to simulate reactions of surrounding agents.
paper_title: Bench2Drive-R: Turning Real World Data into Reactive Closed-Loop Autonomous Driving Benchmark by Generative Model
authors: Junqi You, Xiaosong Jia, Zhiyuan Zhang, Yutao Zhu, Junchi Yan

task_types: task planning, low-level control, language instruction following
description: Kitchen-R unifies evaluation of task planning and low-level control in a simulated kitchen environment with over 500 complex language instructions. It bridges the gap between high-level language instruction benchmarks and low-level control benchmarks for mobile manipulation.
paper_title: Mind and Motion Aligned: A Joint Evaluation IsaacSim Benchmark for Task Planning and Low-Level Policies in Mobile Manipulation
authors: Nikita Kachaev, Andrei Spiridonov, Andrey Gorodetsky, Kirill Muravyev, Nikita Oskolkov

description: First benchmark pioneering LLM application for automated evaluation in robotic manipulation with VLM-based assessment pipeline. Includes comprehensive simulation platform with over 10,000 hours of synthetic data across 200+ tasks for humanoid robot evaluation.
paper_title: Genie Sim 3.0 : A High-Fidelity Comprehensive Simulation Platform for Humanoid Robot
authors: Chenghao Yin, Da Huang, Di Yang, Jichao Wang, Nanshu Zhao

description: Evaluates multi-perspective Theory of Mind in embodied multi-agent social interactions with both first-person and third-person evaluation methods. Contains 35 third-person videos, 363 first-person images, and expert-annotated multiple-choice questions covering diverse crafting goals and social relationships.
paper_title: SoMi-ToM: Evaluating Multi-Perspective Theory of Mind in Embodied Social Interactions
authors: Xianzhe Fan, Xuhui Zhou, Chuanyang Jin, Kolby Nottingham, Hao Zhu

description: Model-agnostic robot agent platform and benchmark for open-world manipulation in unstructured environments. Provides closed-loop agent pipeline for perception, task decomposition, online verification, and replanning with real-robot evaluation.
paper_title: AgenticLab: A Real-World Robot Agent Platform that Can See, Think, and Act
authors: Pengyuan Guo, Zhonghao Mai, Zhengtong Xu, Kaidi Zhang, Heng Zhang

task_types: tabletop manipulation, few-shot learning, long-horizon mobile manipulation
description: A large-scale collection of robot behaviors recorded in authentic human living/working environments with consistent robotic embodiment and precise subtask-level language annotations. Enables training and evaluation of tabletop manipulation, few-shot learning, and long-horizon mobile manipulation.
paper_title: Galaxea Open-World Dataset and G0 Dual-System VLA Model
authors: Tao Jiang, Tianyuan Yuan, Yicheng Liu, Chenhao Lu, Jianning Cui

task_types: high-level embodied tasks, low-level skill evaluation
description: A challenging benchmark using unified, native low-level action space for VLM-driven embodied agents. Includes three high-level tasks in complex scenarios and four types of low-level tasks targeting fundamental embodied skills, enabling joint evaluation across task and skill granularities.
paper_title: How Foundational Skills Influence VLM-based Embodied Agents:A Native Perspective
authors: Bo Peng, Pi Bu, Keyu Pan, Xinrun Xu, Yinxiu Zhao

task_types: form correction, movement analysis, real-time feedback
description: Introduces a dataset of 1,700 expert-annotated user-reference video pairs spanning 22 strength and mobility exercises for evaluating VLMs' ability to provide real-time form correction in fitness training. The benchmark specifically tests vision-language models' capability to detect subtle movement errors and provide tailored corrections for exercise form.
paper_title: FormCoach: Lift Smarter, Not Harder
authors: Xiaoye Zuo, Nikos Athanasiou, Ginger Delmas, Yiming Huang, Xingyu Fu

task_types: physical reachability reasoning, embodied question answering
description: Constructs a challenging benchmark that includes tasks for six different robots in both simulated and real-world environments to evaluate vision-language models' understanding of robotic physical reachability. Tests models' ability to reason about physical constraints and spatial relationships for embodied AI applications.
paper_title: PhysVLM: Enabling Visual Language Models to Understand Robotic Physical Reachability
authors: Weijie Zhou, Manli Tao, Chaoyang Zhao, Haiyun Guo, Honghui Dong

task_types: embodied reasoning, navigation, spatial reasoning, temporal reasoning
description: Introduces a closed-loop benchmark designed as a Turing test for embodied reasoning across three heterogeneous embodiments (autonomous vehicles, aerial drones, robotic manipulators) with approximately 1.1K one-shot reasoning questions and 58 goal-directed navigation tasks. Evaluates four foundational dimensions: semantic, spatial, temporal, and physical reasoning.
paper_title: Embodied4C: Measuring What Matters for Embodied Vision-Language Navigation
authors: Tin Stribor Sohn, Maximilian Dillitzer, Jason J. Corso, Eric Sax

task_types: high-level planning, fine-grained scene understanding, spatial-functional reasoning
description: MomaGraph-Bench is a systematic evaluation suite for embodied task planning that spans six reasoning capabilities from high-level planning to fine-grained scene understanding. It evaluates unified scene representations that integrate spatial-functional relationships and part-level interactive elements in household environments.
paper_title: MomaGraph: State-Aware Unified Scene Graphs with Vision-Language Model for Embodied Task Planning
authors: Yuanchen Ju, Yongyuan Liang, Yen-Jen Wang, Nandiraju Gireesh, Yuanliang Ju

description: EgoOrientBench is a benchmark that evaluates multimodal large language models' orientation understanding across three tasks using images collected from diverse domains. It specifically focuses on assessing models' ability to understand object orientation from the user's egocentric viewpoint, addressing inconsistencies in orientation annotations in training data.
paper_title: Is 'Right' Right? Enhancing Object Orientation Understanding in Multimodal Large Language Models through Egocentric Instruction Tuning
authors: Ji Hyeok Jung, Eun Tae Kim, Seoyeon Kim, Joo Ho Lee, Bumsoo Kim

task_types: video question-answering, hierarchy planning, visual grounding, reward modeling
description: A comprehensive benchmark for evaluating egocentric video understanding capabilities for Embodied AI. It includes four key tasks: video question-answering, hierarchy planning, visual grounding and reward modeling, with data automatically generated from Ego4D dataset using GPT-4o.
paper_title: VidEgoThink: Assessing Egocentric Video Understanding Capabilities for Embodied AI
authors: Sijie Cheng, Kechen Fang, Yangyang Yu, Sicheng Zhou, Bohao Li

task_types: multi-hop video question answering, temporal grounding
description: A high-quality benchmark for Multi-Hop Video Question Answering in long-form egocentric videos. It requires not only answering visual questions but also localizing multiple relevant time intervals within the video as visual evidence.
paper_title: Grounded Multi-Hop VideoQA in Long-Form Egocentric Videos
authors: Qirui Chen, Shangzhe Di, Weidi Xie

task_types: video question answering, open QA, close QA
description: A refined dataset of egocentric videos for Video Question Answering derived from QaEgo4D. Mitigates annotation noise in the original dataset to enable more reliable comparison of model performance on egocentric video understanding tasks.
paper_title: Advancing Egocentric Video Question Answering with Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Alkesh Patel, Vibhav Chitalia, Yinfei Yang

description: EVUD (Egocentric Video Understanding Dataset) is specifically designed for training VLMs on video captioning and question answering tasks for egocentric videos. It addresses the gap in current VLMs that primarily focus on third-person view videos, enabling embodied understanding for AI assistants.
paper_title: AlanaVLM: A Multimodal Embodied AI Foundation Model for Egocentric Video Understanding
authors: Alessandro Suglia, Claudio Greco, Katie Baker, Jose L. Part, Ioannis Papaioannou

description: Contributes a multimodal benchmark dataset for tabletop scenarios that evaluates object state-sensitive task planning capabilities. This benchmark tests whether models can detect object states and generate appropriate plans for robotic manipulation tasks.
paper_title: Details Make a Difference: Object State-Sensitive Neurorobotic Task Planning
authors: Xiaowen Sun, Xufeng Zhao, Jae Hee Lee, Wenhao Lu, Matthias Kerzel

task_types: embodied reasoning, action planning, multimodal alignment
description: Decouples Vision-Language-Action (VLA) capability evaluation into reasoning, intention, action, and alignment dimensions for fine-grained assessment of generalist VLAs. Addresses the evaluation gap for models that need to balance precise action execution with multimodal understanding in embodied AI scenarios.
paper_title: DualVLA: Building a Generalizable Embodied Agent via Partial Decoupling of Reasoning and Action
authors: Zhen Fang, Zhuoyang Liu, Jiaming Liu, Hao Chen, Yu Zeng

task_types: agent activation, task planning, trajectory perception
description: VIKI-Bench is the first hierarchical benchmark tailored for embodied multi-agent cooperation, featuring three structured levels: agent activation, task planning, and trajectory perception. It includes diverse robot embodiments, multi-view visual observations, and structured supervision signals to evaluate reasoning grounded in visual inputs.
paper_title: VIKI-R: Coordinating Embodied Multi-Agent Cooperation via Reinforcement Learning
authors: Li Kang, Xiufeng Song, Heng Zhou, Yiran Qin, Jie Yang

task_types: spatial reasoning, embodied reference, manipulation planning
description: More challenging benchmark for evaluating vision-language models' spatial reasoning and embodied reference abilities in robotic manipulation contexts. Tests models' capabilities in bridging visual understanding with actionable decisions for robotic tasks.
paper_title: From Seeing to Doing: Bridging Reasoning and Decision for Robotic Manipulation
authors: Yifu Yuan, Haiqin Cui, Yibin Chen, Zibin Dong, Fei Ni

task_types: interactive navigation, counterfactual reasoning, object manipulation
description: InterNav dataset enables fine-tuning of VLMs for interactive navigation by incorporating skill affordance and concrete constraint parameters. It teaches models counterfactual reasoning to evaluate causal effects of object removal on navigation connectivity for path clearance tasks.
paper_title: CoINS: Counterfactual Interactive Navigation via Skill-Aware VLM
authors: Kangjie Zhou, Zhejia Wen, Zhiyong Zhuo, Zike Yan, Pengying Wu

task_types: ego-exo transfer, activity understanding, viewpoint transfer
description: Ego-in-Exo Perception is a benchmark of 3.9K questions specifically designed to measure the understanding of egocentric properties (like human-object interactions) from exocentric video observations. It tests VLMs' ability to infer first-person perspective activities from third-person viewpoints.
paper_title: From My View to Yours: Ego-to-Exo Transfer in VLMs for Understanding Activities of Daily Living
authors: Dominick Reilly, Manish Kumar Govind, Le Xue, Srijan Das

task_types: motion understanding, trajectory assessment, robot behavior evaluation
description: MotIF-1K contains 653 human and 369 robot demonstrations across 13 task categories for benchmarking VLMs on robotic motion understanding. It uniquely tests whether success can be determined from observing the full trajectory rather than just initial and final states, using abstract trajectory representations.
paper_title: MotIF: Motion Instruction Fine-tuning
authors: Minyoung Hwang, Joey Hejna, Dorsa Sadigh, Yonatan Bisk

description: UR5-Fail is a newly introduced real-world benchmark for robotic failure detection and reasoning. It evaluates VLMs' ability to detect and reason about manipulation failures in robotic environments, complementing existing failure detection benchmarks.
paper_title: Scaling Cross-Environment Failure Reasoning Data for Vision-Language Robotic Manipulation
authors: Paul Pacaud, Ricardo Garcia, Shizhe Chen, Cordelia Schmid

description: Introduces NIG4VI, a 27k-sample dataset for navigation instruction generation for visually impaired users with diverse navigation scenarios and accurate spatial coordinates. Also proposes LaF-GRPO training method using LLM-as-Follower feedback.
paper_title: LaF-GRPO: In-Situ Navigation Instruction Generation for the Visually Impaired via GRPO with LLM-as-Follower Reward
authors: Yi Zhao, Siqi Wang, Jing Li

task_types: long-horizon manipulation, compositional task generalization, skill composition
description: DeCoBench is a benchmark designed to evaluate compositional generalization in long-horizon manipulation tasks. It tests VLMs' ability to decompose complex manipulation instructions into atomic skills and compose them for novel long-horizon tasks.
paper_title: DeCo: Task Decomposition and Skill Composition for Zero-Shot Generalization in Long-Horizon 3D Manipulation
authors: Zixuan Chen, Junhui Yin, Yangtao Chen, Jing Huo, Pinzhuo Tian

modalities: LiDAR, infrared, mmWave radar, WiFi, text
description: A benchmark for language-grounded human sensing and reasoning using uncommon sensing modalities like LiDAR, infrared, mmWave radar, and WiFi. Tests multimodal understanding across heterogeneous environments with diverse sensory inputs beyond vision.
paper_title: HoloLLM: Multisensory Foundation Model for Language-Grounded Human Sensing and Reasoning
authors: Chuhao Zhou, Jianfei Yang

description: The first large-scale dataset dedicated to walking assistance for visually-impaired individuals, comprising 12,000 video-annotation pairs. Provides a unified benchmark for training and evaluating walking guidance systems.
paper_title: WalkVLM:Aid Visually Impaired People Walking by Vision Language Model
authors: Zhiqiang Yuan, Ting Zhang, Ying Deng, Jiapei Zhang, Yeshuang Zhu

task_types: visual action reasoning, proactive reasoning, decision-making
description: A large-scale benchmark comprising 1,074 videos and 3,733 human-annotated actions across four real-world scenarios for Visual Action Reasoning. Tests models' ability to proactively reason and act based solely on visual inputs.
paper_title: VisualActBench: Can VLMs See and Act like a Human?
authors: Daoan Zhang, Pai Liu, Xiaofei Zhou, Yuan Ge, Guangchen Lan

task_types: object manipulation, knowledge transfer evaluation, embodied control
description: GrinningFace is a diagnostic benchmark for testing knowledge transfer from VLMs to VLAs using an emoji tabletop manipulation task. It specifically tests whether VLAs can effectively inherit Internet-scale emoji knowledge from VLM pre-training for embodied control tasks.
paper_title: How Do VLAs Effectively Inherit from VLMs?
authors: Chuheng Zhang, Rushuai Yang, Xiaoyu Chen, Kaixin Wang, Li Zhao

task_types: object perception, grasp inference, prosthetic control
description: This benchmark evaluates VLMs on unified end-to-end perception and grasp inference for prosthetic hands using 34 snapshots of common objects. It tests both object identification with key properties and appropriate grasp parameter inference from single static images.
paper_title: Using Visual Language Models to Control Bionic Hands: Assessment of Object Perception and Grasp Inference
authors: Ozan Karaali, Hossam Farag, Strahinja Dosen, Cedomir Stefanovic

description: Sanpo-D is a fine-grained re-annotation of the Google Sanpo dataset designed to benchmark VLMs on navigation-oriented spatial queries in long egocentric videos. It focuses on spatial reasoning challenges like pedestrian and obstruction detection in first-person navigation scenarios with viewpoint drift.
paper_title: Spatial-Conditioned Reasoning in Long-Egocentric Videos
authors: James Tribble, Hao Wang, Si-En Hong, Chaoyi Zhou, Ashish Bastola

task_types: language instruction following, vision understanding, object manipulation
description: Unified probing suite of 50 simulation-based tasks across 10 subcategories to evaluate Vision-Language-Action models' generalization boundaries. Tests the gap between VLM intentions and precise motor execution in robotics.
paper_title: From Intention to Execution: Probing the Generalization Boundaries of Vision-Language-Action Models
authors: Irving Fang, Juexiao Zhang, Shengbang Tong, Chen Feng

task_types: situational grounding, event reasoning, multi-actor tracking
description: Fine-grained situational annotation dataset for multi-person and human-robot interactions. Provides benchmarks for actor-action-object relation grounding with temporal consistency across dynamic group interactions.
paper_title: MERGE: Guided Vision-Language Models for Multi-Actor Event Reasoning and Grounding in Human-Robot Interaction
authors: Joerg Deigmoeller, Nakul Agarwal, Stephan Hasler, Daniel Tanneberg, Anna Belardinelli

task_types: property understanding, affordance reasoning, constraint evaluation
description: Comprehensive benchmark evaluating VLMs' understanding of core Properties, Affordances, and Constraints (PAC) for task executability. Features diverse dataset with real-world images, humanoid-view scenarios, and simulated constraint scenarios across manipulation tasks.
paper_title: PAC Bench: Do Foundation Models Understand Prerequisites for Executing Manipulation Policies?
authors: Atharva Gundawar, Som Sagar, Ransalu Senanayake

description: SimplerEnv-Instruct is an 80-task benchmark requiring closed-loop control and high-level instruction understanding for evaluating vision-language-action models. It tests generalization capabilities in embodied AI scenarios with complex manipulation tasks requiring both visual understanding and precise action generation.
paper_title: InstructVLA: Vision-Language-Action Instruction Tuning from Understanding to Manipulation
authors: Shuai Yang, Hao Li, Bin Wang, Yilun Chen, Yang Tian

description: Roadwork-VLM is a new benchmark based on complex roadwork construction scenarios to rigorously evaluate zero-shot generalization of autonomous driving systems. It tests the ability to reason robustly in unseen scenarios with complex construction environments that challenge typical driving assumptions.
paper_title: MTRDrive: Memory-Tool Synergistic Reasoning for Robust Autonomous Driving in Corner Cases
authors: Ziang Luo, Kangan Qian, Jiahua Wang, Yuechen Luo, Jinyu Miao

task_types: interactive scenario understanding, visual question answering
description: An Interactive Enhanced Driving Dataset with million-level interactive segments mined from naturalistic driving data. Includes IEDD-VQA with synthetic BEV videos where semantic actions are aligned with structured language for autonomous driving reasoning.
paper_title: An interactive enhanced driving dataset for autonomous driving
authors: Haojie Feng, Peizhi Zhang, Mengjie Tian, Xinrui Zhang, Zhuoren Li

description: A holistic vision-language dataset for autonomous driving that aligns agent models with 3D driving tasks through counterfactual reasoning. Enhances decision-making by evaluating potential scenarios and outcomes, bridging planning trajectories and language-based reasoning for autonomous driving applications.
paper_title: OmniDrive: A Holistic Vision-Language Dataset for Autonomous Driving with Counterfactual Reasoning
authors: Shihao Wang, Zhiding Yu, Xiaohui Jiang, Shiyi Lan, Min Shi

task_types: box-referring VQA, attribute prediction, motion understanding, spatiotemporal reasoning
description: A box-referring VQA dataset for autonomous driving where users express intent by drawing bounding boxes for focused queries. Features hierarchical evaluation from binary sanity checks to attribute prediction, motion understanding, and spatiotemporal reasoning over inter-object dynamics.
paper_title: Box-QAymo: Box-Referring VQA Dataset for Autonomous Driving
authors: Djamahl Etchegaray, Yuxia Fu, Zi Huang, Yadan Luo

description: A holistic vision-language dataset for autonomous driving that aligns agent models with 3D driving tasks through counterfactual reasoning. Enhances decision-making by evaluating potential scenarios and outcomes, bridging planning trajectories and language-based reasoning for autonomous driving applications.
paper_title: OmniDrive: A Holistic Vision-Language Dataset for Autonomous Driving with Counterfactual Reasoning
authors: Shihao Wang, Zhiding Yu, Xiaohui Jiang, Shiyi Lan, Min Shi

task_types: object identification, reference resolution, action prediction
description: J-ORA bridges the gap in robot perception by providing detailed object attribute annotations within Japanese human-robot dialogue scenarios. It supports object identification, reference resolution, and next-action prediction with comprehensive attribute templates.
paper_title: J-ORA: A Framework and Multimodal Dataset for Japanese Object Identification, Reference, Action Prediction in Robot Perception
authors: Jesse Atuhurra, Hidetaka Kamigaito, Taro Watanabe, Koichiro Yoshino

modalities: image, text, robot trajectories, sensory data
task_types: visual question answering, spatial reasoning, interaction reasoning, goal-conditioned reasoning
description: Robo2VLM-1 is a large-scale Visual Question Answering dataset derived from real robot trajectory data, covering 463 scenes and 3,396 manipulation tasks. It generates ground-truth from non-visual sensory modalities and focuses on spatial, goal-conditioned, and interaction reasoning.
paper_title: Robo2VLM: Visual Question Answering from Large-Scale In-the-Wild Robot Manipulation Datasets
authors: Kaiyuan Chen, Shuangyu Xie, Zehan Ma, Pannag R Sanketi, Ken Goldberg

task_types: embodied reasoning, spatial perception, intuitive physics, common sense reasoning
description: PRISM provides a multi-view video supervised fine-tuning corpus for embodied VLMs in retail environments. It's grounded in a three-dimensional knowledge ontology spanning spatial, temporal/physical, and embodied action knowledge with 20+ capability probes across four evaluation dimensions.
paper_title: PRISM: A Multi-View Multi-Capability Retail Video Dataset for Embodied Vision-Language Models
authors: Amirreza Rouhi, Parikshit Sakurikar, Satya Sai Reddy, Narsimha Menga, Anirudh Govil

modalities: video, force-torque, joint states, robot states
task_types: mobile manipulation, hierarchical learning, contact-rich interaction
description: AIRoA MoMa provides the first large-scale real-world dataset for mobile manipulation that uniquely integrates synchronized force-torque sensing, hierarchical sub-goal annotations, and explicit failure cases. It features a novel two-layer annotation schema for hierarchical learning and supports contact-rich, long-horizon manipulation tasks.
paper_title: AIRoA MoMa Dataset: A Large-Scale Hierarchical Dataset for Mobile Manipulation
authors: Ryosuke Takanami, Petr Khrapchenkov, Shu Morikuni, Jumpei Arima, Yuta Takaba

task_types: multimodal grounding, referential communication, situated dialogue
description: Look and Tell provides the first dataset for studying multimodal grounding across egocentric and exocentric perspectives using Meta Project Aria smart glasses and stationary cameras. It uniquely combines synchronized gaze, speech, and video with 3D scene reconstructions to evaluate how different spatial representations affect multimodal grounding in situated dialogue.
paper_title: Look and Tell: A Dataset for Multimodal Grounding Across Egocentric and Exocentric Views
authors: Anna Deichler, Jonas Beskow

modalities: egocentric RGB, gaze, hand tracking, audio, exocentric RGB, depth, ultrasound
task_types: action recognition, scene graph generation, human-centric perception
description: EgoExOR is the first Operating Room dataset to fuse first-person and third-person perspectives, spanning 94 minutes of two emulated spine procedures. It integrates egocentric data (RGB, gaze, hand tracking, audio) from wearable glasses with exocentric RGB/depth cameras and ultrasound imagery, featuring detailed scene graph annotations with 36 entities and 22 relations.
paper_title: EgoExOR: An Ego-Exo-Centric Operating Room Dataset for Surgical Activity Understanding
authors: Ege Özsoy, Arda Mamur, Felix Tristram, Chantal Pellegrini, Magdalena Wysocki

modalities: egocentric video, exocentric video, eye gaze, audio, motion
task_types: procedural task understanding, mistake detection, question answering
description: IndEgo is a multimodal egocentric and exocentric dataset addressing industrial tasks including assembly/disassembly, logistics, inspection, and woodworking with 3,460 egocentric recordings (197 hours) and 1,092 exocentric recordings (97 hours). It focuses on collaborative work between two workers and includes rich multimodal data with eye gaze, narration, sound, motion, and detailed annotations.
paper_title: IndEgo: A Dataset of Industrial Scenarios and Collaborative Work for Egocentric Assistants
authors: Vivek Chavan, Yasmina Imgrund, Tung Dao, Sanwantri Bai, Bosong Wang

modalities: egocentric video, exocentric video, wearable sensors
task_types: activity classification, open vocabulary captioning, cross-modal alignment
description: Largest multimodal dataset for human action monitoring in manufacturing environments, featuring wearable sensors and distributed cameras across two experimental settings: bicycle assembly/disassembly and collaborative 3D printer assembly, with over 200 independent information channels for industrial action recognition.
paper_title: OpenMarcie: Dataset for Multimodal Action Recognition in Industrial Environments
authors: Hymalai Bello, Lala Ray, Joanna Sorysz, Sungho Suh, Paul Lukowicz

description: Can-Do evaluates embodied planning abilities through diverse and complex scenarios, featuring multimodal samples with natural language instructions, visual environments, state changes, and action plans. It encompasses commonsense knowledge, physical understanding, and safety awareness for embodied AI evaluation.
paper_title: Can-Do! A Dataset and Neuro-Symbolic Grounded Framework for Embodied Planning with Large Multimodal Models
authors: Yew Ken Chia, Qi Sun, Lidong Bing, Soujanya Poria

task_types: visual question answering, expert assistance evaluation
description: Ego-EXTRA features 50 hours of unscripted egocentric videos with expert-trainee assistance scenarios, creating a unique dataset where experts provide real-time guidance through natural language. It comprises over 15k high-quality Visual Question Answer sets for evaluating multimodal assistance capabilities.
paper_title: Ego-EXTRA: video-language Egocentric Dataset for EXpert-TRAinee assistance
authors: Francesco Ragusa, Michele Mazzamuto, Rosario Forte, Irene D'Ambra, James Fort

modalities: egocentric video, motion capture, audio, 3D point clouds
task_types: human motion analysis, 3D object detection, multimodal egocentric understanding
description: NymeriaPlus upgrades the original Nymeria dataset with improved human motion data, dense 3D/2D bounding box annotations, instance-level 3D object reconstructions, and additional modalities. It provides a comprehensive in-the-wild egocentric dataset for embodied AI research with multiple synchronized sensors.
paper_title: NymeriaPlus: Enriching Nymeria Dataset with Additional Annotations and Data
authors: Daniel DeTone, Federica Bogo, Eric-Tuan Le, Duncan Frost, Julian Straub

num_samples: over 20 hours in 233 simulated emergency scenarios
description: Introduces EgoEMS, the first end-to-end multimodal egocentric dataset capturing realistic EMS activities from first-person view. Developed in collaboration with EMS experts and includes keysteps, audio transcripts, action quality metrics for cognitive assistance research.
paper_title: EgoEMS: A High-Fidelity Multimodal Egocentric Dataset for Cognitive Assistance in Emergency Medical Services
authors: Keshara Weerasinghe, Xueren Ge, Tessa Heick, Lahiru Nuwan Wijayasingha, Anthony Cortez

modalities: egocentric video, audio, depth, tracking data
description: Introduces SigmaCollab dataset for physically situated human-AI collaboration with rich multimodal data streams including egocentric camera views, depth maps, head/hand/gaze tracking. Focuses on mixed-reality assistive AI agent interactions.
paper_title: SigmaCollab: An Application-Driven Dataset for Physically Situated Collaboration
authors: Dan Bohus, Sean Andrist, Ann Paradiso, Nick Saw, Tim Schoonbeek

description: A reasoning-oriented multimodal dataset for socially compliant navigation using structured five-step Chain-of-Thought annotations (perception, prediction, reasoning, action, explanation). It provides explicit reasoning supervision with a rationally balanced discrete action space for learning safety-critical navigation behaviors.
paper_title: MUSON: A Reasoning-oriented Multimodal Dataset for Socially Compliant Navigation in Urban Environments
authors: Zhuonan Liu, Xinyu Zhang, Zishuo Wang, Tomohito Kawabata, Xuesu Xiao

task_types: action recognition, object detection, human-object interaction detection, action anticipation, next-active object detection
description: A multimodal egocentric dataset for human behavior understanding in industrial-like settings, combining gaze signals, depth maps, and RGB videos. It addresses the understudied area of egocentric human behavior analysis in industrial scenarios with tasks spanning action recognition to anticipation.
paper_title: MECCANO: A Multimodal Egocentric Dataset for Humans Behavior Understanding in the Industrial-like Domain
authors: Francesco Ragusa, Antonino Furnari, Giovanni Maria Farinella

description: A large-scale multi-modal dataset for robotic spatial awareness and semantic reasoning in heterogeneous environments including underground tunnels, natural fields, and indoor spaces. It addresses the underrepresentation of multi-degraded, vegetated, and feature-sparse environments in existing benchmarks focused on urban terrains.
paper_title: EnvoDat: A Large-Scale Multisensory Dataset for Robotic Spatial Awareness and Semantic Reasoning in Heterogeneous Environments
authors: Linus Nwankwo, Bjoern Ellensohn, Vedant Dave, Peter Hofer, Jan Forstner

description: A multimodal, cross-season dataset collected in commercial strawberry and raspberry polytunnels for agricultural robotics, featuring substantial appearance variation, dynamic foliage, and GNSS-unreliable conditions. It addresses the under-exploration of horticultural environments compared to urban and forestry robotics datasets.
paper_title: HortiMulti: A Multi-Sensor Dataset for Localisation and Mapping in Horticultural Polytunnels
authors: Shuoyuan Xu, Zhipeng Zhong, Tiago Barros, Matthew Coombes, Cristiano Premebida

task_types: action recognition, object detection, action anticipation
description: A large-scale egocentric video benchmark capturing 55 hours of nonscripted daily kitchen activities from 32 participants across 4 countries, featuring dense annotations with 39.6K action segments and 454.2K object bounding boxes. It offers a unique viewpoint on people's interaction with objects and intention through participant-narrated videos.
paper_title: The EPIC-KITCHENS Dataset: Collection, Challenges and Baselines
authors: Dima Damen, Hazel Doughty, Giovanni Maria Farinella, Sanja Fidler, Antonino Furnari

description: Humanoid Everyday presents a large-scale humanoid manipulation dataset with 10.3k trajectories and over 3 million frames across 260 tasks in 7 categories. It features extensive task variety including dextrous manipulation, human-humanoid interaction, and locomotion-integrated actions, with multimodal sensory data and natural language annotations, plus a cloud-based evaluation platform.
paper_title: Humanoid Everyday: A Comprehensive Robotic Dataset for Open-World Humanoid Manipulation
authors: Zhenyu Zhao, Hongyi Jing, Xiawei Liu, Jiageng Mao, Abha Jha

task_types: open-vocabulary object navigation, dynamic scene navigation
description: SD-OVON provides a semantics-aware dataset and benchmark for open-vocabulary object navigation in dynamic scenes using multimodal foundation models. It offers SD-OVON-3k and SD-OVON-10k datasets with photo-realistic scene variants that adhere to real-world semantics, covering dynamic environments with manipulatable objects for both real-to-sim and sim-to-real applications.
paper_title: SD-OVON: A Semantics-aware Dataset and Benchmark Generation Pipeline for Open-Vocabulary Object Navigation in Dynamic Scenes
authors: Dicong Qiu, Jiadi You, Zeying Gong, Ronghe Qiu, Hui Xiong

task_types: egocentric human motion estimation, interaction synthesis, interaction prediction
description: InterVLA presents the first large-scale human-object-human interaction dataset with 11.4 hours and 1.2M frames of multimodal data in a vision-language-action framework. It features assistants providing services following egocentric vision and commands, spanning 2 egocentric and 5 exocentric videos with accurate human/object motions and verbal commands, establishing benchmarks for egocentric motion estimation and interaction synthesis.
paper_title: Perceiving and Acting in First-Person: A Dataset and Benchmark for Egocentric Human-Object-Human Interactions
authors: Liang Xu, Chengqun Yang, Zili Lin, Fei Xu, Yifan Liu

description: JRDB is a novel egocentric dataset from a social mobile robot featuring 64 minutes of multimodal sensor data including 360° RGB video, 3D point clouds, audio, and RGB-D. It provides over 2.3 million bounding boxes and 1.8 million 3D cuboids across 3500 trajectories for 2D/3D person detection and tracking in indoor and pedestrian environments from robot ego-perspective.
paper_title: JRDB: A Dataset and Benchmark of Egocentric Robot Visual Perception of Humans in Built Environments
authors: Roberto Martín-Martín, Mihir Patel, Hamid Rezatofighi, Abhijeet Shenoi, JunYoung Gwak

description: Mobile robot egocentric perception dataset with 8.5 hours of multimodal sensor data including synchronized 3D point clouds, stereo RGB video, RGB-D, and IMU data. Contains 1.3 million 3D bounding boxes with instance IDs for 53 semantic classes, targeting urban autonomous navigation.
paper_title: Towards Robust Robot 3D Perception in Urban Environments: The UT Campus Object Dataset
authors: Arthur Zhang, Chaitanya Eranki, Christina Zhang, Ji-Hwan Park, Raymond Hong

task_types: object affordance recognition, object affordance prediction, spatial affordance localization
description: Generative AI-enhanced dataset for multimodal affordance learning covering object recognition, object affordance prediction for manipulation, and spatial affordance localization for robot navigation. Contains 869,987 images with 2.0 million QA annotations across three critical affordance tasks.
paper_title: RoboAfford++: A Generative AI-Enhanced Dataset for Multimodal Affordance Learning in Robotic Manipulation and Navigation
authors: Xiaoshuai Hao, Yingbo Tang, Lingfeng Zhang, Yanbiao Ma, Yunfeng Diao

description: Large-scale RGB-D dataset focusing on daily activities of the elderly in robot-view with 112,620 samples from 100 subjects performing 55 daily activities. Designed for practical action recognition that reflects robot working environments and service situations for elderly care.
paper_title: ETRI-Activity3D: A Large-Scale RGB-D Dataset for Robots to Recognize Daily Activities of the Elderly
authors: Jinhyeok Jang, Dohyung Kim, Cheonshu Park, Minsu Jang, Jaeyeon Lee

task_types: indoor navigation, spatial reasoning, 3D scene understanding
description: Largest real-world dataset for Vision and Language-guided Action in 3D Scenes with over 11.5K scanned 3D indoor rooms, 23.5M semantic relations between objects, and 9.7M synthetically generated referential statements. Focuses on view-independent spatial relations for robust indoor navigation.
paper_title: VLA-3D: A Dataset for 3D Semantic Scene Understanding and Navigation
authors: Haochen Zhang, Nader Zantout, Pujith Kachana, Zongyuan Wu, Ji Zhang

task_types: cross-view association, cross-view action planning, cross-view referenced skill assessment
description: EgoExoLearn is a large-scale dataset that captures both egocentric and demonstration videos for procedural activities, with a focus on bridging asynchronous ego- and exo-centric viewpoints. It provides benchmarks for cross-view association, cross-view action planning, and cross-view referenced skill assessment, making it unique for studying human demonstration following processes across different perspectives.
paper_title: EgoExoLearn: A Dataset for Bridging Asynchronous Ego- and Exo-centric View of Procedural Activities in Real World
authors: Yifei Huang, Guo Chen, Jilan Xu, Mingfang Zhang, Lijin Yang

description: Novel benchmarks for evaluating VLMs in zero-shot recognition focusing on granularity and specificity. Tests VLMs' consistency in understanding concepts across semantic granularity levels and their response to varying text specificity.
paper_title: Benchmarking Zero-Shot Recognition with Vision-Language Models: Challenges on Granularity and Specificity
authors: Zhenlin Xu, Yi Zhu, Tiffany Deng, Abhay Mittal, Yanbei Chen

description: A new long form response benchmark for more robust and complete VLM evaluation. Designed to overcome limitations of current evaluation approaches by providing comprehensive assessment of open-ended responses.
paper_title: CHIRP: A Fine-Grained Benchmark for Open-Ended Response Evaluation in Vision-Language Models
authors: Alexis Roger, Prateek Humane, Daniel Z. Kaplan, Kshitij Gupta, Qi Sun

description: A novel benchmark for evaluating Japanese capabilities of VLMs consisting of image-question answer pairs tailored to the Japanese context. Addresses the gap in non-English VLM evaluation.
paper_title: Heron-Bench: A Benchmark for Evaluating Vision Language Models in Japanese
authors: Yuichi Inoue, Kento Sasaki, Yuma Ochi, Kazuki Fujii, Kotaro Tanahashi

description: The first benchmark for evaluating speculative decoding in vision-language models containing 600 multimodal samples across six task categories. Integrates ten representative speculative decoding algorithms under a unified evaluation framework.
paper_title: MMSpec: Benchmarking Speculative Decoding for Vision-Language Models
authors: Hui Shen, Xin Wang, Ping Zhang, Yunta Hsieh, Qi Han

description: A dataset focused on Korean culture created through a semi-automated framework for constructing cultural VLM benchmarks. Tests VLMs' ability to address culturally relevant questions through multiple-choice QA.
paper_title: Evaluating Visual and Cultural Interpretation: The K-Viscuit Benchmark with Human-VLM Collaboration
authors: ChaeHun Park, Yujin Baek, Jaeseok Kim, Yu-Jung Heo, Du-Seong Chang

description: CulturalVQA is a visual question-answering benchmark specifically designed to assess VLMs' geo-diverse cultural understanding across 11 countries and 5 continents. It uniquely focuses on cultural facets like clothing, food, drinks, rituals, and traditions rather than general scene understanding.
paper_title: Benchmarking Vision Language Models for Cultural Understanding
authors: Shravan Nayak, Kanishk Jain, Rabiul Awal, Siva Reddy, Sjoerd van Steenkiste

task_types: STEM problem solving, data interpretation, rule-governed visual reasoning, abstract visual reasoning
description: VMMU is a Vietnamese Multitask Multimodal Understanding and Reasoning Benchmark designed to evaluate VLMs beyond English across 7 tasks including STEM problem solving, data interpretation, and visual reasoning. It uniquely requires genuine multimodal integration rather than text-only or OCR-based shortcuts.
paper_title: VMMU: A Vietnamese Multitask Multimodal Understanding and Reasoning Benchmark
authors: Vy Tuong Dang, An Vo, Emilio Villa-Cueva, Quang Tau, Duc Dm

task_types: object recognition, scene understanding, relationship understanding
description: VLURes is a multilingual benchmark featuring eight vision-and-language tasks across English, Japanese, Swahili, and Urdu, with a focus on long-text settings and low-resource languages. It introduces the first vision-language resources for Swahili and Urdu with rich textual context.
paper_title: VLURes: Benchmarking VLM Visual and Linguistic Understanding in Low-Resource Languages
authors: Jesse Atuhurra, Iqra Ali, Tomoya Iwakura, Hidetaka Kamigaito, Tatsuya Hiraoka

task_types: image-text retrieval, visual question answering, visual grounding, visual dialogue
description: CVLUE is a Chinese Vision-Language Understanding Evaluation benchmark where object categories and images are entirely selected by Chinese native speakers to ensure cultural representation. It addresses cultural bias in existing Western-centric VL datasets across four distinct tasks.
paper_title: CVLUE: A New Benchmark Dataset for Chinese Vision-Language Understanding Evaluation
authors: Yuxuan Wang, Yijun Liu, Fei Yu, Chen Huang, Kexin Li

description: JAMMEval is a refined collection of Japanese VQA benchmarks that addresses issues in existing Japanese benchmarks such as ambiguous questions, incorrect answers, and instances solvable without visual grounding. It improves evaluation reliability through systematic human annotation and refinement of seven existing Japanese benchmark datasets.
paper_title: JAMMEval: A Refined Collection of Japanese Benchmarks for Reliable VLM Evaluation
authors: Issa Sugiura, Koki Maeda, Shuhei Kurita, Yusuke Oda, Daisuke Kawahara

task_types: visual question answering, retrieval augmented generation
description: VLR-Bench is a multilingual VQA benchmark for evaluating VLMs based on retrieval augmented generation (RAG) that includes five input passages per query. Unlike existing external knowledge-based VQA datasets, it tests the crucial ability to determine which passage is useful for answering a given query, a capability lacking in previous research.
paper_title: VLR-Bench: Multilingual Benchmark Dataset for Vision-Language Retrieval Augmented Generation
authors: Hyeonseok Lim, Dongjae Shin, Seohyun Song, Inho Won, Minjun Kim

task_types: dynamic knowledge evaluation, factual knowledge assessment, temporal reasoning
description: V-DyKnow is a Visual Dynamic Knowledge benchmark for evaluating time-sensitive factual knowledge in VLMs, addressing the issue that real-world facts change over time while training data and benchmarks are typically static. It evaluates reliability across modalities, efficacy of knowledge editing methods, and sources of outdated predictions through comprehensive analysis.
paper_title: V-DyKnow: A Dynamic Benchmark for Time-Sensitive Knowledge in Vision Language Models
authors: Seyed Mahed Mousavi, Christian Moiola, Massimo Rizzoli, Simone Alghisi, Giuseppe Riccardi

task_types: cultural understanding, art critique, philosophical aesthetics
description: VULCA-Bench evaluates cultural understanding beyond surface-level visual perception using a five-layer framework (L1-L5, from Visual Perception to Philosophical Aesthetics) instantiated as 225 culture-specific dimensions. It uniquely focuses on higher-order cultural interpretation across eight cultural traditions with bilingual coverage.
paper_title: VULCA-Bench: A Multicultural Vision-Language Benchmark for Evaluating Cultural Understanding
authors: Haorui Yu, Diji Yang, Hang He, Fengrui Zhang, Qiufeng Yi

description: RAVENEA advances visual culture understanding through retrieval-augmented generation, focusing on culture-focused visual question answering and culture-informed image captioning. It uniquely integrates curated Wikipedia documents and demonstrates how cultural grounding annotations can enhance multimodal retrieval and downstream tasks.
paper_title: RAVENEA: A Benchmark for Multimodal Retrieval-Augmented Visual Culture Understanding
authors: Jiaang Li, Yifei Yuan, Wenyan Li, Mohammad Aliannejadi, Daniel Hershcovich

description: CapArena is an arena-style evaluation platform with over 6000 pairwise caption battles and human preference votes for benchmarking detailed image captioning quality. It uniquely provides both human preference evaluation and introduces CapArena-Auto for automated assessment, achieving 94.3% correlation with human rankings.
paper_title: CapArena: Benchmarking and Analyzing Detailed Image Captioning in the LLM Era
authors: Kanzhi Cheng, Wenpo Song, Jiaxin Fan, Zheng Ma, Qiushi Sun

task_types: federated learning evaluation, cross-domain adaptation, multi-task learning
description: FedVLMBench is the first systematic benchmark for federated fine-tuning of VLMs, integrating two mainstream VLM architectures, four fine-tuning strategies, five FL algorithms across six multimodal datasets. It uniquely evaluates privacy-preserving federated learning approaches for VLMs across cross-domain scenarios and multi-task settings.
paper_title: FedVLMBench: Benchmarking Federated Fine-Tuning of Vision-Language Models
authors: Weiying Zheng, Ziyue Lin, Pengxin Guo, Yuyin Zhou, Feifei Wang

task_types: uncertainty quantification, modality-specific evaluation, hallucination correlation
description: VLM-UQBench evaluates modality-specific and cross-modal uncertainties in VLMs through curated clean, image-, text-, and cross-modal uncertainty subsets with scalable perturbation pipeline. It uniquely quantifies uncertainty localization to specific modalities and correlations with hallucinations across different perturbation types.
paper_title: VLM-UQBench: A Benchmark for Modality-Specific and Cross-Modality Uncertainties in Vision Language Models
authors: Chenyu Wang, Tianle Chen, H. M. Sabbir Ahmad, Kayhan Batmanghelich, Wenchao Li

task_types: OCR, multimodal machine translation, visual question answering
description: IndicVisionBench is the first large-scale benchmark centered on the Indian subcontinent, covering English and 10 Indian languages across 3 multimodal tasks and 13 culturally grounded topics. It uniquely addresses cultural diversity and multilinguality in VLM evaluation, revealing substantial performance gaps in culturally diverse contexts.
paper_title: IndicVisionBench: Benchmarking Cultural and Multilingual Understanding in VLMs
authors: Ali Faraz, Akash, Shaharukh Khan, Raja Kolla, Akshat Patidar

description: SEAM pairs semantically equivalent inputs across four domains with standardized textual and visual notations to evaluate cross-modal reasoning consistency. It provides rigorous comparative assessment of textual-symbolic versus visual-spatial reasoning capabilities in VLMs.
paper_title: SEAM: Semantically Equivalent Across Modalities Benchmark for Vision-Language Models
authors: Zhenwei Tang, Difan Jiao, Blair Yang, Ashton Anderson

task_types: object detection, zero-shot, few-shot, semi-supervised, fully-supervised
description: Roboflow100-VL provides a large-scale collection of 100 diverse object detection datasets with concepts not commonly found in VLM pre-training. It evaluates VLMs across zero-shot, few-shot, semi-supervised, and fully-supervised settings for out-of-distribution object detection tasks.
paper_title: Roboflow100-VL: A Multi-Domain Object Detection Benchmark for Vision-Language Models
authors: Peter Robicheaux, Matvei Popov, Anish Madan, Isaac Robinson, Joseph Nelson

description: Auto-Bench uses LLMs as automated aligners to measure alignment between VLMs and human intelligence through automatic data curation and assessment. It generates question-answer-reasoning triplets via prompting on visual symbolic representations, covering 4 primary abilities and 16 sub-abilities.
paper_title: Large Language Models as Automated Aligners for benchmarking Vision-Language Models
authors: Yuanfeng Ji, Chongjian Ge, Weikai Kong, Enze Xie, Zhengying Liu

description: KOFFVQA is a Korean language free-form VQA benchmark that eliminates subjective evaluation issues by using pre-determined grading criteria. It covers 10 different aspects of VLM performance and enables objective evaluation using small open-source models as judges.
paper_title: KOFFVQA: An Objectively Evaluated Free-form VQA Benchmark for Large Vision-Language Models in the Korean Language
authors: Yoonshik Kim, Jaeyoon Jung

task_types: visual question answering, dish identification, origin prediction
description: WorldCuisines is the largest multicultural VQA benchmark featuring text-image pairs across 30 languages and dialects from 9 language families. It specifically evaluates culture-specific knowledge understanding in global cuisines context, addressing VLM limitations in non-English and underrepresented cultural contexts.
paper_title: WorldCuisines: A Massive-Scale Benchmark for Multilingual and Multicultural Visual Question Answering on Global Cuisines
authors: Genta Indra Winata, Frederikus Hudi, Patrick Amadeus Irawan, David Anugraha, Rifki Afina Putri

task_types: visual question answering, STEM, cultural grounding
description: HinTel-AlignBench addresses gaps in multilingual VLM evaluation by providing comprehensive benchmarks for Hindi and Telugu with English-aligned samples. It combines back-translation, filtering, and human verification to create culturally grounded datasets including both adapted English tasks and novel Indic datasets.
paper_title: HinTel-AlignBench: A Framework and Benchmark for Hindi-Telugu with English-Aligned Samples
authors: Rishikant Chigrupaatii, Ponnada Sai Tulasi Kanishka, Lalit Chandra Routhu, Martin Patel Sama Supratheek Reddy, Divyam Gupta

description: DRISHTIKON is the first multimodal multilingual benchmark exclusively centered on Indian culture, covering 15 languages across all states and union territories. It uniquely provides deep, fine-grained coverage of India's diverse cultural themes including festivals, attire, cuisines, and art forms.
paper_title: DRISHTIKON: A Multimodal Multilingual Benchmark for Testing Language Models' Understanding on Indian Culture
authors: Arijit Maji, Raghvendra Kumar, Akash Ghosh, Anushka, Nemil Shah

description: Addresses limitations of existing cross-domain datasets through hierarchical domain shift definitions and fine-grained realistic domains. Features 390 domains with 0.5 million images for tuning-free adaptive domain generalization, enabling systematic evaluation of VLMs across diverse realistic scenarios.
paper_title: DomainVerse: A Benchmark Towards Real-World Distribution Shifts For Tuning-Free Adaptive Domain Generalization
authors: Feng Hou, Jin Yuan, Ying Yang, Yang Liu, Yang Zhang

task_types: object detection, OCR, scene detection, content analysis
description: ViLD bridges the gap between academic evaluation and enterprise deployment by evaluating VLMs on ten business-critical tasks using real-world images and videos. It provides industry-grounded, task-driven assessment with innovative evaluation methods like the BlockWeaver Algorithm for OCR comparison.
paper_title: VLMs-in-the-Wild: Bridging the Gap Between Academic Benchmarks and Enterprise Reality
authors: Srihari Bandraupalli, Anupam Purwar

description: This work conducts comprehensive uncertainty benchmarking of 18 state-of-the-art VLMs across 6 multimodal datasets with 3 distinct scoring functions. It establishes a foundation for reliable uncertainty evaluation in multimodal systems using conformal prediction approaches.
paper_title: The Art of Saying "Maybe": A Conformal Lens for Uncertainty Benchmarking in VLMs
authors: Asif Azad, Mohammad Sadat Hossain, MD Sadik Hossain Shanto, M Saifur Rahman, Md Rizwan Parvez

description: MAGB is a comprehensive Multimodal Attributed Graph benchmark featuring curated graphs from various domains with both textual and visual attributes. It systematically evaluates two mainstream paradigms for multimodal attributed graph representation learning and provides insights into the synergy between multimodal attributes and graph topologies.
paper_title: When Graph meets Multimodal: Benchmarking and Meditating on Multimodal Attributed Graphs Learning
authors: Hao Yan, Chaozhuo Li, Jun Yin, Zhigang Yu, Weihao Han

task_types: multilingual reasoning, administrative knowledge, layout understanding
description: EuraGovExam is a multilingual and multimodal benchmark sourced from real-world civil service examinations across five Eurasian regions, containing high-resolution scanned questions covering 17 academic and administrative domains. It embeds all content within single images requiring layout-aware, cross-lingual reasoning directly from visual input.
paper_title: EuraGovExam: A Multilingual Multimodal Benchmark from Real-World Civil Service Exams
authors: JaeSeong Kim, Chaehwan Lim, Sang Hyun Gil, Suan Lee

description: The first Traditional Chinese benchmark for evaluating multimodal models with 900 multiple-choice questions sourced from official proficiency tests. Includes performance and latency evaluation for any-to-any multimodal models.
paper_title: Multi-TW: Benchmarking Multimodal Models on Traditional Chinese Question Answering in Taiwan
authors: Jui-Ming Yao, Bing-Cheng Xie, Sheng-Wei Peng, Hao-Yuan Chen, He-Rong Zheng

task_types: image captioning, visual question answering, robotic control, digital gameplay, manipulation
description: MultiNet provides a comprehensive evaluation framework for vision-language-action models with standardized protocols for VLMs and VLAs. It includes a composite dataset with over 1.3 trillion tokens covering image captioning, VQA, robotic control, digital gameplay, and manipulation tasks.
paper_title: An Open-Source Software Toolkit & Benchmark Suite for the Evaluation and Adaptation of Multimodal Action Models
authors: Pranav Guruprasad, Yangyue Wang, Sudipta Chowdhury, Jaewoo Song, Harshvardhan Sikka

task_types: visual grounding, spatial reasoning, tool use, physical commonsense, multi-agent coordination, continuous robot control
description: A unified benchmark for measuring cross-domain generality of VLMs and VLAs across six foundational capability regimes: visual grounding, spatial reasoning, tool use, physical commonsense, multi-agent coordination, and continuous robot control. It uniquely tests generalization across diverse real-world domains beyond training distributions.
paper_title: Benchmarking the Generality of Vision-Language-Action Models
authors: Pranav Guruprasad, Sudipta Chowdhury, Harsh Sikka, Mridul Sharma, Helen Lu

description: The paper proposes a novel VQA benchmark based on visual classification datasets that allows granular evaluation of text-generative vision-language models. It uses semantic hierarchy to generate follow-up questions and compares traditional NLP metrics with LLM-based metrics for evaluation.
paper_title: Open-ended VQA benchmarking of Vision-Language models by exploiting Classification datasets and their semantic hierarchy
authors: Simon Ging, María A. Bravo, Thomas Brox

description: AdaptMMBench is a comprehensive benchmark for adaptive multimodal reasoning across five domains that evaluates dynamic mode selection between tool-augmented visual reasoning and text reasoning. It uses MCC metric to assess selection rationality and facilitates multi-dimensional process evaluation.
paper_title: AdaptMMBench: Benchmarking Adaptive Multimodal Reasoning for Mode Selection and Reasoning Process
authors: Xintong Zhang, Xiaowen Zhang, Jongrong Wu, Zhi Gao, Shilin Yan

description: DevBench is a multimodal developmental benchmark comprising seven language evaluation tasks spanning lexical, syntactic, and semantic abilities with behavioral data from children and adults. It enables comparison of vision-language models to human language development trajectories.
paper_title: DevBench: A multimodal developmental benchmark for language learning
authors: Alvin Wei Ming Tan, Sunny Yu, Bria Long, Wanjing Anya Ma, Tonya Murray

description: VisTW is the first comprehensive VLM evaluation suite for Traditional Chinese, consisting of VisTW-MCQ (exam questions from 21 subjects) and VisTW-Dialogue (131 image-question pairs in Taiwanese cultural contexts). It addresses the gap in evaluation for Traditional Chinese used in Taiwan and Hong Kong.
paper_title: VisTW: Benchmarking Vision-Language Models for Traditional Chinese in Taiwan
authors: Zhi Rui Tam, Ya-Ting Pai, Yen-Wei Lee, Yun-Nung Chen

description: SugarCrepe addresses hackable biases in vision-language compositionality benchmarks by using large language models to generate fluent hard negatives and employing adversarial refinement to reduce biases. It provides a more robust evaluation of compositional understanding compared to existing benchmarks.
paper_title: SugarCrepe: Fixing Hackable Benchmarks for Vision-Language Compositionality
authors: Cheng-Yu Hsieh, Jieyu Zhang, Zixian Ma, Aniruddha Kembhavi, Ranjay Krishna

task_types: visual question answering, disaster response, situational awareness
description: DisasterVQA provides 1,395 real-world disaster images with 4,405 expert-curated question-answer pairs spanning floods, wildfires, and earthquakes. Grounded in humanitarian frameworks (FEMA ESF, OCHA MIRA), it evaluates situational awareness and operational decision-making in crisis contexts.
paper_title: DisasterVQA: A Visual Question Answering Benchmark Dataset for Disaster Scenes
authors: Aisha Al-Mohannadi, Ayisha Firoz, Yin Yang, Muhammad Imran, Ferda Ofli

task_types: instruction following, visual question answering, creative generation
description: VisIT-Bench evaluates instruction-following vision-language models on 70 diverse instruction families ranging from basic recognition to game playing and creative generation. It features human-authored instruction-conditioned captions that enable both human verification and automatic evaluation using text-only LLMs.
paper_title: VisIT-Bench: A Benchmark for Vision-Language Instruction Following Inspired by Real-World Use
authors: Yonatan Bitton, Hritik Bansal, Jack Hessel, Rulin Shao, Wanrong Zhu

task_types: multi-turn conversation, dialogue evaluation, contextual reasoning
description: MultiVerse is a multi-turn conversation benchmark featuring 647 dialogues (averaging four turns) derived from 12 popular VLM evaluation benchmarks. It covers 484 tasks across diverse topics from perception to advanced reasoning, using checklist-based evaluation across 37 key aspects.
paper_title: MultiVerse: A Multi-Turn Conversation Benchmark for Evaluating Large Vision and Language Models
authors: Young-Jun Lee, Byung-Kwan Lee, Jianshu Zhang, Yechan Hwang, Byungsoo Ko

task_types: age estimation, facial analysis, zero-shot evaluation
description: VLAgeBench provides comprehensive zero-shot evaluation of LVLMs for facial age estimation on UTKFace and FG-NET datasets. It uses eight evaluation metrics and highlights performance disparities linked to image quality and demographic subgroups.
paper_title: VLAgeBench: Benchmarking Large Vision-Language Models for Zero-Shot Human Age Estimation
authors: Rakib Hossain Sajib, Md Kishor Morol, Rajan Das Gupta, Mohammad Sakib Mahmood, Shuvra Smaran Das

description: A-OKVQA is a crowdsourced dataset of about 25K questions requiring broad commonsense and world knowledge that cannot be answered by simply querying knowledge bases. It requires commonsense reasoning about scenes depicted in images rather than basic visual recognition.
paper_title: A-OKVQA: A Benchmark for Visual Question Answering using World Knowledge
authors: Dustin Schwenk, Apoorv Khandelwal, Christopher Clark, Kenneth Marino, Roozbeh Mottaghi

modalities: text, diagrams, function graphs, maps, photos
description: A multimodal benchmark based on Chinese College Entrance Examination (GAOKAO) comprising 8 subjects and 12 types of images. It sets human-level requirements for perception, understanding, knowledge and reasoning derived from native Chinese context.
paper_title: GAOKAO-MM: A Chinese Human-Level Benchmark for Multimodal Models Evaluation
authors: Yi Zong, Xipeng Qiu

description: A benchmark for detailed image captioning with high-quality evaluation datasets annotated by human experts, GPT-4V and Gemini-1.5-Pro. It introduces CAPTURE metric that extracts visual elements and matches them through three stages for more reliable caption evaluation.
paper_title: Benchmarking and Improving Detail Image Caption
authors: Hongyuan Dong, Jiawen Li, Bohong Wu, Jiacong Wang, Yuan Zhang

description: Introduces a knowledge-based VQA evaluation benchmark that focuses on factuality and accuracy of vision-language models. The benchmark uses human judgment and perception to evaluate the accuracy of AI-generated answers relative to standard answers in knowledge-based VQA tasks.
paper_title: KNVQA: A Benchmark for evaluation knowledge-based VQA
authors: Sirui Cheng, Siyu Zhang, Jiayi Wu, Muchen Lan

task_types: prompt inversion, style retrieval, image captioning, visual question answering
description: Comprehensive dataset for generative image understanding with 4 million high-quality generated images paired with text prompts. Includes four benchmarks: prompt inversion, style retrieval, image captioning, and VQA for evaluating comprehension of synthetic content.
paper_title: JourneyDB: A Benchmark for Generative Image Understanding
authors: Keqiang Sun, Junting Pan, Yuying Ge, Hao Li, Haodong Duan

description: Large Vision-Language Model Knowledge Editing Benchmark that extends evaluation metrics to include Portability alongside Reliability, Locality, and Generality. Uses multi-modal knowledge graph with entity-bound image data for comprehensive knowledge editing assessment.
paper_title: VLKEB: A Large Vision-Language Model Knowledge Editing Benchmark
authors: Han Huang, Haitian Zhong, Tao Yu, Qiang Liu, Shu Wu

description: Culturally-diverse multilingual VQA benchmark covering 31 languages with 13 scripts from 30 countries on four continents. Features culturally-driven images and questions collected by native speakers and cultural experts, addressing Western-centric bias in existing VQA datasets.
paper_title: CVQA: Culturally-diverse Multilingual Visual Question Answering Benchmark
authors: David Romero, Chenyang Lyu, Haryo Akbarianto Wibowo, Teresa Lynn, Injy Hamed

modalities: text, images, tables, figures, diagrams, maps, scientific_symbols, equations
description: Multi-discipline multilingual multimodal exam benchmark covering 20 school disciplines across natural science, social science, and miscellaneous studies in 11 languages from 7 language families. Features diverse multimodal elements and requires region-specific knowledge from various education systems.
paper_title: EXAMS-V: A Multi-Discipline Multilingual Multimodal Exam Benchmark for Evaluating Vision Language Models
authors: Rocktim Jyoti Das, Simeon Emilov Hristov, Haonan Li, Dimitar Iliyanov Dimitrov, Ivan Koychev

description: Large-scale knowledge-intensive benchmark for evaluating interleaved multimodal comprehension and generation spanning 3 categories, 12 fields, and 102 subfields. Supports both interleaved inputs and outputs with automated evaluation using scoring model fine-tuned on human-annotated data.
paper_title: MMIE: Massive Multimodal Interleaved Comprehension Benchmark for Large Vision-Language Models
authors: Peng Xia, Siwei Han, Shi Qiu, Yiyang Zhou, Zhaoyang Wang

num_samples: 96 visual and 87 textual corruptions across 7 benchmark datasets
description: First comprehensive benchmark assessing robustness of 11 adaptation methods on pre-trained vision-language models under multimodal corruptions. Introduces systematic evaluation of adaptation methods' sensitivity to distribution shifts across both visual and textual modalities.
paper_title: Benchmarking Robustness of Adaptation Methods on Pre-trained Vision-Language Models
authors: Shuo Chen, Jindong Gu, Zhen Han, Yunpu Ma, Philip Torr

description: Multi-task multi-dimension benchmark for evaluating generalization capabilities and efficiency-performance trade-off of VLP models. Uses out-of-distribution test sets with images from diverse cultural distributions to measure true generalization beyond pre-training data.
paper_title: VLUE: A Multi-Task Benchmark for Evaluating Vision-Language Models
authors: Wangchunshu Zhou, Yan Zeng, Shizhe Diao, Xinsong Zhang

description: First multi-turn conversation evaluation benchmark for LVLMs with three-level hierarchical capability assessment (perception, reasoning, creativity). Enables precise attribution of conversation mistakes to specific cognitive levels, mimicking human cognitive progression.
paper_title: ConvBench: A Multi-Turn Conversation Evaluation Benchmark with Hierarchical Capability for Large Vision-Language Models
authors: Shuo Liu, Kaining Ying, Hao Zhang, Yue Yang, Yuqi Lin

task_types: spatial reasoning, memory tasks, vocabulary understanding
description: Developmentally grounded benchmark adapting NIH Baby Toolbox vision measures into ten multimodal tasks covering spatial reasoning, memory, and vocabulary understanding aligned with early children's capabilities. Part of BabyVLM-V2 framework for infant-inspired vision-language modeling.
paper_title: BabyVLM-V2: Toward Developmentally Grounded Pretraining and Benchmarking of Vision Foundation Models
authors: Shengao Wang, Wenqi Wang, Zecheng Wang, Max Whitton, Michael Wakeham

description: Multimodal fact-seeking benchmark enabling streamlined and decoupled evaluation of LVLMs in visual and linguistic modalities. Incorporates well-defined difficulty criteria with challenging subset VisualSimpleQA-hard, addressing limitations of current benchmarks in modality-specific assessment.
paper_title: VisualSimpleQA: A Benchmark for Decoupled Evaluation of Large Vision-Language Models in Fact-Seeking Question Answering
authors: Yanling Wang, Yihan Zhao, Xiaodong Chen, Shasha Guo, Lixin Liu

task_types: cross-modal topical matching, text-only topical matching
description: MVL-SIB is a massively multilingual vision-language benchmark covering 205 languages (over 100 more than existing benchmarks) for cross-modal topical matching. It uniquely evaluates both cross-modal and text-only topical matching to reveal how VL support declines disproportionately for lower-resource languages compared to textual support.
paper_title: MVL-SIB: A Massively Multilingual Vision-Language Benchmark for Cross-Modal Topical Matching
authors: Fabian David Schmidt, Florian Schneider, Chris Biemann, Goran Glavaš

task_types: visual dialogue, embodied navigation, multimodal reasoning
description: MMT-Bench provides comprehensive evaluation across massive multimodal tasks with 31,325 multi-choice visual questions covering 32 core meta-tasks and 162 subtasks. It enables task map evaluation for discovering in- and out-of-domain tasks, focusing on expert knowledge and deliberate visual recognition, localization, reasoning, and planning from scenarios like vehicle driving and embodied navigation.
paper_title: MMT-Bench: A Comprehensive Multimodal Benchmark for Evaluating Large Vision-Language Models Towards Multitask AGI
authors: Kaining Ying, Fanqing Meng, Jin Wang, Zhiqian Li, Han Lin

description: CompreCap evaluates detailed image captioning through directed scene graphs, manually segmenting images into semantic regions with object attributes and directional relationships. It assesses generated captions on multiple levels including object-level coverage, attribute accuracy, and key relationships, with evaluation closely aligned to human scores.
paper_title: Benchmarking Large Vision-Language Models via Directed Scene Graph for Comprehensive Image Captioning
authors: Fan Lu, Wei Wu, Kecheng Zheng, Shuailei Ma, Biao Gong

description: GIMMICK is an extensive multimodal benchmark assessing cultural knowledge across 144 countries representing six global macro-regions. It comprises six tasks spanning 728 unique cultural events/facets, systematically examining regional biases, model size effects, input modalities, and external cues to advance globally inclusive LVLM research.
