paper_title: GIMMICK -- Globally Inclusive Multimodal Multitask Cultural Knowledge Benchmarking
authors: Florian Schneider, Carolin Holtermann, Chris Biemann, Anne Lauscher

description: MME is the first comprehensive MLLM evaluation benchmark that measures both perception and cognition abilities across 14 subtasks. It features manually designed instruction-answer pairs to avoid data leakage and uses concise instructions for fair comparison without prompt engineering challenges.
paper_title: MME: A Comprehensive Evaluation Benchmark for Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Chaoyou Fu, Peixian Chen, Yunhang Shen, Yulei Qin, Mengdan Zhang

description: GOLD benchmark applies structural equation modeling principles and a Piaget-inspired capability hierarchy to reorganize MLLM evaluation into Perception, Memory, and Reasoning dimensions. It provides superior interpretability, lower indicator redundancy, and clearer cognitive consistency compared to existing heuristically-grouped benchmarks.
paper_title: Aligning MLLM Benchmark With Human Preferences via Structural Equation Modeling
authors: Shengwu. Xiong, Tianyu. Zou, Cong. Wang, Xuelong Li

task_types: visual question answering, visual memory, cross-lingual consistency
description: Two benchmarks evaluating cross-lingual consistency in MLLMs: KnowRecall measures factual knowledge consistency in 15 languages with cultural/historical questions about landmarks, while VisRecall assesses visual memory consistency in 9 languages without image access.
paper_title: Traveling Across Languages: Benchmarking Cross-Lingual Consistency in Multimodal LLMs
authors: Hao Wang, Pinzhi Huang, Jihan Yang, Saining Xie, Daisuke Kawahara

task_types: citation generation, text generation with grounding
description: MCiteBench is the first benchmark to assess MLLMs' ability to generate text with citations in multimodal contexts. It features data from academic papers and review-rebuttal interactions with diverse information sources to evaluate citation grounding capabilities.
paper_title: MCiteBench: A Multimodal Benchmark for Generating Text with Citations
authors: Caiyu Hu, Yikai Zhang, Tinghui Zhu, Yiwei Ye, Yanghua Xiao

task_types: few-shot learning, in-context learning, chain-of-thought reasoning
description: FewMMBench is a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating MLLMs under few-shot conditions, focusing on In-Context Learning and Chain-of-Thought prompting. It covers diverse multimodal understanding tasks from attribute recognition to temporal reasoning.
paper_title: FewMMBench: A Benchmark for Multimodal Few-Shot Learning
authors: Mustafa Dogan, Ilker Kesen, Iacer Calixto, Aykut Erdem, Erkut Erdem

task_types: continual learning, instruction following, knowledge retention
description: CoIN is a comprehensive benchmark for assessing MLLMs in sequential instruction tuning paradigm, comprising 10 datasets spanning 8 task categories. It evaluates both Instruction Following and General Knowledge to assess alignment and knowledge preservation.
paper_title: CoIN: A Benchmark of Continual Instruction tuNing for Multimodel Large Language Model
authors: Cheng Chen, Junchen Zhu, Xu Luo, Hengtao Shen, Lianli Gao

description: HERM-Bench evaluates MLLMs' human-centric understanding capabilities across various human-centered scenarios. It reveals limitations in existing models and introduces HERM-100K dataset with multi-level human-centric annotations for enhanced training.
paper_title: HERM: Benchmarking and Enhancing Multimodal LLMs for Human-Centric Understanding
authors: Keliang Li, Zaifei Yang, Jiahe Zhao, Hongze Shen, Ruibing Hou

description: SEED-Bench-2 evaluates hierarchical capabilities of MLLMs across different modality levels (L0-L4) with 24K multiple-choice questions spanning 27 dimensions. It assesses both text and image generation capabilities with objective human annotations.
paper_title: SEED-Bench-2: Benchmarking Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Bohao Li, Yuying Ge, Yixiao Ge, Guangzhi Wang, Rui Wang

description: HSSBench is a dedicated benchmark designed to assess MLLMs' capabilities on Humanities and Social Sciences (HSS) tasks in multiple languages, including six UN official languages. It contains over 13,000 meticulously designed samples covering six key categories, addressing the gap in current benchmarks that emphasize STEM reasoning while overlooking HSS needs for horizontal, interdisciplinary thinking.
paper_title: HSSBench: Benchmarking Humanities and Social Sciences Ability for Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Zhaolu Kang, Junhao Gong, Jiaxu Yan, Wanke Xia, Yian Wang

task_types: facial expression recognition, visual question answering
description: FERBench provides systematic evaluation of 20 state-of-the-art MLLMs across four widely used facial expression recognition datasets. The benchmark incorporates VQA formats for FER tasks and introduces specialized datasets for improving facial expression reasoning capabilities.
paper_title: Rethinking Facial Expression Recognition in the Era of Multimodal Large Language Models: Benchmark, Datasets, and Beyond
authors: Fan Zhang, Haoxuan Li, Shengju Qian, Xin Wang, Zheng Lian

description: OMHBench evaluates omni-modal multi-hop reasoning with balanced reasoning paths jointly grounded across text, vision, and speech modalities. It addresses critical limitations in existing frameworks including modality shortcuts and biased reasoning paths.
paper_title: OMHBench: Benchmarking Balanced and Grounded Omni-Modal Multi-Hop Reasoning
authors: Seunghee Kim, Ingyu Bang, Seokgyu Jang, Changhyeon Kim, Sanghwan Bae

task_types: misinformation detection, hate speech detection, social context generation
description: MM-Soc is a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating MLLMs' understanding of multimodal social media content. It compiles prominent multimodal datasets and incorporates a novel large-scale YouTube tagging dataset, targeting tasks from misinformation detection to hate speech detection and social context generation.
paper_title: MM-Soc: Benchmarking Multimodal Large Language Models in Social Media Platforms
authors: Yiqiao Jin, Minje Choi, Gaurav Verma, Jindong Wang, Srijan Kumar

task_types: intent recognition, emotion recognition, dialogue act classification, sentiment analysis, speaking style analysis, communication behavior analysis
description: MMLA is a comprehensive benchmark with over 61K multimodal utterances for evaluating cognitive-level semantics understanding. It covers six core dimensions: intent, emotion, dialogue act, sentiment, speaking style, and communication behavior across both staged and real-world scenarios.
paper_title: Can Large Language Models Help Multimodal Language Analysis? MMLA: A Comprehensive Benchmark
authors: Hanlei Zhang, Zhuohang Li, Yeshuang Zhu, Hua Xu, Peiwu Wang

task_types: punchline comprehension, humor understanding, sarcasm detection
description: PunchBench is a multimodal punchline comprehension benchmark that addresses limitations in existing benchmarks by generating synonymous and antonymous captions to mitigate language shortcuts. It incorporates diverse question formats and image-captions from various domains for comprehensive humor/sarcasm evaluation.
paper_title: PunchBench: Benchmarking MLLMs in Multimodal Punchline Comprehension
authors: Kun Ouyang, Yuanxin Liu, Shicheng Li, Yi Liu, Hao Zhou

task_types: information extraction, multi-turn reasoning, information update, image management, memory recall, answer refusal
description: Introduces a Multi-Modal Real-world Conversation benchmark for evaluating six core open-ended abilities of MLLMs in sustained interactions. Comprises 5,120 conversations and 28,720 manually labeled questions from real-world scenarios, evaluating information extraction, multi-turn reasoning, and memory recall capabilities.
paper_title: MMRC: A Large-Scale Benchmark for Understanding Multimodal Large Language Model in Real-World Conversation
authors: Haochen Xue, Feilong Tang, Ming Hu, Yexin Liu, Qidong Huang

task_types: emotion recognition, emotion understanding, multimodal emotion analysis
description: Establishes a new benchmark for MLLM-based emotion understanding with a novel dataset (MER-Caption) featuring over 2K fine-grained emotion categories across 115K samples. Introduces unified evaluation metrics tailored for both typical MER tasks and free-form natural language output.
paper_title: AffectGPT: A New Dataset, Model, and Benchmark for Emotion Understanding with Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Zheng Lian, Haoyu Chen, Lan Chen, Haiyang Sun, Licai Sun

task_types: confidence evaluation, robustness testing, calibration assessment
description: Introduces the first comprehensive benchmark to systematically evaluate reliability of step-level confidence scores generated by MLLM-based process judges. Features three types of adversarially perturbed reasoning steps and three novel evaluation metrics for robustness, sensitivity, and calibration assessment.
paper_title: ConfProBench: A Confidence Evaluation Benchmark for MLLM-Based Process Judges
authors: Yue Zhou, Yi Chang, Yuan Wu

task_types: multiple choice questions, spatial understanding, temporal understanding
description: A benchmark with 19K multiple choice questions spanning 12 evaluation dimensions for both image and video modality comprehension. Unique for its large scale, precise human annotations, and objective assessment eliminating need for human or GPT intervention during evaluation through multiple-choice format.
paper_title: SEED-Bench: Benchmarking Multimodal LLMs with Generative Comprehension
authors: Bohao Li, Rui Wang, Guangzhi Wang, Yuying Ge, Yixiao Ge

description: A benchmark to evaluate unified models capable of generating both images and text, comprising expert-curated questions requiring multimodal outputs from 8 real-world tasks. Unique for its rubric-based scoring system with 10,417 validated criteria enabling scalable fine-grained automatic scoring of open-ended multimodal generation.
paper_title: UEval: A Benchmark for Unified Multimodal Generation
authors: Bo Li, Yida Yin, Wenhao Chai, Xingyu Fu, Zhuang Liu

description: CA-MER is designed to examine MLLMs under realistic emotion conflicts where emotional cues from different modalities are inconsistent. It consists of three subsets: video-aligned, audio-aligned, and consistent, where only one or all modalities reflect the true emotion.
paper_title: Benchmarking and Bridging Emotion Conflicts for Multimodal Emotion Reasoning
authors: Zhiyuan Han, Beier Zhu, Yanlong Xu, Peipei Song, Xun Yang

task_types: facial attribute recognition, face perception, visual question answering
description: FaceBench features hierarchical multi-view and multi-level facial attributes specifically designed to assess comprehensive face perception abilities of MLLMs. It encompasses five views with up to three levels of attributes, totaling over 210 attributes and 700 attribute values, with 49,919 VQA pairs for evaluation.
paper_title: FaceBench: A Multi-View Multi-Level Facial Attribute VQA Dataset for Benchmarking Face Perception MLLMs
authors: Xiaoqin Wang, Xusen Ma, Xianxu Hou, Meidan Ding, Yudong Li

task_types: infrared image understanding, visual question answering
description: IF-Bench is the first high-quality benchmark designed for evaluating multimodal understanding of infrared images. It consists of 499 images from 23 infrared datasets and 680 visual question-answer pairs, covering 10 essential dimensions of image understanding in the infrared domain.
paper_title: IF-Bench: Benchmarking and Enhancing MLLMs for Infrared Images with Generative Visual Prompting
authors: Tao Zhang, Yuyang Hong, Yang Xia, Kun Ding, Zeyu Zhang

description: Multi-view benchmark evaluating visual captioning across 12 dimensions spanning six critical views. Introduces precision and hit metrics for assessing both correctness and thoroughness, plus heuristic metric for QA-caption capability gaps.
paper_title: CAPability: A Comprehensive Visual Caption Benchmark for Evaluating Both Correctness and Thoroughness
authors: Zhihang Liu, Chen-Wei Xie, Bin Wen, Feiwu Yu, Jixuan Chen

task_types: emotion recognition, scene reasoning, intent understanding
description: Hierarchical benchmark for multimodal emotion understanding using social media data. Features progression from basic perception to advanced cognition tasks requiring scene reasoning, intent understanding, and deep empathy integration.
paper_title: Seeing is Not Understanding: A Benchmark on Perception-Cognition Disparities in Large Language Models
authors: Haokun Li, Yazhou Zhang, Jizhi Ding, Qiuchi Li, Peng Zhang

task_types: visual search, textual search, visual question answering
description: Assesses Vision-DeepResearch systems under realistic conditions where visual search cannot rely on near-exact image matching and textual search is sufficiently challenging. Addresses information leakage through cross-textual cues in existing benchmarks.
paper_title: Vision-DeepResearch Benchmark: Rethinking Visual and Textual Search for Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Yu Zeng, Wenxuan Huang, Zhen Fang, Shuang Chen, Yufan Shen

task_types: continual learning, instruction tuning, reasoning diagnosis
description: Comprehensive benchmark for continual instruction tuning covering seven challenging tasks across six diverse domains. Features multidimensional evaluation of final-answer accuracy and process-level reasoning quality using Chain-of-Thought traces.
paper_title: MLLM-CTBench: A Benchmark for Continual Instruction Tuning with Reasoning Process Diagnosis
authors: Haiyun Guo, Zhiyan Hou, Yandu Sun, Jinghan He, Yu Chen

task_types: proactive assistance, maintenance monitoring, safety monitoring
description: Benchmark for training and evaluating proactive agents across diverse domains with 91,581 step-level annotations enriched with explicit task graphs. Enables entropy-driven heuristic search and parallel action execution evaluation.
paper_title: ProAct: A Benchmark and Multimodal Framework for Structure-Aware Proactive Response
authors: Xiaomeng Zhu, Fengming Zhu, Weijie Zhou, Ye Tian, Zhenlin Hu

description: Introduces the first benchmark for evaluating deception detection in dynamic multi-party conversations using multimodal data from the Werewolf social deduction game. The dataset provides synchronized video and text with verifiable ground-truth labels for every statement, testing MLLMs' ability to distinguish truth from falsehood using both verbal and non-verbal visual cues.
paper_title: Can MLLMs Read the Room? A Multimodal Benchmark for Verifying Truthfulness in Multi-Party Social Interactions
authors: Caixin Kang, Yifei Huang, Liangyang Ouyang, Mingfang Zhang, Yoichi Sato

task_types: multimodal retrieval, cross-modal reasoning, evidence localization
description: First benchmark designed to evaluate both retrieval and reasoning under large-scale, cross-modal conditions with over 46,000 multimodal retrieval candidates across documents, images, and videos. Each of 747 questions requires evidence localization across modalities and fine-grained reasoning from a unique validated evidence item within the heterogeneous retrieval pool.
paper_title: MultiHaystack: Benchmarking Multimodal Retrieval and Reasoning over 40K Images, Videos, and Documents
authors: Dannong Xu, Zhongyu Yang, Jun Chen, Yingfang Yuan, Ming Hu

task_types: meme reappraisal, emotion transformation, multimodal generation
description: Novel benchmark for Meme Reappraisal task that transforms negatively framed memes into constructive ones while preserving scenario, entities, and layout. Features real-world memes with fine-grained multimodal annotations including source/target emotions, rewritten text, visual editing specifications, and taxonomy labels covering visual type, sentiment polarity, and layout structure.
paper_title: MER-Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Multimodal Meme Reappraisal
authors: Yiqi Nie, Fei Wang, Junjie Chen, Kun Li, Yudi Cai

modalities: text, image, audio, video, document, code, 3D
task_types: any-to-any multimodal, interleaved understanding, multimodal generation
description: First Unified Any-to-Any Interleaved Multimodal dataset containing 31K high-quality instances across 30 domains and 7 modalities (text, image, audio, video, document, code, 3D). Evaluates models along three dimensions: Semantic Correctness & Generation Quality, Response Structure Integrity, and Interleaved Coherence, requiring multiple intertwined reasoning and generation capabilities.
paper_title: UniM: A Unified Any-to-Any Interleaved Multimodal Benchmark
authors: Yanlin Li, Minghui Guo, Kaiwen Zhang, Shize Zhang, Yiran Zhao

task_types: multimodal knowledge editing, error correction, modality consistency
description: Fine-grained Multimodal Knowledge Editing benchmark emphasizing Modality Consistency that decomposes multimodal knowledge into visual and textual components. Facilitates independent correction of misreading and misrecognition errors by editing corresponding knowledge components, with systematic analysis of different error types and editing formats.
paper_title: MC-MKE: A Fine-Grained Multimodal Knowledge Editing Benchmark Emphasizing Modality Consistency
authors: Junzhe Zhang, Huixuan Zhang, Xunjian Yin, Baizhou Huang, Xu Zhang

description: FysicsWorld is the first unified full-modality benchmark supporting bidirectional any-to-any evaluation across understanding, generation, and reasoning with image, video, audio, and text. It uniquely enables comprehensive omni-modal evaluation with Cross-Modal Complementarity Screening for fusion-dependent reasoning.
paper_title: FysicsWorld: A Unified Full-Modality Benchmark for Any-to-Any Understanding, Generation, and Reasoning
authors: Yue Jiang, Dingkang Yang, Minghao Han, Jinghang Han, Zizhi Chen

description: OpenING evaluates interleaved image-text generation across 56 real-world tasks covering diverse daily scenarios like travel guides and design. It uniquely challenges integrated multimodal understanding and generation abilities with high-quality human annotations and introduces IntJudge for evaluation.
paper_title: OpenING: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Judging Open-ended Interleaved Image-Text Generation
authors: Pengfei Zhou, Xiaopeng Peng, Jiajun Song, Chuanhao Li, Zhaopan Xu

description: A massive image embedding benchmark spanning 38 languages across 130 individual tasks grouped into 8 high-level categories. Evaluates both image and image-text embedding models across the broadest spectrum to date, revealing capabilities like visual text representation and limitations in interleaved encodings.
paper_title: MIEB: Massive Image Embedding Benchmark
authors: Chenghao Xiao, Isaac Chung, Imene Kerboua, Jamie Stirling, Xin Zhang

description: The first cognitive-driven multi-lingual and multi-modal benchmark evaluating general intelligence ability based on the Cattell-Horn-Carrol (CHC) model. Covers six languages (English, Chinese, French, Spanish, Portuguese, Korean) with culturally appropriate native content to avoid English-centric bias.
paper_title: M3GIA: A Cognition Inspired Multilingual and Multimodal General Intelligence Ability Benchmark
authors: Wei Song, Yadong Li, Jianhua Xu, Guowei Wu, Lingfeng Ming

task_types: image editing, commonsense QA with generation, geometric reasoning
description: A comprehensive evaluation framework for Unified MLLMs including standardized traditional task evaluation from 12 datasets covering 10 tasks with 30 subtasks, plus five novel unified tasks testing multimodal reasoning like image editing and geometric reasoning with mixed-modality generation.
paper_title: MME-Unify: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Unified Multimodal Understanding and Generation Models
authors: Wulin Xie, Yi-Fan Zhang, Chaoyou Fu, Yang Shi, Bingyan Nie

description: MCIF is the first crosslingual human-annotated multimodal benchmark based on scientific talks, evaluating instruction following across four languages and three core modalities. It uniquely assesses crosslingual and multimodal capabilities over both short and long-form inputs across four macro-tasks: recognition, translation, question answering, and summarization.
paper_title: MCIF: Multimodal Crosslingual Instruction-Following Benchmark from Scientific Talks
authors: Sara Papi, Maike Züfle, Marco Gaido, Beatrice Savoldi, Danni Liu

task_types: instruction tuning, 2D vision, 3D vision, human-AI interaction
description: LAMM provides a comprehensive Language-Assisted Multi-Modal instruction tuning dataset, framework, and benchmark covering a wide range of 2D and 3D vision tasks. It establishes a growing ecosystem for training and evaluating MLLMs with focus on bridging ideas and execution for seamless human-AI interaction.
paper_title: LAMM: Language-Assisted Multi-Modal Instruction-Tuning Dataset, Framework, and Benchmark
authors: Zhenfei Yin, Jiong Wang, Jianjian Cao, Zhelun Shi, Dingning Liu

description: Multi-Modal Retrieval-Augmented Generation benchmark evaluating MLLMs' effectiveness in leveraging knowledge from multi-modal retrieval documents across four tasks: image captioning, multi-modal QA, fact verification, and image reranking in open-domain settings.
paper_title: Benchmarking Retrieval-Augmented Generation in Multi-Modal Contexts
authors: Zhenghao Liu, Xingsheng Zhu, Tianshuo Zhou, Xinyi Zhang, Xiaoyuan Yi

task_types: proactive interaction, object recognition, image quality assessment
description: ProactiveBench is built from seven repurposed datasets to test proactiveness in MLLMs - the ability to request simple user interventions when needed. It evaluates whether models can exhibit human-like collaborative behavior by asking for help in tasks like recognizing occluded objects or enhancing image quality.
paper_title: ProactiveBench: Benchmarking Proactiveness in Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Thomas De Min, Subhankar Roy, Stéphane Lathuilière, Elisa Ricci, Massimiliano Mancini

task_types: visual query pre-processing, agentic decision-making, multimodal retrieval
description: V-QPP-Bench is the first comprehensive benchmark for Visual Query Pre-processing in multimodal RAG, comprising 46,700 imperfect queries. It uniquely formulates visual query pre-processing as an agentic decision-making task where MLLMs must autonomously diagnose visual imperfections and deploy perceptual tools to refine queries before retrieval.
paper_title: Fix Before Search: Benchmarking Agentic Query Visual Pre-processing in Multimodal Retrieval-augmented Generation
authors: Jiankun Zhang, Shenglai Zeng, Kai Guo, Xinnan Dai, Hui Liu

description: This paper proposes a multimodal Judge Model framework for reliable evaluation across text, audio, image, and video modalities, spanning 280 multimodal samples. It uniquely focuses on providing explainable evaluation and diagnostic feedback rather than simple scoring, aggregating multimodal judgments for comprehensive assessment.
paper_title: Judge Model for Large-scale Multimodality Benchmarks
authors: Min-Han Shih, Yu-Hsin Wu, Yu-Wei Chen

task_types: visual question answering, text-to-image retrieval, visual evidence integration
description: Visual-RAG is a question-answering benchmark that targets visually grounded, knowledge-intensive questions requiring text-to-image retrieval and integration of retrieved clue images. It uniquely isolates and measures the contribution of retrieved images in RAG systems, unlike prior work that predominantly retrieves from textual corpora.
paper_title: Visual-RAG: Benchmarking Text-to-Image Retrieval Augmented Generation for Visual Knowledge Intensive Queries
authors: Yin Wu, Quanyu Long, Jing Li, Jianfei Yu, Wenya Wang

description: CompBench addresses the limitation of existing image editing benchmarks that oversimplify task complexity. It features challenging editing scenarios with fine-grained instruction following, spatial and contextual reasoning, using an instruction decoupling strategy that disentangles editing intents into location, appearance, dynamics, and objects dimensions.
paper_title: CompBench: Benchmarking Complex Instruction-guided Image Editing
authors: Bohan Jia, Wenxuan Huang, Yuntian Tang, Junbo Qiao, Jincheng Liao

description: MDSEval is the first meta-evaluation benchmark for Multimodal Dialogue Summarization, consisting of image-sharing dialogues with human judgments across eight well-defined quality aspects. It introduces a novel filtering framework using Mutually Exclusive Key Information (MEKI) to ensure data quality and richness.
paper_title: MDSEval: A Meta-Evaluation Benchmark for Multimodal Dialogue Summarization
authors: Yinhong Liu, Jianfeng He, Hang Su, Ruixue Lian, Yi Nian

description: Face-Human-Bench provides comprehensive evaluation of face and human understanding abilities using a hierarchical ability taxonomy with three levels. It includes 1800 problems each in development and test sets, supporting both English and Chinese, and focuses on abilities crucial for social interaction in everyday photos and videos.
paper_title: Face-Human-Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark of Face and Human Understanding for Multi-modal Assistants
authors: Lixiong Qin, Shilong Ou, Miaoxuan Zhang, Jiangning Wei, Yuhang Zhang

description: Youku-mPLUG provides the largest public Chinese high-quality video-language dataset with human-annotated benchmarks covering cross-modal retrieval, video captioning, and video category classification. It addresses the lack of large-scale Chinese video-language resources for multimodal research.
paper_title: Youku-mPLUG: A 10 Million Large-scale Chinese Video-Language Dataset for Pre-training and Benchmarks
authors: Haiyang Xu, Qinghao Ye, Xuan Wu, Ming Yan, Yuan Miao

description: VideoMCC introduces Video Multiple Choice Caption as a new well-defined task with an easy-to-interpret performance measure for video comprehension. It provides a general semi-automatic procedure to create benchmarks and includes a large-scale video benchmark with human performance assessment.
paper_title: VideoMCC: a New Benchmark for Video Comprehension
authors: Du Tran, Maksim Bolonkin, Manohar Paluri, Lorenzo Torresani

task_types: multi-image understanding, visual perception, document understanding, video understanding, medical imaging
description: CAMEL-Bench is a comprehensive Arabic LMM evaluation benchmark comprising eight diverse domains and 38 sub-domains with around 29,036 questions. It addresses the lack of Arabic-centric evaluation benchmarks, covering multi-image understanding, complex visual perception, handwritten documents, video understanding, medical imaging, and more.
paper_title: CAMEL-Bench: A Comprehensive Arabic LMM Benchmark
authors: Sara Ghaboura, Ahmed Heakl, Omkar Thawakar, Ali Alharthi, Ines Riahi

task_types: video question answering, object tracking, point tracking, temporal action localization, temporal sound localization
description: Perception Test is a multimodal video benchmark with 11.6k real-world videos (23s average length) that evaluates perception and reasoning skills across video, audio, and text modalities. It focuses on skills (Memory, Abstraction, Physics, Semantics) and reasoning types (descriptive, explanatory, predictive, counterfactual) with dense annotations including six types of labels.
paper_title: Perception Test: A Diagnostic Benchmark for Multimodal Video Models
authors: Viorica Pătrăucean, Lucas Smaira, Ankush Gupta, Adrià Recasens Continente, Larisa Markeeva

description: A multilingual video benchmark across 14 languages including low-resource languages, designed to evaluate cultural and linguistic inclusivity in video LMMs. Features 8 culturally diverse categories spanning lifestyles, festivals, foods, rituals, landmarks and cultural personalities with both open-ended and multiple-choice questions.
paper_title: A Culturally-diverse Multilingual Multimodal Video Benchmark & Model
authors: Bhuiyan Sanjid Shafique, Ashmal Vayani, Muhammad Maaz, Hanoona Abdul Rasheed, Dinura Dissanayake

task_types: object_categorization, object_localization, referring_expression_grounding, visual_question_answering, segmentation, human_keypoint_detection, surface_normal_estimation
description: GRIT evaluates the performance, robustness, and calibration of vision systems across seven diverse image prediction tasks including object categorization, localization, VQA, segmentation, and more. It is designed to assess robustness under image perturbations, distribution shifts, and concept shifts in a unified platform.
paper_title: GRIT: General Robust Image Task Benchmark
authors: Tanmay Gupta, Ryan Marten, Aniruddha Kembhavi, Derek Hoiem

description: LinguaMark evaluates state-of-the-art LMMs on multilingual VQA across 11 languages and five social attributes. The benchmark specifically addresses linguistic bias and fairness in multimodal models, comprising 6,875 image-text pairs with evaluation metrics for Bias, Answer Relevancy, and Faithfulness.
paper_title: LinguaMark: Do Multimodal Models Speak Fairly? A Benchmark-Based Evaluation
authors: Ananya Raval, Aravind Narayanan, Vahid Reza Khazaie, Shaina Raza

description: MangaVQA is the first benchmark for multimodal manga understanding, featuring 526 high-quality, manually constructed question-answer pairs designed to evaluate contextual understanding of Japanese comics. It addresses the complex multimodal narrative form that blends images and text in ways unique to manga.
paper_title: MangaVQA and MangaLMM: A Benchmark and Specialized Model for Multimodal Manga Understanding
authors: Jeonghun Baek, Kazuki Egashira, Shota Onohara, Atsuyuki Miyai, Yuki Imajuku

description: IGLUE brings together vision-and-language tasks across 20 diverse languages, aggregating pre-existing datasets and creating new ones for VQA, cross-modal retrieval, grounded reasoning, and grounded entailment. It enables evaluation in zero-shot and newly defined few-shot learning setups for multilingual multimodal models.
paper_title: IGLUE: A Benchmark for Transfer Learning across Modalities, Tasks, and Languages
authors: Emanuele Bugliarello, Fangyu Liu, Jonas Pfeiffer, Siva Reddy, Desmond Elliott

description: BabelBench evaluates LLM proficiency in managing multimodal multistructured data with code execution, incorporating 247 meticulously curated problems that challenge models with perception, commonsense reasoning, logical reasoning, and advanced capabilities in exploration, planning, reasoning and debugging.
paper_title: BabelBench: An Omni Benchmark for Code-Driven Analysis of Multimodal and Multistructured Data
authors: Xuwu Wang, Qiwen Cui, Yunzhe Tao, Yiran Wang, Ziwei Chai

description: AVA-Bench explicitly disentangles 14 Atomic Visual Abilities (AVAs) like localization, depth estimation, and spatial understanding. It addresses gaps in existing VQA evaluation by matching training and test distributions within each ability and pinpointing exactly where VFMs excel or fail.
paper_title: AVA-Bench: Atomic Visual Ability Benchmark for Vision Foundation Models
authors: Zheda Mai, Arpita Chowdhury, Zihe Wang, Sooyoung Jeon, Lemeng Wang

task_types: image classification, object detection, visual question answering
description: SNAP analyzes the impact of camera capture conditions (shutter speed, ISO sensitivity, aperture) on deep learning model performance across vision tasks. It provides controlled lighting conditions with densely sampled camera settings to study capture bias in vision datasets and model susceptibility to exposure variations.
paper_title: SNAP: A Benchmark for Testing the Effects of Capture Conditions on Fundamental Vision Tasks
authors: Iuliia Kotseruba, John K. Tsotsos

description: S3VQA builds around the structural idiom of Select, Substitute, and Search for knowledge-augmented visual question answering. It addresses limitations in OKVQA by providing questions that require consulting knowledge graphs or corpus passages about entities identified in images, with provisions for assessing semantic interpretation of queries.
paper_title: Select, Substitute, Search: A New Benchmark for Knowledge-Augmented Visual Question Answering
authors: Aman Jain, Mayank Kothyari, Vishwajeet Kumar, Preethi Jyothi, Ganesh Ramakrishnan

description: FRAMES-VQA evaluates robust fine-tuning for VQA tasks across multi-modal distribution shifts using ten existing benchmarks categorized into ID, near OOD, and far OOD datasets. It systematically analyzes uni-modal and multi-modal distribution shifts and their interactions to provide guidance for developing robust fine-tuning methods.
paper_title: FRAMES-VQA: Benchmarking Fine-Tuning Robustness across Multi-Modal Shifts in Visual Question Answering
authors: Chengyue Huang, Brisa Maneechotesuwan, Shivang Chopra, Zsolt Kira

task_types: multimodal judging, criterion-level evaluation, open-ended generation, verifiable reasoning
description: Evaluates multimodal judges on their ability to follow diverse, fine-grained evaluation criteria and produce reliable criterion-level judgments. Introduces metrics for pluralistic adherence, criterion-switching flexibility, and preference conflict recognition.
paper_title: Multi-Crit: Benchmarking Multimodal Judges on Pluralistic Criteria-Following
authors: Tianyi Xiong, Yi Ge, Ming Li, Zuolong Zhang, Pranav Kulkarni

task_types: academic question answering, caption generation, VQA, cultural knowledge assessment
description: First comprehensive multimodal Ukrainian-centric benchmark derived from standardized university entrance exams spanning 12 academic disciplines. Addresses the lack of evaluation suites for low- and mid-resource languages in multimodal settings.
paper_title: Benchmarking Multimodal Models for Ukrainian Language Understanding Across Academic and Cultural Domains
authors: Yurii Paniv, Artur Kiulian, Dmytro Chaplynskyi, Mykola Khandoga, Anton Polishko

description: Comprehensive evaluation benchmark for multimodal knowledge updating covering both updated knowledge and unknown knowledge scenarios. Enables systematic analysis of cross-modal consistency in knowledge learning.
paper_title: MMKU-Bench: A Multimodal Update Benchmark for Diverse Visual Knowledge
authors: Baochen Fu, Yuntao Du, Cheng Chang, Baihao Jin, Wenzhi Deng

description: Disentangled bimodal dataset designed to comprehensively evaluate joint generation and cross-generation capabilities of multimodal VAEs across multiple difficulty levels. Includes toolkit for systematic training and comparison.
paper_title: Benchmarking Multimodal Variational Autoencoders: CdSprites+ Dataset and Toolkit
authors: Gabriela Sejnova, Michal Vavrecka, Karla Stepanova, Tadahiro Taniguchi

task_types: visual reasoning, document understanding, OCR, scientific analysis, cultural interpretation
description: First benchmark evaluating step-by-step reasoning in Arabic across textual and visual modalities, spanning 11 domains including cultural interpretation. Provides structured framework for diagnosing multimodal reasoning in underrepresented languages.
paper_title: ARB: A Comprehensive Arabic Multimodal Reasoning Benchmark
authors: Sara Ghaboura, Ketan More, Wafa Alghallabi, Omkar Thawakar, Jorma Laaksonen

description: Systematic unified large-scale benchmark spanning 15 datasets, 10 modalities, 20 prediction tasks, and 6 research areas. Provides comprehensive methodology to assess generalization, complexity, and modality robustness with automated ML pipeline.
paper_title: MultiBench: Multiscale Benchmarks for Multimodal Representation Learning
authors: Paul Pu Liang, Yiwei Lyu, Xiang Fan, Zetian Wu, Yun Cheng

task_types: continual learning, task adaptation, knowledge transfer
description: First continual learning benchmark for vision-and-language tasks, addressing the challenge of learning multimodal tasks sequentially. Evaluates task adaptation and catastrophic forgetting in multimodal settings.
paper_title: CLiMB: A Continual Learning Benchmark for Vision-and-Language Tasks
authors: Tejas Srinivasan, Ting-Yun Chang, Leticia Leonor Pinto Alva, Georgios Chochlakis, Mohammad Rostami

description: Omni-dimensional benchmark for unified multimodal models assessing understanding, generation, and editing within single evaluation process. Covers 13 major domains and 200+ concepts with comprehensive capability assessment.
paper_title: UmniBench: Unified Understand and Generation Model Oriented Omni-dimensional Benchmark
authors: Kai Liu, Leyang Chen, Wenbo Li, Zhikai Chen, Zhixin Wang

task_types: knowledge editing, visual entity editing, visual semantic editing
description: MMKE-Bench is a comprehensive multimodal knowledge editing benchmark that evaluates the ability of large multimodal models to edit diverse visual knowledge. It incorporates three types of editing tasks including visual entity editing, visual semantic editing, and user-specific editing with 8,363 images across 33 broad categories.
paper_title: MMKE-Bench: A Multimodal Editing Benchmark for Diverse Visual Knowledge
authors: Yuntao Du, Kailin Jiang, Zhi Gao, Chenrui Shi, Zilong Zheng

description: MMKC-Bench is designed to evaluate factual knowledge conflicts in both context-memory and inter-context scenarios for large multimodal models. The benchmark encompasses three types of multimodal knowledge conflicts and includes 1,573 knowledge instances with 3,381 images across 23 broad types.
paper_title: Benchmarking Multimodal Knowledge Conflict for Large Multimodal Models
authors: Yifan Jia, Kailin Jiang, Yuyang Liang, Qihan Ren, Yi Xin

description: MULTIBENCH++ is a large-scale, domain-adaptive benchmark for multimodal fusion evaluation that integrates over 30 datasets encompassing 15 modalities and 20 predictive tasks. It provides a unified evaluation pipeline and establishes new performance baselines across multiple specialized domains.
paper_title: MULTIBENCH++: A Unified and Comprehensive Multimodal Fusion Benchmarking Across Specialized Domains
authors: Leyan Xue, Changqing Zhang, Kecheng Xue, Xiaohong Liu, Guangyu Wang

description: M-BEIR is a multimodal retrieval benchmark with comprehensive results designed to standardize the evaluation of universal multimodal information retrieval. It accompanies UniIR, a unified instruction-guided multimodal retriever capable of handling eight distinct retrieval tasks across modalities.
paper_title: UniIR: Training and Benchmarking Universal Multimodal Information Retrievers
authors: Cong Wei, Yang Chen, Haonan Chen, Hexiang Hu, Ge Zhang

task_types: multilingual assessment, educational evaluation, exam questions
description: M3Exam is a novel benchmark sourced from real official human exam questions for evaluating LLMs in multilingual, multimodal, and multilevel contexts. It contains 12,317 questions in 9 diverse languages across three educational levels, with about 23% requiring image processing for successful solving.
paper_title: M3Exam: A Multilingual, Multimodal, Multilevel Benchmark for Examining Large Language Models
authors: Wenxuan Zhang, Sharifah Mahani Aljunied, Chang Gao, Yew Ken Chia, Lidong Bing

description: GEM is a General Evaluation benchmark for Multimodal tasks consisting of GEM-I for image-language tasks and GEM-V for video-language tasks. It represents the largest vision-language dataset covering both image-language and video-language tasks simultaneously while being labeled in multiple languages.
paper_title: GEM: A General Evaluation Benchmark for Multimodal Tasks
authors: Lin Su, Nan Duan, Edward Cui, Lei Ji, Chenfei Wu

task_types: multimodal understanding, visual reasoning, integrated capabilities
description: MMMU-Pro is a robust version of the MMMU benchmark that rigorously assesses multimodal models' understanding and reasoning through filtering text-only answerable questions, augmenting candidate options, and introducing vision-only input settings. It provides a more rigorous evaluation tool closely mimicking real-world scenarios.
paper_title: MMMU-Pro: A More Robust Multi-discipline Multimodal Understanding Benchmark
authors: Xiang Yue, Tianyu Zheng, Yuansheng Ni, Yubo Wang, Kai Zhang

description: SURE is a multimodal recommendation dialog dataset containing 12K shopping dialogs in complex store scenes that demonstrates diverse expressions of user subjective preferences and recommendation acts. It's well-annotated with subjective preferences and recommendation acts proposed by sales experts for realistic shopping scenarios.
paper_title: Multimodal Recommendation Dialog with Subjective Preference: A New Challenge and Benchmark
authors: Yuxing Long, Binyuan Hui, Caixia Yuan1, Fei Huang, Yongbin Li

description: COMMA is a novel puzzle benchmark designed to evaluate collaborative performance of multimodal multi-agent systems through language communication. It features multimodal puzzles providing comprehensive evaluation across four key categories of agentic capability in communicative collaboration settings.
paper_title: COMMA: A Communicative Multimodal Multi-Agent Benchmark
authors: Timothy Ossowski, Danyal Maqbool, Jixuan Chen, Zefan Cai, Tyler Bradshaw

task_types: integrated VL capabilities, sequence understanding, OCR, spatial reasoning
description: MM-Vet v2 introduces a new VL capability called "image-text sequence understanding" to evaluate models' ability to process VL sequences, addressing the limitation of single image-text pairs. It maintains high evaluation quality while expanding the evaluation set size with interleaved image and text sequences.
paper_title: MM-Vet v2: A Challenging Benchmark to Evaluate Large Multimodal Models for Integrated Capabilities
authors: Weihao Yu, Zhengyuan Yang, Lingfeng Ren, Linjie Li, Jianfeng Wang

task_types: controllable captioning, instruction following, caption quality assessment
description: AnyCapEval is a new benchmark that provides more reliable evaluation metrics for controllable captioning by decoupling content accuracy and stylistic fidelity. It accompanies AnyCapDataset covering three modalities, 28 user-instruction types, and 300k high-quality data entries for omni-modal captioning.
paper_title: AnyCap Project: A Unified Framework, Dataset, and Benchmark for Controllable Omni-modal Captioning
authors: Yiming Ren, Zhiqiang Lin, Yu Li, Gao Meng, Weiyun Wang

task_types: multimodal retrieval, contradiction retrieval, reasoning-intensive tasks
description: MRMR is the first expert-level multidisciplinary multimodal retrieval benchmark requiring intensive reasoning, containing 1,502 queries spanning 23 domains. It introduces Contradiction Retrieval as a novel task and features image-text interleaved sequences with multi-image queries and mixed-modality corpus documents.
paper_title: MRMR: A Realistic and Expert-Level Multidisciplinary Benchmark for Reasoning-Intensive Multimodal Retrieval
authors: Siyue Zhang, Yuan Gao, Xiao Zhou, Yilun Zhao, Tingyu Song

task_types: complex reasoning, multi-disciplinary assessment, graduate-level evaluation
description: R-Bench is a graduate-level, multi-disciplinary benchmark spanning 1,094 questions for language models and 665 questions for multimodal models across multiple subjects in English and Chinese. It provides Olympiad-level multi-disciplinary assessment with rigorous difficulty calibration and cross-linguistic alignment.
paper_title: R-Bench: Graduate-level Multi-disciplinary Benchmarks for LLM & MLLM Complex Reasoning Evaluation
authors: Meng-Hao Guo, Jiajun Xu, Yi Zhang, Jiaxi Song, Haoyang Peng

task_types: image-text retrieval, visual reasoning, visual entailment, image captioning, text-to-image generation
description: Evaluates robustness of multimodal image-text models under distribution shifts by applying 17 image perturbation and 16 text perturbation techniques on existing datasets. Introduces new robustness metrics MMI (MultiModal Impact score) and MOR (Missing Object Rate) for proper evaluation of multimodal models.
paper_title: Benchmarking Robustness of Multimodal Image-Text Models under Distribution Shift
authors: Jielin Qiu, Yi Zhu, Xingjian Shi, Florian Wenzel, Zhiqiang Tang

task_types: API call decision-making, domain identification, function selection, argument generation
description: Pioneering comprehensive large-scale API benchmark dataset aimed at expanding LLMs' proficiency in multimodal contexts. Consists of 235 diverse API calls and 2,038 contextual prompts, offering evaluation platform for tool-augmented LLMs handling multimodal tasks.
paper_title: Beyond Text: Unveiling Multimodal Proficiency of Large Language Models with MultiAPI Benchmark
authors: Xiao Liu, Jianfeng Lin, Jiawei Zhang

description: Comprehensive benchmark for missing modality issue in robust multimodal affective computing. Establishes fair evaluation standards from cross-modal synergy perspective with protocols for both fixed and random missing patterns at dataset and instance levels.
paper_title: MissMAC-Bench: Building Solid Benchmark for Missing Modality Issue in Robust Multimodal Affective Computing
authors: Ronghao Lin, Honghao Lu, Ruixing Wu, Aolin Xiong, Qinggong Chu

task_types: note comprehension, user engagement analysis, information retrieval, personalized recommendation
description: Comprehensive multimodal benchmark designed to assess performance of Vision-Language LLMs in real-world social media scenarios. Incorporates 4,001 question-answer pairs across 8 multimodal tasks including note comprehension, user engagement analysis, and personalized recommendation.
paper_title: SNS-Bench-VL: Benchmarking Multimodal Large Language Models in Social Networking Services
authors: Hongcheng Guo, Zheyong Xie, Shaosheng Cao, Boyang Wang, Weiting Liu

task_types: source attribution, visual question answering, multimodal retrieval
description: MAVIS is the first benchmark for multimodal source attribution in long-form visual question answering, where systems must understand visual questions, retrieve multimodal evidence, and generate answers with fact-level citations. It evaluates informativeness, groundedness, and fluency with fine-grained automatic metrics.
paper_title: MAVIS: A Benchmark for Multimodal Source Attribution in Long-form Visual Question Answering
authors: Seokwon Song, Minsu Park, Gunhee Kim

task_types: multilingual vision-language tasks, visio-linguistic outlier detection
description: First comprehensive benchmark for evaluating large multimodal models across multilingual and multicultural vision-language tasks. Covers 8 datasets, 5 tasks, and 41 languages with focus on underrepresented languages and culturally diverse images.
paper_title: M5 -- A Diverse Benchmark to Assess the Performance of Large Multimodal Models Across Multilingual and Multicultural Vision-Language Tasks
authors: Florian Schneider, Sunayana Sitaram

description: First comprehensive evaluation benchmark for assessing multimodal search performance of large multimodal models. Evaluates requery, rerank, summarization tasks and end-to-end searching across 14 subfields with no overlap with training data.
paper_title: MMSearch: Benchmarking the Potential of Large Models as Multi-modal Search Engines
authors: Dongzhi Jiang, Renrui Zhang, Ziyu Guo, Yanmin Wu, Jiayi Lei

task_types: reciprocal cross-modal reasoning, verbally-augmented visual generation, visually-augmented reasoning
description: First benchmark explicitly targeting reciprocal cross-modal reasoning in unified multimodal models. Evaluates verbally-augmented reasoning for visual generation and visually-augmented reasoning for verbal generation across complementary settings.
paper_title: ROVER: Benchmarking Reciprocal Cross-Modal Reasoning for Omnimodal Generation
authors: Yongyuan Liang, Wei Chow, Feng Li, Ziqiao Ma, Xiyao Wang

description: Multimodal classification benchmark with eight datasets from game data covering tabular, textual, and visual modalities. Provides multi-aspect data analysis including label balance, missing features, and modality correlations.
paper_title: MuG: A Multimodal Classification Benchmark on Game Data with Tabular, Textual, and Visual Fields
authors: Jiaying Lu, Yongchen Qian, Shifan Zhao, Yuanzhe Xi, Carl Yang

task_types: cognitive reasoning, multi-discipline problem solving, Olympic-level reasoning
description: OlympicArena is a comprehensive benchmark featuring bilingual problems across text-only and interleaved text-image modalities from 62 international Olympic competitions spanning seven fields. It uniquely evaluates AI's cognitive reasoning abilities through Olympic competition problems that are ideal for assessing complex, interdisciplinary reasoning capabilities.
paper_title: OlympicArena: Benchmarking Multi-discipline Cognitive Reasoning for Superintelligent AI
authors: Zhen Huang, Zengzhi Wang, Shijie Xia, Xuefeng Li, Haoyang Zou

task_types: text-to-image, image-to-text, text-to-speech, speech-to-text, robustness evaluation
description: MMCBench is a comprehensive benchmark examining self-consistency of large multimodal models under common corruptions across text, image, and speech modalities. It uniquely covers more than 100 popular LMMs with over 150 model checkpoints, focusing on cross-modal robustness evaluation for four essential generation tasks.
paper_title: Benchmarking Large Multimodal Models against Common Corruptions
authors: Jiawei Zhang, Tianyu Pang, Chao Du, Yi Ren, Bo Li

task_types: question answering, multi-granularity evaluation, ability assessment
description: AGIBench is a multi-granularity, multimodal, human-referenced benchmark that uses a four-tuple system to label questions by ability branch, knowledge, difficulty, and modality. It uniquely provides comprehensive evaluation across multiple granularities and classifies questions into five difficulty degrees based on human performance rates.
paper_title: AGIBench: A Multi-granularity, Multimodal, Human-referenced, Auto-scoring Benchmark for Large Language Models
authors: Fei Tang, Wanling Gao, Luzhou Peng, Jianfeng Zhan

task_types: emotion recognition, cultural alignment, affective processing
description: CEDAR captures culturally elicited distinct affective responses across 7 languages and 14 emotion categories, focusing on subjective interpretative variance inherent to diverse sociocultural lenses. It uniquely evaluates cultural alignment in LLMs through affective processing rather than declarative knowledge, using scenarios that yield cross-cultural emotional distinctions.
paper_title: Tears or Cheers? Benchmarking LLMs via Culturally Elicited Distinct Affective Responses
authors: Chongyuan Dai, Yaling Shen, Jinpeng Hu, Zihan Gao, Jia Li

task_types: multilingual evaluation, cross-lingual assessment, multimodal understanding
description: MEGAVERSE provides thorough evaluation of non-English capabilities of LLMs across 22 datasets covering 83 languages including low-resource African languages. It uniquely focuses on multilingual evaluation with multimodal components, addressing the gap in comprehensive non-English LLM assessment beyond English-centric benchmarks.
paper_title: MEGAVERSE: Benchmarking Large Language Models Across Languages, Modalities, Models and Tasks
authors: Sanchit Ahuja, Divyanshu Aggarwal, Varun Gumma, Ishaan Watts, Ashutosh Sathe

task_types: semantic filtering, joins, mappings, ranking, classification
description: Presents a benchmark targeting semantic query processing engines that extend SQL with semantic operators configured by natural language instructions. Introduces diversity across scenarios, modalities, and operators including semantic filters, joins, mappings, ranking, and classification.
paper_title: SemBench: A Benchmark for Semantic Query Processing Engines
authors: Jiale Lao, Andreas Zimmerer, Olga Ovcharenko, Tianji Cong, Matthew Russo

description: Introduces a benchmark and framework for multimodal affective tasks that standardizes both shared and imbalanced missing-rate protocols on four sentiment and emotion datasets. Defines diagnostic metrics MEI and MLI to measure modality equity and learning imbalance.
paper_title: MissBench: Benchmarking Multimodal Affective Analysis under Imbalanced Missing Modalities
authors: Tien Anh Pham, Phuong-Anh Nguyen, Duc-Trong Le, Cam-Van Thi Nguyen

description: Introduces a dataset of entrance exams from leading Brazilian universities (UNICAMP and USP) with annotated metadata for evaluating NLP models on various subjects. Includes recently administered exams and indicates image positions for multimodal language understanding.
paper_title: BLUEX: A benchmark based on Brazilian Leading Universities Entrance eXams
authors: Thales Sales Almeida, Thiago Laitz, Giovana K. Bonás, Rodrigo Nogueira

description: Constructs a benchmark to evaluate the performance of LLMs on Multimodal Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis (MABSA) tasks and compares them with state-of-the-art supervised learning methods. Investigates LLM suitability for extracting aspect terms and sentiment polarities from text and images.
paper_title: Exploring Large Language Models for Multimodal Sentiment Analysis: Challenges, Benchmarks, and Future Directions
authors: Shezheng Song

description: Proposes the most comprehensive exam benchmark for multilingual evaluation of vision-language models covering 18 languages and 14 subjects with 20,911 multiple-choice questions. Built through open science collaboration ensuring linguistic and cultural authenticity rather than English translations.
paper_title: Kaleidoscope: In-language Exams for Massively Multilingual Vision Evaluation
authors: Israfel Salazar, Manuel Fernández Burda, Shayekh Bin Islam, Arshia Soltani Moakhar, Shivalika Singh

description: This benchmark incorporates uncertainty quantification into VLM evaluation using conformal prediction on multiple-choice VQA tasks. It evaluates 20+ VLMs across 5 datasets to assess the alignment between model uncertainty and accuracy, revealing that high-accuracy models may also have high uncertainty.
paper_title: Uncertainty-Aware Evaluation for Vision-Language Models
authors: Vasily Kostumov, Bulat Nutfullin, Oleg Pilipenko, Eugene Ilyushin

description: VHELM extends the HELM framework to provide holistic evaluation of VLMs across 9 aspects: visual perception, knowledge, reasoning, bias, fairness, multilinguality, robustness, toxicity, and safety. It standardizes evaluation procedures across 21 datasets and 22 VLMs to enable fair comparisons and comprehensive assessment.
paper_title: VHELM: A Holistic Evaluation of Vision Language Models
authors: Tony Lee, Haoqin Tu, Chi Heem Wong, Wenhao Zheng, Yiyang Zhou

task_types: VLM evaluation, response assessment, visual grounding evaluation
description: The Perception Collection contains 15K customized score rubrics for evaluating long-form VLM responses, focusing on both instruction following and visual grounding. It enables training Prometheus-Vision, the first open-source VLM evaluator that can understand user-defined score criteria during evaluation.
paper_title: Prometheus-Vision: Vision-Language Model as a Judge for Fine-Grained Evaluation
authors: Seongyun Lee, Seungone Kim, Sue Hyun Park, Geewook Kim, Minjoon Seo

task_types: reward model evaluation, preference assessment, response ranking
description: Multimodal RewardBench is an expert-annotated benchmark with 5,211 (prompt, chosen response, rejected response) triplets covering six domains: general correctness, preference, knowledge, reasoning, safety, and visual question-answering. It provides comprehensive evaluation of multimodal reward models for VLMs with challenging testbeds across multiple domains.
paper_title: Multimodal RewardBench: Holistic Evaluation of Reward Models for Vision Language Models
authors: Michihiro Yasunaga, Luke Zettlemoyer, Marjan Ghazvininejad

description: VMCBench transforms 20 existing VQA datasets into unified multiple-choice format using AutoConverter framework, totaling 9,018 questions. It enables objective evaluation while reducing costly multiple-choice question creation, providing scalable, consistent, and reproducible VLM evaluation across diverse tasks.
paper_title: Automated Generation of Challenging Multiple-Choice Questions for Vision Language Model Evaluation
authors: Yuhui Zhang, Yuchang Su, Yiming Liu, Xiaohan Wang, James Burgess

description: FIRE-Bench comprehensively evaluates VLMs' feedback-refining capabilities through multi-turn conversations across diverse tasks. It uniquely tests models' ability to spontaneously improve responses based on user feedback, with evaluation settings and automated feedback provision.
paper_title: FIRE: A Dataset for Feedback Integration and Refinement Evaluation of Multimodal Models
authors: Pengxiang Li, Zhi Gao, Bofei Zhang, Tao Yuan, Yuwei Wu

task_types: human preference evaluation, real-world interaction assessment
description: WV-Bench evaluates VLMs using human preferences from real-world multimodal interactions, curated from 8,000 user submissions in WildVision-Arena. It provides comprehensive analysis of VLM failure cases in authentic user scenarios with high correlation to human preferences.
paper_title: WildVision: Evaluating Vision-Language Models in the Wild with Human Preferences
authors: Yujie Lu, Dongfu Jiang, Wenhu Chen, William Yang Wang, Yejin Choi

task_types: visual question answering, visual grounding, cultural understanding
description: RICE-VL evaluates VLM cultural understanding across 11 ASEAN countries through culturally informed expert annotations. It includes diverse VQA formats and visual grounding tasks, introducing SEA-LAVE metric for assessing textual accuracy, cultural alignment, and country identification.
paper_title: Rice-VL: Evaluating Vision-Language Models for Cultural Understanding Across ASEAN Countries
authors: Tushar Pranav, Eshan Pandey, Austria Lyka Diane Bala, Aman Chadha, Indriyati Atmosukarto

task_types: alignment evaluation, single-turn dialogue, multi-turn dialogue
description: First benchmark specifically designed for Chinese visual contexts to evaluate alignment capabilities of VLMs. Encompasses 1,054 images and 4,978 question-answer pairs across thirteen specific tasks in three categories, including both single-turn and multi-turn dialogue scenarios with real-world Chinese visual contexts.
paper_title: AlignMMBench: Evaluating Chinese Multimodal Alignment in Large Vision-Language Models
authors: Yuhang Wu, Wenmeng Yu, Yean Cheng, Yan Wang, Xiaohan Zhang

description: Introduces MMHE benchmark comprising 18,000 expert human judgments across four multi-modal tasks to assess the generalizability of automatic evaluation metrics in multi-task scenarios. Uniquely focuses on criterion-wise evaluation across different modalities and tasks.
paper_title: Multi-modal, Multi-task, Multi-criteria Automatic Evaluation with Vision Language Models
authors: Masanari Ohi, Masahiro Kaneko, Naoaki Okazaki, Nakamasa Inoue

description: Novel German-language benchmark dataset drawing from real middle school curricula across nine domains including mathematics, history, biology, and religion. Includes over 2,000 open-ended questions grounded in 486 images, ensuring integration of visual interpretation with factual reasoning.
paper_title: VLM@school -- Evaluation of AI image understanding on German middle school knowledge
authors: René Peinl, Vincent Tischler

description: DatBench curates and transforms existing benchmarks to maximize fidelity and discriminability by converting multiple-choice to generative tasks and filtering blindly solvable/mislabeled samples. It provides both DatBench-Full (33 datasets) and a discriminative subset achieving 13x speedup while maintaining discriminative power.
paper_title: DatBench: Discriminative, Faithful, and Efficient VLM Evaluations
authors: DatologyAI, :, Siddharth Joshi, Haoli Yin, Rishabh Adiga

description: PISA-Bench derives from expert-created PISA tests and provides a multilingual benchmark covering six languages (English, Spanish, German, Chinese, French, Italian) with human-extracted instructions and questions. It uniquely offers fully parallel corpus for multilingual multimodal reasoning evaluation.
paper_title: PISA-Bench: The PISA Index as a Multilingual and Multimodal Metric for the Evaluation of Vision-Language Models
authors: Patrick Haller, Fabio Barth, Jonas Golde, Georg Rehm, Alan Akbik

task_types: instruction following evaluation, content consistency assessment, visual quality measurement
description: Object-centric evaluation framework for multi-turn instruction-based image editing with fine-grained assessment. Tests instruction following, content consistency, and visual quality across editing turns using object detection and semantic verification.
paper_title: EdiVal-Agent: An Object-Centric Framework for Automated, Fine-Grained Evaluation of Multi-Turn Editing
authors: Tianyu Chen, Yasi Zhang, Zhi Zhang, Peiyu Yu, Shu Wang

task_types: image captioning evaluation, explanation generation, structured assessment
description: Reference-free evaluation metric for image captioning with structured explanations based on fluency, relevance, and descriptiveness criteria. Provides both scoring and explanation generation with two-stage evaluation template supervision.
paper_title: EXPERT: An Explainable Image Captioning Evaluation Metric with Structured Explanations
authors: Hyunjong Kim, Sangyeop Kim, Jongheon Jeong, Yeongjae Cho, Sungzoon Cho

task_types: image captioning, visual question answering, reasoning
description: Refined and efficient benchmark curated through semi-automated pipeline that filters uninformative samples and eliminates answer leakage. Reduces evaluation samples by 76% while providing more effective model capability distinction.
paper_title: LIME: Less Is More for MLLM Evaluation
authors: King Zhu, Qianbo Zang, Shian Jia, Siwei Wu, Feiteng Fang

task_types: creative tasks, associative tasks, cognitive evaluation
description: Introduces a new evaluation paradigm using per-sample criteria with potent MLLMs as judges for open-ended and subjective tasks. Curates evaluation samples across six comprehensive cognitive levels to address limitations in existing automatic evaluation methodologies.
paper_title: MLLM-Bench: Evaluating Multimodal LLMs with Per-sample Criteria
authors: Wentao Ge, Shunian Chen, Guiming Hardy Chen, Junying Chen, Zhihong Chen

task_types: instruction following, pattern generation, adherence evaluation
description: Evaluates MLLMs on their ability to strictly adhere to complex layered instructions in generating responses with specific requested patterns. Features 400 diverse image-prompt pairs designed to challenge instruction fidelity across various scenarios.
paper_title: MIA-Bench: Towards Better Instruction Following Evaluation of Multimodal LLMs
authors: Yusu Qian, Hanrong Ye, Jean-Philippe Fauconnier, Peter Grasch, Yinfei Yang

task_types: calibration, in-context learning, instruction following, hallucination detection, robustness testing
description: First comprehensive evaluation framework with modular components (Scenario, Instruction, Inferencer, Metric) that enables standardized and holistic assessment of MLLMs. Introduces 6 new evaluation recipes for key capabilities like calibration, instruction following, and robustness.
paper_title: ChEF: A Comprehensive Evaluation Framework for Standardized Assessment of Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Zhelun Shi, Zhipin Wang, Hongxing Fan, Zhenfei Yin, Lu Sheng

task_types: retrieval across universals, cultural visual grounding
description: GlobalRG evaluates multicultural understanding in vision-language models through two tasks: retrieval across universals (retrieving culturally diverse images for universal concepts from 50 countries) and cultural visual grounding (grounding culture-specific concepts within images from 15 countries). It addresses limitations in cultural diversity coverage across both universal and culture-specific local concepts.
paper_title: From Local Concepts to Universals: Evaluating the Multicultural Understanding of Vision-Language Models
authors: Mehar Bhatia, Sahithya Ravi, Aditya Chinchure, Eunjeong Hwang, Vered Shwartz

description: MC² constructs controlled evidence-conflict scenarios to systematically evaluate modality preference in MLLMs' decision-making. It tests whether models exhibit tendencies to favor one modality over another when processing multi-modal contexts, providing insights into how MLLMs handle conflicting information across modalities.
paper_title: Evaluating and Steering Modality Preferences in Multimodal Large Language Model
authors: Yu Zhang, Jinlong Ma, Yongshuai Hou, Xuefeng Bai, Kehai Chen

task_types: visual question answering, captioning, cultural understanding
description: BanglaVerse is a culturally grounded benchmark for evaluating multilingual VLMs on Bengali culture across historically linked languages and regional dialects. Built from 1,152 manually curated images across nine domains, it expands into four languages and five Bangla dialects, yielding ~32.3K artifacts to test cultural understanding under linguistic variation.
paper_title: Many Dialects, Many Languages, One Cultural Lens: Evaluating Multilingual VLMs for Bengali Culture Understanding Across Historically Linked Languages and Regional Dialects
authors: Nurul Labib Sayeedi, Md. Faiyaz Abdullah Sayeedi, Shubhashis Roy Dipta, Rubaya Tabassum, Ariful Ekraj Hridoy

description: FLIP provides a comprehensive evaluation framework for federated prompt learning algorithms, assessing 8 state-of-the-art methods across 4 federated learning protocols and 12 open datasets with 6 distinct evaluation scenarios. It specifically evaluates vision-language models in federated settings considering data scarcity, unseen classes, and cross-domain distributional shifts.
paper_title: FLIP: Towards Comprehensive and Reliable Evaluation of Federated Prompt Learning
authors: Dongping Liao, Xitong Gao, Yabo Xu, Chengzhong Xu

task_types: counting, OCR, structural data understanding, perception evaluation
description: Presents a unified benchmark to evaluate MLLMs across six key perception abilities (counting, OCR, structural data understanding) focusing on both accuracy and stability. Addresses evaluation variance issues by providing comprehensive assessment across diverse question types, domains, and metrics.
paper_title: Evaluating and Advancing Multimodal Large Language Models in Perception Ability Lens
authors: Feng Chen, Chenhui Gou, Jing Liu, Yang Yang, Zhaoyang Li

description: Presents the first comprehensive multi-modal benchmark to evaluate factuality ability of MLLMs in answering natural language questions. Categorizes VQA items into 9 different tasks around objective events and common knowledge with rigorous quality control for high-quality, concise answers.
paper_title: SimpleVQA: Multimodal Factuality Evaluation for Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Xianfu Cheng, Wei Zhang, Shiwei Zhang, Jian Yang, Xiangyuan Guan

description: AI GameStore introduces a scalable platform for evaluating machine general intelligence through human games, representing the "Multiverse of Human Games." It uniquely uses LLMs with humans-in-the-loop to synthesize games from popular gaming platforms, testing world-model learning, memory, and planning capabilities.
paper_title: AI Gamestore: Scalable, Open-Ended Evaluation of Machine General Intelligence with Human Games
authors: Lance Ying, Ryan Truong, Prafull Sharma, Kaiya Ivy Zhao, Nathan Cloos

description: UPME introduces an unsupervised peer review framework for MLLM evaluation that uses only image data, allowing models to automatically generate questions and conduct peer reviews. It uniquely addresses human workload and bias issues through automated evaluation with a vision-language scoring system.
paper_title: UPME: An Unsupervised Peer Review Framework for Multimodal Large Language Model Evaluation
authors: Qihui Zhang, Munan Ning, Zheyuan Liu, Yanbo Wang, Jiayi Ye

task_types: multidisciplinary evaluation, dynamic evaluation, knowledge-driven reasoning
description: MDK12-Bench is a large-scale multidisciplinary benchmark built from real-world K-12 exams spanning six disciplines with 6,225 knowledge points in a six-layer taxonomy. It uniquely provides dynamic evaluation framework with unfamiliar shifts and comprehensive evaluation across difficulty, temporal, contextual, and knowledge-driven dimensions.
paper_title: MDK12-Bench: A Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Large Language Models on Multidisciplinary Exams
authors: Pengfei Zhou, Xiaopeng Peng, Fanrui Zhang, Zhaopan Xu, Jiaxin Ai

description: MM-BigBench provides comprehensive assessment framework with diverse metrics for evaluating MLLMs on multimodal content comprehension tasks, introducing adaptability metrics between models and instructions.
paper_title: MM-BigBench: Evaluating Multimodal Models on Multimodal Content Comprehension Tasks
authors: Xiaocui Yang, Wenfang Wu, Shi Feng, Ming Wang, Daling Wang

description: Introduces a novel binary relevancy dataset covering various tasks for image-text relevancy evaluation. Presents LLaVA-RE framework using MLLMs for binary relevancy evaluation with detailed task instructions and multimodal in-context samples to handle diverse text formats and definition variations.
paper_title: LLaVA-RE: Binary Image-Text Relevancy Evaluation with Multimodal Large Language Model
authors: Tao Sun, Oliver Liu, JinJin Li, Lan Ma

description: Comprehensive benchmark for evaluating schema-grounded information extraction and reasoning over visual inputs. Built from over 6.5K diverse JSON schemas and 1.8K curated image-schema pairs covering four visual domains: UI screens, natural images, documents, and charts with human-verified quality.
paper_title: SO-Bench: A Structural Output Evaluation of Multimodal LLMs
authors: Di Feng, Kaixin Ma, Feng Nan, Haofeng Chen, Bohan Zhai

description: First benchmark for agentic multimodal retrieval-augmented generation with long, step-wise annotated reasoning chains spanning five reasoning structures. Features HAVE verification process and introduces process-level metrics for reasoning quality, stepwise retrieval and planning accuracy.
paper_title: MC-Search: Evaluating and Enhancing Multimodal Agentic Search with Structured Long Reasoning Chains
authors: Xuying Ning, Dongqi Fu, Tianxin Wei, Mengting Ai, Jiaru Zou

description: Comprehensive multimodal knowledge editing benchmark comprising eight diverse tasks from multiple datasets. Introduces Knowledge Generalization Index (KGI) and Knowledge Preservation Index (KPI) to evaluate editing effects on in-domain samples without AI-synthetic data.
paper_title: ComprehendEdit: A Comprehensive Dataset and Evaluation Framework for Multimodal Knowledge Editing
authors: Yaohui Ma, Xiaopeng Hong, Shizhou Zhang, Huiyun Li, Zhilin Zhu

description: Comprehensive evaluation framework for multimodal reasoning tasks with vision-text contexts. Introduces innovative metrics including Best Performance, Mean Relative Gain, Stability, and Adaptability across 16 multimodal datasets encompassing 6 distinct tasks using 10 different instructions.
paper_title: MM-InstructEval: Zero-Shot Evaluation of (Multimodal) Large Language Models on Multimodal Reasoning Tasks
authors: Xiaocui Yang, Wenfang Wu, Shi Feng, Ming Wang, Daling Wang

description: Evaluates multimodal models' ability to match objects with their colors using a novel Winoground-style schema where captions have identical words but color words are rearranged to modify different objects. Tests fine-grained color-object associations through both image-text matching and visual language understanding.
paper_title: ColorSwap: A Color and Word Order Dataset for Multimodal Evaluation
authors: Jirayu Burapacheep, Ishan Gaur, Agam Bhatia, Tristan Thrush

description: Multi-domain benchmark for zero-shot semantic segmentation covering 22 datasets across diverse domains like medicine, engineering, earth monitoring, biology, and agriculture. Provides holistic analysis of performance across domain-specific datasets using a developed taxonomy.
paper_title: What a MESS: Multi-Domain Evaluation of Zero-Shot Semantic Segmentation
authors: Benedikt Blumenstiel, Johannes Jakubik, Hilde Kühne, Michael Vössing

description: A live leaderboard that ranks models based on human feedback from real-world AI applications rather than static datasets. Integrates pairwise model comparisons into natural user interactions with novel mechanisms like Placement Matches and Proximity Sampling for robust ranking.
paper_title: Inclusion Arena: An Open Platform for Evaluating Large Foundation Models with Real-World Apps
authors: Kangyu Wang, Hongliang He, Lin Liu, Ruiqi Liang, Zhenzhong Lan

description: A large-scale multimodal evaluation dataset combining human and GPT evaluation data across both image-to-text and text-to-image generation tasks. Enables development of multimodal evaluation models that can assess bidirectional generation between image and text.
paper_title: Minos: A Multimodal Evaluation Model for Bidirectional Generation Between Image and Text
authors: Junzhe Zhang, Huixuan Zhang, Xinyu Hu, Li Lin, Mingqi Gao

task_types: image generation, image editing, multimodal evaluation
description: A comprehensive benchmark spanning six core image generation/editing tasks and six topical domains with 20K fine-grained human annotations. Features an explainable evaluation schema with localized object-level and segment-level error tagging, going beyond opaque automated metrics.
paper_title: ImagenWorld: Stress-Testing Image Generation Models with Explainable Human Evaluation on Open-ended Real-World Tasks
authors: Samin Mahdizadeh Sani, Max Ku, Nima Jamali, Matina Mahdizadeh Sani, Paria Khoshtab

description: A benchmark for assessing referenceless metrics for image description generation with human ratings across quality dimensions and ten robustness checks. Uniquely presents images and descriptions in context, reflecting the importance of context dependence for description quality evaluation.
paper_title: ContextRef: Evaluating Referenceless Metrics For Image Description Generation
authors: Elisa Kreiss, Eric Zelikman, Christopher Potts, Nick Haber

description: A dynamic multimodal evaluation protocol that generates new visual question-answering samples by modifying both images and language while maintaining consistency. Addresses data contamination and provides flexible complexity variants of existing benchmarks that co-evolve with model capabilities.
paper_title: Dynamic Multimodal Evaluation with Flexible Complexity by Vision-Language Bootstrapping
authors: Yue Yang, Shuibai Zhang, Wenqi Shao, Kaipeng Zhang, Yi Bin

task_types: bias evaluation, judge robustness, text-to-image evaluation
description: A fine-grained, multi-domain meta-evaluation benchmark designed to exhibit diverse score distributions and expose visual biases in LVLM judges. Tests whether adversarial visual manipulations can systematically fool LVLM-based evaluation systems into inflating scores unfairly.
paper_title: Fooling the LVLM Judges: Visual Biases in LVLM-Based Evaluation
authors: Yerin Hwang, Dongryeol Lee, Kyungmin Min, Taegwan Kang, Yong-il Kim

description: Introduces Markup-QA annotation technique where QAs are enclosed within markups for autonomous driving datasets. Enables simultaneous evaluation of sentence generation and VQA capabilities on driving scenes from the NuScenes dataset.
paper_title: NuScenes-MQA: Integrated Evaluation of Captions and QA for Autonomous Driving Datasets using Markup Annotations
authors: Yuichi Inoue, Yuki Yada, Kotaro Tanahashi, Yu Yamaguchi

description: Purpose-built for evaluating video foundation models across four core abilities: video understanding, generation, editing, and video reconstruction. Features high-quality diverse multi-shot videos with detailed captions and unified agentic evaluation system.
paper_title: UniVBench: Towards Unified Evaluation for Video Foundation Models
authors: Jianhui Wei, Xiaotian Zhang, Yichen Li, Yuan Wang, Yan Zhang

description: Large-scale benchmark for continuous multi-modal 3D human action understanding with 1076 long video sequences in 51 action categories. Provides multi-modality data sources including RGB, depth, infrared, and skeleton with comprehensive action annotations.
paper_title: PKU-MMD: A Large Scale Benchmark for Continuous Multi-Modal Human Action Understanding
authors: Chunhui Liu, Yueyu Hu, Yanghao Li, Sijie Song, Jiaying Liu

description: Introduces PM4Bench, the first Multilingual Multi-Modal Multi-task Benchmark constructed on strictly parallel corpus across 10 languages. The benchmark enables fair comparison of LVLM capabilities across languages and introduces a vision setting where textual queries are visually fused into images.
paper_title: PM4Bench: Benchmarking Large Vision-Language Models with Parallel Multilingual Multi-Modal Multi-task Corpus
authors: Junyuan Gao, Jiahe Song, Jiang Wu, Runchuan Zhu, Guanlin Shen

description: Multi-vision sensor perception and reasoning benchmark that evaluates LVLMs on images from thermal, depth, and medical X-ray sensors beyond RGB. Tests physical sensor knowledge proficiency and multi-vision sensory reasoning considering physical environment characteristics.
paper_title: SPARK: Multi-Vision Sensor Perception and Reasoning Benchmark for Large-scale Vision-Language Models
authors: Youngjoon Yu, Sangyun Chung, Byung-Kwan Lee, Yong Man Ro

task_types: dialog-based QA, emotion prediction, affective explanation generation
description: Large-scale dataset for affective visual dialog with 50K 10-turn visually grounded dialogs, emotion attributions, and textual emotion explanations. Tests emotional reasoning abilities through dialog-based QA, emotion prediction, and affective explanation generation.
paper_title: Affective Visual Dialog: A Large-Scale Benchmark for Emotional Reasoning Based on Visually Grounded Conversations
authors: Kilichbek Haydarov, Xiaoqian Shen, Avinash Madasu, Mahmoud Salem, Li-Jia Li

description: Unified evaluation benchmark for visually-grounded dialogue defining five core multi-modal dialogue tasks across six datasets. Introduces VDscore metric based on Analytic Hierarchy Process for comprehensive assessment across all tasks.
paper_title: VDialogUE: A Unified Evaluation Benchmark for Visually-grounded Dialogue
authors: Yunshui Li, Binyuan Hui, Zhaochao Yin, Wanwei He, Run Luo

description: ReForm-Eval reformulates existing benchmarks into unified LVLM-compatible formats to comprehensively evaluate large vision-language models. It addresses the challenge of assessing free-form text outputs by systematically converting task-oriented benchmarks into formats suitable for automatic evaluation.
paper_title: ReForm-Eval: Evaluating Large Vision Language Models via Unified Re-Formulation of Task-Oriented Benchmarks
authors: Zejun Li, Ye Wang, Mengfei Du, Qingwen Liu, Binhao Wu

description: G-VUE is a comprehensive benchmark covering the full spectrum of visual cognitive abilities through four functional domains: Perceive, Ground, Reason, and Act. It encompasses 11 carefully curated tasks from 3D reconstruction to visual reasoning and manipulation, providing a holistic evaluation standard for general-purpose visual systems.
paper_title: Perceive, Ground, Reason, and Act: A Benchmark for General-purpose Visual Representation
authors: Jiangyong Huang, William Yicheng Zhu, Baoxiong Jia, Zan Wang, Xiaojian Ma

task_types: recognition, knowledge, language generation, spatial awareness, visual reasoning, hallucination detection, visual illusion identification, toxicity detection, stereotype detection, bias detection
