It shifts detection from binary classification to semantic segmentation, enabling localization of edits and identification of editing models through multi-turn editing scenarios.
paper_title: DiffSeg30k: A Multi-Turn Diffusion Editing Benchmark for Localized AIGC Detection
authors: Hai Ci, Ziheng Peng, Pei Yang, Yingxin Xuan, Mike Zheng Shou

description: RoCOCO creates altered variants of MS-COCO test set by replacing words in text and generating visually altered images through mixing techniques. It evaluates robustness of vision-language models to subtle semantic changes, revealing that SOTA models often favor altered content over original.
paper_title: RoCOCO: Robustness Benchmark of MS-COCO to Stress-test Image-Text Matching Models
authors: Seulki Park, Daeho Um, Hajung Yoon, Sanghyuk Chun, Sangdoo Yun

description: OODBench contains 40K instance-level out-of-distribution instance-category pairs with automated construction and minimal human verification. It comprehensively assesses VLMs' performance on OOD data using a Basic-to-Advanced progression of prompted questions across varying difficulty levels.
paper_title: OODBench: Out-of-Distribution Benchmark for Large Vision-Language Models
authors: Ling Lin, Yang Bai, Heng Su, Congcong Zhu, Yaoxing Wang

task_types: hallucination detection, faithfulness evaluation, factuality assessment
description: Scalable benchmark for fine-grained hallucination evaluation in LVLMs using automated data construction pipeline. Features hierarchical hallucination categorization assessing both faithfulness and factuality across 12 visual perception aspects and 6 knowledge domains with clean and noisy scenarios.
paper_title: SHALE: A Scalable Benchmark for Fine-grained Hallucination Evaluation in LVLMs
authors: Bei Yan, Zhiyuan Chen, Yuecong Min, Jie Zhang, Jiahao Wang

description: HQH is a High-Quality Hallucination benchmark that demonstrates superior reliability and validity under the proposed Hallucination benchmark Quality Measurement framework (HQM). It addresses inconsistent evaluation results in existing benchmarks and provides credible evaluation using specific indicators for reliability and validity assessment.
paper_title: Measuring the Measurers: Quality Evaluation of Hallucination Benchmarks for Large Vision-Language Models
authors: Bei Yan, Jie Zhang, Zheng Yuan, Shiguang Shan, Xilin Chen

description: MM-SAP (Self-Awareness in Perception for MLLMs) introduces the knowledge quadrant framework to define what MLLMs know and don't know about images. It's the first benchmark specifically designed to assess MLLMs' self-awareness capabilities in perception, addressing their struggle with understanding their own perceptual limitations.
paper_title: MM-SAP: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Assessing Self-Awareness of Multimodal Large Language Models in Perception
authors: Yuhao Wang, Yusheng Liao, Heyang Liu, Hongcheng Liu, Yu Wang

task_types: hallucination detection, fine-grained perception, detailed visual analysis
description: FREAK is a comprehensive multimodal benchmark for fine-grained hallucination assessment in MLLMs using high-quality photorealistic images with fine-grained counter-commonsense edits. It innovatively evaluates hallucination phenomena in detailed visual perception.
paper_title: FREAK: A Fine-grained Hallucination Evaluation Benchmark for Advanced MLLMs
authors: Zhihan Yin, Jianxin Liang, Yueqian Wang, Yifeng Yao, Huishuai Zhang

description: PENDULUM comprises approximately 2,000 human-curated Visual Question Answering pairs specifically designed to elicit sycophantic responses in MLLMs. It spans six distinct image domains and introduces novel metrics to quantify sycophancy in visual reasoning.
paper_title: PENDULUM: A Benchmark for Assessing Sycophancy in Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: A. B. M. Ashikur Rahman, Saeed Anwar, Muhammad Usman, Irfan Ahmad, Ajmal Mian

task_types: knowledge validation, rumor detection, event verification
description: RealFactBench is a comprehensive benchmark designed to assess fact-checking capabilities of LLMs and MLLMs across diverse real-world tasks, including Knowledge Validation, Rumor Detection, and Event Verification. It consists of 6K high-quality claims from authoritative sources, encompassing multimodal content and introducing the Unknown Rate (UnR) metric for handling uncertainty.
paper_title: RealFactBench: A Benchmark for Evaluating Large Language Models in Real-World Fact-Checking
authors: Shuo Yang, Yuqin Dai, Guoqing Wang, Xinran Zheng, Jinfeng Xu

description: Comprehensive benchmark targeting relation hallucinations in MLLMs, integrating perceptive and cognitive perspectives. Built using Visual Genome scene graph dataset with systematic definition of relation hallucinations, addressing more complex reasoning compared to object/attribute-level hallucinations.
paper_title: Reefknot: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Relation Hallucination Evaluation, Analysis and Mitigation in Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Kening Zheng, Junkai Chen, Yibo Yan, Xin Zou, Xuming Hu

task_types: object existence hallucination, object count hallucination, identity consistency hallucination
description: First benchmark specifically tailored for evaluating object-related hallucinations across multiple images in MLLMs. Comprises three core tasks: Multi-Image Object Existence Hallucination, Multi-Image Object Count Hallucination, and Object Identity Consistency Hallucination, targeting semantic understanding across object existence, quantity reasoning, and cross-view identity consistency.
paper_title: MIHBench: Benchmarking and Mitigating Multi-Image Hallucinations in Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Jiale Li, Mingrui Wu, Zixiang Jin, Hao Chen, Jiayi Ji

task_types: visual fidelity assessment, text bias evaluation
description: Diagnostic benchmark comprising 4,026 VQA instances across six semantic domains to quantify text bias in MLLMs. Employs Three-Level Evaluation Framework systematically increasing conflict between visual evidence and textual information, with Visual Robustness Score (VRS) metric designed to penalize linguistic shortcuts and reward true visual fidelity.
paper_title: V-FAT: Benchmarking Visual Fidelity Against Text-bias
authors: Ziteng Wang, Yujie He, Guanliang Li, Siqi Yang, Jiaqi Xiong

task_types: generative task, discriminative task, existence hallucination, attribute hallucination, relation hallucination
description: AMBER is an LLM-free multi-dimensional benchmark for evaluating MLLM hallucinations across generative and discriminative tasks. It evaluates existence, attribute, and relation hallucinations with a low-cost evaluation pipeline that doesn't require expensive human annotation or advanced LLMs.
paper_title: AMBER: An LLM-free Multi-dimensional Benchmark for MLLMs Hallucination Evaluation
authors: Junyang Wang, Yuhang Wang, Guohai Xu, Jing Zhang, Yukai Gu

description: CertainlyUncertain presents a taxonomy of uncertainty specific to vision-language AI systems, distinguishing epistemic from aleatoric uncertainty. The benchmark features 178K VQA samples as contrastive pairs created by inpainting images and using captions to generate both answerable and unanswerable questions.
paper_title: Certainly Uncertain: A Benchmark and Metric for Multimodal Epistemic and Aleatoric Awareness
authors: Khyathi Raghavi Chandu, Linjie Li, Anas Awadalla, Ximing Lu, Jae Sung Park

description: T2I-FactualBench is the largest benchmark for evaluating factuality of text-to-image models with knowledge-intensive concepts. It features a three-tiered framework from basic memorization to complex composition of knowledge concepts, using multi-round VQA for evaluation.
paper_title: T2I-FactualBench: Benchmarking the Factuality of Text-to-Image Models with Knowledge-Intensive Concepts
authors: Ziwei Huang, Wanggui He, Quanyu Long, Yandi Wang, Haoyuan Li

description: ORIC-Bench studies object recognition under contextual incongruity, where objects appear unexpectedly or fail to appear in expected contexts. It uses LLM-guided and CLIP-guided sampling strategies to construct incongruous object-context pairs that increase recognition difficulty for LVLMs.
paper_title: ORIC: Benchmarking Object Recognition under Contextual Incongruity in Large Vision-Language Models
authors: Zhaoyang Li, Zhan Ling, Yuchen Zhou, Litian Gong, Erdem Bıyık

description: This benchmark investigates selection bias in LVLMs through fine-grained MCQA tasks spanning easy, medium, and hard difficulty levels defined by semantic similarity. It reveals how selection biases intensify with task difficulty and provides tools for bias mitigation.
paper_title: Benchmarking and Mitigating MCQA Selection Bias of Large Vision-Language Models
authors: Md. Atabuzzaman, Ali Asgarov, Chris Thomas

description: TUBench evaluates LVLM reliability using unanswerable questions crafted with ten distinct strategies across four diverse domains: code snippets, natural images, geometry diagrams, and statistical tables. It specifically tests trustworthiness in code reasoning, commonsense reasoning, geometric reasoning, and mathematical reasoning when questions cannot be answered due to insufficient visual information.
paper_title: TUBench: Benchmarking Large Vision-Language Models on Trustworthiness with Unanswerable Questions
authors: Xingwei He, Qianru Zhang, A-Long Jin, Yuan Yuan, Siu-Ming Yiu

task_types: contradiction detection, multiple-choice QA, open-ended QA
description: CLASH features COCO images paired with contradictory captions containing controlled object-level or attribute-level contradictions. It evaluates cross-modal contradiction detection capabilities in both multiple-choice and open-ended formats, addressing a fundamental capability for preventing hallucinations.
paper_title: CLASH: A Benchmark for Cross-Modal Contradiction Detection
authors: Teodora Popordanoska, Jiameng Li, Matthew B. Blaschko

task_types: reward model evaluation, hallucination detection, multimodal reasoning assessment
description: VL-RewardBench is a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating vision-language generative reward models, spanning general multimodal queries, visual hallucination detection, and complex reasoning tasks. It uniquely uses an AI-assisted annotation pipeline combined with human verification to create high-quality examples specifically designed to probe VL-GenRMs limitations.
paper_title: VL-RewardBench: A Challenging Benchmark for Vision-Language Generative Reward Models
authors: Lei Li, Yuancheng Wei, Zhihui Xie, Xuqing Yang, Yifan Song

description: VeriTaS is the first dynamic benchmark for multimodal automated fact-checking, designed to remain robust under ongoing large-scale pretraining of foundation models. It uniquely addresses data leakage issues with quarterly updates via automated pipeline, covering 108 fact-checking organizations across 54 languages with textual and audiovisual content.
paper_title: VeriTaS: The First Dynamic Benchmark for Multimodal Automated Fact-Checking
authors: Mark Rothermel, Marcus Kornmann, Marcus Rohrbach, Anna Rohrbach

task_types: uncertainty estimation, object counting, calibration assessment
description: JUS dataset tests VLM capabilities through difficult queries and object counting scenarios to evaluate verbalized uncertainty. It introduces Net Calibration Error (NCE) to measure miscalibration direction and reveals that both LLMs and VLMs have high calibration error and tend to be overconfident.
paper_title: Overconfidence is Key: Verbalized Uncertainty Evaluation in Large Language and Vision-Language Models
authors: Tobias Groot, Matias Valdenegro-Toro

task_types: programmatic evaluation, hallucination detection, truthfulness assessment
description: PROVE introduces programmatic VLM evaluation using scene-graph representations to generate diverse QA pairs with executable verification programs. It constructs 10.5k challenging but visually grounded QA pairs and proposes programmatic evaluation strategy that measures both helpfulness and truthfulness of responses within a unified framework.
paper_title: Trust but Verify: Programmatic VLM Evaluation in the Wild
authors: Viraj Prabhu, Senthil Purushwalkam, An Yan, Caiming Xiong, Ran Xu

description: SegSub introduces a framework for applying targeted image perturbations to investigate VLM resilience against knowledge conflicts. It systematically evaluates VLM robustness to parametric conflicts, counterfactual conditions, and source conflicts through controlled multimodal manipulations.
paper_title: SegSub: Evaluating Robustness to Knowledge Conflicts and Hallucinations in Vision-Language Models
authors: Peter Carragher, Nikitha Rao, Abhinand Jha, R Raghav, Kathleen M. Carley

task_types: retrieval relevancy assessment, response correctness evaluation
description: RAG-Check evaluates multimodal retrieval-augmented generation through relevancy scores for retrieved content and correctness scores for generated responses. It addresses hallucination sources in multi-modal RAG systems with both automated metrics and human-annotated evaluation data.
paper_title: RAG-Check: Evaluating Multimodal Retrieval Augmented Generation Performance
authors: Matin Mortaheb, Mohammad A. Amir Khojastepour, Srimat T. Chakradhar, Sennur Ulukus

description: A before-after hallucination evaluation dataset that manipulates visual scenes through image editing and introduces new metrics (True Understanding, IGnorance, StuBbornness, InDecision) to assess VLM understanding of scene changes.
paper_title: BEAF: Observing BEfore-AFter Changes to Evaluate Hallucination in Vision-language Models
authors: Moon Ye-Bin, Nam Hyeon-Woo, Wonseok Choi, Tae-Hyun Oh

description: A benchmark with 1,400 VLM-generated paragraph captions featuring over 10,216 sentence-level human annotations of factual correctness with explanatory rationales. Enables fine-grained evaluation of detailed caption factuality.
paper_title: Unblocking Fine-Grained Evaluation of Detailed Captions: An Explaining AutoRater and Critic-and-Revise Pipeline
authors: Brian Gordon, Yonatan Bitton, Andreea Marzoca, Yasumasa Onoe, Xiao Wang

task_types: emotion hallucination detection, emotion psychology evaluation, multimodal emotion perception
description: First benchmark specifically designed to detect and analyze emotion-related hallucinations in MLLMs using adversarial binary QA framework. Evaluates from two dimensions: emotion psychology knowledge and real-world multimodal perception with carefully crafted basic and hallucinated pairs.
paper_title: EmotionHallucer: Evaluating Emotion Hallucinations in Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Bohao Xing, Xin Liu, Guoying Zhao, Chengyu Liu, Xiaolan Fu

task_types: hallucination discrimination, hallucination completion, long-context evaluation
description: LLM-free hallucination benchmark with 6K long and complex hallucination text aligned with real-world scenarios. Introduces hallucination discrimination and completion tasks in unified multiple-choice format, featuring GPT4V-generated data with 14/130 and 189 words on average.
paper_title: LongHalQA: Long-Context Hallucination Evaluation for MultiModal Large Language Models
authors: Han Qiu, Jiaxing Huang, Peng Gao, Qin Qi, Xiaoqin Zhang

task_types: image captioning, factuality evaluation, hallucination detection
description: This work introduces an evaluation framework and benchmark dataset for systematic analysis of hyper-detailed image captions, focusing on factuality and coverage metrics. It addresses the challenge of existing hallucination detection methods struggling with detailed captions and proposes dual evaluation metrics specifically designed for factuality assessment in detailed captioning tasks.
paper_title: Toward Robust Hyper-Detailed Image Captioning: A Multiagent Approach and Dual Evaluation Metrics for Factuality and Coverage
authors: Saehyung Lee, Seunghyun Yoon, Trung Bui, Jing Shi, Sungroh Yoon

task_types: iterative description and generation, semantic coherence measurement
description: Introduces GenCeption, an annotation-free evaluation method inspired by the DrawCeption game that iteratively generates descriptions and images to measure semantic drift and hallucination tendencies. The MMECeption benchmark implements this for Vision LLMs, eliminating costly data annotation and reducing training data contamination risks.
paper_title: GenCeption: Evaluate Vision LLMs with Unlabeled Unimodal Data
authors: Lele Cao, Valentin Buchner, Zineb Senane, Fangkai Yang

description: Contributes a ChatGPT-Prompted visual hallucination evaluation Dataset with over 102k VQA triplets across multiple modes including normal QA, specious context, incorrect context, and counter-common-sense images. Uses a semi-automated pipeline for large-scale objective visual hallucination evaluation.
paper_title: PhD: A ChatGPT-Prompted Visual hallucination Evaluation Dataset
authors: Jiazhen Liu, Yuhan Fu, Ruobing Xie, Runquan Xie, Xingwu Sun

description: Proposes an open-set, dynamic protocol for evaluating object hallucinations in MLLMs using a graph-based structure to represent object concepts and attributes. Generates varied samples through dynamic concept combinations to mitigate data contamination risks and broaden evaluation scope.
paper_title: ODE: Open-Set Evaluation of Hallucinations in Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Yahan Tu, Rui Hu, Jitao Sang

task_types: uncertainty evaluation, misleading robustness, consistency testing
description: Reveals response uncertainty phenomenon where MLLMs overturn correct answers when given misleading information, proposing a two-stage evaluation pipeline to measure misleading rates. Curates the Multimodal Uncertainty Benchmark stratified by difficulty based on how many models they mislead.
paper_title: Exploring Response Uncertainty in MLLMs: An Empirical Evaluation under Misleading Scenarios
authors: Yunkai Dang, Mengxi Gao, Yibo Yan, Xin Zou, Yanggan Gu

description: Hallu-PI is the first benchmark designed to evaluate hallucination in MLLMs within perturbed inputs such as image cropping or blurring. It uniquely addresses real-world scenarios with seven perturbed scenarios across 11 object types, testing fine-grained hallucination types including existence, attribute, and relation.
paper_title: Hallu-PI: Evaluating Hallucination in Multi-modal Large Language Models within Perturbed Inputs
authors: Peng Ding, Jingyu Wu, Jun Kuang, Dan Ma, Xuezhi Cao

description: Coarse-to-fine-grained benchmark that systematically assesses hallucination in object existence and attributes using hierarchical polling-based probing evaluation. Evaluates whether models rely on visual input to formulate output texts and reveals models are more prone to hallucinations on fine-grained attributes than object existence.
paper_title: H-POPE: Hierarchical Polling-based Probing Evaluation of Hallucinations in Large Vision-Language Models
authors: Nhi Pham, Michael Schott

description: Introduces a refined taxonomy of hallucinations including a new Event Hallucination category and provides fine-grained hallucinatory data. Integrates discriminative and generative evaluation methods within a universal framework to assess LVLMs' ability to handle complex narrative hallucinations.
paper_title: Hal-Eval: A Universal and Fine-grained Hallucination Evaluation Framework for Large Vision Language Models
authors: Chaoya Jiang, Hongrui Jia, Wei Ye, Mengfan Dong, Haiyang Xu

task_types: hallucination detection, visual grounding assessment, computer vision evaluation
description: Multi-modal evaluation benchmark for Large Image-Language Models with over 300K image-question pairs, with strong focus on detecting cross-modal hallucination events. Standardized test-bed for assessing IT-LVLM capabilities on fundamental computer vision tasks.
paper_title: Behind the Magic, MERLIM: Multi-modal Evaluation Benchmark for Large Image-Language Models
authors: Andrés Villa, Juan Carlos León Alcázar, Alvaro Soto, Bernard Ghanem

description: A novel benchmark specifically designed to trigger single-modality visual hallucination in MLMs through text-interference in color perception. Uses a simple method to test how textual information can interfere with visual color perception, revealing vulnerabilities in multimodal models.
paper_title: What Color Is It? A Text-Interference Multimodal Hallucination Benchmark
authors: Jinkun Zhao, Lei Huang, Haixin Ge, Wenjun Wu

description: Multi-dimensional benchmark covering objects, attributes, and relations with challenging images selected based on associative biases. Proposes LLM-based two-stage evaluation framework incorporating both faithfulness and coverage, generalizing the CHAIR metric.
paper_title: VALOR-EVAL: Holistic Coverage and Faithfulness Evaluation of Large Vision-Language Models
authors: Haoyi Qiu, Wenbo Hu, Zi-Yi Dou, Nanyun Peng

description: Perception-Reasoning Evaluation Hallucination dataset enabling systematic evaluation of both perception and reasoning capabilities across multiple visual semantics. First to address hallucinations from advanced reasoning capabilities beyond just perception-based assessment.
paper_title: Visual hallucination detection in large vision-language models via evidential conflict
authors: Tao Huang, Zhekun Liu, Rui Wang, Yang Zhang, Liping Jing

benchmark_name: Extended multi-modal benchmark with adversarial dialogues
description: An evaluation benchmark that extends popular multi-modal datasets with prepended hallucinatory dialogues to measure how preceding user-system dialogues exacerbate hallucinations in LVLMs. Uses an Adversarial Question Generator to automatically create image-related yet adversarial dialogues.
paper_title: Mitigating Dialogue Hallucination for Large Vision Language Models via Adversarial Instruction Tuning
authors: Dongmin Park, Zhaofang Qian, Guangxing Han, Ser-Nam Lim

description: A reference-free and fine-grained evaluation metric that measures faithfulness of generated answers from LVLMs by extracting atomic facts from sub-sentences and conducting consistency verification with input images. Includes LLaVA-1k and MSCOCO-Cap benchmark datasets.
paper_title: FaithScore: Fine-grained Evaluations of Hallucinations in Large Vision-Language Models
authors: Liqiang Jing, Ruosen Li, Yunmo Chen, Xinya Du

description: First benchmark specifically designed to measure language priors or blindness in LVLMs. Uses counterfactual images and ensures basic capabilities (commonsense knowledge, visual perception, commonsense biases) are passed before evaluating language priors, minimizing confounding factors.
paper_title: VLind-Bench: Measuring Language Priors in Large Vision-Language Models
authors: Kang-il Lee, Minbeom Kim, Seunghyun Yoon, Minsung Kim, Dongryeol Lee

task_types: context sufficiency detection, insufficient context identification
description: Context Ambiguity and Sufficiency Evaluation (CASE) set designed to benchmark the performance of insufficient context detectors in vision-language understanding. Addresses the pervasive issue of samples where answers rely on assumptions unsupported by provided context.
paper_title: Detecting Multimodal Situations with Insufficient Context and Abstaining from Baseless Predictions
authors: Junzhang Liu, Zhecan Wang, Hammad Ayyubi, Haoxuan You, Chris Thomas

description: FIHA-v1 introduces autonomous hallucination evaluation in LVLMs using Davidson Scene Graphs to organize Q&A pair structures. It generates diverse questions on MSCOCO and Foggy images at minimal cost, enabling comprehensive hallucination assessment from both image and caption perspectives without requiring costly annotations.
paper_title: FIHA: Autonomous Hallucination Evaluation in Vision-Language Models with Davidson Scene Graphs
authors: Bowen Yan, Zhengsong Zhang, Liqiang Jing, Eftekhar Hossain, Xinya Du

task_types: visual question answering, misinformation resistance, multimodal conflict resolution
description: CONTEXT-VQA (Conflicting Text) dataset consists of image-question pairs together with systematically generated persuasive prompts that deliberately conflict with visual evidence. It evaluates the susceptibility of Vision-Language Models to misleading textual prompts that override clear visual evidence, revealing how VLMs arbitrate between contradictory information from different modalities.
paper_title: Do Images Speak Louder than Words? Investigating the Effect of Textual Misinformation in VLMs
authors: Chi Zhang, Wenxuan Ding, Jiale Liu, Mingrui Wu, Qingyun Wu

description: HOPE introduces a content-aware hallucination searching approach that leverages CLIP to generate the most misleading distractors for triggering hallucinations in LVLMs. It expands beyond simple negative object categories to include description-based hallucination searching with false descriptions paired with true objects.
paper_title: What Makes "Good" Distractors for Object Hallucination Evaluation in Large Vision-Language Models?
authors: Ming-Kun Xie, Jia-Hao Xiao, Gang Niu, Lei Feng, Zhiqiang Kou

task_types: mismatch detection, text correction, aspect-based analysis
description: FineMatch introduces aspect-based fine-grained text and image mismatch detection and correction, requiring models to identify mismatched aspect phrases, determine aspect classes, and propose corrections. It focuses on compositional information matching between images and text with 0-3 mismatches per pair.
paper_title: FINEMATCH: Aspect-based Fine-grained Image and Text Mismatch Detection and Correction
authors: Hang Hua, Jing Shi, Kushal Kafle, Simon Jenni, Daoan Zhang

description: OHD-Caps evaluates object hallucination in a discriminative, retrieval-style setting rather than free-form caption generation. It focuses on object-level granularity detection and introduces F-CLIPScore metric that incorporates text embeddings at the noun level for enhanced evaluation.
paper_title: Do Vision Encoders Truly Explain Object Hallucination?: Mitigating Object Hallucination via Simple Fine-Grained CLIPScore
authors: Hongseok Oh, Wonseok Hwang

task_types: object hallucination, relation hallucination, triplet evaluation
description: Tri-HE introduces triplet-level hallucination evaluation that measures both object and relation hallucination simultaneously through (object, relation, object) triplets extracted from LVLM responses. It uniquely evaluates relation hallucination alongside object hallucination in a unified framework.
paper_title: Unified Triplet-Level Hallucination Evaluation for Large Vision-Language Models
authors: Junjie Wu, Tsz Ting Chung, Kai Chen, Dit-Yan Yeung

description: A culturally grounded multimodal benchmark built from images spanning 17 MENA countries, paired with contrastive true and counterfactual statements in English, Arabic, and its dialects. Introduces the CounterFactual Hallucination Rate (CFHR) metric to isolate hallucination beyond raw accuracy by measuring counterfactual acceptance conditioned on correctly answering the true statement.
paper_title: Once Correct, Still Wrong: Counterfactual Hallucination in Multilingual Vision-Language Models
authors: Basel Mousi, Fahim Dalvi, Shammur Chowdhury, Firoj Alam, Nadir Durrani

description: Exposes fundamental vulnerabilities in visual-language models by demonstrating that models can achieve high scores on multimodal benchmarks without image access through 'mirage reasoning'. Introduces a principled solution for fair, vision-grounded evaluation that eliminates textual cues enabling non-visual inference.
paper_title: MIRAGE: The Illusion of Visual Understanding
authors: Mohammad Asadi, Jack W. O'Sullivan, Fang Cao, Tahoura Nedaee, Kamyar Rajabalifardi

description: DASH is an automatic, large-scale pipeline designed to identify systematic hallucinations of VLMs on real-world images in open-world settings. It finds clusters of real and semantically similar images for which VLMs hallucinate specific objects, with over 19k clusters containing 950k images across 380 object classes.
paper_title: DASH: Detection and Assessment of Systematic Hallucinations of VLMs
authors: Maximilian Augustin, Yannic Neuhaus, Matthias Hein

description: A paired image-text dataset built by automatically filtering and augmenting visual counterfactual data to challenge VLMs with hard contrastive cases involving Minimal Visual Contrasts. Tests model ability to ground text in fine-grained visual details and reduce hallucinations.
paper_title: Symmetrical Visual Contrastive Optimization: Aligning Vision-Language Models with Minimal Contrastive Images
authors: Shengguang Wu, Fan-Yun Sun, Kaiyue Wen, Nick Haber

description: Novel visual question answering dataset with over 7.7K examples that captures various aspects of multimodal hallucination including false premises, insufficient contexts, and visual challenges. Uniquely leverages both synthetic and real images to enable dataset creation at scale.
paper_title: HaloQuest: A Visual Hallucination Dataset for Advancing Multimodal Reasoning
authors: Zhecan Wang, Garrett Bingham, Adams Yu, Quoc Le, Thang Luong

task_types: text-to-image generation, image-text matching, text-to-image retrieval
description: The first benchmark for evaluating text-to-image generation models on prompts containing negation. Assesses models' ability to produce semantically accurate images when given negative descriptions.
paper_title: TNG-CLIP:Training-Time Negation Data Generation for Negation Awareness of CLIP
authors: Yuliang Cai, Jesse Thomason, Mohammad Rostami

description: Evaluates cognitive biases in VLMs from a psychological perspective, categorizing hallucination behaviors into sycophancy, logical inconsistency, and appeal to authority. Tests psychological tendencies in model response patterns through strategically manipulated questions.
paper_title: Investigating VLM Hallucination from a Cognitive Psychology Perspective: A First Step Toward Interpretation with Intriguing Observations
authors: Xiangrui Liu, Man Luo, Agneet Chatterjee, Hua Wei, Chitta Baral

description: MATS (Multimodal Audit for Truthful Spatialization) is a compact behavioral audit measuring whether models reject visually contradicted statements. Includes metrics for Spatial Consistency Score and Incorrect Agreement Rate.
paper_title: Pathological Truth Bias in Vision-Language Models
authors: Yash Thube

description: Visual Narrative Intent Alignment (VNIA) is a multimodal dataset specifically created to facilitate the development and evaluation of VLM steering techniques. It focuses on steering and hallucination mitigation benchmarks for VLMs through activation engineering.
paper_title: SteerVLM: Robust Model Control through Lightweight Activation Steering for Vision Language Models
authors: Anushka Sivakumar, Andrew Zhang, Zaber Hakim, Chris Thomas

description: LGIP measures VLM robustness by testing (i) invariance to meaning-preserving paraphrases and (ii) sensitivity to meaning-changing semantic flips in image-text matching. Using 40k MS COCO images, it automatically generates paraphrases and rule-based flips that alter object category, color or count to evaluate linguistic robustness beyond conventional accuracy scores.
paper_title: Language-Guided Invariance Probing of Vision-Language Models
authors: Jae Joong Lee

task_types: multimodal manipulation detection, global inconsistency detection
description: Extension of DGM4 dataset addressing global inconsistencies in multimodal disinformation, introducing Foreground-Background mismatches where authentic figures are placed in absurd contexts. Creates new manipulation categories (FG-BG, FG-BG+TA, FG-BG+TS) for testing both local and global reasoning in multimodal manipulation detection.
paper_title: DGM4+: Dataset Extension for Global Scene Inconsistency
authors: Gagandeep Singh, Samudi Amarsinghe, Priyanka Singh, Xue Li

description: Novel dataset exclusively composed of news videos published by media organizations for fake news detection. Addresses the gap in existing datasets that predominantly comprise user-generated videos by focusing on professionally crafted fake news videos with greater societal impact.
paper_title: FMNV: A Dataset of Media-Published News Videos for Fake News Detection
authors: Yihao Wang, Zhong Qian, Peifeng Li

task_types: scene-level reasoning, long-context understanding, temporal reasoning
description: SceneBench defines scenes as coherent video segments with consistent visual and semantic contexts, providing scene-level challenges for long video understanding. It uniquely evaluates whether VLMs can reason effectively over long, scene-level contexts and reveals significant forgetting of long-range context in current models.
paper_title: Seeing the Scene Matters: Revealing Forgetting in Video Understanding Models with a Scene-Aware Long-Video Benchmark
authors: Seng Nam Chen, Hao Chen, Chenglam Ho, Xinyu Mao, Jinping Wang

description: A benchmark for evaluating holistic understanding of full-length movies (50-170 minutes) with over 50 open-licensed movies paired with manually constructed claim pairs totaling over 850 pairs. It uniquely targets core narrative elements and uses binary claim evaluation protocol to assess reasoning over key narrative information.
paper_title: Movie Facts and Fibs (MF$^2$): A Benchmark for Long Movie Understanding
authors: Emmanouil Zaranis, António Farinhas, Saul Santos, Beatriz Canaverde, Miguel Moura Ramos

task_types: Visual RAG, Many-Shot ICL, long-context understanding
description: MMLongBench is the first benchmark for long-context vision-language models, featuring 13,331 examples across five task categories like Visual RAG and Many-Shot ICL. It standardizes input lengths from 8K-128K tokens via cross-modal tokenization to assess robustness across different context lengths.
paper_title: MMLongBench: Benchmarking Long-Context Vision-Language Models Effectively and Thoroughly
authors: Zhaowei Wang, Wenhao Yu, Xiyu Ren, Jipeng Zhang, Yu Zhao

description: MMLongCite evaluates the fidelity of LVLMs in long-context scenarios with 8 distinct tasks across 6 context length intervals. It incorporates diverse modalities including text, images, and videos to assess faithfulness in handling long multimodal contexts.
paper_title: MMLongCite: A Benchmark for Evaluating Fidelity of Long-Context Vision-Language Models
authors: Keyan Zhou, Zecheng Tang, Lingfeng Ming, Guanghao Zhou, Qiguang Chen

description: Designed to evaluate LVLMs' proficiency in long-form video understanding using lengthy YouTube videos and free-form questions. Features human-annotated questions according to a carefully constructed ability taxonomy for temporal reasoning.
paper_title: MMBench-Video: A Long-Form Multi-Shot Benchmark for Holistic Video Understanding
authors: Xinyu Fang, Kangrui Mao, Haodong Duan, Xiangyu Zhao, Yining Li

num_samples: 3,119 QA pairs from 1,805 scenes across 200 movies
task_types: contextual reasoning, narrative comprehension, multiple-choice QA
description: First comprehensive benchmark for long-form movie understanding with fine-grained contextual reasoning categories. Uses GPT-4o to generate context-rich questions requiring deep narrative comprehension, with two-stage filtering for context-independence and factual consistency.
paper_title: Cinéaste: A Fine-grained Contextual Movie Question Answering Benchmark
authors: Nisarg A. Shah, Amir Ziai, Chaitanya Ekanadham, Vishal M. Patel

task_types: perceptual tasks, reasoning tasks, countercommonsense comprehension, trajectory state tracking
description: H2VU-Benchmark evaluates hierarchical and holistic video understanding spanning videos from 3-second clips to 1.5-hour recordings. It introduces modules for countercommonsense comprehension and trajectory state tracking, with expanded first-person streaming video datasets for comprehensive MLLM assessment.
paper_title: H2VU-Benchmark: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Hierarchical Holistic Video Understanding
authors: Qi Wu, Quanlong Zheng, Yanhao Zhang, Junlin Xie, Jinguo Luo

description: EC-Bench jointly evaluates enumeration, counting, and temporal evidence grounding in long-form videos. It contains 152 videos longer than 30 minutes and 1,699 queries paired with explicit evidence spans, specifically targeting sparse, diverse events in ultra-long videos.
paper_title: EC-Bench: Enumeration and Counting Benchmark for Ultra-Long Videos
authors: Fumihiko Tsuchiya, Taiki Miyanishi, Mahiro Ukai, Nakamasa Inoue, Shuhei Kurita

description: A novel benchmark for clue-grounded question answering in long videos with 1,219 manually curated videos. Features granular categorization system and introduces clue-based evaluation methods to assess whether models understand videos correctly rather than just answering through elimination.
paper_title: CG-Bench: Clue-grounded Question Answering Benchmark for Long Video Understanding
authors: Guo Chen, Yicheng Liu, Yifei Huang, Yuping He, Baoqi Pei

task_types: long-term activity analysis, temporal localization, context aggregation
description: X-LeBench focuses on extremely long egocentric video understanding with recordings spanning 23 minutes to 16.4 hours. It uniquely develops a life-logging simulation pipeline integrating synthetic daily plans with real-world Ego4D footage for comprehensive long-term behavioral analysis.
paper_title: X-LeBench: A Benchmark for Extremely Long Egocentric Video Understanding
authors: Wenqi Zhou, Kai Cao, Hao Zheng, Yunze Liu, Xinyi Zheng

task_types: text needle-in-haystack, temporal grounding, text dynamics captioning
description: TextVidBench is the first benchmark specifically designed for long-video text question answering (>3 minutes), spanning 9 categories with average video length of 2306 seconds. It introduces a unique three-stage evaluation framework: Text Needle-in-Haystack → Temporal Grounding → Text Dynamics Captioning.
paper_title: TextVidBench: A Benchmark for Long Video Scene Text Understanding
authors: Yangyang Zhong, Ji Qi, Yuan Yao, Pengxin Luo, Yunfeng Yan

description: MMOU evaluates omni-modal understanding and reasoning in long, complex real-world videos with 15,000 questions paired with 9,038 web-collected videos. It uniquely requires integrating evidence across visual, audio, and textual modalities over time, covering 13 fundamental skill categories with tightly coupled audio-visual content.
paper_title: MMOU: A Massive Multi-Task Omni Understanding and Reasoning Benchmark for Long and Complex Real-World Videos
authors: Arushi Goel, Sreyan Ghosh, Vatsal Agarwal, Nishit Anand, Kaousheik Jayakumar

description: An all-in-one long video understanding benchmark integrating 9 major video understanding tasks converted to QA formats. Contains 1,376 videos averaging nearly 2 hours each with 252k QAs, representing the largest long video benchmark by number of videos, average duration, and QA count.
paper_title: ALLVB: All-in-One Long Video Understanding Benchmark
authors: Xichen Tan, Yuanjing Luo, Yunfan Ye, Fang Liu, Zhiping Cai

description: A benchmark specifically designed for long video understanding with publicly sourced videos spanning several hours. Addresses real-world applications requiring extended comprehension like embodied intelligence, movie reviews, and sports commentary that current short video benchmarks cannot evaluate.
paper_title: LVBench: An Extreme Long Video Understanding Benchmark
authors: Weihan Wang, Zehai He, Wenyi Hong, Yean Cheng, Xiaohan Zhang

description: Multi-task Long Video Understanding Benchmark with substantial and flexible video length extension, diverse video genres (movies, surveillance, egocentric, cartoons, games), and diversified evaluation tasks for comprehensive examination of MLLMs' long-video understanding capabilities.
paper_title: MLVU: Benchmarking Multi-task Long Video Understanding
authors: Junjie Zhou, Yan Shu, Bo Zhao, Boya Wu, Zhengyang Liang

task_types: multi-timescale video understanding, hierarchical temporal reasoning
description: ScaleLong is the first benchmark to disentangle multi-timescale video understanding by embedding questions targeting four hierarchical timescales (clip, shot, event, story) all within the same video content. It features 269 long videos (avg. 86 min) with 4-8 carefully designed questions per video, enabling direct comparison of model performance across timescales.
paper_title: ScaleLong: A Multi-Timescale Benchmark for Long Video Understanding
authors: David Ma, Huaqing Yuan, Xingjian Wang, Qianbo Zang, Tianci Liu

task_types: hour-long video understanding, time-specific reasoning, multi-level video analysis
description: HLV-1K is a large-scale hour-long video benchmark comprising 1009 hour-long videos with 14,847 high-quality QA pairs featuring time-aware queries. It uniquely focuses on hour-long video understanding with tens of thousands of visual frames, covering frame-level to long-term reasoning tasks across diverse annotation levels.
paper_title: HLV-1K: A Large-scale Hour-Long Video Benchmark for Time-Specific Long Video Understanding
authors: Heqing Zou, Tianze Luo, Guiyang Xie, Victor Xiao Jie Zhang, Fengmao Lv

task_types: moment retrieval, temporal localization, multi-level video analysis
description: MomentSeeker is a novel benchmark for long-video moment retrieval (LMVR) featuring videos averaging over 1200 seconds from various domains. It uniquely covers three levels (global, event, object) and incorporates rich query forms including text-only, image-conditioned, and video-conditioned queries for comprehensive moment localization evaluation.
paper_title: MomentSeeker: A Task-Oriented Benchmark For Long-Video Moment Retrieval
authors: Huaying Yuan, Jian Ni, Zheng Liu, Yueze Wang, Junjie Zhou

description: CinePile addresses the limitation of existing long-form video datasets where tasks can be solved using just one or few random frames. It creates questions that genuinely require long-form comprehension, covering temporal understanding, human-object interactions, and event reasoning across extended video sequences.
paper_title: CinePile: A Long Video Question Answering Dataset and Benchmark
authors: Ruchit Rawal, Khalid Saifullah, Miquel Farré, Ronen Basri, David Jacobs

description: CURVE introduces multicultural and multilingual video reasoning evaluation across 18 global locales with entirely human-generated annotations in native languages. It requires deeply situated understanding of visual cultural context and provides multi-step reasoning traces for error analysis.
paper_title: CURVE: A Benchmark for Cultural and Multilingual Long Video Reasoning
authors: Darshan Singh, Arsha Nagrani, Kawshik Manikantan, Harman Singh, Dinesh Tewari

num_samples: over 5000 multiple choice question answer pairs
description: EgoSchema introduces temporal certificate sets to measure intrinsic temporal understanding length, showing 5.7x longer temporal requirements than the second closest dataset. It provides very long-form video QA requiring understanding of 3-minute clips with genuine temporal complexity.
paper_title: EgoSchema: A Diagnostic Benchmark for Very Long-form Video Language Understanding
authors: Karttikeya Mangalam, Raiymbek Akshulakov, Jitendra Malik

num_samples: over 1000 hours of densely annotated Cricket videos
description: LCric leverages an automated Annotation and Video Stream Alignment Pipeline (ASAP) to create large-scale long video understanding benchmarks by aligning unlabeled sports videos with dense web annotations. It provides over 1000 hours of densely annotated long videos with average sample length of ~50 minutes.
paper_title: Building Scalable Video Understanding Benchmarks through Sports
authors: Aniket Agarwal, Alex Zhang, Karthik Narasimhan, Igor Gilitschenski, Vishvak Murahari

description: LongVALE is the first Vision-Audio-Language Event understanding benchmark providing omni-modal events with precise temporal boundaries and relation-aware captions. It uses an automatic pipeline for multi-modal video filtering, event boundary detection, and cross-modal correlation-aware captioning.
paper_title: LongVALE: Vision-Audio-Language-Event Benchmark Towards Time-Aware Omni-Modal Perception of Long Videos
authors: Tiantian Geng, Jinrui Zhang, Qingni Wang, Teng Wang, Jinming Duan

task_types: video question answering, long-form video understanding
description: LvBench is a long-form video understanding benchmark with videos ranging from 70 seconds to 4 hours across single-scene, multi-scene, and full-scene contexts. It introduces six distinct question types that evaluate various perceptual and cognitive capabilities utilizing both video frames and subtitles, with 20,061 question-answer pairs from 100 movies.
paper_title: LvBench: A Benchmark for Long-form Video Understanding with Versatile Multi-modal Question Answering
authors: Hongjie Zhang, Lu Dong, Yi Liu, Yifei Huang, Yali Wang

task_types: multimodal reasoning, tool use, question answering
description: LongShOTBench is a diagnostic benchmark for omni-modal reasoning and tool use in long videos with open-ended, intent-driven questions and single- and multi-turn dialogues. It requires multimodal reasoning across video, audio, and speech with reference answers and graded rubrics for interpretable evaluation.
paper_title: A Benchmark and Agentic Framework for Omni-Modal Reasoning and Tool Use in Long Videos
authors: Mohammed Irfan Kurpath, Jaseel Muhammad Kaithakkodan, Jinxing Zhou, Sahal Shaji Mullappilly, Mohammad Almansoori

task_types: scene transitions, character actions, deep context understanding, multi-event linking
description: InfiniBench is a comprehensive benchmark for long-form video understanding with over 1,000 hours of content (average 53 minutes per video) and 87.7K question-answer pairs. It evaluates eight diverse skills spanning grounding-based and reasoning-based tasks across movies and TV shows with rich annotation formats.
paper_title: InfiniBench: A Benchmark for Large Multi-Modal Models in Long-Form Movies and TV Shows
authors: Kirolos Ataallah, Eslam Abdelrahman, Mahmoud Ahmed, Chenhui Gou, Khushbu Pahwa

task_types: video question answering, hour-long video understanding
description: 1h-walk VQA is a novel hour-long video QA benchmark introduced as part of the Perception Test 2024 challenge. It specifically addresses hour-long video understanding, representing a significant extension from existing shorter video benchmarks to test models on extremely long video content.
paper_title: Perception Test 2024: Challenge Summary and a Novel Hour-Long VideoQA Benchmark
authors: Joseph Heyward, João Carreira, Dima Damen, Andrew Zisserman, Viorica Pătrăucean

description: A benchmark for long-context interleaved video-language understanding featuring videos up to an hour long with subtitles. Introduces a novel 'referring reasoning' task where questions contain referring queries that reference specific video contexts, requiring models to retrieve and reason over detailed information from long inputs.
paper_title: LongVideoBench: A Benchmark for Long-context Interleaved Video-Language Understanding
authors: Haoning Wu, Dongxu Li, Bei Chen, Junnan Li

task_types: temporal reasoning, multimodal reasoning, long-term understanding
description: A benchmark for long video understanding requiring reasoning over long time horizons and across different modalities, featuring video segments up to 15 minutes in length. Uses a scalable dataset creation pipeline leveraging large models to automatically generate dense, time-aligned video captions and challenging question-answer sets, with emphasis on multimodal reasoning.
paper_title: Neptune: The Long Orbit to Benchmarking Long Video Understanding
authors: Arsha Nagrani, Mingda Zhang, Ramin Mehran, Rachel Hornung, Nitesh Bharadwaj Gundavarapu

description: A benchmark designed to assess VLM trustworthiness under uncertainty in long video understanding. Constructs multiple frame-sampling levels and provides ground truths distinguishing answerable from unanswerable cases to evaluate model refusal behaviors.
paper_title: VirtueBench: Evaluating Trustworthiness under Uncertainty in Long Video Understanding
authors: Xueqing Yu, Bohan Li, Yan Li, Zhenheng Yang

description: A realistic long video understanding benchmark containing questions with open-ended short-answer format that truly require understanding the entire video. Assesses both segment-level and full-video understanding through perception and reasoning tasks.
paper_title: VideoEval-Pro: Robust and Realistic Long Video Understanding Evaluation
authors: Wentao Ma, Weiming Ren, Yiming Jia, Zhuofeng Li, Ping Nie

description: First benchmark for key frame sampling in long video question answering featuring multi-scene annotations. Enables direct evaluation of sampling strategies by providing ground-truth annotations of multiple disjoint scenes required per question.
paper_title: KFS-Bench: Comprehensive Evaluation of Key Frame Sampling in Long Video Understanding
authors: Zongyao Li, Kengo Ishida, Satoshi Yamazaki, Xiaotong Ji, Jianquan Liu

description: First long narrative video benchmark for evaluating multi-step reasoning capabilities with videos averaging 1.6 hours. Features human-labeled QA pairs with timestamped reasoning steps and multi-phase evaluation at both outcome and process levels.
paper_title: VRBench: A Benchmark for Multi-Step Reasoning in Long Narrative Videos
authors: Jiashuo Yu, Yue Wu, Meng Chu, Zhifei Ren, Zizheng Huang

task_types: multi-hop retrieval, evidence planning, video question answering
description: Benchmark for evaluating agentic multi-hop evidence retrieval planning in long videos under standardized access constraints. Enforces retrieval necessity where Hop-k questions require exactly k evidence clips, with unified tool interface for fair evaluation.
paper_title: LongVidSearch: An Agentic Benchmark for Multi-hop Evidence Retrieval Planning in Long Videos
authors: Rongyi Yu, Chenyuan Duan, Wentao Zhang

description: Large-scale benchmark for long-term referring video object segmentation with 2000+ videos averaging 60+ seconds. Covers objects undergoing occlusion, disappearance-reappearance, and shot changing with three types of descriptions for different evaluation aspects.
paper_title: Long-RVOS: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Long-term Referring Video Object Segmentation
authors: Tianming Liang, Haichao Jiang, Yuting Yang, Chaolei Tan, Shuai Li

task_types: temporal localization, causal inference, multi-modal understanding
description: First benchmark for omni-modal models on human-centric long-video understanding integrating visual, audio, and text modalities. Features information-dense videos like lectures and interviews with six challenging task scenarios including Intra-Event and Inter-Event tasks.
paper_title: LongInsightBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Evaluating Omni-Modal Models on Human-Centric Long-Video Understanding
authors: ZhaoYang Han, Qihan Lin, Hao Liang, Bowen Chen, Zhou Liu

description: LSDBench is the first benchmark designed to evaluate LVLMs on long-video tasks by constructing high Necessary Sampling Density (NSD) questions. It focuses on dense, short-duration actions to rigorously assess sampling strategies and addresses the unique challenge of the 'Sampling Dilemma' in long video processing.
paper_title: Does Your Vision-Language Model Get Lost in the Long Video Sampling Dilemma?
authors: Tianyuan Qu, Longxiang Tang, Bohao Peng, Senqiao Yang, Bei Yu

description: The paper introduces MMAT dataset constructed for training the RAG-Adapter framework using Grouped-supervised Contrastive Learning. This dataset specifically tests frame sampling effectiveness for long video understanding by providing question-relevant frame sampling supervision.
paper_title: RAG-Adapter: A Plug-and-Play RAG-enhanced Framework for Long Video Understanding
authors: Xichen Tan, Yunfan Ye, Yuanjing Luo, Qian Wan, Fang Liu

task_types: keyframe selection, temporal grounding, video highlights
description: A dataset of 200K video highlights conditioned by query for scene-driven keyframe selection. It enables learning clip-to-frame selection that preserves temporal continuity and semantic coherence for long video understanding.
paper_title: K-frames: Scene-Driven Any-k Keyframe Selection for long video understanding
authors: Yifeng Yao, Yike Yun, Jing Wang, Huishuai Zhang, Dongyan Zhao

description: SpecTemp-80K features synchronized dual-level annotations for coarse evidence spans and fine-grained frame-level evidence, supporting speculative temporal reasoning in long videos. It uniquely provides hierarchical evidence annotations to train cooperative dual-model systems.
paper_title: Thinking with Drafts: Speculative Temporal Reasoning for Efficient Long Video Understanding
authors: Pengfei Hu, Meng Cao, Yingyao Wang, Yi Wang, Jiahua Dong

description: LongViTU is a large-scale dataset for long-form video understanding with average context length of 4.6 minutes, featuring rich knowledge and condensed reasoning including commonsense, causality, and planning. It provides explicit timestamp annotations of relevant events for each QA pair.
paper_title: LongViTU: Instruction Tuning for Long-Form Video Understanding
authors: Rujie Wu, Xiaojian Ma, Hai Ci, Yue Fan, Yuxuan Wang

task_types: long video understanding, temporal reasoning, fine-grained detail detection
description: EgoMem is a new benchmark specifically designed for long video understanding that comprehensively evaluates a model's ability to understand complex events that unfold over time and capture fine-grained details in extremely long videos. It focuses on testing temporal understanding and detail retention in extended video sequences.
paper_title: VideoLucy: Deep Memory Backtracking for Long Video Understanding
authors: Jialong Zuo, Yongtai Deng, Lingdong Kong, Jingkang Yang, Rui Jin

description: GLVC (Grounding Long Video Clues) is a long video question-answering dataset that features grounding critical and concrete clues scattered throughout entire videos. It measures models' ability to seek and identify crucial information across extended video content for question answering.
paper_title: Video Detective: Seek Critical Clues Recurrently to Answer Question from Long Videos
authors: Henghui Du, Chunjie Zhang, Xi Chen, Chang Zhou, Di Hu

task_types: video question answering, long-context recall, temporal grounding
description: Introduces Needle in a Montage (NeMo) task for assessing VideoLLMs' critical reasoning capabilities including long-context recall and temporal grounding. Uses scalable automated data generation pipeline to create QA pairs from videos with various durations from seconds to hours.
paper_title: NeMo: Needle in a Montage for Video-Language Understanding
authors: Zi-Yuan Hu, Shuo Liang, Duo Zheng, Yanyang Li, Yeyao Tao

description: Two new benchmarks for long-form video question answering featuring videos of considerably long length: Ego-QA with videos averaging 17.5 minutes and MAD-QA with videos averaging 1.9 hours. These benchmarks rigorously evaluate long-form video QA capacity.
paper_title: Encoding and Controlling Global Semantics for Long-form Video Question Answering
authors: Thong Thanh Nguyen, Zhiyuan Hu, Xiaobao Wu, Cong-Duy T Nguyen, See-Kiong Ng

description: M3-Bench introduces a long-video QA benchmark with 100 robot-perspective videos and 920 web-sourced videos specifically designed to test multimodal agent capabilities. It evaluates person understanding, general knowledge extraction, and cross-modal reasoning essential for agent applications with long-term memory.
paper_title: Seeing, Listening, Remembering, and Reasoning: A Multimodal Agent with Long-Term Memory
authors: Lin Long, Yichen He, Wentao Ye, Yiyuan Pan, Yuan Lin

description: LVSQA introduces SceneQA, a new scenario for long video question answering that emphasizes scene-based detail perception and reasoning abilities. Built upon LVBench videos, it provides question-answer pairs to evaluate MLLMs' scene perception capabilities rather than just object identification.
paper_title: Enhancing Long Video Question Answering with Scene-Localized Frame Grouping
authors: Xuyi Yang, Wenhao Zhang, Hongbo Jin, Lin Liu, Hongbo Xu

description: A novel dataset that integrates both story-level and clip-level annotations for long video understanding. The benchmark facilitates long-context multimodal learning by providing comprehensive annotations at multiple granularities.
paper_title: Eagle 2.5: Boosting Long-Context Post-Training for Frontier Vision-Language Models
authors: Guo Chen, Zhiqi Li, Shihao Wang, Jindong Jiang, Yicheng Liu

task_types: long-form video understanding, sparse event detection
description: SparseKV-QA is a unified benchmark curated from VideoMME, EgoSchema, and NextQA subsets that highlights real-world scenarios with sparse but crucial events in long-form videos. It specifically tests VLMs' ability to understand contextual continuity while efficiently processing long videos.
paper_title: Keyframe-oriented Vision Token Pruning: Enhancing Efficiency of Large Vision Language Models on Long-Form Video Processing
authors: Yudong Liu, Jingwei Sun, Yueqian Lin, Jingyang Zhang, Ming Yin

task_types: spatial localization, temporal reasoning, layout-aware sequential understanding
description: VMB assesses human-like reasoning in long-form videos including spatial localization, temporal reasoning, and layout-aware sequential understanding. It specifically tests the ability to organize and reason about critical video moments structured as topological semantic graphs with hand-object tracking, activity zones, and environment layout mapping.
paper_title: Building a Mind Palace: Structuring Environment-Grounded Semantic Graphs for Effective Long Video Analysis with LLMs
authors: Zeyi Huang, Yuyang Ji, Xiaofang Wang, Nikhil Mehta, Tong Xiao

task_types: ultra-long video analytics, open-ended video reasoning
description: A benchmark for ultra-long and open-world video scenarios comprising 8 videos each exceeding 10 hours in duration, with 120 manually annotated, diverse, and complex question-answer pairs. It evaluates video analytics capabilities on extremely long continuous content.
paper_title: AVA: Towards Agentic Video Analytics with Vision Language Models
authors: Yuxuan Yan, Shiqi Jiang, Ting Cao, Yifan Yang, Qianqian Yang

description: Designed for multimodal lifelong understanding with 181.1 hours of footage structured across Day, Week, and Month scales. Tests models on natural daily life scenarios with varying temporal densities and long-term memory requirements.
paper_title: Towards Multimodal Lifelong Understanding: A Dataset and Agentic Baseline
authors: Guo Chen, Lidong Lu, Yicheng Liu, Liangrui Dong, Lidong Zou

description: DynaMath is a dynamic visual math benchmark with 501 seed questions represented as Python programs that automatically generate larger sets of concrete questions with visual and textual variations. It uniquely evaluates mathematical reasoning robustness by assessing VLM performance under varying input conditions of the same problem.
paper_title: DynaMath: A Dynamic Visual Benchmark for Evaluating Mathematical Reasoning Robustness of Vision Language Models
authors: Chengke Zou, Xingang Guo, Rui Yang, Junyu Zhang, Bin Hu

task_types: cross-source reasoning, scientific paper comprehension, multi-table comprehension
description: MMCR evaluates VLMs' capacity for reasoning with cross-source information from scientific papers across fragmented and heterogeneous sources. It uniquely challenges models to reason across multiple information sources (text, tables, figures) within scientific documents, representing a high-level AGI capability.
paper_title: MMCR: Benchmarking Cross-Source Reasoning in Scientific Papers
authors: Yang Tian, Zheng Lu, Mingqi Gao, Zheng Liu, Bo Zhao

description: MathSight evaluates university-level multimodal mathematical reasoning with multiple visual variants and text-only conditions for controlled comparison. It isolates and quantifies the effect of visual input to determine whether VLMs genuinely leverage visual understanding or rely on linguistic priors.
paper_title: MathSight: A Benchmark Exploring Have Vision-Language Models Really Seen in University-Level Mathematical Reasoning?
authors: Yuandong Wang, Yao Cui, Yuxin Zhao, Zhen Yang, Yangfu Zhu

description: AVI-Math is the first benchmark to evaluate multimodal mathematical reasoning in aerial vehicle imagery from UAV perspectives. It goes beyond simple counting tasks to include domain-specific knowledge in geometry, logic, and algebra within real-world UAV scenarios.
paper_title: Multimodal Mathematical Reasoning Embedded in Aerial Vehicle Imagery: Benchmarking, Analysis, and Exploration
authors: Yue Zhou, Litong Feng, Mengcheng Lan, Xue Yang, Qingyun Li

task_types: spatial transformation, molecular recognition, hydrogen bond identification, relational reasoning
description: Introduces Microscopic Spatial Intelligence concept and systematic benchmark framework with 163,000 QA pairs from 4,000 molecular structures. Evaluates nine complementary tasks ranging from spatial transformations to complex relational identifications for scientific discovery applications.
paper_title: From Macro to Micro: Benchmarking Microscopic Spatial Intelligence on Molecules via Vision-Language Models
authors: Zongzhao Li, Xiangzhe Kong, Jiahui Su, Zongyang Ma, Mingze Li

task_types: scientific reasoning, symbolic program synthesis, perturbation robustness, reasoning correction
description: Synthetic, fully dynamic multimodal benchmark for scientific reasoning via grounded Python code with 24,750 university-level physics and math problems. Features executable code for ground truth generation, step-by-step reasoning, and five targeted evaluation tasks including generalization and perturbation robustness.
paper_title: PRiSM: An Agentic Multimodal Benchmark for Scientific Reasoning via Python-Grounded Evaluation
authors: Shima Imani, Seungwhan Moon, Adel Ahmadyan, Lu Zhang, Kirmani Ahmed

description: CSVQA is a Chinese multimodal benchmark specifically designed for evaluating scientific reasoning through domain-grounded visual question answering across STEM disciplines. It features carefully constructed question-answer pairs that require domain knowledge integration with visual evidence analysis and higher-order reasoning in authentic scientific contexts.
paper_title: CSVQA: A Chinese Multimodal Benchmark for Evaluating STEM Reasoning Capabilities of VLMs
authors: Ai Jian, Weijie Qiu, Xiaokun Wang, Peiyu Wang, Yunzhuo Hao

description: The first dynamic benchmark for spatial mathematical reasoning in solid geometry. Contains 503 expert-curated seed questions that can generate unbounded diverse instances, with process evaluation based on expert-annotated reasoning chains.
paper_title: DynaSolidGeo: A Dynamic Benchmark for Genuine Spatial Mathematical Reasoning of VLMs in Solid Geometry
authors: Changti Wu, Shijie Lian, Zihao Liu, Lei Zhang, Laurence Tianruo Yang

description: A bi-lingual (English and Hindi) mathematical reasoning benchmark with 1,527 vision-only questions from JEE Advanced and CBSE Board examinations. Features questions as images with figures and text essential for mathematical problem solving.
paper_title: GanitBench: A bi-lingual benchmark for evaluating mathematical reasoning in Vision Language Models
authors: Ashutosh Bandooni, Brindha Subburaj

description: A bilingual (English and Hindi) multimodal benchmark with 1,460 questions from India's JEE Advanced examination spanning Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics. Designed to evaluate true scientific reasoning vs pattern-matching with meta-cognitive reasoning evaluation.
paper_title: mmJEE-Eval: A Bilingual Multimodal Benchmark for Evaluating Scientific Reasoning in Vision-Language Models
authors: Arka Mukherjee, Shreya Ghosh

description: The first large-scale multimodal benchmark for evaluating foundation models on strength of materials engineering problems with 1,065 annotated problems. It uniquely combines textual problem statements with schematic diagrams and introduces a novel Descriptions of Images prompting strategy to address visual understanding limitations.
paper_title: SoM-1K: A Thousand-Problem Benchmark Dataset for Strength of Materials
authors: Qixin Wan, Zilong Wang, Jingwen Zhou, Wanting Wang, Ziheng Geng

task_types: matchstick puzzles, symbolic manipulation, arithmetic reasoning
description: MathSticks evaluates Visual Symbolic Compositional Reasoning through matchstick equation puzzles that must be corrected by moving sticks under conservation rules. It unifies visual perception, symbolic manipulation, and arithmetic consistency with 1.4M generated instances.
paper_title: MathSticks: A Benchmark for Visual Symbolic Compositional Reasoning with Matchstick Puzzles
authors: Yuheng Ji, Huajie Tan, Cheng Chi, Yijie Xu, Yuting Zhao

task_types: mathematical reasoning, physical reasoning, visual-textual integration
description: A benchmark for evaluating mathematical and physical reasoning through text-only and text-image formats with human-annotated difficulty levels, solutions with detailed explanations, and taxonomic mappings. It focuses on Chinese multimodal scientific problems.
paper_title: MMSciBench: Benchmarking Language Models on Chinese Multimodal Scientific Problems
authors: Xinwu Ye, Chengfan Li, Siming Chen, Wei Wei, Xiangru Tang

task_types: structure reconstruction, inverse design, performance prediction
description: Benchmark for VLM-assisted metamaterial generation with three core capabilities: structure reconstruction, property-driven inverse design, and performance prediction. Includes MetaDB repository with over 150,000 parameterized programs and their derivatives.
paper_title: MetaGen: A DSL, Database, and Benchmark for VLM-Assisted Metamaterial Generation
authors: Liane Makatura, Benjamin Jones, Siyuan Bian, Wojciech Matusik

description: Comprehensive benchmark for multimodal mathematical reasoning with explicit visual dependencies, featuring multi-image reasoning (averaging 3.9 images per question) across six cognitive domains. Tests elementary-level math problems requiring visual integration and commonsense knowledge.
paper_title: Benchmarking Multimodal Mathematical Reasoning with Explicit Visual Dependency
authors: Zhikai Wang, Jiashuo Sun, Wenqi Zhang, Zhiqiang Hu, Xin Li

task_types: variable identification, process formulation, solution derivation
description: PhysicsArena is the first multimodal physics reasoning benchmark that holistically evaluates MLLMs across three critical dimensions: variable identification, physical process formulation, and solution derivation. It addresses limitations of existing physics benchmarks that focus only on text inputs or problem-solving without intermediate steps.
paper_title: PhysicsArena: The First Multimodal Physics Reasoning Benchmark Exploring Variable, Process, and Solution Dimensions
authors: Song Dai, Yibo Yan, Jiamin Su, Dongfang Zihao, Yubo Gao

description: MAC is a live benchmark that continuously evolves with scientific advancement, leveraging over 25,000 image-text pairs from top-tier scientific journals. It challenges MLLMs to reason across abstract visual and textual scientific content and can update with new journal covers.
paper_title: MAC: A Live Benchmark for Multimodal Large Language Models in Scientific Understanding
authors: Mohan Jiang, Jin Gao, Jiahao Zhan, Dequan Wang

task_types: scientific reasoning, lab safety analysis, data extraction, mechanism reasoning
description: PolyReal is a multimodal benchmark grounded in real-world polymer science practices, covering five critical capabilities: foundational knowledge application, lab safety analysis, experiment mechanism reasoning, raw data extraction, and performance exploration.
paper_title: PolyReal: A Benchmark for Real-World Polymer Science Workflows
authors: Wanhao Liu, Weida Wang, Jiaqing Xie, Suorong Yang, Jue Wang

description: MaRVL-QA evaluates mathematical and spatial reasoning over visual landscapes using mathematical surface plots. It features two tasks: Topological Counting (identifying features like local maxima) and Transformation Recognition (recognizing geometric transformations).
paper_title: MaRVL-QA: A Benchmark for Mathematical Reasoning over Visual Landscapes
authors: Nilay Pande, Sahiti Yerramilli, Jayant Sravan Tamarapalli, Rynaa Grover

description: MMReason is designed to precisely and comprehensively evaluate MLLM long-chain reasoning capability with diverse, open-ended, challenging questions from 6 disciplines and multiple difficulty levels. It features questions reformulated into open-ended format, filtered using multi-model voting to eliminate shortcuts, and annotated with detailed step-by-step solutions using a reference-based ternary scoring mechanism.
paper_title: MMReason: An Open-Ended Multi-Modal Multi-Step Reasoning Benchmark for MLLMs Toward AGI
authors: Huanjin Yao, Jiaxing Huang, Yawen Qiu, Michael K. Chen, Wenzheng Liu

task_types: multimodal reasoning, mathematics, physics, chemistry, coding
description: EMMA (Enhanced MultiModal reAsoning) is a benchmark targeting organic multimodal reasoning across mathematics, physics, chemistry, and coding. EMMA tasks demand advanced cross-modal reasoning that cannot be addressed by reasoning independently in each modality, offering an enhanced test suite that goes beyond text-dominant reasoning or shallow visual cues found in existing benchmarks.
paper_title: Can MLLMs Reason in Multimodality? EMMA: An Enhanced MultiModal ReAsoning Benchmark
authors: Yunzhuo Hao, Jiawei Gu, Huichen Will Wang, Linjie Li, Zhengyuan Yang

description: ErrorRadar is the first benchmark designed to assess MLLMs' capabilities in multimodal error detection, formally formulating this new task with two sub-tasks: error step identification and error categorization. It consists of 2,500 high-quality multimodal K-12 mathematical problems collected from real-world student interactions, providing a comprehensive framework for evaluating complex mathematical reasoning ability.
paper_title: ErrorRadar: Benchmarking Complex Mathematical Reasoning of Multimodal Large Language Models Via Error Detection
authors: Yibo Yan, Shen Wang, Jiahao Huo, Hang Li, Boyan Li

task_types: insight discovery, operation formulation, operation execution
description: MMTutorBench is the first benchmark for AI math tutoring, featuring 685 problems with pedagogically significant key-steps and problem-specific rubrics. It enables fine-grained evaluation across six dimensions through three structured tasks: Insight Discovery, Operation Formulation, and Operation Execution.
paper_title: MMTutorBench: The First Multimodal Benchmark for AI Math Tutoring
authors: Tengchao Yang, Sichen Guo, Mengzhao Jia, Jiaming Su, Yuanyang Liu

description: SceMQA is a scientific multimodal question answering benchmark at the college entrance level, focusing on Mathematics, Physics, Chemistry, and Biology. It features multiple-choice and free-response formats with specific knowledge points and detailed explanations for comprehensive MLLM evaluation.
paper_title: SceMQA: A Scientific College Entrance Level Multimodal Question Answering Benchmark
authors: Zhenwen Liang, Kehan Guo, Gang Liu, Taicheng Guo, Yujun Zhou

task_types: astronomical image interpretation, astrophysical reasoning, scientific domain understanding
description: Introduces the first comprehensive benchmark designed to evaluate MLLMs in astronomical image understanding. Comprises 621 multiple-choice questions across six astrophysical subfields, curated and reviewed by 15 domain experts to assess specialized scientific capabilities.
paper_title: AstroMMBench: A Benchmark for Evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models Capabilities in Astronomy
authors: Jinghang Shi, Xiaoyu Tang, Yang Huang, Yuyang Li, Xiao Kong

task_types: geometry problem solving, auxiliary line construction, mathematical reasoning
description: A geometry benchmark comprising problems requiring an average of 6.51 reasoning steps with 41.8% needing auxiliary line construction. Unique for incorporating auxiliary line construction evaluation and fine-grained process evaluation with a novel five-dimensional assessment strategy for long-step geometric reasoning.
paper_title: GeoLaux: A Benchmark for Evaluating MLLMs' Geometry Performance on Long-Step Problems Requiring Auxiliary Lines
authors: Yumeng Fu, Jiayin Zhu, Lingling Zhang, Bo Zhao, Shaoxuan Ma

task_types: financial reasoning, mathematical reasoning, visual interpretation
description: FinMR is a high-quality, knowledge-intensive multimodal dataset explicitly designed to evaluate expert-level financial reasoning capabilities at a professional analyst's standard. It comprises over 3,200 meticulously curated question-answer pairs across 15 diverse financial topics, integrating sophisticated mathematical reasoning, advanced financial knowledge, and nuanced visual interpretation.
paper_title: FinMR: A Knowledge-Intensive Multimodal Benchmark for Advanced Financial Reasoning
authors: Shuangyan Deng, Haizhou Peng, Jiachen Xu, Rui Mao, Ciprian Doru Giurcăneanu

task_types: chemical reaction analysis, cross-modal integration, mechanistic reasoning
description: Multi-tiered benchmark for chemical reaction understanding from scientific PDFs. Features Single-Figure QA testing fine-grained visual perception and Full-Document QA requiring cross-modal integration of text, schemes, and tables from authentic literature.
paper_title: RxnBench: A Multimodal Benchmark for Evaluating Large Language Models on Chemical Reaction Understanding from Scientific Literature
authors: Hanzheng Li, Xi Fang, Yixuan Li, Chaozheng Huang, Junjie Wang

description: Comprehensive science benchmark with 1,019 high-quality QA pairs covering mathematics, physics, chemistry, and biology across five languages. Features three evaluation modes and fine-grained annotation of scientific knowledge points, addressing multilingual and multimodal coverage gaps.
paper_title: MME-SCI: A Comprehensive and Challenging Science Benchmark for Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Jiacheng Ruan, Dan Jiang, Xian Gao, Ting Liu, Yuzhuo Fu

description: A multi-disciplinary benchmark using real-world K-12 examinations across six subjects (math, physics, chemistry, biology, geography, information science). Features 6,827 knowledge point annotations and a dynamic evaluation framework to mitigate data contamination through question form bootstrapping.
paper_title: MDK12-Bench: A Multi-Discipline Benchmark for Evaluating Reasoning in Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Pengfei Zhou, Fanrui Zhang, Xiaopeng Peng, Zhaopan Xu, Jiaxin Ai

task_types: scientific figure captioning, multiple choice questions, open-ended reasoning
description: Multimodal scientific benchmark for Earth science phenomena uncovering across five major spheres. Features over 289K figures with enriched captions from scientific publications, supporting graduate-level geoscientific reasoning tasks.
paper_title: MSEarth: A Multimodal Scientific Dataset and Benchmark for Phenomena Uncovering in Earth Science
authors: Xiangyu Zhao, Wanghan Xu, Bo Liu, Yuhao Zhou, Fenghua Ling

description: Real-world mathematical reasoning benchmark using authentic mobile device captured images from K-12 educational contexts. Systematically classifies real image challenges into quality degradation, perspective variation, and irrelevant content interference.
paper_title: MathReal: We Keep It Real! A Real Scene Benchmark for Evaluating Math Reasoning in Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Jun Feng, Zixin Wang, Zhentao Zhang, Yue Guo, Zhihan Zhou

task_types: physics problem solving, diagram interpretation, mathematical reasoning
description: Large-scale multimodal benchmark for undergraduate-level physics problems spanning 8 major sub-disciplines, each with visual diagrams. Features rigorous multi-stage curation with difficulty grading system and expert-level evaluation.
paper_title: PhysUniBench: A Multi-Modal Physics Reasoning Benchmark at Undergraduate Level
authors: Lintao Wang, Encheng Su, Jiaqi Liu, Pengze Li, Jiabei Xiao

task_types: table understanding, numerical reasoning, scientific reasoning
description: Comprehensive framework for multimodal scientific table understanding and reasoning with dynamic input image resolutions. The benchmark specifically evaluates numerical reasoning capabilities through 3,114 testing samples designed to assess MLLMs' ability to process scientific tables with proper visual processing rather than text conversion.
paper_title: Does Table Source Matter? Benchmarking and Improving Multimodal Scientific Table Understanding and Reasoning
authors: Bohao Yang, Yingji Zhang, Dong Liu, André Freitas, Chenghua Lin

description: CMER-Bench categorizes mathematical expressions into three difficulty levels (easy, moderate, complex) specifically targeting complex multi-line expressions with many tokens. It addresses the gap in evaluating mathematical expression recognition on complex expressions that existing datasets primarily composed of simple samples cannot handle.
paper_title: Complex Mathematical Expression Recognition: Benchmark, Large-Scale Dataset and Strong Baseline
authors: Weikang Bai, Yongkun Du, Yuchen Su, Yazhen Xie, Zhineng Chen

task_types: geometry, visual algebra, logic, patterns, combinatorics
description: A multilingual benchmark based on Kangaroo mathematics tests spanning English, French, Spanish, and Catalan, focusing on visual mathematics including geometry, visual algebra, logic, patterns, and combinatorics. Unique in its multilingual scope and emphasis on diagram-based mathematical reasoning.
paper_title: Evaluating Visual Mathematics in Multimodal LLMs: A Multilingual Benchmark Based on the Kangaroo Tests
authors: Arnau Igualde Sáez, Lamyae Rhomrasi, Yusef Ahsini, Ricardo Vinuesa, Sergio Hoyas

task_types: mathematical reasoning, pattern recognition, spatial reasoning
description: A challenging mathematical reasoning benchmark evaluating cognitive reasoning abilities of MLLMs across 10 categories including pattern recognition, spatial reasoning, and relative reasoning. Features manually collected high-quality images with focus on logical and visual complexity requiring drawn-out, high-level reasoning.
paper_title: Polymath: A Challenging Multi-modal Mathematical Reasoning Benchmark
authors: Himanshu Gupta, Shreyas Verma, Ujjwala Anantheswaran, Kevin Scaria, Mihir Parmar

description: MathOPEval evaluates MLLMs' code-based visual operations capabilities in mathematical reasoning through Multi-modal Code Generation (MCG) and Multi-modal Code Editing (MCE) tasks. It uniquely focuses on fine-grained visual operations including deletion, modification, and annotation across five mathematical figure types, addressing a gap in evaluating code-based visual manipulation abilities.
paper_title: MathOPEval: A Fine-grained Evaluation Benchmark for Visual Operations of MLLMs in Mathematical Reasoning
authors: Xiaoyuan Li, Moxin Li, Wenjie Wang, Rui Men, Yichang Zhang

description: MathScape evaluates multimodal mathematical reasoning capabilities using human-captured real-world images rather than digitally rendered content, closely reflecting practical educational settings. It bridges the gap between synthetic evaluation and real-world application by demonstrating that strong performance on rendered images doesn't guarantee effectiveness on real-world mathematical tasks.
paper_title: MathScape: Benchmarking Multimodal Large Language Models in Real-World Mathematical Contexts
