authors: Hao Liang, Linzhuang Sun, Minxuan Zhou, Zirong Chen, Meiyi Qiang

description: VisScience provides comprehensive multi-modal scientific reasoning evaluation across mathematics, physics, and chemistry disciplines from K12 education. It spans 21 distinct subjects across five difficulty levels, addressing the critical gap in existing benchmarks that predominantly focus on mathematics while overlooking other key scientific disciplines.
paper_title: VisScience: An Extensive Benchmark for Evaluating K12 Educational Multi-modal Scientific Reasoning
authors: Zhihuan Jiang, Zhen Yang, Jinhao Chen, Zhengxiao Du, Weihan Wang

description: CMMaTH is the largest Chinese multimodal mathematical problem benchmark covering K12 education levels with fine-grained assessment capabilities. It provides increased diversity in problem types, visual elements, detailed knowledge points, and includes the open-source GradeGPT evaluation tool for stable and cost-free model assessment in Chinese mathematical contexts.
paper_title: CMMaTH: A Chinese Multi-modal Math Skill Evaluation Benchmark for Foundation Models
authors: Zhong-Zhi Li, Ming-Liang Zhang, Fei Yin, Zhi-Long Ji, Jin-Feng Bai

task_types: physics reasoning, multiple choice questions, chain-of-thought evaluation, visual reasoning
description: Multi-Physics is a comprehensive benchmark for Chinese physics reasoning with 1,412 image-associated multiple-choice questions spanning 11 high-school physics subjects across 5 difficulty levels. It employs a dual evaluation framework analyzing both final answer accuracy and step-by-step chain-of-thought integrity, systematically studying the impact of visual information.
paper_title: Multi-Physics: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Multimodal LLMs Reasoning on Chinese Multi-Subject Physics Problems
authors: Zhongze Luo, Zhenshuai Yin, Yongxin Guo, Zhichao Wang, Jionghao Zhu

description: First comprehensive benchmark for evaluating MLLM-based process judges in scientific problem-solving. Features 50,118 step-level labels across four scientific disciplines with human expert annotations for correctness, error type, and explanation, enabling systematic evaluation of error detection, classification, and diagnosis capabilities.
paper_title: ProJudge: A Multi-Modal Multi-Discipline Benchmark and Instruction-Tuning Dataset for MLLM-based Process Judges
authors: Jiaxin Ai, Pengfei Zhou, Zhaopan Xu, Ming Li, Fanrui Zhang

task_types: crystallography question answering, scientific reasoning, context assimilation
description: OPENXRD is a comprehensive benchmarking framework for evaluating LLMs and MLLMs in crystallography question answering, featuring 217 expert-curated X-ray diffraction questions. It uniquely measures context assimilation capabilities with closed-book and open-book evaluation conditions using domain-specific supporting information.
paper_title: OPENXRD: A Comprehensive Benchmark Framework for LLM/MLLM XRD Question Answering
authors: Ali Vosoughi, Ayoub Shahnazari, Yufeng Xi, Zeliang Zhang, Griffin Hess

task_types: circuit analysis, symbolic reasoning, engineering design
description: CircuitSense evaluates circuit understanding across hierarchical abstraction from system specifications to component implementations through 8,006+ problems. It uniquely examines the complete engineering workflow (Perception, Analysis, Design) with emphasis on deriving symbolic equations from visual inputs, addressing a critical gap between visual parsing and symbolic reasoning.
paper_title: CircuitSense: A Hierarchical MLLM Benchmark Bridging Visual Comprehension and Symbolic Reasoning in Engineering Design Process
authors: Arman Akbari, Jian Gao, Yifei Zou, Mei Yang, Jinru Duan

task_types: chemical reasoning, multi-step reasoning, expert-level problem solving
description: SUPERChem is a benchmark of 500 expert-curated reasoning-intensive chemistry problems covering diverse subfields in both multimodal and text-only formats. Each problem is paired with expert-authored solution paths enabling Reasoning Path Fidelity (RPF) scoring to evaluate reasoning quality beyond final-answer accuracy.
paper_title: SUPERChem: A Multimodal Reasoning Benchmark in Chemistry
authors: Zehua Zhao, Zhixian Huang, Junren Li, Siyu Lin, Junting Zhou

task_types: scientific image synthesis, logic validation, scientific reasoning
description: SciGenBench evaluates generated scientific images based on information utility and logical validity to assess scientific correctness in synthetic image generation. It accompanies a systematic study of scientific image synthesis and introduces ImgCoder, a logic-driven framework for improving structural precision.
paper_title: Scientific Image Synthesis: Benchmarking, Methodologies, and Downstream Utility
authors: Honglin Lin, Chonghan Qin, Zheng Liu, Qizhi Pei, Yu Li

description: Chinese Massive Multi-discipline Multimodal Understanding benchmark designed to evaluate LMMs on tasks demanding college-level subject knowledge in Chinese context. Includes 12k questions covering six core disciplines with 39 heterogeneous image types.
paper_title: CMMMU: A Chinese Massive Multi-discipline Multimodal Understanding Benchmark
authors: Ge Zhang, Xinrun Du, Bei Chen, Yiming Liang, Tongxu Luo

description: Benchmark designed to evaluate multimodal models on massive multi-discipline tasks demanding college-level subject knowledge and deliberate reasoning. Includes 11.5K questions covering six core disciplines with 30 highly heterogeneous image types.
paper_title: MMMU: A Massive Multi-discipline Multimodal Understanding and Reasoning Benchmark for Expert AGI
authors: Xiang Yue, Yuansheng Ni, Kai Zhang, Tianyu Zheng, Ruoqi Liu

task_types: visual keypoint identification, multimodal explanation generation, mathematical reasoning
description: ME2 evaluates whether models can identify visual keypoints in mathematical diagrams and generate explanations that incorporate these key elements. It focuses on mathematical visual grounding and visually grounded reasoning for educational contexts, requiring models to identify auxiliary lines, points, and angles.
paper_title: Explain with Visual Keypoints Like a Real Mentor! A Benchmark for Multimodal Solution Explanation
authors: Jaewoo Park, Jungyang Park, Dongju Jang, Jiwan Chung, Byungwoo Yoo

task_types: scientific reasoning, experimental interpretation, theory understanding
description: MATRIX evaluates foundational theory, research-level reasoning, and interpretation of real experimental artifacts across multiple characterization modalities in materials science. It isolates the effect of visual grounding by comparing text-only versus multimodal post-training on structured materials science reasoning tasks.
paper_title: MATRIX: A Multimodal Benchmark and Post-Training Framework for Materials Science
authors: Delia McGrath, Curtis Chong, Rohil Kulkarni, Gerbrand Ceder, Adeesh Kolluru

task_types: scientific literacy, literature parsing, literature-based question answering, literature review generation, scientific discovery
description: HiSciBench is a hierarchical benchmark that evaluates foundation models across five levels mirroring the complete scientific workflow, from basic scientific literacy to creative discovery. Unlike existing fragmented benchmarks, it provides an integrated, dependency-aware framework spanning six scientific disciplines with multimodal inputs including text, equations, figures, and tables.
paper_title: HiSciBench: A Hierarchical Multi-disciplinary Benchmark for Scientific Intelligence from Reading to Discovery
authors: Yaping Zhang, Qixuan Zhang, Xingquan Zhang, Zhiyuan Chen, Wenwen Zhuang

task_types: inconsistency identification, inconsistency remedy, pair matching
description: PRISMM-Bench is the first benchmark grounded in real reviewer-flagged inconsistencies in scientific papers, featuring 384 inconsistencies from 353 papers. It assesses models' capacity to detect, correct, and reason over inconsistencies across text, figures, tables, and equations, using structured JSON-based answers to minimize linguistic biases.
paper_title: PRISMM-Bench: A Benchmark of Peer-Review Grounded Multimodal Inconsistencies
authors: Lukas Selch, Yufang Hou, M. Jehanzeb Mirza, Sivan Doveh, James Glass

description: M2-Verify is a large-scale multimodal dataset for checking scientific claim consistency, providing over 469K instances across 16 domains sourced from PubMed and arXiv. It evaluates the strict consistency between claims and underlying multimodal evidence, addressing gaps in scale, domain diversity, and visual complexity of existing benchmarks.
paper_title: M2-Verify: A Large-Scale Multidomain Benchmark for Checking Multimodal Claim Consistency
authors: Abolfazl Ansari, Delvin Ce Zhang, Zhuoyang Zou, Wenpeng Yin, Dongwon Lee

task_types: mathematical problem solving, physics problem solving, step-by-step reasoning
description: OlympiadBench provides Olympiad-level bilingual multimodal scientific problems from mathematics and physics competitions, including the Chinese college entrance exam. Each problem includes expert-level annotations for step-by-step reasoning, creating an extremely challenging benchmark that tests advanced scientific problem-solving capabilities.
paper_title: OlympiadBench: A Challenging Benchmark for Promoting AGI with Olympiad-Level Bilingual Multimodal Scientific Problems
authors: Chaoqun He, Renjie Luo, Yuzhuo Bai, Shengding Hu, Zhen Leng Thai

description: SciAssess provides comprehensive evaluation of LLMs in scientific literature analysis across three levels: Memorization (L1), Comprehension (L2), and Analysis & Reasoning (L3). It uniquely covers diverse scientific fields (biology, chemistry, material, medicine) with rigorous quality control measures ensuring accuracy, anonymization, and copyright compliance.
paper_title: SciAssess: Benchmarking LLM Proficiency in Scientific Literature Analysis
authors: Hengxing Cai, Xiaochen Cai, Junhan Chang, Sihang Li, Lin Yao

task_types: geometric problem solving, visual perception, theorem application, logical reasoning
description: GeoBench provides hierarchical evaluation of geometric problem-solving with four reasoning levels: Visual Perception, Goal-Oriented Planning, Rigorous Theorem Application, and Self-Reflective Backtracking. It uses six formally verified tasks generated via TrustGeoGen to systematically assess capabilities from attribute extraction to logical error correction.
paper_title: GeoBench: Rethinking Multimodal Geometric Problem-Solving via Hierarchical Evaluation
authors: Yuan Feng, Yue Yang, Xiaohan He, Jiatong Zhao, Jianlong Chen

task_types: error detection, error localization, error correction, handwritten math evaluation
description: FERMAT assesses VLMs' ability to detect, localize, and correct errors in handwritten mathematical content across four error dimensions: computational, conceptual, notational, and presentation. It comprises over 2,200 handwritten math solutions from 609 manually curated problems from grades 7-12 with intentionally introduced perturbations.
paper_title: Can Vision-Language Models Evaluate Handwritten Math?
authors: Oikantik Nath, Hanani Bathina, Mohammed Safi Ur Rahman Khan, Mitesh M. Khapra

description: GeomVerse provides systematic evaluation of VLMs' geometric reasoning through procedurally created synthetic geometry problems with controllable difficulty levels. It enables multi-axis evaluation across various depth levels requiring long chains of reasoning rather than memorized knowledge.
paper_title: GeomVerse: A Systematic Evaluation of Large Models for Geometric Reasoning
authors: Mehran Kazemi, Hamidreza Alvari, Ankit Anand, Jialin Wu, Xi Chen

description: A dataset of 2,030 images of students' handwritten K-12 math work with teacher annotations including 11,661 QA pairs. Captures pedagogical insights about problem-solving strategies and composition of drawings/diagrams for educational VLM evaluation.
paper_title: DrawEduMath: Evaluating Vision Language Models with Expert-Annotated Students' Hand-Drawn Math Images
authors: Sami Baral, Li Lucy, Ryan Knight, Alice Ng, Luca Soldaini

description: M3Kang is the first massively multilingual, multimodal mathematical reasoning dataset derived from the Kangaroo Math Competition, featuring problems in 108 culturally diverse languages with diagrams essential for solving. It uniquely enables comparison with human performance data from over 68,000 students.
paper_title: M3Kang: Evaluating Multilingual Multimodal Mathematical Reasoning in Vision-Language Models
authors: Aleix Torres-Camps, Nathaniel Mitrani Hadida, Víctor Conchello Vendrell, Àlex Batlle Casellas, Arnau Padrés Masdemont

task_types: geometric reasoning, principle identification, principle application, geometry problem solving
description: First comprehensive bilingual benchmark for systematic evaluation of geometric reasoning abilities through geometric principles identification and application. Features five-level hierarchical framework spanning plane and solid geometry with 1,789 intricately annotated problems.
paper_title: GeoSense: Evaluating Identification and Application of Geometric Principles in Multimodal Reasoning
authors: Liangyu Xu, Yingxiu Zhao, Jingyun Wang, Yingyao Wang, Bu Pi

task_types: number estimation, visual counting, numerical reasoning
description: Introduces Visual Number Benchmark to evaluate number sense abilities of MLLMs across visual numerical tasks, covering seven visual numerical attributes and four types of estimation tasks. Uses both synthetic and real-world visual data to assess intuitive number understanding.
paper_title: VisNumBench: Evaluating Number Sense of Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Tengjin Weng, Jingyi Wang, Wenhao Jiang, Zhong Ming

description: Presents a unified framework with MathSpatial-Bench (2K problems) and MathSpatial-Corpus (8K problems) to evaluate mathematical spatial reasoning in MLLMs across 2D and 3D relations. Introduces structured reasoning traces with atomic operations to disentangle perception from reasoning.
paper_title: Do MLLMs Really Understand Space? A Mathematical Reasoning Evaluation
authors: Shuo Lu, Jianjie Cheng, Yinuo Xu, Yongcan Yu, Lijun Sheng

description: MV-MATH addresses the gap in multimodal math benchmarks by focusing on multi-visual contexts rather than single-visual scenarios. It uniquely integrates multiple images interleaved with text from authentic K-12 scenarios, testing mathematical reasoning across 11 subject areas and 3 difficulty levels.
paper_title: MV-MATH: Evaluating Multimodal Math Reasoning in Multi-Visual Contexts
authors: Peijie Wang, Zhong-Zhi Li, Fei Yin, Xin Yang, Dekang Ran

task_types: complex reasoning, knowledge comprehension, educational assessment
description: Evaluates complex reasoning grounded in domain-specific knowledge and long-context understanding for STEM Project-Based Learning. Uses Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP) for expert-driven evaluation criteria and challenges models with tasks resembling human expert responsibilities in educational settings.
paper_title: Towards Robust Evaluation of STEM Education: Leveraging MLLMs in Project-Based Learning
authors: Xinyi Wu, Yanhao Jia, Qinglin Zhang, Yiran Qin, Luwei Xiao

description: Domain-authentic benchmark for Visual-Textual-Symbolic reasoning in chemistry across organic molecules, inorganic materials, and 3D crystal structures. Each task presented in three complementary modes: visual-only, visual-text hybrid, and SMILES-based symbolic input for fine-grained modality analysis.
paper_title: ChemVTS-Bench: Evaluating Visual-Textual-Symbolic Reasoning of Multimodal Large Language Models in Chemistry
authors: Zhiyuan Huang, Baichuan Yang, Zikun He, Yanhong Wu, Fang Hongyu

num_samples: thousands of submissions from nearly 800 students
description: Presents a large-scale study of AI grading on real handwritten calculus work using OCR-conditioned LLMs. The work introduces a multi-perspective evaluation protocol and outlines a standardized benchmark for AI grading of handwritten mathematics.
paper_title: Evaluating AI Grading on Real-World Handwritten College Mathematics: A Large-Scale Study Toward a Benchmark
authors: Zhiqi Yu, Xingping Liu, Haobin Mao, Mingshuo Liu, Long Chen

task_types: geometric reasoning, proof generation, multiple-choice QA
description: GeoChallenge provides automatically generated multiple-choice geometry proof problems requiring multi-step reasoning over aligned textual descriptions and diagrams. It features fine-grained complexity ratings and formal language annotations to enable controlled evaluation of symbolic reasoning in geometric contexts.
paper_title: GeoChallenge: A Multi-Answer Multiple-Choice Benchmark for Geometric Reasoning with Diagrams
authors: Yushun Zhang, Weiping Fu, Zesheng Yang, Bo Zhao, Lingling Zhang

description: EduVisBench evaluates foundation models' ability to generate pedagogically effective visual explanations across multiple STEM domains and difficulty levels. It features diverse problem sets requiring visually grounded solutions with evaluation rubrics informed by pedagogical theory, focusing on educational visualization quality.
paper_title: From EduVisBench to EduVisAgent: A Benchmark and Multi-Agent Framework for Reasoning-Driven Pedagogical Visualization
authors: Haonian Ji, Shi Qiu, Siyang Xin, Siwei Han, Zhaorun Chen

description: SeePhys is a large-scale multimodal benchmark for LLM reasoning grounded in physics questions from middle school to PhD level. It covers 7 fundamental physics domains with 21 categories of heterogeneous diagrams, featuring 75% vision-essential problems that mandate visual information extraction for correct solutions.
paper_title: SeePhys: Does Seeing Help Thinking? -- Benchmarking Vision-Based Physics Reasoning
authors: Kun Xiang, Heng Li, Terry Jingchen Zhang, Yinya Huang, Zirong Liu

task_types: figure interpretation, quantitative analysis, materials science problem solving
description: A benchmark designed to evaluate multimodal LLMs' ability to solve university-level materials science problems that require accurate interpretation of figures like phase diagrams, stress-strain curves, and microstructural schematics. It addresses the gap in figure-based understanding for domain-specific scientific reasoning.
paper_title: MaterialFigBENCH: benchmark dataset with figures for evaluating college-level materials science problem-solving abilities of multimodal large language models
authors: Michiko Yoshitake, Yuta Suzuki, Ryo Igarashi, Yoshitaka Ushiku, Keisuke Nagato

task_types: cognitive skill diagnosis, handwritten math analysis
description: A benchmark for diagnosing cognitive skills from students' handwritten math work, containing diagnostic verdicts across 639 student responses to 110 math problems. Uniquely focuses on evidence strength labels (Evident/Vague) and tests LLMs' ability to diagnose cognitive skills from often incomplete or contextually implicit handwritten work.
paper_title: Benchmarking Large Language Models for Diagnosing Students' Cognitive Skills from Handwritten Math Work
authors: Yoonsu Kim, Hyoungwook Jin, Hayeon Doh, Eunhye Kim, Dongyun Jung

task_types: mathematical reasoning, diagram comparison, visual similarity assessment
description: A curated benchmark of 1,800 high-quality K-12 mathematics problems where all candidate answers are diagrams with subtle visual similarities. Tests LMMs' capacity for fine-grained comparative reasoning over visually similar mathematical diagrams, addressing a gap in existing evaluation approaches.
paper_title: VisioMath: Benchmarking Figure-based Mathematical Reasoning in LMMs
authors: Can Li, Ying Liu, Ting Zhang, Mei Wang, Hua Huang

task_types: educational video generation, physics concept visualization
description: First benchmark for evaluating Text-to-Video models in physics education context. Decomposes physics concepts into granular teaching points with carefully crafted prompts for visual explanation, systematically exploring T2V feasibility for generating curriculum-aligned educational content.
paper_title: PhyEduVideo: A Benchmark for Evaluating Text-to-Video Models for Physics Education
authors: Megha Mariam K. M, Aditya Arun, Zakaria Laskar, C. V. Jawahar

task_types: scientific reasoning evaluation, physics and chemistry understanding
description: First benchmark evaluating undergraduate-level scientific understanding in video models with 200 prompts spanning 14 topics and 103 concepts in physics and chemistry. Each prompt encodes composite scientific scenarios requiring understanding and reasoning across multiple concepts to generate correct phenomena, evaluating models as both generators and reasoners.
paper_title: Benchmarking Scientific Understanding and Reasoning for Video Generation using VideoScience-Bench
authors: Lanxiang Hu, Abhilash Shankarampeta, Yixin Huang, Zilin Dai, Haoyang Yu

task_types: mathematical solution assessment, grading, mistake identification
description: This benchmark evaluates VLMs on assessing hand-written mathematical solutions rather than solving problems. It compiles 122 scanned solutions from Russian Unified State Exam with official expert grades for evaluating mathematical reasoning and human-rubric alignment.
paper_title: CHECK-MAT: Checking Hand-Written Mathematical Answers for the Russian Unified State Exam
authors: Ruslan Khrulev

description: GPSM4K is a comprehensive geometry multimodal dataset with 5340 problems spanning grades 7-12, featuring detailed step-by-step solutions for both numerical and theorem-proving questions. Unlike existing datasets with only objective-type questions, it provides consistent solution formats enabling comprehensive evaluation of geometric reasoning capabilities.
paper_title: Improving Multimodal LLMs Ability In Geometry Problem Solving, Reasoning, And Multistep Scoring
authors: Avinash Anand, Raj Jaiswal, Abhishek Dharmadhikari, Atharva Marathe, Harsh Parimal Popat

task_types: data-driven discovery, exploratory data analysis, scientific reasoning
description: The paper presents a 10-task benchmark for data-driven scientific discovery that evaluates autonomous agents' ability to perform exploratory data analysis in cosmology and astrochemistry. It uniquely uses VLM-as-a-judge to evaluate plots against domain-specific rubrics for scientific reasoning.
paper_title: Enhancing Agentic Autonomous Scientific Discovery with Vision-Language Model Capabilities
authors: Kahaan Gandhi, Boris Bolliet, Inigo Zubeldia

description: The benchmark introduces Referring Expression Comprehension for geometric problems, evaluating whether models can localize points, shapes, and spatial relations in geometric diagrams based on natural language queries. It tests foundational capabilities needed for geometric problem solving.
paper_title: GeoRef: Referring Expressions in Geometry via Task Formulation, Synthetic Supervision, and Reinforced MLLM-based Solutions
authors: Bing Liu, Wenqiang Yv, Xuzheng Yang, Shichang Wang, Junzhuo Liu

task_types: STEM reasoning, mathematical reasoning, scientific problem solving
description: A newly curated, high-difficulty STEM benchmark designed to evaluate complex multimodal reasoning in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics domains. The benchmark provides structured, verifiable assessment of fine-grained reasoning capabilities.
paper_title: StructVRM: Aligning Multimodal Reasoning with Structured and Verifiable Reward Models
authors: Xiangxiang Zhang, Jingxuan Wei, Donghong Zhong, Qi Chen, Caijun Jia

task_types: claim verification, scientific reasoning, figure understanding
description: Evaluates multimodal scientific claim verification by automatically extracting supported claims from scientific articles and manually perturbing them to produce contradicted claims. Tests specific claim verification capabilities over information-rich figures in scientific literature with diagnostic tasks to understand model failures.
paper_title: MuSciClaims: Multimodal Scientific Claim Verification
authors: Yash Kumar Lal, Manikanta Bandham, Mohammad Saqib Hasan, Apoorva Kashi, Mahnaz Koupaee

description: A reaction-level benchmark for assessing vision-based chemical reaction recognition and prediction tasks. Requires models to predict reaction products from molecular images, integrating both visual recognition and chemical reasoning capabilities.
paper_title: TinyChemVL: Advancing Chemical Vision-Language Models via Efficient Visual Token Reduction and Complex Reaction Tasks
authors: Xuanle Zhao, Shuxin Zeng, Xinyuan Cai, Xiang Cheng, Duzhen Zhang

task_types: mathematical visual reasoning, code-driven reasoning, function plotting
description: Math-VR is the first large-scale, bilingual dataset and benchmark for Mathematics problems with Visual Reasoning, comprising 178K samples. It specifically tests mathematical reasoning that requires visual assistance like drawing auxiliary lines or plotting functions to solve problems.
paper_title: CodePlot-CoT: Mathematical Visual Reasoning by Thinking with Code-Driven Images
authors: Chengqi Duan, Kaiyue Sun, Rongyao Fang, Manyuan Zhang, Yan Feng

description: A benchmark comprising diagram instances paired with domain-specific language (DSL) representations for isolated evaluation of geometric perception in VLMs. Enables separation of geometric perception from reasoning capabilities.
paper_title: Enhancing Geometric Perception in VLMs via Translator-Guided Reinforcement Learning
authors: Hao Yu, Shuning Jia, Guanghao Li, Wenhao Jiang, Chun Yuan

task_types: multimodal reasoning, meteorological analysis, logical consistency evaluation
description: WeatherQA is a multimodal reasoning benchmark specifically designed for meteorology that addresses domain gaps in weather-related reasoning. It enables evaluation of VLMs' logical consistency and reasoning faithfulness in the high-stakes domain of meteorological analysis.
paper_title: Weather-R1: Logically Consistent Reinforcement Fine-Tuning for Multimodal Reasoning in Meteorology
authors: Kaiyu Wu, Pucheng Han, Hualong Zhang, Naigeng Wu, Keze Wang

description: HiSciVQA provides high-quality, hand-annotated Hindi multimodal Q&A pairs for scientific visual question answering. It addresses the critical need for low-resource language evaluation in science domains and includes a novel LLM-based evaluation scheme that goes beyond traditional n-gram matching metrics.
paper_title: Enhancing Scientific Visual Question Answering via Vision-Caption aware Supervised Fine-Tuning
authors: Janak Kapuriya, Anwar Shaikh, Arnav Goel, Medha Hira, Apoorv Singh

description: GSM8K-V is a purely visual multi-image mathematical reasoning benchmark that systematically converts text-based GSM8K word problems into visual form. It addresses the gap in visual math word problem evaluation and tests VLMs' ability to perform mathematical reasoning across multiple images rather than just geometry problems.
paper_title: GSM8K-V: Can Vision Language Models Solve Grade School Math Word Problems in Visual Contexts
authors: Fan Yuan, Yuchen Yan, Yifan Jiang, Haoran Zhao, Tao Feng

description: A comprehensive dataset compiled from peer-reviewed Nature Communications articles covering 72 scientific fields with complex visualizations like schematic diagrams, microscopic images, and experimental data requiring graduate-level expertise. Focuses on scientific figure interpretation capabilities of AI systems.
paper_title: MMSci: A Dataset for Graduate-Level Multi-Discipline Multimodal Scientific Understanding
authors: Zekun Li, Xianjun Yang, Kyuri Choi, Wanrong Zhu, Ryan Hsieh

task_types: figure captioning, question answering, scientific comprehension
description: Consists of ArXivCap (6.4M scientific figure-caption pairs from 572K ArXiv papers) and ArXivQA (question-answering on scientific figures). Addresses the scarcity of training data for abstract scientific figures and plots, enhancing LVLMs' scientific comprehension capabilities.
paper_title: Multimodal ArXiv: A Dataset for Improving Scientific Comprehension of Large Vision-Language Models
authors: Lei Li, Yuqi Wang, Runxin Xu, Peiyi Wang, Xiachong Feng

description: An auto-verified dataset for Scientific Visual Question Answering created through cross-modal verification framework. Generates questions from figure-citing paragraphs then verifies against figures themselves, spanning 20 scientific domains and 12 figure types with improved quality over existing synthetic datasets.
paper_title: VeriSciQA: An Auto-Verified Dataset for Scientific Visual Question Answering
authors: Yuyi Li, Daoyuan Chen, Zhen Wang, Yutong Lu, Yaliang Li

task_types: mathematical reasoning, visual reflection, RLVR training
description: DeepVision-103K is a comprehensive dataset for Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) training in mathematical reasoning. It covers diverse K12 mathematical topics with rich visual elements and extensive knowledge points, specifically designed to enhance visual perception, reflection and reasoning capabilities in multimodal models.
paper_title: DeepVision-103K: A Visually Diverse, Broad-Coverage, and Verifiable Mathematical Dataset for Multimodal Reasoning
authors: Haoxiang Sun, Lizhen Xu, Bing Zhao, Wotao Yin, Wei Wang

description: First Chinese multimodal math dataset featuring variety of problem types across 12 grade levels with detailed solutions. Visual context can appear in questions or solutions, making it more challenging than existing English counterparts.
paper_title: CMM-Math: A Chinese Multimodal Math Dataset To Evaluate and Enhance the Mathematics Reasoning of Large Multimodal Models
authors: Wentao Liu, Qianjun Pan, Yi Zhang, Zhuo Liu, Ji Wu

description: Meticulously curated collection of high-quality mathematical problems with visual contexts from real math competitions. Spans 16 mathematical disciplines across 5 difficulty levels, providing comprehensive challenges for evaluating mathematical reasoning in visual contexts.
paper_title: Measuring Multimodal Mathematical Reasoning with MATH-Vision Dataset
authors: Ke Wang, Junting Pan, Weikang Shi, Zimu Lu, Mingjie Zhan

description: GeoCode synthesizes complex multimodal geometry problems from scratch with substantially higher structural complexity than existing benchmarks. It decouples problem generation into symbolic construction, grounded instantiation, and code-based diagram rendering while introducing code prediction as an explicit alignment objective.
paper_title: Synthesizing Multimodal Geometry Datasets from Scratch and Enabling Visual Alignment via Plotting Code
authors: Haobo Lin, Tianyi Bai, Chen Chen, Jiajun Zhang, Bohan Zeng

task_types: multi-modal question answering, knowledge graph reasoning
description: BioMol-MQA is a multi-modal question answering dataset focused on polypharmacy, combining a multimodal knowledge graph with text and molecular structures. It tests LLM capabilities in retrieving and reasoning over multimodal knowledge graphs to answer complex bio-molecular interaction questions.
paper_title: BioMol-MQA: A Multi-Modal Question Answering Dataset For LLM Reasoning Over Bio-Molecular Interactions
authors: Saptarshi Sengupta, Shuhua Yang, Paul Kwong Yu, Fali Wang, Suhang Wang

description: Comprehensive geometric reasoning corpus with two subsets (Geo-Thought-6K and Geo-Thought-Augmented-10K) containing visual descriptions, step-by-step solutions, explicit reasoning chains, and reflection steps for enhancing mathematical geometry reasoning in vision-language models.
paper_title: GeoThought: A Dataset for Enhancing Mathematical Geometry Reasoning in Vision-Language Models
authors: Nannan Shi, Chuanyu Qin, Shipeng Song, Man Luo

description: An open-source toolkit for fair evaluation of medical VLMs across 6 multi-modal tasks, 23 datasets, and 11 medical domains. Provides comprehensive assessment of model generalizability in medical applications.
paper_title: MultiMedEval: A Benchmark and a Toolkit for Evaluating Medical Vision-Language Models
authors: Corentin Royer, Bjoern Menze, Anjany Sekuboyina

task_types: visual explanation evaluation, medical image grounding
description: XBench is the first systematic benchmark for evaluating cross-modal interpretability in chest X-rays across seven CLIP-style VLM variants. It uniquely quantitatively assesses visual explanation alignment with radiologist-annotated regions using cross-attention and similarity-based localization maps.
paper_title: XBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Visual-Language Explanations in Chest Radiography
authors: Haozhe Luo, Shelley Zixin Shu, Ziyu Zhou, Sebastian Otalora, Mauricio Reyes

task_types: detection, size estimation, measurement, quantitative analysis
description: MedVision is a large-scale dataset and benchmark specifically designed to evaluate VLMs on quantitative medical image analysis tasks that are crucial for clinical decision-making. It focuses on three representative quantitative tasks: detection of anatomical structures and abnormalities, tumor/lesion size estimation, and angle/distance measurement across 22 public datasets covering diverse anatomies and modalities.
paper_title: MedVision: Dataset and Benchmark for Quantitative Medical Image Analysis
authors: Yongcheng Yao, Yongshuo Zong, Raman Dutt, Yongxin Yang, Sotirios A Tsaftaris

task_types: instrument classification, action classification, explainability analysis
description: SurgXBench is an explainable vision-language model benchmark for surgery that evaluates VLMs' zero-shot performance on robotic-assisted laparoscopic datasets for instrument and action classification. It uniquely integrates explainable AI to visualize VLM attention and uncover causal explanations, providing metrics to assess reliability of model predictions in surgical contexts.
paper_title: SurgXBench: Explainable Vision-Language Model Benchmark for Surgery
authors: Jiajun Cheng, Xianwu Zhao, Sainan Liu, Xiaofan Yu, Ravi Prakash

task_types: hallucination detection, medical image analysis, report generation
description: Gut-VLM is a hallucination-aware multimodal benchmark for gastrointestinal image analysis that uniquely features tags identifying hallucinated sentences and their corrections. Unlike traditional datasets with only descriptive texts, it enables evaluation and mitigation of hallucination in medical VLMs through a two-stage pipeline involving ChatGPT generation followed by medical expert review and correction.
paper_title: Hallucination-Aware Multimodal Benchmark for Gastrointestinal Image Analysis with Large Vision-Language Models
authors: Bidur Khanal, Sandesh Pokhrel, Sanjay Bhandari, Ramesh Rana, Nikesh Shrestha

task_types: hallucination detection, medical VQA, instruction following
description: MedHallTune is a large-scale benchmark designed specifically to evaluate and mitigate hallucinations in medical VLMs, comprising over 100,000 images and 1,000,000 instruction pairs. It includes both hallucination and non-hallucination samples with ground-truth annotations, evaluating models across clinical accuracy, relevance, detail level, and risk level metrics.
paper_title: MedHallTune: An Instruction-Tuning Benchmark for Mitigating Medical Hallucination in Vision-Language Models
authors: Qiao Yan, Yuchen Yuan, Xiaowei Hu, Yihan Wang, Jiaqi Xu

task_types: VQA, report generation, cross-modal retrieval, disease classification, lesion localization
description: Gastric-X is a large-scale multimodal benchmark for gastric cancer analysis providing paired resting/dynamic CT scans, endoscopic images, biochemical indicators, diagnostic notes, and bounding box annotations. It uniquely reflects realistic clinical workflows and examines whether VLMs can correlate biochemical signals with spatial tumor features and textual reports.
paper_title: Gastric-X: A Multimodal Multi-Phase Benchmark Dataset for Advancing Vision-Language Models in Gastric Cancer Analysis
authors: Sheng Lu, Hao Chen, Rui Yin, Juyan Ba, Yu Zhang

description: CT-3DRRG establishes the first comprehensive benchmark for 3D radiograph report generation using the largest publicly available 3D CT-report dataset. It uniquely focuses on 3D medical imaging contexts and provides training recipes for high-performing VLMs in medical report generation.
paper_title: Argus: Benchmarking and Enhancing Vision-Language Models for 3D Radiology Report Generation
authors: Che Liu, Zhongwei Wan, Yuqi Wang, Hui Shen, Haozhe Wang

task_types: medical diagnosis, anatomical identification, visual grounding, clinical assessment
description: First and largest visual question answering benchmark for fetal ultrasound imaging evaluation. Covers anatomical plane identification, visual grounding, fetal orientation assessment, clinical view conformity, and diagnosis across over 42,000 images addressing critical gaps in prenatal care evaluation.
paper_title: FETAL-GAUGE: A Benchmark for Assessing Vision-Language Models in Fetal Ultrasound
authors: Hussain Alasmawi, Numan Saeed, Mohammad Yaqub

task_types: biomedical analysis, microscopy understanding, pathological assessment, biological reasoning
description: Expert-curated benchmark encompassing 22 biomedical tasks across various scientific disciplines, microscopy modalities, scales, and organisms. Addresses lack of standardized vision-language benchmarks for biological image analysis revealing current model limitations and catastrophic forgetting issues.
paper_title: μ-Bench: A Vision-Language Benchmark for Microscopy Understanding
authors: Alejandro Lozano, Jeffrey Nirschl, James Burgess, Sanket Rajan Gupte, Yuhui Zhang

task_types: visual question answering, medical diagnosis, tumor detection
description: DeepTumorVQA is a diagnostic visual question answering benchmark targeting abdominal tumors in CT scans with 395K expert-level questions spanning Recognition, Measurement, Visual Reasoning, and Medical Reasoning categories. It introduces unique challenges including small tumor detection and clinical reasoning across 3D anatomy.
paper_title: Are Vision Language Models Ready for Clinical Diagnosis? A 3D Medical Benchmark for Tumor-centric Visual Question Answering
authors: Yixiong Chen, Wenjie Xiao, Pedro R. A. S. Bassi, Xinze Zhou, Sezgin Er

task_types: structured information extraction, medical report interpretation
description: MedRepBench is a comprehensive benchmark built from de-identified real-world Chinese medical reports spanning diverse departments and demographics. It evaluates structured medical report interpretation through both objective field-level recall metrics and automated subjective evaluation using LLMs as scoring agents.
paper_title: MedRepBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Medical Report Interpretation
authors: Fangxin Shang, Yuan Xia, Dalu Yang, Yahui Wang, Binglin Yang

task_types: Visual Question Answering, Image Editing, Contextual Multimodal Generation
description: A comprehensive multimodal medical benchmark with 6,422 expert-validated image-text pairs spanning six imaging modalities and 28 subtasks. It uniquely evaluates both text generation and image generation capabilities through contextually intertwined instructions requiring sophisticated cross-modal reasoning, moving beyond multiple-choice formats to open-ended generative outputs.
paper_title: MedGEN-Bench: Contextually entangled benchmark for open-ended multimodal medical generation
authors: Junjie Yang, Yuhao Yan, Gang Wu, Yuxuan Wang, Ruoyu Liang

description: A clinician-annotated dermatology VQA benchmark built on the Diverse Dermatology Images dataset with 656 clinical images from 570 unique patients spanning Fitzpatrick skin types I-VI. It uniquely uses a hierarchical annotation schema with 22 main questions covering diagnosis, morphology, and clinical reasoning rather than simple classification.
paper_title: DermaBench: A Clinician-Annotated Benchmark Dataset for Dermatology Visual Question Answering and Reasoning
authors: Abdurrahim Yilmaz, Ozan Erdem, Ece Gokyayla, Ayda Acar, Burc Bugra Dagtas

task_types: differential diagnosis, lesion recognition, rationale generation
description: A compact yet reasoning-intensive benchmark specifically designed to probe multimodal clinical reasoning in neurology, integrating multi-sequence MRI scans, structured EHRs, and clinical notes. It uniquely focuses on differential diagnosis, lesion recognition, and rationale generation rather than classification accuracy.
paper_title: Beyond Classification Accuracy: Neural-MedBench and the Need for Deeper Reasoning Benchmarks
authors: Miao Jing, Mengting Jia, Junling Lin, Zhongxia Shen, Huan Gao

description: A Korean medical licensing-exam-style multimodal benchmark with 1,534 questions and 2,043 associated images from Korean Medical Licensing Examinations (2012-2023). It uniquely requires cross-image evidence integration with about 30% containing multiple images and covers diverse clinical modalities including X-ray, CT, ECG, ultrasound, and endoscopy.
paper_title: KorMedMCQA-V: A Multimodal Benchmark for Evaluating Vision-Language Models on the Korean Medical Licensing Examination
authors: Byungjin Choi, Seongsu Bae, Sunjun Kweon, Edward Choi

description: The first language-conditioned, instance-level surgical grounding benchmark where each instance pairs a surgical image with natural-language description targeting a single instrument. It uniquely spans multiple surgical procedures (ophthalmic, laparoscopic, robotic, open) and evaluates both linguistic reference resolution and pixel-level localization.
paper_title: GroundedSurg: A Multi-Procedure Benchmark for Language-Conditioned Surgical Tool Segmentation
authors: Tajamul Ashraf, Abrar Ul Riyaz, Wasif Tak, Tavaheed Tariq, Sonia Yadav

description: TuneVLSeg is an open-source benchmarking framework for prompt tuning techniques in Vision-Language Segmentation Models. It integrates 6 prompt tuning strategies on 2 VLSMs tested across 8 medical datasets including radiology and non-radiology domains.
paper_title: TuneVLSeg: Prompt Tuning Benchmark for Vision-Language Segmentation Models
authors: Rabin Adhikari, Safal Thapaliya, Manish Dhakal, Bishesh Khanal

description: B&J Benchmark comprises 1,245 questions from real-world orthopedics and sports medicine cases across 7 clinical reasoning tasks. It assesses integrated, multimodal reasoning essential for patient care beyond narrow examination success, revealing gaps in current AI clinical competency.
paper_title: The Illusion of Clinical Reasoning: A Benchmark Reveals the Pervasive Gap in Vision-Language Models for Clinical Competency
authors: Dingyu Wang, Zimu Yuan, Jiajun Liu, Shanggui Liu, Nan Zhou

task_types: surgical phase identification, Go Zone detection
description: ResGo is a benchmark of laparoscopic frames annotated with Go Zone bounding boxes and clinician-authored rationales covering surgical phase, exposure quality reasoning, next actions and risk reminders. It addresses phase-dependent nature of intraoperative reasoning in minimally invasive surgery.
paper_title: SurGo-R1: Benchmarking and Modeling Contextual Reasoning for Operative Zone in Surgical Video
authors: Guanyi Qin, Xiaozhen Wang, Zhu Zhuo, Chang Han Low, Yuancan Xiao

task_types: medical visual question answering, sycophancy evaluation
description: The paper introduces a Medical benchmark that applies multiple templates to VLMs in hierarchical medical visual question answering to evaluate sycophancy. It systematically assesses susceptibility to visual cues and authority-based biases in medical contexts.
paper_title: Benchmarking and Mitigating Sycophancy in Medical Vision Language Models
authors: Zikun Guo, Jingwei Lv, Xinyue Xu, Shu Yang, Jun Wen

task_types: surgical perception, temporal analysis, surgical reasoning
description: SurgVLM-Bench consists of 6 popular surgical datasets covering crucial downstream tasks for surgical intelligence. It evaluates vision-language models on surgical visual perception, temporal analysis, and reasoning across 16 surgical types and 18 anatomical structures.
paper_title: SurgVLM: A Large Vision-Language Model and Systematic Evaluation Benchmark for Surgical Intelligence
authors: Zhitao Zeng, Zhu Zhuo, Xiaojun Jia, Erli Zhang, Junde Wu

description: HistoVL is a comprehensive histopathology benchmark comprising images from 11 acquisition tools across 26 organs and 31 cancer types with over 5 million patches. It evaluates histopathology VLMs on diverse clinical scenarios with specifically crafted captions incorporating pathology descriptions.
paper_title: How Good is my Histopathology Vision-Language Foundation Model? A Holistic Benchmark
authors: Roba Al Majzoub, Hashmat Malik, Muzammal Naseer, Zaigham Zaheer, Tariq Mahmood

task_types: anomaly localization, visual captioning, diagnostic reasoning
description: NOVA is an evaluation-only benchmark of brain MRI scans spanning 281 rare pathologies with rich clinical narratives and expert bounding-box annotations. It serves as an extreme stress-test for out-of-distribution generalization, enabling joint assessment of anomaly localization, visual captioning, and diagnostic reasoning.
paper_title: NOVA: A Benchmark for Anomaly Localization and Clinical Reasoning in Brain MRI
authors: Cosmin I. Bercea, Jun Li, Philipp Raffler, Evamaria O. Riedel, Lena Schmitzer

task_types: medical report generation, multimodal medical analysis
description: This is the first dataset providing comprehensive PET/CT-report pairs in Vietnamese, addressing gaps in functional imaging data and low-resource language representation in medical VLMs. It includes 2,757 whole-body PET/CT volumes with corresponding Vietnamese clinical reports.
paper_title: Toward a Vision-Language Foundation Model for Medical Data: Multimodal Dataset and Benchmarks for Vietnamese PET/CT Report Generation
authors: Huu Tien Nguyen, Dac Thai Nguyen, The Minh Duc Nguyen, Trung Thanh Nguyen, Thao Nguyen Truong

task_types: spot diagnosis, medical imaging, expert-level cases
description: RadLE (Radiology's Last Exam) is a pilot benchmark of 50 expert-level 'spot diagnosis' cases across multiple imaging modalities to evaluate frontier AI models against board-certified radiologists. It includes a taxonomy of visual reasoning errors and tests models through native web interfaces.
paper_title: Radiology's Last Exam (RadLE): Benchmarking Frontier Multimodal AI Against Human Experts and a Taxonomy of Visual Reasoning Errors in Radiology
authors: Suvrankar Datta, Divya Buchireddygari, Lakshmi Vennela Chowdary Kaza, Mrudula Bhalke, Kautik Singh

task_types: telemedical consultation, multi-agent dialogue, diagnostic accuracy
description: 3MDBench simulates and evaluates LVLM-driven telemedical consultations with 2996 cases across 34 diagnoses from real-world telemedicine interactions. It uses temperament-based Patient Agents and Assessor Agents to evaluate both diagnostic accuracy and dialogue quality.
paper_title: 3MDBench: Medical Multimodal Multi-agent Dialogue Benchmark
authors: Ivan Sviridov, Amina Miftakhova, Artemiy Tereshchenko, Galina Zubkova, Pavel Blinov

description: ReXInTheWild provides 955 clinician-verified multiple-choice questions spanning seven clinical topics across 484 photographs from biomedical literature. It combines fine-grained natural image understanding with domain-specific medical reasoning for everyday medical photographs.
paper_title: ReXInTheWild: A Unified Benchmark for Medical Photograph Understanding
authors: Oishi Banerjee, Sung Eun Kim, Alexandra N. Willauer, Julius M. Kernbach, Abeer Rihan Alomaish

task_types: trustfulness evaluation, fairness assessment, safety testing, privacy evaluation, robustness testing
description: A comprehensive benchmark for evaluating trustworthiness of Medical Large Vision Language Models across five dimensions: trustfulness, fairness, safety, privacy, and robustness. It covers 16 medical image modalities and 27 anatomical regions with both closed and open-ended question formats.
paper_title: CARES: A Comprehensive Benchmark of Trustworthiness in Medical Vision Language Models
authors: Peng Xia, Ze Chen, Juanxi Tian, Yangrui Gong, Ruibo Hou

description: First bilingual multimodal VQA benchmark for ophthalmology with 30,120 QA pairs across 9 subspecialties and 548 conditions. Features real-world context from WeChat posts with multiple examinations per patient reflecting clinical scenarios.
paper_title: Benchmarking Large Multimodal Models for Ophthalmic Visual Question Answering with OphthalWeChat
authors: Pusheng Xu, Xia Gong, Xiaolan Chen, Weiyi Zhang, Jiancheng Yang

description: First comprehensive benchmark for evaluating LVLMs on ultrasound understanding across 15 anatomical regions and 50 application scenarios. Covers classification, detection, regression, and text generation tasks including diagnosis, view recognition, lesion localization, and report generation.
paper_title: U2-BENCH: Benchmarking Large Vision-Language Models on Ultrasound Understanding
authors: Anjie Le, Henan Liu, Yue Wang, Zhenyu Liu, Rongkun Zhu

description: Comprehensive multimodal oro-dental dataset with 50,000 intraoral images, 8,056 radiographs, and detailed textual records collected over eight years. Tests classification of six oro-dental anomalies and generation of complete diagnostic reports from multimodal inputs.
paper_title: A benchmark multimodal oro-dental dataset for large vision-language models
authors: Haoxin Lv, Ijazul Haq, Jin Du, Jiaxin Ma, Binnian Zhu

description: Emergency Room Visual Question Answering dataset with expert-annotated open-ended questions covering diverse emergency room scenarios. Benchmarks healthcare knowledge of LVLMs in hospital settings with detailed error taxonomy and adapted evaluation metrics including Entailment Score and CLIPScore Confidence.
paper_title: ERVQA: A Dataset to Benchmark the Readiness of Large Vision Language Models in Hospital Environments
authors: Sourjyadip Ray, Kushal Gupta, Soumi Kundu, Payal Arvind Kasat, Somak Aditya

description: Large-scale surgical video QA dataset harvesting reasoning signals from academic surgical videos with 12 question categories covering safety assessment, decision rationale, and forecasting. Extracts structured supervision from expert narrations across 170 procedure types with expert-validated benchmark.
paper_title: SUREON: A Benchmark and Vision-Language-Model for Surgical Reasoning
authors: Alejandra Perez, Anita Rau, Lee White, Busisiwe Mlambo, Chinedu Nwoye

description: Large-scale comprehensive medical VQA benchmark collected from 73 datasets spanning 12 modalities and 20+ anatomical regions from authentic medical scenarios. Addresses scarcity of diverse medical images for evaluating LVLMs in real-world medical applications.
paper_title: OmniMedVQA: A New Large-Scale Comprehensive Evaluation Benchmark for Medical LVLM
authors: Yutao Hu, Tianbin Li, Quanfeng Lu, Wenqi Shao, Junjun He

task_types: diagnostic reasoning, visual grounding, medical measurement
description: Structured benchmark for evaluating diagnostic reasoning in chest X-rays using automated pipeline that derives intermediate reasoning steps. Supports multi-path, multi-stage evaluation including visual grounding and diagnostic measurements across 12 diagnostic tasks.
paper_title: CXReasonBench: A Benchmark for Evaluating Structured Diagnostic Reasoning in Chest X-rays
authors: Hyungyung Lee, Geon Choi, Jung-Oh Lee, Hangyul Yoon, Hyuk Gi Hong

description: Most comprehensive general medical AI benchmark with well-categorized data structure and multi-perceptual granularity. Constructed from 284 datasets spanning 18 clinical tasks, 18 departments, and 4 perceptual granularities in VQA format with lexical tree structure for customizable evaluation.
paper_title: GMAI-MMBench: A Comprehensive Multimodal Evaluation Benchmark Towards General Medical AI
authors: Pengcheng Chen, Jin Ye, Guoan Wang, Yanjun Li, Zhongying Deng

description: Comprehensive benchmark for evaluating LVLM performance on intelligent ophthalmic diagnosis tasks across multiple dimensions. Part of Eyecare Kit addressing ophthalmology-specific challenges with high-quality ophthalmic visual instruction dataset and specialized model architecture.
paper_title: EyecareGPT: Boosting Comprehensive Ophthalmology Understanding with Tailored Dataset, Benchmark and Model
authors: Sijing Li, Tianwei Lin, Lingshuai Lin, Wenqiao Zhang, Jiang Liu

task_types: knowledge editing, counterfactual correction, semantic generalization, knowledge transfer
description: MedMKEB is the first comprehensive benchmark for evaluating knowledge editing in medical multimodal large language models, assessing reliability, generality, locality, portability, and robustness with carefully constructed editing tasks and human expert validation.
paper_title: MedMKEB: A Comprehensive Knowledge Editing Benchmark for Medical Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Dexuan Xu, Jieyi Wang, Zhongyan Chai, Yongzhi Cao, Hanpin Wang

description: FunBench is a visual question answering benchmark that comprehensively evaluates MLLMs' fundus reading skills for ophthalmology. It features hierarchical task organization across four levels and offers three targeted evaluation modes including modular analysis of LLM and vision encoder components.
paper_title: FunBench: Benchmarking Fundus Reading Skills of MLLMs
authors: Qijie Wei, Kaiheng Qian, Xirong Li

description: ECG-Reasoning-Benchmark is a novel multi-turn evaluation framework comprising over 6,400 samples to systematically assess step-by-step reasoning across 17 core ECG diagnoses. It specifically investigates whether MLLMs perform actual reasoning or rely on superficial visual cues in ECG interpretation, revealing critical failures in multi-step logical deduction.
paper_title: ECG-Reasoning-Benchmark: A Benchmark for Evaluating Clinical Reasoning Capabilities in ECG Interpretation
authors: Jungwoo Oh, Hyunseung Chung, Junhee Lee, Min-Gyu Kim, Hangyul Yoon

task_types: spinal disease diagnosis, spinal lesion localization
description: SpineBench is a comprehensive Visual Question Answering (VQA) benchmark designed for fine-grained analysis and evaluation of MLLMs in the spinal domain. It comprises 64,878 QA pairs from 40,263 spine images, covering 11 spinal diseases through two critical clinical tasks: spinal disease diagnosis and spinal lesion localization, with challenging hard negative options based on visual similarity.
paper_title: SpineBench: Benchmarking Multimodal LLMs for Spinal Pathology Analysis
authors: Chenghanyu Zhang, Zekun Li, Peipei Li, Xing Cui, Shuhan Xia

task_types: closed-ended VQA, open-ended VQA, clinical reasoning
description: OmniBrainBench is the first comprehensive multimodal VQA benchmark for brain imaging analysis across 15 distinct brain imaging modalities. It comprises 9,527 validated VQA pairs and encompasses 15 multi-stage clinical tasks simulating clinical workflows for comprehensive MLLM assessment.
paper_title: OmniBrainBench: A Comprehensive Multimodal Benchmark for Brain Imaging Analysis Across Multi-stage Clinical Tasks
authors: Zhihao Peng, Cheng Wang, Shengyuan Liu, Zhiying Liang, Zanting Ye

task_types: error classification, error detection, error correction
description: MedErr-CT is a novel benchmark for evaluating medical MLLMs' ability to identify and correct errors in CT reports through VQA. It includes six error categories (Omission, Insertion, Direction, Size, Unit, Typo) organized into three task levels: classification, detection, and correction.
paper_title: MedErr-CT: A Visual Question Answering Benchmark for Identifying and Correcting Errors in CT Reports
authors: Sunggu Kyung, Hyungbin Park, Jinyoung Seo, Jimin Sung, Jihyun Kim

task_types: clinical diagnosis, evidence retrieval, medical reasoning
description: Presents the Clinical Understanding and Retrieval Evaluation benchmark comprising 500 multimodal clinical cases mapped to physician-cited reference literature. It evaluates both clinical reasoning and evidence retrieval capabilities under controlled settings to disentangle their respective contributions.
paper_title: CURE: A Multimodal Benchmark for Clinical Understanding and Retrieval Evaluation
authors: Yannian Gu, Zhongzhen Huang, Linjie Mu, Xizhuo Zhang, Shaoting Zhang

description: A comprehensive benchmark for evaluating medical MLLMs under image quality degradations across 18 degradation types, 30 capability dimensions, and 7 imaging modalities. Unique for introducing Calibration Shift metric to assess metacognitive reliability and systematically testing model performance under various quality degradations.
paper_title: MedQ-Deg: A Multidimensional Benchmark for Evaluating MLLMs Across Medical Image Quality Degradations
authors: Jiyao Liu, Junzhi Ning, Chenglong Ma, Wanying Qu, Jianghan Shen

description: The first multimodal benchmark explicitly created to evaluate anatomical understanding in the neurosurgical domain, consisting of 9 hours of annotated neurosurgical videos covering 89 procedures. Unique for focusing on anatomical structure identification (68 clinical structures) rather than surgical procedure understanding.
paper_title: NeuroABench: A Multimodal Evaluation Benchmark for Neurosurgical Anatomy Identification
authors: Ziyang Song, Zelin Zang, Xiaofan Ye, Boqiang Xu, Long Bai

description: The first benchmark designed to evaluate fairness of MLLMs across diverse demographic attributes in medical tasks. What makes it unique is its focus on demographic fairness across race, ethnicity, language, and gender in medical VQA and report generation, introducing Fairness-Aware Performance metric.
paper_title: FMBench: Benchmarking Fairness in Multimodal Large Language Models on Medical Tasks
authors: Peiran Wu, Che Liu, Canyu Chen, Jun Li, Cosmin I. Bercea

description: A benchmark specifically designed to evaluate Chain-of-Thought reasoning correctness, efficiency, impact, and consistency in medical image understanding across 24 examination types and 13 varying-difficulty tasks. Unique for focusing on reasoning paths rather than just final answers in medical diagnosis.
paper_title: M3CoTBench: Benchmark Chain-of-Thought of MLLMs in Medical Image Understanding
authors: Juntao Jiang, Jiangning Zhang, Yali Bi, Jinsheng Bai, Weixuan Liu

description: The first benchmark specifically designed to evaluate medical reward models and judges across 13 organ systems and 8 clinical departments. Unique for focusing on reward model evaluation in medical scenarios with expert-annotated cases across six clinically critical dimensions including diagnostic accuracy and clinical relevance.
paper_title: Med-RewardBench: Benchmarking Reward Models and Judges for Medical Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Meidan Ding, Jipeng Zhang, Wenxuan Wang, Cheng-Yi Li, Wei-Chieh Fang

description: The first expert-driven multimodal in-context learning benchmark for medical tasks, with 111 problems curated by medical experts covering 6 specialties and 13 imaging modalities. Unique for focusing on multimodal ICL abilities in medical domain with task demonstrations and expert validation.
paper_title: SMMILE: An Expert-Driven Benchmark for Multimodal Medical In-Context Learning
authors: Melanie Rieff, Maya Varma, Ossian Rabow, Subathra Adithan, Julie Kim

task_types: VQA, image difference grounding, image consistency grounding
description: First benchmark for Medical Image Sequences Grounding, focusing on sequential medical images rather than single images. It evaluates two paradigms: Image Difference Grounding (detecting change regions across images) and Image Consistency Grounding (detecting consistent semantics), covering 76 public datasets and 10 medical imaging modalities.
paper_title: MedSG-Bench: A Benchmark for Medical Image Sequences Grounding
authors: Jingkun Yue, Siqi Zhang, Zinan Jia, Huihuan Xu, Zongbo Han

description: Large-scale benchmark for surgical action planning evaluation using cholecystectomy procedures. Provides temporally-grounded surgical action annotations with 1,226 clinically validated action clips, enabling MLLMs to generate next action recommendations from current surgical scenes.
paper_title: SAP-Bench: Benchmarking Multimodal Large Language Models in Surgical Action Planning
authors: Mengya Xu, Zhongzhen Huang, Dillan Imans, Yiru Ye, Xiaofan Zhang

modalities: multiple medical image types, text, clinical data
description: Multilingual, multimodal benchmark of highly complex clinical cases with up to 7 distinct visual clinical evidence types per case. Mirrors clinical workflow by evaluating differential diagnosis generation and final diagnosis selection, revealing synthesis failure modes in MLLMs.
paper_title: MEDSYN: Benchmarking Multi-EviDence SYNthesis in Complex Clinical Cases for Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Boqi Chen, Xudong Liu, Jiachuan Peng, Marianne Frey-Marti, Bang Zheng

description: Ophthalmic benchmark for evaluating MLLMs with fundus photographs and OCT images. Addresses limitations in existing benchmarks through rigorous quality control and expert annotation, focusing on real-world clinical practice complexities in ophthalmology.
paper_title: A Novel Ophthalmic Benchmark for Evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models with Fundus Photographs and OCT Images
authors: Xiaoyi Liang, Mouxiao Bian, Moxin Chen, Lihao Liu, Junjun He

task_types: anatomical localization, lesion identification, diagnosis, findings description, management
description: A comprehensive medical benchmark covering gastrointestinal endoscopy workflow evaluation with 20 fine-grained lesion categories. Uniquely evaluates models across five clinical stages (anatomical localization, lesion identification, diagnosis, description, and management) comparing against human endoscopists.
paper_title: GI-Bench: A Panoramic Benchmark Revealing the Knowledge-Experience Dissociation of Multimodal Large Language Models in Gastrointestinal Endoscopy Against Clinical Standards
authors: Yan Zhu, Te Luo, Pei-Yao Fu, Zhen Zhang, Zi-Long Wang

task_types: visual question answering, medical knowledge comprehension, clinical reasoning
description: Comprehensive benchmark for ophthalmic surgery analysis grounded in structured clinical knowledge. Features over 210k VQAs covering fine-grained attributes for perception, medical knowledge graph for comprehension, and clinical reasoning tasks.
paper_title: EyePCR: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Fine-Grained Perception, Knowledge Comprehension and Clinical Reasoning in Ophthalmic Surgery
authors: Gui Wang, Yang Wennuo, Xusen Ma, Zehao Zhong, Zhuoru Wu

task_types: spatial intent, temporal intent, standard intent, safety protocol compliance
description: First benchmark leveraging clinician gaze as Cognitive Cursor for egocentric clinical intent understanding. Evaluates spatial, temporal, and standard intent through Three-Dimensional Clinical Intent Framework with Trap QA mechanisms.
paper_title: Benchmarking Egocentric Clinical Intent Understanding Capability for Medical Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Shaonan Liu, Guo Yu, Xiaoling Luo, Shiyi Zheng, Wenting Chen

task_types: anatomical structure recognition, disease screening, disease staging, demographic prediction
description: Large-scale multimodal ophthalmology benchmark with multi-granular annotations across 12 common conditions and 5 imaging modalities. Supports anatomical recognition, disease screening/staging, and demographic prediction for bias evaluation.
paper_title: LMOD+: A Comprehensive Multimodal Dataset and Benchmark for Developing and Evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models in Ophthalmology
authors: Zhenyue Qin, Yang Liu, Yu Yin, Jinyu Ding, Haoran Zhang

task_types: scientific reasoning, hypothesis generation, experiment proposal, image understanding
description: MicroVQA assesses three research-level reasoning capabilities in microscopy: expert image understanding, hypothesis generation, and experiment proposal. It uniquely targets research-level scientific reasoning rather than college-level difficulty, with biology expert curation across diverse microscopy modalities.
paper_title: MicroVQA: A Multimodal Reasoning Benchmark for Microscopy-Based Scientific Research
authors: James Burgess, Jeffrey J Nirschl, Laura Bravo-Sánchez, Alejandro Lozano, Sanket Rajan Gupte

task_types: modality recognition, disease classification, anatomical identification, symptom diagnosis, surgical procedures
description: MedBookVQA systematically derives medical questions from open-access textbooks with automated figure extraction and contextual alignment. It provides hierarchical taxonomies across 42 imaging modalities, 125 anatomical structures, and 31 clinical specialties, enabling nuanced analysis across medical subdomains.
paper_title: MedBookVQA: A Systematic and Comprehensive Medical Benchmark Derived from Open-Access Book
authors: Sau Lai Yip, Sunan He, Yuxiang Nie, Shu Pui Chan, Yilin Ye

description: MedSPOT is a workflow-aware sequential grounding benchmark for clinical GUI environments with 216 task-driven videos and 597 annotated keyframes. Unlike existing benchmarks treating grounding as standalone tasks, it models procedural interaction as sequences of structured spatial decisions within realistic medical workflows.
paper_title: MedSPOT: A Workflow-Aware Sequential Grounding Benchmark for Clinical GUI
authors: Rozain Shakeel, Abdul Rahman Mohammad Ali, Muneeb Mushtaq, Tausifa Jan Saleem, Tajamul Ashraf

description: A specialized ophthalmology benchmark designed to evaluate hallucinations in medical MLLMs. Categorizes hallucinations into Visual Understanding and Logical Composition classes, addressing the specific challenges of ophthalmic diagnosis including limited knowledge, insufficient visual localization, and reasoning capabilities.
paper_title: EH-Benchmark Ophthalmic Hallucination Benchmark and Agent-Driven Top-Down Traceable Reasoning Workflow
authors: Xiaoyu Pan, Yang Bai, Ke Zou, Yang Zhou, Jun Zhou

task_types: medical diagnosis, disease analysis, medical perception
description: A comprehensive medical MLLM benchmark spanning 15 medical specialties with stratification into 3 main categories and 8 sub-categories of clinical tasks. Ensures evaluation across distinct specialties and different diagnostic capacities including perception and disease analysis while avoiding overlap with existing VQA datasets.
paper_title: A Spectrum Evaluation Benchmark for Medical Multi-Modal Large Language Models
authors: Jie Liu, Wenxuan Wang, Yihang Su, Jingyuan Huan, Wenting Chen

description: SurgMLLMBench unifies multimodal evaluation for surgical scene understanding across laparoscopic, robot-assisted, and micro-surgical domains under a unified taxonomy. It uniquely integrates pixel-level instrument segmentation masks with structured VQA annotations, enabling comprehensive evaluation beyond traditional VQA tasks and supporting richer visual-conversational interactions.
paper_title: SurgMLLMBench: A Multimodal Large Language Model Benchmark Dataset for Surgical Scene Understanding
authors: Tae-Min Choi, Tae Kyeong Jeong, Garam Kim, Jaemin Lee, Yeongyoon Koh

task_types: hallucination detection, medical diagnosis, clinical evaluation
description: MedHallBench is a comprehensive benchmark framework for evaluating and mitigating hallucinations specifically in Medical Large Language Models. It integrates expert-validated medical case scenarios with established medical databases and employs automated ACHMI scoring combined with clinical expert evaluations.
paper_title: MedHallBench: A New Benchmark for Assessing Hallucination in Medical Large Language Models
authors: Kaiwen Zuo, Yirui Jiang

task_types: visual question answering, report generation, surgical diagnosis
description: SurgWound-Bench is the first benchmark for surgical wound diagnosis featuring 697 surgical wound images annotated by professional surgeons with eight fine-grained clinical attributes. It includes VQA and report generation tasks to evaluate comprehensive surgical wound assessment capabilities.
paper_title: SurgWound-Bench: A Benchmark for Surgical Wound Diagnosis
authors: Jiahao Xu, Changchang Yin, Odysseas Chatzipanagiotou, Diamantis Tsilimigras, Kevin Clear

task_types: diagnostic narrative generation, medical diagnosis, clinical evaluation
description: DermBench is a meticulously curated benchmark pairing 4,000 real-world dermatology images with expert-certified diagnostic narratives. It uses an LLM-based judge to score candidate narratives across clinically grounded dimensions, enabling consistent evaluation of dermatology diagnostic capabilities in multimodal models.
paper_title: Towards Trustworthy Dermatology MLLMs: A Benchmark and Multimodal Evaluator for Diagnostic Narratives
authors: Yuhao Shen, Jiahe Qian, Shuping Zhang, Zhangtianyi Chen, Tao Lu

description: Fine-grained Medical Complex Multimodal Reasoning benchmark that systematically decomposes medical reasoning into visual understanding (small-object detection, fine-detail discrimination, spatial understanding) and multi-step reasoning across four clinical scenarios. Covers 11 organ systems and 12 imaging modalities with rigorous clinical validation.
paper_title: Med-CMR: A Fine-Grained Benchmark Integrating Visual Evidence and Clinical Logic for Medical Complex Multimodal Reasoning
authors: Haozhen Gong, Xiaozhong Ji, Yuansen Liu, Wenbin Wu, Xiaoxiao Yan

description: First benchmark for multi-image medical VQA testing comparative reasoning through educationally-validated diagnostic sequences. Uses narrative transcripts from medical education videos to create high-quality multi-image VQA pairs with explicit, transcript-grounded reasoning chains.
paper_title: MedFrameQA: A Multi-Image Medical VQA Benchmark for Clinical Reasoning
authors: Suhao Yu, Haojin Wang, Juncheng Wu, Luyang Luo, Jingshen Wang

description: Agentic benchmark simulating Molecular Tumor Board decision-making through multimodal, longitudinal oncology questions. Tests sequential clinical decision-making in multi-agent environments requiring integration of heterogeneous data and evolving insights over time with clinician-validated annotations.
paper_title: MTBBench: A Multimodal Sequential Clinical Decision-Making Benchmark in Oncology
authors: Kiril Vasilev, Alexandre Misrahi, Eeshaan Jain, Phil F Cheng, Petros Liakopoulos

task_types: multi-image medical analysis, composite medical understanding
description: PMC-MI-Bench is constructed for composite understanding of medical compound figures from biomedical literature, manually validated by medical experts. It uniquely focuses on multi-image analysis capabilities in medical contexts, enabling MLLMs to synthesize information across multiple images from different modalities or time points for medical diagnosis.
paper_title: From Compound Figures to Composite Understanding: Developing a Multi-Modal LLM from Biomedical Literature with Medical Multiple-Image Benchmarking and Validation
authors: Zhen Chen, Yihang Fu, Gabriel Madera, Mauro Giuffre, Serina Applebaum

task_types: visual question answering, medical image analysis, compositional generalization
description: CrossMed is designed to evaluate compositional generalization in medical multimodal LLMs using a structured Modality-Anatomy-Task (MAT) schema. It reformulates four public medical datasets into a unified VQA format with 20,200 multiple-choice QA instances, uniquely testing generalization across unseen combinations of imaging modality, anatomy, and task type.
paper_title: CrossMed: A Multimodal Cross-Task Benchmark for Compositional Generalization in Medical Imaging
authors: Pooja Singh, Siddhant Ujjain, Tapan Kumar Gandhi, Sandeep Kumar

task_types: phase classification, camera motion, tool recognition, disease diagnosis, action classification, organ detection
description: SurgBench is a unified surgical video benchmarking framework with SurgBench-P (53 million frames across 22 surgical procedures) for pretraining and SurgBench-E for evaluation across six categories spanning 72 fine-grained tasks. It provides extensive coverage of diverse surgical scenarios for developing surgical video foundation models.
paper_title: SurgBench: A Unified Large-Scale Benchmark for Surgical Video Analysis
authors: Jianhui Wei, Zikai Xiao, Danyu Sun, Luqi Gong, Zongxin Yang

description: The first benchmark for Multi-lingual, Multi-modal, and Multi-hop reasoning in medical instructional video understanding. Features a novel multi-hop reasoning task requiring models to locate key entities in text, find corresponding visual evidence in video, and synthesize cross-modal information. Includes two tasks: TAGSV (single video) and TAGVC (video corpus).
paper_title: M$^3$-Med: A Benchmark for Multi-lingual, Multi-modal, and Multi-hop Reasoning in Medical Instructional Video Understanding
authors: Shenxi Liu, Kan Li, Mingyang Zhao, Yuhang Tian, Bin Li

description: PediatricsMQA addresses age bias in medical AI with 3,417 text-based and 2,067 vision-based MCQs covering 131 pediatric topics across seven developmental stages. It uses 634 pediatric images from 67 imaging modalities, specifically designed to evaluate pediatric medical understanding where current models show dramatic performance drops.
paper_title: PediatricsMQA: a Multi-modal Pediatrics Question Answering Benchmark
authors: Adil Bahaj, Oumaima Fadi, Mohamed Chetouani, Mounir Ghogho

description: CT-Bench is the first comprehensive benchmark for multimodal lesion understanding in computed tomography, comprising 20,335 lesions from 7,795 CT studies with detailed annotations and 2,850 VQA pairs. It covers lesion localization, description, size estimation, and attribute categorization with hard negative examples reflecting real diagnostic challenges.
paper_title: CT-Bench: A Benchmark for Multimodal Lesion Understanding in Computed Tomography
authors: Qingqing Zhu, Qiao Jin, Tejas S. Mathai, Yin Fang, Zhizheng Wang

description: PathVG introduces Pathology Visual Grounding as a new benchmark task for detecting regions based on pathological expressions with different attributes. The RefPath dataset contains 27,610 images with 33,500 language-grounded boxes, presenting multi-scale pathological images with expressions requiring specialized pathological knowledge.
paper_title: PathVG: A New Benchmark and Dataset for Pathology Visual Grounding
authors: Chunlin Zhong, Shuang Hao, Junhua Wu, Xiaona Chang, Jiwei Jiang

task_types: hierarchical visual question answering, structured reporting
description: Rad-ReStruct provides fine-grained, hierarchically ordered annotations for structured radiology reporting on X-Ray images. It models structured reporting as hierarchical VQA and is the first public benchmark for evaluating automated structured radiology reporting methods.
paper_title: Rad-ReStruct: A Novel VQA Benchmark and Method for Structured Radiology Reporting
authors: Chantal Pellegrini, Matthias Keicher, Ege Özsoy, Nassir Navab

modalities: image, signal, text, laboratory results, vital signs
task_types: classification, medical visual question answering, signal processing
description: FEDMEKI addresses medical knowledge injection into foundation models under privacy constraints using federated learning. It handles multi-site, multi-modal medical data across 7 modalities and 8 medical tasks including both classification and generation tasks while preserving data privacy.
paper_title: FEDMEKI: A Benchmark for Scaling Medical Foundation Models via Federated Knowledge Injection
authors: Jiaqi Wang, Xiaochen Wang, Lingjuan Lyu, Jinghui Chen, Fenglong Ma

task_types: medical report generation, medical image-text retrieval
description: RGC provides high-quality, multi-modality radiographic dataset for medical vision-language pre-training. It enables comprehensive analysis of key factors affecting medical VLP performance and serves as both a pre-training dataset and benchmark for medical report generation and image-text retrieval.
paper_title: Multi-modal Pre-training for Medical Vision-language Understanding and Generation: An Empirical Study with A New Benchmark
authors: Li Xu, Bo Liu, Ameer Hamza Khan, Lu Fan, Xiao-Ming Wu

task_types: visual question answering, medical diagnosis, explanation generation
description: GEMeX is the largest chest X-ray VQA dataset featuring multi-modal explainability with detailed visual and textual explanations for each QA pair. It includes four question types (open-ended, closed-ended, single-choice, multiple-choice) and groundable explanations to enhance answer comprehensibility.
paper_title: GEMeX: A Large-Scale, Groundable, and Explainable Medical VQA Benchmark for Chest X-ray Diagnosis
authors: Bo Liu, Ke Zou, Liming Zhan, Zexin Lu, Xiaoyu Dong

task_types: medical visual question answering, lay summary generation, EHR predictive modeling, clinical workflow automation
description: MedAgentBoard provides systematic evaluation of multi-agent collaboration, single-LLM, and conventional approaches across four diverse medical task categories including medical VQA, lay summary generation, EHR predictive modeling, and clinical workflow automation. It offers evidence-based insights for selecting appropriate AI solutions in medicine across text, medical images, and structured EHR data.
paper_title: MedAgentBoard: Benchmarking Multi-Agent Collaboration with Conventional Methods for Diverse Medical Tasks
authors: Yinghao Zhu, Ziyi He, Haoran Hu, Xiaochen Zheng, Xichen Zhang

description: CXRTrek introduces multi-stage visual question answering for chest X-ray interpretation that explicitly simulates the diagnostic reasoning process of radiologists. It covers 8 sequential diagnostic stages with over 11 million Q&A pairs, modeling dependencies between diagnostic stages and capturing reasoning patterns within radiological context.
paper_title: Interpreting Chest X-rays Like a Radiologist: A Benchmark with Clinical Reasoning
authors: Jinquan Guan, Qi Chen, Lizhou Liang, Yuhang Liu, Vu Minh Hieu Phan

task_types: attribute extraction, report generation, visual question answering, image-grounded dialogue
description: MMOral is the first large-scale multimodal instruction dataset and benchmark for panoramic X-ray interpretation in dentistry, consisting of 20,563 annotated images with 1.3 million instruction-following instances. MMOral-Bench provides comprehensive evaluation across five key diagnostic dimensions in dentistry, addressing the specialized challenges of dense anatomical structures and subtle pathological cues in oral radiology.
paper_title: Towards Better Dental AI: A Multimodal Benchmark and Instruction Dataset for Panoramic X-ray Analysis
authors: Jing Hao, Yuxuan Fan, Yanpeng Sun, Kaixin Guo, Lizhuo Lin

description: ReXVQA is the largest comprehensive VQA benchmark for chest radiology with approximately 696,000 questions across five core radiological reasoning skills: presence assessment, location analysis, negation detection, differential diagnosis, and geometric reasoning. It introduces clinically authentic tasks that go beyond template-based queries and includes comprehensive human reader studies with radiology residents.
paper_title: ReXVQA: A Large-scale Visual Question Answering Benchmark for Generalist Chest X-ray Understanding
authors: Ankit Pal, Jung-Oh Lee, Xiaoman Zhang, Malaikannan Sankarasubbu, Seunghyeon Roh

benchmark_name: MC-BEC (Multimodal Clinical Benchmark for Emergency Care)
modalities: text, vital signs, ECG waveforms, photoplethysmograph, medical imaging reports
task_types: patient decompensation prediction, disposition prediction, ED revisit prediction
