description: Comprehensive plant pathology benchmark with 186,000 leaf images across 97 disease classes generating 13,950 QA pairs. Evaluates six agricultural tasks including visual symptom recognition, taxonomic relationships, and diagnostic reasoning for AI-assisted plant disease diagnosis.
paper_title: LeafNet: A Large-Scale Dataset and Comprehensive Benchmark for Foundational Vision-Language Understanding of Plant Diseases
authors: Khang Nguyen Quoc, Phuong D. Dao, Luyl-Da Quach

task_types: question answering, visual question answering, information retrieval
description: RoD-TAL is a novel multimodal dataset comprising Romanian driving test questions with both text-based and image-based content. It includes annotated legal references and explanations written by human experts to evaluate LLMs and VLMs on understanding Romanian driving law through legal reasoning tasks.
paper_title: RoD-TAL: A Benchmark for Answering Questions in Romanian Driving License Exams
authors: Andrei Vlad Man, Răzvan-Alexandru Smădu, Cristian-George Craciun, Dumitru-Clementin Cercel, Florin Pop

description: This benchmark tests vision-language models on urban perception using Montreal street images with annotations across 30 dimensions mixing physical attributes and subjective impressions. It uniquely evaluates how well VLMs align with human perception of urban scenes compared to community groups' assessments.
paper_title: Do Vision-Language Models See Urban Scenes as People Do? An Urban Perception Benchmark
authors: Rashid Mushkani

description: PCMDE proposes a physics-based evaluation metric combining large language models with reasoning and vision-language models to assess semantic and structural accuracy in multimodal synthetic images. It addresses limitations of current measures like BLEU and CLIPScore for domain-specific scenarios through physics-guided reasoning.
paper_title: Physics-Based Benchmarking Metrics for Multimodal Synthetic Images
authors: Kishor Datta Gupta, Marufa Kamal, Md. Mahfuzur Rahman, Fahad Rahman, Mohd Ariful Haque

task_types: face verification, biometric attribute prediction, iris recognition, presentation attack detection
description: This benchmark evaluates zero-shot and few-shot performance of VLMs and MLLMs across six biometric tasks spanning face and iris modalities including face verification, soft biometric attribute prediction, iris recognition, and presentation attack detection. It demonstrates the potential of foundation models for diverse biometric applications.
paper_title: Benchmarking Foundation Models for Zero-Shot Biometric Tasks
authors: Redwan Sony, Parisa Farmanifard, Hamzeh Alzwairy, Nitish Shukla, Arun Ross

description: A robot-captured, multi-view anomaly detection dataset covering 13 everyday object categories with four realistic defect types. Captured from over 60 robot viewpoints per object under uncontrolled lighting to stress test pose variation and viewpoint-dependent defect visibility.
paper_title: RAD: A Dataset and Benchmark for Real-Life Anomaly Detection with Robotic Observations
authors: Kaichen Zhou, Xinhai Chang, Taewhan Kim, Jiadong Zhang, Yang Cao

description: An expert-annotated video advertisement dataset for detecting framing and greenwashing in oil & gas company communications. Features 13 framing types across 50+ companies and 20 countries, specifically designed for VLM evaluation.
paper_title: A Multimodal Benchmark for Framing of Oil & Gas Advertising and Potential Greenwashing Detection
authors: Gaku Morio, Harri Rowlands, Dominik Stammbach, Christopher D. Manning, Peter Henderson

description: The first publicly available video-to-text e-commerce Attribute Value Extraction dataset across 14 domains covering 172 unique attributes. Includes 224k training data and 25k evaluation data with CLIP-based filtering for quality assurance.
paper_title: VideoAVE: A Multi-Attribute Video-to-Text Attribute Value Extraction Dataset and Benchmark Models
authors: Ming Cheng, Tong Wu, Jiazhen Hu, Jiaying Gong, Hoda Eldardiry

task_types: image captioning, visual grounding, visual question answering
description: UWBench is the first comprehensive vision-language benchmark specifically designed for underwater environments. It features 15,003 underwater images with 15,281 referring expressions and 124,983 QA pairs covering marine ecosystems, addressing unique challenges like light attenuation, color distortion, and requiring specialized marine knowledge.
paper_title: UWBench: A Comprehensive Vision-Language Benchmark for Underwater Understanding
authors: Da Zhang, Chenggang Rong, Bingyu Li, Feiyu Wang, Zhiyuan Zhao

description: CaMMT evaluates culturally aware multimodal machine translation with over 5,800 triples of images and parallel captions in English and regional languages. It specifically tests whether visual context can help translate cultural content and handle Culturally-Specific Items that language alone cannot adequately convey.
paper_title: CaMMT: Benchmarking Culturally Aware Multimodal Machine Translation
authors: Emilio Villa-Cueva, Sholpan Bolatzhanova, Diana Turmakhan, Kareem Elzeky, Henok Biadglign Ademtew

task_types: image captioning, visual question answering, remote sensing analysis
description: SARLANG-1M is the first large-scale benchmark for SAR (Synthetic Aperture Radar) image understanding with vision-language integration. It comprises over 1 million SAR image-text pairs from 59+ cities with hierarchical resolutions, fine-grained semantic descriptions, and multi-task QA covering seven applications.
paper_title: SARLANG-1M: A Benchmark for Vision-Language Modeling in SAR Image Understanding
authors: Yimin Wei, Aoran Xiao, Yexian Ren, Yuting Zhu, Hongruixuan Chen

task_types: visual question answering, object counting, remote sensing analysis
description: RSVLM-QA is a large-scale VQA dataset for remote sensing with 13,820 images and 162,373 QA pairs. It features innovative dual-track annotation generation using GPT-4.1 for detailed annotations and a specialized automated process for object counting, addressing limitations in existing RS VQA datasets.
paper_title: RSVLM-QA: A Benchmark Dataset for Remote Sensing Vision Language Model-based Question Answering
authors: Xing Zi, Jinghao Xiao, Yunxiao Shi, Xian Tao, Jun Li

description: RGB-Th-Bench is the first benchmark for RGB-Thermal image pair understanding, covering 14 skill dimensions with 1,600+ expert-annotated questions. It addresses the critical gap in evaluating VLM capabilities on infrared vision tasks, using both standard and stricter skill-level accuracy metrics.
paper_title: RGB-Th-Bench: A Dense benchmark for Visual-Thermal Understanding of Vision Language Models
authors: Mehdi Moshtaghi, Siavash H. Khajavi, Joni Pajarinen

description: ConvoInsight-DB is a large-scale dataset for psychological analysis in conversations with expert annotations for micro-expressions and deep psychological inference. It addresses Articulatory-Affective Ambiguity and includes the PRISM evaluation metric for multidimensional performance assessment.
paper_title: Measuring the Unspoken: A Disentanglement Model and Benchmark for Psychological Analysis in the Wild
authors: Yigui Feng, Qinglin Wang, Haotian Mo, Yang Liu, Ke Liu

description: The paper introduces benchmarks for evaluating VLMs and LLMs on Christian Iconography classification using three datasets (ArtDL, ICONCLASS, Wikidata) filtered to top 10 most frequent classes. It tests zero-shot and few-shot performance across different prompt enrichment strategies.
paper_title: Benchmarking Vision-Language and Multimodal Large Language Models in Zero-shot and Few-shot Scenarios: A study on Christian Iconography
authors: Gianmarco Spinaci, Lukas Klic, Giovanni Colavizza

description: OODFace introduces three robustness benchmarks (LFW-C/V, CFP-FP-C/V, YTF-C/V) with 30 OOD scenarios across 9 categories for facial recognition. It systematically evaluates robustness under common corruptions and appearance variations with both digital and physical experiments.
paper_title: OODFace: Benchmarking Robustness of Face Recognition under Common Corruptions and Appearance Variations
authors: Caixin Kang, Yubo Chen, Shouwei Ruan, Shiji Zhao, Ruochen Zhang

description: SPoRC-VIST combines the Structured Podcast Research Corpus with the Visual Storytelling Dataset to evaluate generative natural narrative capabilities in VLMs. It uses synthetic-to-real training strategy and comprehensive evaluation beyond textual overlap metrics.
paper_title: SPoRC-VIST: A Benchmark for Evaluating Generative Natural Narrative in Vision-Language Models
authors: Yunlin Zeng

description: ImgEdit introduces a large-scale dataset of 1.2 million curated edit pairs and ImgEdit-Bench for comprehensive evaluation of image editing performance. It includes basic, challenging single-turn, and multi-turn test suites assessing instruction adherence, editing quality, and detail preservation.
paper_title: ImgEdit: A Unified Image Editing Dataset and Benchmark
authors: Yang Ye, Xianyi He, Zongjian Li, Bin Lin, Shenghai Yuan

task_types: traffic sign recognition, cross-region generalization, rare-class identification
description: TS-1M provides over one million real-world traffic sign images across 454 standardized categories with diagnostic challenges including cross-region recognition, rare-class identification, and semantic text understanding. It enables systematic assessment of traffic sign recognition capabilities across different modeling paradigms.
paper_title: Traffic Sign Recognition in Autonomous Driving: Dataset, Benchmark, and Field Experiment
authors: Guoyang Zhao, Weiqing Qi, Kai Zhang, Chenguang Zhang, Zeying Gong

description: VectorEdits is a large-scale dataset for instruction-guided vector image editing with over 270,000 pairs of SVG images paired with natural language edit instructions. It enables training and evaluation of models that modify vector graphics based on textual commands.
paper_title: VectorEdits: A Dataset and Benchmark for Instruction-Based Editing of Vector Graphics
authors: Josef Kuchař, Marek Kadlčík, Michal Spiegel, Michal Štefánik

description: RAER is the first dataset for academic emotion recognition comprising 2,700 video clips from natural learning contexts like classrooms and libraries. It captures diverse learning scenarios with context-aware emotion annotations, emphasizing the role of environmental context.
paper_title: Context-Aware Academic Emotion Dataset and Benchmark
authors: Luming Zhao, Jingwen Xuan, Jiamin Lou, Yonghui Yu, Wenwu Yang

task_types: fine-grained classification, OOD detection, historical artifact dating
description: ShiftedBronzes focuses on fine-grained bronze ware dating with two types of bronze ware data and seven types of OOD data exhibiting distribution shifts. It addresses domain-specific OOD challenges in the specialized domain of ancient Chinese history.
paper_title: ShiftedBronzes: Benchmarking and Analysis of Domain Fine-Grained Classification in Open-World Settings
authors: Rixin Zhou, Honglin Pang, Qian Zhang, Ruihua Qi, Xi Yang

description: The first large-scale benchmark dataset for Ground-to-Aerial Video-based person Re-Identification featuring drastic view changes, large number of annotated identities, rich outdoor scenarios, and huge differences in resolution across platforms.
paper_title: Cross-Platform Video Person ReID: A New Benchmark Dataset and Adaptation Approach
authors: Shizhou Zhang, Wenlong Luo, De Cheng, Qingchun Yang, Lingyan Ran

task_types: persona understanding, relational reasoning, temporal reasoning, aggregative reasoning
description: A comprehensive, synthetically generated multimodal benchmark built on simulated user digital footprints for advanced personalization study. It evaluates capabilities from persona understanding to complex reasoning over historical data, expanding far beyond prior benchmarks.
paper_title: A Benchmark and Knowledge-Grounded Framework for Advanced Multimodal Personalization Study
authors: Xia Hu, Honglei Zhuang, Brian Potetz, Alireza Fathi, Bo Hu

task_types: time series recognition, time series differentiation, time series description generation
description: The first benchmark dataset to assess whether models can produce generic visual descriptions of time series data. It proposes three new tasks for recognizing, differentiating, and generating language descriptions of time series across multiple modalities.
paper_title: BEDTime: A Unified Benchmark for Automatically Describing Time Series
authors: Medhasweta Sen, Zachary Gottesman, Jiaxing Qiu, C. Bayan Bruss, Nam Nguyen

task_types: acceleration evaluation, performance assessment, generalization testing
description: A unified framework for evaluating training-free acceleration techniques in Large Vision-Language Models. It assesses not only absolute performance but also generalization and loyalty while exploring Pareto-optimal trade-offs across diverse backbones and benchmarks.
paper_title: EffiVLM-BENCH: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Evaluating Training-Free Acceleration in Large Vision-Language Models
authors: Zekun Wang, Minghua Ma, Zexin Wang, Rongchuan Mu, Liping Shan

task_types: misinformation detection, multimodal grounding, segment localization
description: The first real-world dataset for Grounding Multimodal Misinformation featuring taxonomy of misinformation types, fine-grained annotations across text, speech, and visuals, with validation through Snopes evidence. It enables verification and localization of misleading segments across modalities.
paper_title: A New Dataset and Benchmark for Grounding Multimodal Misinformation
authors: Bingjian Yang, Danni Xu, Kaipeng Niu, Wenxuan Liu, Zheng Wang

task_types: sentiment analysis, emotion detection, affective intensity prediction
description: The first extensive open-source benchmark for multilingual multimodal affective analysis encompassing text, image, and video modalities across 35 languages. It covers four key affective analysis tasks: sentiment polarity, sentiment intensity, emotion classification, and emotion intensity.
paper_title: MMAFFBen: A Multilingual and Multimodal Affective Analysis Benchmark for Evaluating LLMs and VLMs
authors: Zhiwei Liu, Lingfei Qian, Qianqian Xie, Jimin Huang, Kailai Yang

modalities: natural world images, expert text queries
task_types: text-to-image retrieval, species identification, expert-level querying
description: A text-to-image retrieval benchmark with iNaturalist 2024 dataset and 250 expert-level queries for natural world images. It emphasizes tasks requiring nuanced image understanding and domain expertise with queries spanning species identification, context, behavior, and appearance.
paper_title: INQUIRE: A Natural World Text-to-Image Retrieval Benchmark
authors: Edward Vendrow, Omiros Pantazis, Alexander Shepard, Gabriel Brostow, Kate E. Jones

task_types: few-shot adaptation, remote sensing scene classification
description: The first structured benchmark for evaluating few-shot adaptation methods on Remote Sensing Vision-Language Models across ten scene classification datasets. It reveals that models with similar zero-shot performance can exhibit markedly different behavior under few-shot adaptation.
paper_title: Few-Shot Adaptation Benchmark for Remote Sensing Vision-Language Models
authors: Karim El Khoury, Maxime Zanella, Christophe De Vleeschouwer, Benoit Macq

description: Framework that disentangles prompt phrasing from visual generation capabilities through iterative prompt optimization. Uses multimodal optimization pipeline with vision-language model feedback to evaluate and improve text-to-image model assessment.
paper_title: ConceptMix++: Leveling the Playing Field in Text-to-Image Benchmarking via Iterative Prompt Optimization
authors: Haosheng Gan, Berk Tinaz, Mohammad Shahab Sepehri, Zalan Fabian, Mahdi Soltanolkotabi

description: First multimodal benchmark for comprehensive road damage understanding, pairing high-resolution road damage images with detailed textual descriptions. Provides richer contextual understanding for infrastructure monitoring applications.
paper_title: RoadBench: A Vision-Language Foundation Model and Benchmark for Road Damage Understanding
authors: Xi Xiao, Yunbei Zhang, Janet Wang, Lin Zhao, Yuxiang Wei

description: Benchmark for measuring reliability in image editing models using reference-based marketing/design edits with binary pass/fail judging. Estimates per-attempt pass rates and effective costs including model pricing and human review time to assess real-world deployment viability.
paper_title: HYPE-EDIT-1: Benchmark for Measuring Reliability in Frontier Image Editing Models
authors: Wing Chan, Richard Allen

description: Comprehensive benchmark with novel 40-category emotion taxonomy for synthetic emotion recognition, featuring AI-generated datasets with controlled demographic balance. Addresses limitations of existing benchmarks through nuanced emotional states and explicit, full-face expressions with expert annotations.
paper_title: EmoNet-Face: An Expert-Annotated Benchmark for Synthetic Emotion Recognition
authors: Christoph Schuhmann, Robert Kaczmarczyk, Gollam Rabby, Felix Friedrich, Maurice Kraus

description: First large-scale benchmark for complete musical score understanding across textual (ABC notation) and visual (PDF) modalities. Tests integrated reasoning over pitch, rhythm, harmony, and structure through four difficulty levels from classical composers including Bach, Beethoven, and Chopin.
paper_title: Musical Score Understanding Benchmark: Evaluating Large Language Models' Comprehension of Complete Musical Scores
authors: Congren Dai, Yue Yang, Krinos Li, Huichi Zhou, Shijie Liang

description: Crop Disease Domain Multimodal dataset with 137,000 disease images and 1 million question-answer pairs spanning agricultural knowledge from disease identification to management practices. Pioneering resource for advancing agricultural research through multimodal learning techniques.
paper_title: A Multimodal Benchmark Dataset and Model for Crop Disease Diagnosis
authors: Xiang Liu, Zhaoxiang Liu, Huan Hu, Zezhou Chen, Kohou Wang

description: Extensive Text-Image-Expression dataset spanning wide spectrum of human emotions paired with images and 3D blendshapes. Introduces new evaluation metric for 3D facial expression synthesis that measures conveyed emotion more directly than traditional MSE metrics.
paper_title: Emo3D: Metric and Benchmarking Dataset for 3D Facial Expression Generation from Emotion Description
authors: Mahshid Dehghani, Amirahmad Shafiee, Ali Shafiei, Neda Fallah, Farahmand Alizadeh

description: First benchmark designed for systematic evaluation of models across Mars-related tasks using orbital and surface imagery. Comprises 20 datasets spanning classification, segmentation, and object detection focused on key geologic features like craters, cones, boulders, and frost.
paper_title: Mars-Bench: A Benchmark for Evaluating Foundation Models for Mars Science Tasks
authors: Mirali Purohit, Bimal Gajera, Vatsal Malaviya, Irish Mehta, Kunal Kasodekar

description: INS-MMBench is the first hierarchical benchmark tailored for the insurance domain, encompassing 22 fundamental tasks, 12 meta-tasks, and 5 scenario tasks across 4 types of insurance (auto, property, health, agricultural). It enables comprehensive assessment from basic capabilities to real-world insurance use cases with rich multimodal data.
paper_title: INS-MMBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Evaluating LVLMs' Performance in Insurance
authors: Chenwei Lin, Hanjia Lyu, Xian Xu, Jiebo Luo

description: MME-Industry is a cross-industry evaluation benchmark covering 21 distinct industrial domains with 1050 manually crafted question-answer pairs validated by domain experts. It incorporates both non-OCR questions and tasks requiring specialized domain knowledge, available in both Chinese and English versions.
paper_title: MME-Industry: A Cross-Industry Multimodal Evaluation Benchmark
authors: Dongyi Yi, Guibo Zhu, Chenglin Ding, Zongshu Li, Dong Yi

description: HVSBench is the first large-scale benchmark designed to test MLLM alignment with human visual system (HVS) behaviors. It covers 13 categories across 5 key fields (Prominence, Subitizing, Prioritizing, Free-Viewing, Searching) to evaluate whether MLLMs exhibit human-like perceptual behaviors.
paper_title: Do MLLMs Exhibit Human-like Perceptual Behaviors? HVSBench: A Benchmark for MLLM Alignment with Human Perceptual Behavior
authors: Jiaying Lin, Shuquan Ye, Dan Xu, Wanli Ouyang, Rynson W. H. Lau

task_types: anomaly detection, defect analysis, industrial inspection
description: MMAD is the first comprehensive benchmark for MLLMs in industrial anomaly detection, featuring seven key subtasks across 39,672 questions for 8,366 industrial images. It systematically evaluates MLLMs' capabilities in detecting and analyzing industrial anomalies and defects across various manufacturing scenarios.
paper_title: MMAD: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Multimodal Large Language Models in Industrial Anomaly Detection
authors: Xi Jiang, Jian Li, Hanqiu Deng, Yong Liu, Bin-Bin Gao

task_types: cultural recognition, cultural conflict understanding, cultural content generation
description: C3B (Comics Cross-Cultural Benchmark) evaluates cultural awareness capabilities using comic-based scenarios with over 2000 images and 18000 QA pairs. It features three progressive difficulty tasks from basic visual recognition to cultural conflict understanding and content generation, addressing cross-lingual and multicultural challenges.
paper_title: Culture In a Frame: C$^3$B as a Comic-Based Benchmark for Multimodal Culturally Awareness
authors: Yuchen Song, Andong Chen, Wenxin Zhu, Kehai Chen, Xuefeng Bai

description: This benchmark systematically evaluates state-of-the-art MLLMs for face recognition on standard datasets (LFW, CALFW, CPLFW, CFP, AgeDB, RFW). It provides the first comprehensive comparison of open-source MLLMs with existing face recognition models using similar protocols and zero-shot evaluation.
paper_title: Benchmarking Multimodal Large Language Models for Face Recognition
authors: Hatef Otroshi Shahreza, Sébastien Marcel

description: UCVL is a benchmark for crime surveillance video analysis with large models, featuring 1,829 videos with reorganized annotations and six types of QA pairs. It uses GPT-4o for assessment and focuses on anomaly detection in surveillance videos.
paper_title: A Benchmark for Crime Surveillance Video Analysis with Large Models
authors: Haoran Chen, Dong Yi, Moyan Cao, Chensen Huang, Guibo Zhu

task_types: design comprehension, element recognition, style understanding
description: DesignProbe is a graphic design benchmark for MLLMs with eight tasks across fine-grained element level (color, font, layout) and overall design level (style, metaphor). It investigates MLLMs' capability to both recognize design elements and understand overall design.
paper_title: DesignProbe: A Graphic Design Benchmark for Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Jieru Lin, Danqing Huang, Tiejun Zhao, Dechen Zhan, Chin-Yew Lin

task_types: affordance perception, object understanding, contextual reasoning
description: A4Bench evaluates affordance perception abilities of MLLMs across Constitutive Affordance (inherent object properties) with 1,282 QA pairs and Transformative Affordance (dynamic/contextual nuances) with 718 QA pairs, spanning nine sub-disciplines.
paper_title: Affordance Benchmark for MLLMs
authors: Junying Wang, Wenzhe Li, Yalun Wu, Yingji Liang, Yijin Guo

description: MOSABench is a novel evaluation dataset for multi-object sentiment analysis, including approximately 1,000 images with multiple objects requiring independent sentiment assessment. It features distance-based target annotation and improved scoring mechanisms.
paper_title: MOSABench: Multi-Object Sentiment Analysis Benchmark for Evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models Understanding of Complex Image
authors: Shezheng Song, Chengxiang He, Shan Zhao, Chengyu Wang, Qian Wan

task_types: creative content generation, video comment art, cultural understanding
description: GODBench integrates video and text modalities to systematically evaluate MLLMs' abilities to compose creative Video Comment Art, requiring nuanced understanding of cultural and contextual subtleties for humor, satire, or emotional resonance.
paper_title: GODBench: A Benchmark for Multimodal Large Language Models in Video Comment Art
authors: Yiming Lei, Chenkai Zhang, Zeming Liu, Haitao Leng, Shaoguo Liu

description: Henna is a benchmark specifically designed for assessing MLLMs on aspects related to Arabic culture, serving as the first stone for culturally-aware Arabic MLLMs. It evaluates models on cultural understanding and dialectal capabilities in Arabic contexts.
paper_title: Peacock: A Family of Arabic Multimodal Large Language Models and Benchmarks
authors: Fakhraddin Alwajih, El Moatez Billah Nagoudi, Gagan Bhatia, Abdelrahman Mohamed, Muhammad Abdul-Mageed

description: This work introduces a fine-grained MLLM-as-a-Judge framework for image editing evaluation that decomposes evaluation into twelve interpretable factors spanning image preservation, edit quality, and instruction fidelity. It provides a new human-validated benchmark integrating human judgments with MLLM-based evaluations.
paper_title: Human-Aligned MLLM Judges for Fine-Grained Image Editing Evaluation: A Benchmark, Framework, and Analysis
authors: Runzhou Liu, Hailey Weingord, Sejal Mittal, Prakhar Dungarwal, Anusha Nandula

description: WildScore is the first in-the-wild multimodal symbolic music reasoning and analysis benchmark. Each instance is sourced from genuine musical compositions with authentic user-generated questions, evaluating MLLMs' capacity to interpret real-world music scores and answer complex musicological queries.
paper_title: WildScore: Benchmarking MLLMs in-the-Wild Symbolic Music Reasoning
authors: Gagan Mundada, Yash Vishe, Amit Namburi, Xin Xu, Zachary Novack

task_types: Scoring Evaluation, Pair Comparison, Batch Ranking
description: MLLM-as-a-Judge assesses the ability of MLLMs to serve as judges across diverse modalities, encompassing three distinct tasks: Scoring Evaluation, Pair Comparison, and Batch Ranking. It evaluates how well MLLMs can assess and rank outputs in multimodal contexts.
paper_title: MLLM-as-a-Judge: Assessing Multimodal LLM-as-a-Judge with Vision-Language Benchmark
authors: Dongping Chen, Ruoxi Chen, Shilin Zhang, Yinuo Liu, Yaochen Wang

description: ImplicitAVE is the first publicly available multimodal dataset for implicit attribute value extraction, sourced from the MAVE dataset but carefully curated to include implicit AVE and multimodality. It provides 68k training and 1.6k testing samples across five domains, addressing limitations in existing datasets that focus only on explicit attribute values.
paper_title: ImplicitAVE: An Open-Source Dataset and Multimodal LLMs Benchmark for Implicit Attribute Value Extraction
authors: Henry Peng Zou, Vinay Samuel, Yue Zhou, Weizhi Zhang, Liancheng Fang

description: MCITlib is a comprehensive library and benchmark for Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning that addresses both catastrophic forgetting and cross-modal coordination in MLLMs. It implements 8 representative algorithms and conducts evaluations on 3 benchmarks under 2 backbone models, focusing on continual learning challenges specific to multimodal models.
paper_title: MCITlib: Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning Library and Benchmark
authors: Haiyang Guo, Fei Zhu, Hongbo Zhao, Fanhu Zeng, Wenzhuo Liu

task_types: memory extraction, memory reasoning, memory knowledge management
description: Mem-Gallery is a new benchmark for evaluating multimodal long-term conversational memory in MLLM agents, featuring high-quality multi-session conversations grounded in both visual and textual information. It proposes a systematic evaluation framework that assesses memory capabilities along three functional dimensions: memory extraction and test-time adaptation, memory reasoning, and memory knowledge management.
paper_title: Mem-Gallery: Benchmarking Multimodal Long-Term Conversational Memory for MLLM Agents
authors: Yuanchen Bei, Tianxin Wei, Xuying Ning, Yanjun Zhao, Zhining Liu

task_types: vanilla name answering, entity-level caption, complex-scenario recognition, multi-step editing
description: MIKE is a comprehensive benchmark and dataset specifically designed for fine-grained multimodal entity knowledge editing. It encompasses tasks including Vanilla Name Answering, Entity-Level Caption, and Complex-Scenario Recognition, and introduces Multi-step Editing to evaluate editing efficiency, addressing the gap in fine-grained entity knowledge editing for MLLMs.
paper_title: MIKE: A New Benchmark for Fine-grained Multimodal Entity Knowledge Editing
authors: Jiaqi Li, Miaozeng Du, Chuanyi Zhang, Yongrui Chen, Nan Hu

task_types: emotion recognition, conversational emotion understanding, socially complex emotion analysis
description: EmoBench-M is a novel benchmark designed to evaluate the emotional intelligence (EI) capability of MLLMs across 13 evaluation scenarios from three key dimensions: foundational emotion recognition, conversational emotion understanding, and socially complex emotion analysis. It addresses limitations of existing static, text-based benchmarks by capturing dynamic, multimodal nature of emotional expressions.
paper_title: EmoBench-M: Benchmarking Emotional Intelligence for Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: He Hu, Yucheng Zhou, Lianzhong You, Hongbo Xu, Qianning Wang

description: EventBench is the first comprehensive benchmark for event-based MLLMs, offering eight diverse task metrics with a large-scale event stream dataset. It pioneers 3D spatial reasoning tasks for event-based vision and includes over one million event-text pairs for large-scale training and evaluation.
paper_title: EventBench: Towards Comprehensive Benchmarking of Event-based MLLMs
authors: Shaoyu Liu, Jianing Li, Guanghui Zhao, Yunjian Zhang, Xiangyang Ji

description: MMCircuitEval is the first multimodal benchmark specifically designed to assess MLLM performance across diverse Electronic Design Automation (EDA) tasks. It comprises 3614 QA pairs spanning digital and analog circuits across critical EDA stages from general knowledge to front-end and back-end design.
paper_title: MMCircuitEval: A Comprehensive Multimodal Circuit-Focused Benchmark for Evaluating LLMs
authors: Chenchen Zhao, Zhengyuan Shi, Xiangyu Wen, Chengjie Liu, Yi Liu

task_types: quality inspection, defect detection, defect localization
description: UniPCB is the first unified vision-language benchmark for open-ended PCB quality inspection. It addresses the unique challenges of PCB inspection including densely packed components, complex wiring structures, and subtle defect patterns requiring specialized domain expertise.
paper_title: UniPCB: A Unified Vision-Language Benchmark for Open-Ended PCB Quality Inspection
authors: Fuxiang Sun, Xi Jiang, Jiansheng Wu, Haigang Zhang, Feng Zheng

description: CineTechBench is a pioneering benchmark for cinematographic technique understanding and generation, founded on precise manual annotation by cinematography experts. It covers seven essential aspects including shot scale, angle, composition, camera movement, lighting, color, and focal length across 600+ annotated movie images and 120 movie clips.
paper_title: CineTechBench: A Benchmark for Cinematographic Technique Understanding and Generation
authors: Xinran Wang, Songyu Xu, Xiangxuan Shan, Yuxuan Zhang, Muxi Diao

task_types: missing modality completion, image-to-text generation, text-to-image generation
description: Proposes Missing Modality Product Completion Benchmark with two sub-benchmarks for evaluating MLLMs' ability to generate missing modalities in e-commerce scenarios. Focuses on image-to-text and text-to-image completion tasks across nine real-world e-commerce categories.
paper_title: Benchmarking Multimodal Large Language Models for Missing Modality Completion in Product Catalogues
authors: Junchen Fu, Wenhao Deng, Kaiwen Zheng, Ioannis Arapakis, Yu Ye

task_types: pairwise comparison, length bias detection, error detection
description: A ten-dimensional capability-oriented benchmark designed to assess judgment abilities of MLLMs through pairwise Chain-of-Thought comparison, length bias avoidance, and process error detection. What makes it unique is its decomposition of evaluation into fine-grained subtasks that diagnose model reliability across reasoning styles and response lengths.
paper_title: Advancing Multimodal Judge Models through a Capability-Oriented Benchmark and MCTS-Driven Data Generation
authors: Zeyu Chen, Huanjin Yao, Ziwang Zhao, Min Yang

description: The first benchmark for materials characterization image understanding, comprising questions that demand expert-level domain expertise across four key stages of materials research and 21 distinct tasks. Unique for focusing on real-world materials science characterization imaging data and processing-microstructure-property relationships.
paper_title: Can Multimodal LLMs See Materials Clearly? A Multimodal Benchmark on Materials Characterization
authors: Zhengzhao Lai, Youbin Zheng, Zhenyang Cai, Haonan Lyu, Jinpu Yang

description: A comprehensive benchmark comprising samples across 12 resolution levels and six core capability dimensions to assess resolution robustness of MLLMs. Unique for evaluating whether performance remains stable across varying input resolutions using novel robustness metrics like Spearman's correlation and Continuous Error measures.
paper_title: Res-Bench: Benchmarking the Robustness of Multimodal Large Language Models to Dynamic Resolution Input
authors: Chenxu Li, Zhicai Wang, Yuan Sheng, Xingyu Zhu, Yanbin Hao

task_types: emotion recognition, emotion reasoning, action prediction
description: A multi-turn multimodal emotion understanding and reasoning benchmark with 1,451 video data from real-life scenarios and 5,101 progressive questions covering emotion recognition, causes, and future action prediction. Unique for its focus on emotion reasoning rather than just recognition, with multi-turn progressive questioning.
paper_title: Beyond Emotion Recognition: A Multi-Turn Multimodal Emotion Understanding and Reasoning Benchmark
authors: Jinpeng Hu, Hongchang Shi, Chongyuan Dai, Zhuo Li, Peipei Song

description: A unified benchmark that isolates the multimodal routing problem for query-level model selection, providing controlled environment with modality-aware inputs and variable compute budgets. Unique for enabling comparison of routing policies under fixed candidate sets and cost models across OCR, VQA, and multimodal reasoning.
paper_title: MMR-Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Multimodal LLM Routing
authors: Haoxuan Ma, Guannan Lai, Han-Jia Ye

description: The largest emotional intelligence benchmark for MLLMs containing over 6,000 curated video clips spanning eight emotional tasks across broad scenarios. Unique for assessing both emotional understanding and reasoning capabilities with holistic evaluation suite including hybrid metrics analyzed through multi-agent system framework.
paper_title: MME-Emotion: A Holistic Evaluation Benchmark for Emotional Intelligence in Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Fan Zhang, Zebang Cheng, Chong Deng, Haoxuan Li, Zheng Lian

task_types: social scene understanding, social state reasoning, social dynamics prediction
description: A novel video benchmark for evaluating MLLMs across Social Scene Understanding, Social State Reasoning, and Social Dynamics Prediction with 2,792 video clips from TikTok and YouTube. Unique for its comprehensive analysis of social interaction understanding including impact of different textual cues and cultural backgrounds.
paper_title: SIV-Bench: A Video Benchmark for Social Interaction Understanding and Reasoning
authors: Fanqi Kong, Weiqin Zu, Xinyu Chen, Yaodong Yang, Song-Chun Zhu

task_types: compositional image generation evaluation, 3D-spatial relationship, numeracy
description: T2I-CompBench++ is an enhanced benchmark for compositional text-to-image generation comprising 8,000 compositional text prompts categorized into attribute binding, object relationships, generative numeracy, and complex compositions. It introduces new evaluation metrics including detection-based metrics for 3D-spatial relationships and MLLM-based analysis.
paper_title: T2I-CompBench++: An Enhanced and Comprehensive Benchmark for Compositional Text-to-image Generation
authors: Kaiyi Huang, Chengqi Duan, Kaiyue Sun, Enze Xie, Zhenguo Li

description: Novel video humor understanding benchmark featuring curated non-verbal short videos where humor can be appreciated through visual cues alone. Includes rich annotations and incorporates environmental sound analysis, with an open-ended QA task format.
paper_title: v-HUB: A Benchmark for Video Humor Understanding from Vision and Sound
authors: Zhengpeng Shi, Yanpeng Zhao, Jianqun Zhou, Yuxuan Wang, Qinrong Cui

task_types: object detection, scene understanding, spatial reasoning
description: Comprehensive benchmark for low-altitude UAV vision-language understanding comprising 43 test units across 10 tasks at image and region levels. Features real-world visual images with rich weather conditions and manual verification for high quality assessment of MLLMs in drone scenarios.
paper_title: UAVBench and UAVIT-1M: Benchmarking and Enhancing MLLMs for Low-Altitude UAV Vision-Language Understanding
authors: Yang Zhan, Yuan Yuan

description: Comprehensive benchmark for evaluating both T2I models and MLLMs in human emotion analysis. Features 7,145 portraits across six emotions generated by 12 T2I models, using progressive evaluation from coarse-grained to fine-grained metrics.
paper_title: MEMO-Bench: A Multiple Benchmark for Text-to-Image and Multimodal Large Language Models on Human Emotion Analysis
authors: Yingjie Zhou, Zicheng Zhang, Jiezhang Cao, Jun Jia, Yanwei Jiang

task_types: art analysis, cultural heritage understanding, symbolic interpretation
description: Large-scale VQA benchmark for cultural heritage domain constructed using multi-agent pipeline for nuanced question generation. Structured along visual understanding dimensions probing symbolic meaning, narratives, and complex visual relationships in art analysis.
paper_title: VQArt-Bench: A semantically rich VQA Benchmark for Art and Cultural Heritage
authors: A. Alfarano, L. Venturoli, D. Negueruela del Castillo

task_types: situation understanding, impact estimation, decision reasoning
description: First multimodal benchmark tailored to port operations under cyclone threat. Assembles 2,917 real-world disruption scenarios from 2015-2023 spanning 145 U.S. ports and 90 named storms, fusing multisource meteorological and operational data.
paper_title: CyPortQA: Benchmarking Multimodal Large Language Models for Cyclone Preparedness in Port Operation
authors: Chenchen Kuai, Chenhao Wu, Yang Zhou, Xiubin Bruce Wang, Tianbao Yang

description: A fictitious dataset benchmark for evaluating machine unlearning in MLLMs, featuring personal entities and event scenes. Addresses cross-concept interference challenges and evaluates unlearning of both person and event concepts simultaneously in multimodal settings.
paper_title: PEBench: A Fictitious Dataset to Benchmark Machine Unlearning for Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Zhaopan Xu, Pengfei Zhou, Weidong Tang, Jiaxin Ai, Wangbo Zhao

description: A bilingual VQA benchmark for assessing traditional Chinese culture understanding in MLLMs. Features culturally rich and visually diverse data from museum artifacts, daily scenes, and comics, with questions designed to avoid language bias by preventing direct disclosure of cultural concepts.
paper_title: TCC-Bench: Benchmarking the Traditional Chinese Culture Understanding Capabilities of MLLMs
authors: Pengju Xu, Yan Wang, Shuyuan Zhang, Xuan Zhou, Xin Li

description: Provides systematic cross-category evaluation for industrial anomaly detection with reflection-aware multimodal framework. Features multi-modal, multi-category, and multi-dimensional evaluation across fine-grained and structurally complex industrial scenarios.
paper_title: M3-AD: Reflection-aware Multi-modal, Multi-category, and Multi-dimensional Benchmark and Framework for Industrial Anomaly Detection
authors: Chao Huang, Yanhui Li, Yunkang Cao, Wei Wang, Hongxi Huang

task_types: signal comprehension, decision-making, analysis and planning
description: Evaluates last-mile subseasonal-to-seasonal climate services requiring multimodal understanding and decision-making under uncertainty. Covers 10 service products across six application domains with three service levels for climate resilience applications.
paper_title: S2SServiceBench: A Multimodal Benchmark for Last-Mile S2S Climate Services
authors: Chenyue Li, Wen Deng, Zhuotao Sun, Mengxi Jin, Hanzhe Cui

task_types: traffic rule reasoning, scene understanding, planning
description: Evaluates compositional traffic rule reasoning in autonomous driving through systematic Five-Level Cognitive Ladder. Uniquely focuses on multi-rule concurrency and conflicts rather than single-rule scenarios like traffic sign recognition.
paper_title: DriveCombo: Benchmarking Compositional Traffic Rule Reasoning in Autonomous Driving
authors: Enhui Ma, Jiahuan Zhang, Guantian Zheng, Tao Tang, Shengbo Eben Li

description: Evaluation framework for visual token compression methods that uses downsampling as discriminator to denoise existing benchmarks. Addresses task mismatch between general perception benchmarks and compression-specific evaluation needs.
paper_title: Are We Using the Right Benchmark: An Evaluation Framework for Visual Token Compression Methods
authors: Chenfei Liao, Wensong Wang, Zichen Wen, Xu Zheng, Yiyu Wang

task_types: urban development understanding, temporal change detection, subjective perception
description: Comprehensive benchmark for evaluating MLLMs in urban development understanding and subjective environmental perception across three progressive dimensions: Static Scene Perception, Temporal Change Understanding, and Subjective Environmental Perception. Uses multi-temporal single-view and panoramic street-view images from 11 representative cities worldwide with hybrid annotation pipeline.
paper_title: UrbanFeel: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Temporal and Perceptual Understanding of City Scenes through Human Perspective
authors: Jun He, Yi Lin, Zilong Huang, Jiacong Yin, Junyan Ye

task_types: symbol recognition, coordinate localization, bearing calculation, distance measurement, route legality, safety assessment, emergency planning
description: ENC-Bench is the first benchmark for Electronic Navigational Chart understanding, requiring specialized maritime expertise to interpret standardized vector symbols, scale-dependent rendering, and geometric structures. It evaluates three levels: perception, spatial reasoning, and maritime decision-making with safety-critical navigation tasks.
paper_title: ENC-Bench: A Benchmark for Evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models in Electronic Navigational Chart Understanding
authors: Ao Cheng, Xingming Li, Xuanyu Ji, Xixiang He, Qiyao Sun

description: TransportationGames is a comprehensive evaluation benchmark specifically designed to assess (M)LLMs' transportation domain knowledge across memorizing, understanding, and applying transportation knowledge. It comprehensively considers real-world transportation applications and uses Bloom's Taxonomy to structure evaluation tasks.
paper_title: TransportationGames: Benchmarking Transportation Knowledge of (Multimodal) Large Language Models
authors: Xue Zhang, Xiangyu Shi, Xinyue Lou, Rui Qi, Yufeng Chen

description: V2X-QA is a real-world dataset evaluating MLLMs across vehicle-side, infrastructure-side, and cooperative viewpoints in autonomous driving. It introduces a view-decoupled evaluation protocol with twelve-task taxonomy spanning perception, prediction, and reasoning, uniquely enabling systematic assessment across different driving perspectives.
paper_title: V2X-QA: A Comprehensive Reasoning Dataset and Benchmark for Multimodal Large Language Models in Autonomous Driving Across Ego, Infrastructure, and Cooperative Views
authors: Junwei You, Pei Li, Zhuoyu Jiang, Weizhe Tang, Zilin Huang

description: A comprehensive multimodal emotion understanding benchmark aggregating twelve datasets including IEMOCAP, MELD, DFEW, and MAFW into unified instruction format. Provides 130k training clips and 36k testing clips across 18 evaluation benchmarks with re-annotation via multi-agent pipeline for emotion recognition and reasoning.
paper_title: Emotion-LLaMAv2 and MMEVerse: A New Framework and Benchmark for Multimodal Emotion Understanding
authors: Xiaojiang Peng, Jingyi Chen, Zebang Cheng, Bao Peng, Fengyi Wu

task_types: circuit schematic perception, circuit analysis, circuit design
description: A comprehensive benchmark evaluating MLLM capabilities in Analog/Mixed-Signal circuits across circuit schematic perception, analysis, and design tasks. Addresses the gap in systematic assessment of MLLMs for AMS-related challenges spanning multiple difficulty levels in the IC industry domain.
paper_title: AMSbench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Evaluating MLLM Capabilities in AMS Circuits
authors: Yichen Shi, Ze Zhang, Hongyang Wang, Zhuofu Tao, Zhongyi Li

description: AgriBench is the first agriculture-specific benchmark for evaluating multimodal large language models on agricultural applications. It includes the MM-LUCAS dataset with landscape images, segmentation masks, depth maps, and detailed agricultural annotations based on the European Union's LUCAS land use survey.
paper_title: AgriBench: A Hierarchical Agriculture Benchmark for Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Yutong Zhou, Masahiro Ryo

description: CMMU is a Chinese multimodal benchmark covering domain-specific knowledge from primary to high school across 7 subjects. It uniquely includes multiple question types (multiple-choice, multiple-response, fill-in-the-blank) beyond just multiple-choice, and introduces Positional Error Variance evaluation strategy to assess position bias.
paper_title: CMMU: A Benchmark for Chinese Multi-modal Multi-type Question Understanding and Reasoning
authors: Zheqi He, Xinya Wu, Pengfei Zhou, Richeng Xuan, Guang Liu

description: EEmo-Bench evaluates multimodal large language models on understanding image-evoked emotions using Valence-Arousal-Dominance attributes and emotion ranking strategies. It includes four evaluation tasks (Perception, Ranking, Description, Assessment) and introduces image-pairwise analysis for comparative emotion assessment across diverse content categories.
paper_title: EEmo-Bench: A Benchmark for Multi-modal Large Language Models on Image Evoked Emotion Assessment
authors: Lancheng Gao, Ziheng Jia, Yunhao Zeng, Wei Sun, Yiming Zhang

description: FPBench is the first comprehensive benchmark for evaluating multimodal large language models on fingerprint analysis across 8 biometric and forensic tasks. It evaluates 20 MLLMs across 7 real and synthetic datasets using zero-shot and chain-of-thought prompting, establishing the foundation for fingerprint domain understanding with MLLMs.
paper_title: FPBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark of Multimodal Large Language Models for Fingerprint Analysis
authors: Ekta Balkrishna Gavas, Sudipta Banerjee, Chinmay Hegde, Nasir Memon

description: J-EDI QA is a specialized benchmark for understanding deep-sea organisms using multimodal LLMs, based on JAMSTEC's deep-sea image archive. It provides questions and answers in Japanese for assessing deep-sea species comprehension, representing a unique domain-specific benchmark for marine biology applications.
paper_title: J-EDI QA: Benchmark for deep-sea organism-specific multimodal LLM
authors: Takero Yoshida, Yuikazu Ito, Yoshihiro Fujiwara, Shinji Tsuchida, Daisuke Sugiyama

task_types: image captioning, activity understanding, educational assessment
description: ECAC is a large-scale benchmark for Early Childhood Education daily activity image captioning, comprising 256,121 real-world images annotated with expert-level captions and fine-grained labels. It includes a domain-oriented evaluation protocol (Teaching Toy Recognition Score) to measure professional object naming accuracy in ECE scenarios.
paper_title: Captioning Daily Activity Images in Early Childhood Education: Benchmark and Algorithm
authors: Sixing Li, Zhibin Gu, Ziqi Zhang, Weiguo Pan, Bing Li

task_types: sentiment analysis, sextuple extraction, sentiment flipping analysis, conversational analysis
description: PanoSent introduces multimodal conversational aspect-based sentiment analysis with two novel subtasks: Panoptic Sentiment Sextuple Extraction (recognizing holder, target, aspect, opinion, sentiment, rationale) and Sentiment Flipping Analysis (detecting dynamic sentiment transformation). It features high quality, large scale, multimodal, multilingual data across multiple scenarios.
paper_title: PanoSent: A Panoptic Sextuple Extraction Benchmark for Multimodal Conversational Aspect-based Sentiment Analysis
authors: Meng Luo, Hao Fei, Bobo Li, Shengqiong Wu, Qian Liu

task_types: multiple choice questions, cultural understanding, sports knowledge, history-based, rule-based, scenario-based
description: CultSportQA assesses LMs' understanding of traditional sports across 60 countries and 6 continents with 33,000 MCQs across text and image modalities. It encompasses four distinct cultural categories and features three key question types: history-based, rule-based, and scenario-based, establishing a new standard for multicultural sports understanding.
paper_title: Let's Play Across Cultures: A Large Multilingual, Multicultural Benchmark for Assessing Language Models' Understanding of Sports
authors: Punit Kumar Singh, Nishant Kumar, Akash Ghosh, Kunal Pasad, Khushi Soni

description: First multimodal dataset for Weather and Climate Event Forecasting (WCEF) that aligns environmental news articles with ERA5 meteorological raster data. Tests ability to predict weather and climate events by combining numerical meteorological variables with textual event narratives.
paper_title: CLLMate: A Multimodal Benchmark for Weather and Climate Events Forecasting
authors: Haobo Li, Zhaowei Wang, Jiachen Wang, Yueya Wang, Alexis Kai Hon Lau

benchmark_name: The New Yorker Cartoon Caption Contest Dataset
description: Massive-scale multimodal preference dataset for creative humor generation with over 250 million human ratings on cartoon captions. Introduces novel benchmarks for evaluating AI-generated humorous captions using both GPT4 and human judgments with ranking-based strategies.
paper_title: Humor in AI: Massive Scale Crowd-Sourced Preferences and Benchmarks for Cartoon Captioning
authors: Jifan Zhang, Lalit Jain, Yang Guo, Jiayi Chen, Kuan Lok Zhou

description: First benchmark to systematically evaluate MLLMs' multimodal planning capabilities with complex constraints across three real-world tasks (Flight, Calendar, Meeting Planning). Features graded difficulty levels and tests constraint-aware reasoning with budget, temporal, and spatial constraints.
paper_title: MPCC: A Novel Benchmark for Multimodal Planning with Complex Constraints in Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Yiyan Ji, Haoran Chen, Qiguang Chen, Chengyue Wu, Libo Qin

description: Multi-Modal Tamper Tracing dataset for interpretable facial image forgery localization featuring deepfake-manipulated facial images paired with manual textual annotations. Focuses on generating salient region-focused interpretations rather than basic binary forgery masks.
paper_title: A Large-scale Interpretable Multi-modality Benchmark for Facial Image Forgery Localization
authors: Jingchun Lian, Lingyu Liu, Yaxiong Wang, Yujiao Wu, Li Zhu

description: First Chain-of-Thought benchmark specifically designed for autonomous driving under adverse weather and complex scenes. Features over 5.4k manually annotated CoT instances with atomic ground truth for each reasoning step, enabling fine-grained analysis under various visual prompts.
paper_title: AD^2-Bench: A Hierarchical CoT Benchmark for MLLM in Autonomous Driving under Adverse Conditions
authors: Zhaoyang Wei, Chenhui Qiang, Bowen Jiang, Xumeng Han, Xuehui Yu

description: Bird species identification benchmark that requires systems to either answer with a species or abstain with evidence-based rationales (requires vocalization, low quality image, view obstructed). Tests abstention-aware fine-grained recognition by including both answerable and unanswerable examples with labeled rationales.
paper_title: RealBirdID: Benchmarking Bird Species Identification in the Era of MLLMs
authors: Logan Lawrence, Mustafa Chasmai, Rangel Daroya, Wuao Liu, Seoyun Jeong

task_types: visual question answering, cultural heritage reasoning
description: VaseVQA introduces expert-level cultural heritage understanding evaluation with 31,773 images and 67,614 QA pairs across seven expert-defined categories for ancient Greek pottery. It enables systematic evaluation of domain-specific reasoning that requires expert-level knowledge, addressing the challenge of limited domain-specific data for cultural heritage artifacts.
paper_title: VaseVQA: Multimodal Agent and Benchmark for Ancient Greek Pottery
authors: Jinchao Ge, Tengfei Cheng, Biao Wu, Zeyu Zhang, Shiya Huang

description: RoadSceneVQA is a large-scale VQA dataset specifically tailored for roadside scenarios with 34,736 diverse QA pairs under varying conditions. It targets not only object attributes but also intent, legality, and interaction patterns of traffic participants, challenging models to perform both explicit recognition and implicit commonsense reasoning grounded in traffic rules.
paper_title: RoadSceneVQA: Benchmarking Visual Question Answering in Roadside Perception Systems for Intelligent Transportation System
authors: Runwei Guan, Rongsheng Hu, Shangshu Chen, Ningyuan Xiao, Xue Xia

task_types: road event understanding, video question answering
description: RoadSocial captures global complexity of road events with varied geographies and camera viewpoints (CCTV, handheld, drones), derived from social media videos spanning 14M frames. It includes 13.2K videos with 260K QA pairs across 12 challenging tasks, addressing regional bias and expert-driven annotation limitations of existing datasets.
paper_title: RoadSocial: A Diverse VideoQA Dataset and Benchmark for Road Event Understanding from Social Video Narratives
authors: Chirag Parikh, Deepti Rawat, Rakshitha R. T., Tathagata Ghosh, Ravi Kiran Sarvadevabhatla

description: UVLM introduces the first underwater observation benchmark covering 419 classes of marine animals and various underwater challenges like light variations and water turbidity. It includes 20 distinct task types categorized into biological and environmental observations with content and change/action observation subtasks.
paper_title: UVLM: Benchmarking Video Language Model for Underwater World Understanding
authors: Xizhe Xue, Yang Zhou, Dawei Yan, Lijie Tao, Junjie Li

description: OVIS focuses on occluded video instance segmentation, providing a large-scale dataset for simultaneously detecting, segmenting, and tracking instances in heavily occluded scenes. It addresses the challenging scenario of understanding objects under occlusion that current systems struggle with.
paper_title: Occluded Video Instance Segmentation: A Benchmark
authors: Jiyang Qi, Yan Gao, Yao Hu, Xinggang Wang, Xiaoyu Liu

description: SPACT18 is the first video action recognition dataset using spike cameras alongside synchronized RGB and thermal modalities. It provides a unique platform for exploring multimodal video understanding and enables direct comparison of spiking, thermal, and RGB modalities for energy-efficient action recognition.
paper_title: SPACT18: Spiking Human Action Recognition Benchmark Dataset with Complementary RGB and Thermal Modalities
authors: Yasser Ashraf, Ahmed Sharshar, Velibor Bojkovic, Bin Gu

description: ERATO is a large-scale multi-modal dataset for Pairwise Emotional Relationship Recognition (PERR) task with 31,182 video clips from dramas and movies. It contains interaction-centric videos with multi-shots, varied video length, and multiple modalities including visual, audio and text to recognize emotional relationships between interactive characters.
paper_title: Pairwise Emotional Relationship Recognition in Drama Videos: Dataset and Benchmark
authors: Xun Gao, Yin Zhao, Jie Zhang, Longjun Cai

description: This benchmark addresses low-bitrate video understanding with three downstream tasks on eight datasets. It introduces a traditional-neural mixed coding framework that preserves semantic representation of videos while being transportation-efficient, focusing on video analysis tasks under compression constraints.
paper_title: A Coding Framework and Benchmark towards Low-Bitrate Video Understanding
authors: Yuan Tian, Guo Lu, Yichao Yan, Guangtao Zhai, Li Chen

task_types: commercial intent reasoning, video question answering
description: E-VAds is the first benchmark specifically designed for e-commerce short video understanding, featuring 3,961 high-quality videos from Taobao with 19,785 Q&A pairs. It addresses the reasoning of commercial intent in goal-driven format videos with dense multi-modal signals, organized into Perception and Cognition and Reasoning dimensions.
paper_title: E-VAds: An E-commerce Short Videos Understanding Benchmark for MLLMs
authors: Xianjie Liu, Yiman Hu, Liang Wu, Ping Hu, Yixiong Zou

description: UTD (Unbiased through Textual Description) creates unbiased subsets of existing video classification and retrieval datasets to enable robust assessment of video understanding capabilities. It tackles representation biases like object bias and single-frame bias through VLM and LLM analysis across concept, temporal, and common sense dimensions.
paper_title: Unbiasing through Textual Descriptions: Mitigating Representation Bias in Video Benchmarks
authors: Nina Shvetsova, Arsha Nagrani, Bernt Schiele, Hilde Kuehne, Christian Rupprecht

task_types: video generation, conditional video generation, video understanding
description: PAI-Bench is a unified benchmark for Physical AI that evaluates perception and prediction capabilities across video generation, conditional video generation, and video understanding. It comprises 2,808 real-world cases with task-aligned metrics designed to capture physical plausibility and domain-specific reasoning.
paper_title: PAI-Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark For Physical AI
authors: Fengzhe Zhou, Jiannan Huang, Jialuo Li, Deva Ramanan, Humphrey Shi

task_types: video question answering, object captioning, object grounding
description: TUMTraffic-VideoQA is a benchmark for unified spatio-temporal video understanding in traffic scenes with 1,000 videos, 85,000 multiple-choice QA pairs, 2,300 object captioning, and 5,700 object grounding annotations. It incorporates tuple-based spatio-temporal object expressions and addresses diverse real-world conditions like adverse weather and traffic anomalies.
paper_title: TUMTraffic-VideoQA: A Benchmark for Unified Spatio-Temporal Video Understanding in Traffic Scenes
authors: Xingcheng Zhou, Konstantinos Larintzakis, Hao Guo, Walter Zimmer, Mingyu Liu

description: VDC is a video detailed captioning benchmark with over one thousand carefully annotated structured captions, addressing the limitation of existing video caption benchmarks that only include simple descriptions. It introduces VDCscore, a new LLM-assisted evaluation metric using divide-and-conquer strategy.
paper_title: AuroraCap: Efficient, Performant Video Detailed Captioning and a New Benchmark
authors: Wenhao Chai, Enxin Song, Yilun Du, Chenlin Meng, Vashisht Madhavan

description: COM Kitchens provides unedited overhead-view videos captured by smartphones of food preparation based on recipes. It introduces the novel Online Recipe Retrieval (OnRR) task for video-to-text retrieval and Dense Video Captioning on unedited Overhead-View videos (DVC-OV), addressing environmental diversity in fixed-viewpoint datasets.
paper_title: COM Kitchens: An Unedited Overhead-view Video Dataset as a Vision-Language Benchmark
authors: Koki Maeda, Tosho Hirasawa, Atsushi Hashimoto, Jun Harashima, Leszek Rybicki

task_types: object segmentation, object tracking, super-voxel segmentation
description: UVO (Unidentified Video Objects) is a benchmark for open-world class-agnostic object segmentation in videos, significantly larger than existing datasets with 8 times more videos than DAVIS and 7 times more mask annotations per video than YouTube-VOS. It focuses on novel object categories never seen during training with crowded scenes and complex background motions.
paper_title: Unidentified Video Objects: A Benchmark for Dense, Open-World Segmentation
authors: Weiyao Wang, Matt Feiszli, Heng Wang, Du Tran

description: LoVR is a benchmark specifically designed for long video-text retrieval containing 467 long videos and over 40,804 fine-grained clips with high-quality captions. It introduces an efficient caption generation framework integrating VLM automatic generation, caption quality scoring, and dynamic refinement to overcome poor machine-generated annotations.
paper_title: LoVR: A Benchmark for Long Video Retrieval in Multimodal Contexts
authors: Qifeng Cai, Hao Liang, Zhaoyang Han, Hejun Dong, Meiyi Qiang

description: NuScenes-QA is the first VQA benchmark for autonomous driving scenarios, incorporating multi-modal data (images and LiDAR point clouds), multi-frame sequences, and dynamic outdoor scenes with both moving foreground and static background elements.
paper_title: NuScenes-QA: A Multi-modal Visual Question Answering Benchmark for Autonomous Driving Scenario
authors: Tianwen Qian, Jingjing Chen, Linhai Zhuo, Yang Jiao, Yu-Gang Jiang

task_types: visual question answering, plant disease diagnosis
description: PlantVillageVQA is a large-scale VQA dataset for agricultural decision-making covering 14 crop species and 38 disease conditions. Questions are organized into 3 cognitive complexity levels and 9 categories, designed to enhance diagnostic accuracy for plant disease identification.
paper_title: PlantVillageVQA: A Visual Question Answering Dataset for Benchmarking Vision-Language Models in Plant Science
authors: Syed Nazmus Sakib, Nafiul Haque, Mohammad Zabed Hossain, Shifat E. Arman

task_types: classification, object detection, semantic segmentation, vision-language question answering
description: PaveBench provides a comprehensive evaluation framework for pavement distress perception and interactive vision-language analysis on real-world highway inspection images. It supports four core tasks and includes PaveVQA dataset for single-turn, multi-turn, and expert-corrected interactions covering recognition, localization, quantitative estimation, and maintenance reasoning.
paper_title: PaveBench: A Versatile Benchmark for Pavement Distress Perception and Interactive Vision-Language Analysis
authors: Dexiang Li, Zhenning Che, Haijun Zhang, Dongliang Zhou, Zhao Zhang

description: Traffic-VQA is the first large-scale optical-thermal infrared benchmark for cognitive UAV traffic understanding, comprising 8,180 aligned image pairs and 1.3 million question-answer pairs across 31 diverse types. It enables evaluation of traffic scene understanding with regulatory knowledge embedding and multi-modal (optical-thermal) reasoning capabilities.
paper_title: UAV traffic scene understanding: A regulation embedded multi-modal network and a unified benchmark
authors: Yu Zhang, Zhicheng Zhao, Ze Luo, Chenglong Li, Jin Tang

description: MME-Finance is a bilingual open-ended VQA benchmark for financial multimodal understanding, featuring unique graphical images like candlestick charts and technical indicators. It includes questions annotated by experts with 10+ years financial experience and introduces visual information in the multi-modal evaluation process with custom-designed financial evaluation system.
paper_title: MME-Finance: A Multimodal Finance Benchmark for Expert-level Understanding and Reasoning
authors: Ziliang Gan, Yu Lu, Dong Zhang, Haohan Li, Che Liu

task_types: object recognition, event recognition, object counting, object localization, background analysis, event reasoning
description: MITS is the first large-scale multimodal benchmark for Intelligent Traffic Surveillance with 170,400 real-world ITS images from traffic surveillance cameras. It generates 5 million instruction-following VQA pairs addressing five critical ITS tasks: object/event recognition, object counting, object localization, background analysis, and event reasoning under diverse environmental conditions.
paper_title: MITS: A Large-Scale Multimodal Benchmark Dataset for Intelligent Traffic Surveillance
authors: Kaikai Zhao, Zhaoxiang Liu, Peng Wang, Xin Wang, Zhicheng Ma

task_types: citation-grounded report generation, multimodal evidence synthesis
description: Evaluates multimodal deep research agents on citation-rich report generation using image-text bundles. Emphasizes end-to-end multimodal evidence use and consistency across narrative, citations, and visual references.
paper_title: MMDeepResearch-Bench: A Benchmark for Multimodal Deep Research Agents
authors: Peizhou Huang, Zixuan Zhong, Zhongwei Wan, Donghao Zhou, Samiul Alam

modalities: text, images, various visual data types
task_types: multimodal retrieval, knowledge-intensive retrieval, reasoning-intensive retrieval
description: Dual-axis multimodal retrieval benchmark analyzing retrieval from knowledge domains (5 domains, 17 subtypes) and reasoning skills (6 categories). Features professional knowledge and complex reasoning with 16 heterogeneous visual data types.
paper_title: ARK: A Dual-Axis Multimodal Retrieval Benchmark along Reasoning and Knowledge
authors: Yijie Lin, Guofeng Ding, Haochen Zhou, Haobin Li, Mouxing Yang

task_types: text-to-text retrieval, multimodal-to-text retrieval, multimodal-to-image retrieval, multimodal-to-multimodal retrieval
description: First multimodal benchmark for reasoning-intensive retrieval with real-world queries containing diagrams, charts, and screenshots. Features four tasks of increasing complexity across 29 technical domains requiring intensive reasoning.
paper_title: MM-BRIGHT: A Multi-Task Multimodal Benchmark for Reasoning-Intensive Retrieval
authors: Abdelrahman Abdallah, Mohamed Darwish Mounis, Mahmoud Abdalla, Mahmoud SalahEldin Kasem, Mostafa Farouk Senussi

task_types: deep research, synthesis quality, factuality verification, process evaluation
description: Benchmark and evaluation framework for deep research systems assessing both process and outcome. Features live/evolving setting with periodic updates and three-dimensional evaluation including process-centric auditing.
paper_title: MiroEval: Benchmarking Multimodal Deep Research Agents in Process and Outcome
authors: Fangda Ye, Yuxin Hu, Pengxiang Zhu, Yibo Li, Ziqi Jin

task_types: graph learning, multimodal node classification, graph reasoning
description: First comprehensive benchmark incorporating visual and textual information into graph learning tasks. Extends beyond text-attributed graphs to assess multimodal graph learning frameworks in complex environments.
paper_title: Mosaic of Modalities: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Multimodal Graph Learning
authors: Jing Zhu, Yuhang Zhou, Shengyi Qian, Zhongmou He, Tong Zhao

description: Comprehensive benchmark for Multimodal Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Generation spanning three domains. Enables evaluation of models generating both text and image answers using multimodal corpus data.
paper_title: MRAMG-Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Advancing Multimodal Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Generation
authors: Qinhan Yu, Zhiyou Xiao, Binghui Li, Zhengren Wang, Chong Chen

description: Comprehensive benchmark for image-to-image editing models featuring 10 task categories across single-image and multi-image editing tasks. Uses 30 decoupled evaluation dimensions with automated hybrid evaluation methods integrating specialized tools and large multimodal models.
paper_title: I2I-Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark Suite for Image-to-Image Editing Models
authors: Juntong Wang, Jiarui Wang, Huiyu Duan, Jiaxiang Kang, Guangtao Zhai

description: High-fidelity multimodal benchmark for time series forecasting built from live APIs to address data contamination and temporal leakage issues. Focuses on data sourcing integrity, leak-free and causally sound design, and structural clarity.
paper_title: Fidel-TS: A High-Fidelity Multimodal Benchmark for Time Series Forecasting
authors: Zhijian Xu, Wanxu Cai, Xilin Dai, Zhaorong Deng, Qiang Xu

description: First expert-annotated multilingual (five languages) and multimodal benchmark for evaluating LLMs in realistic financial contexts. Introduces multilingual financial reasoning and financial OCR task families with structured, difficulty-aware selection.
paper_title: MultiFinBen: Benchmarking Large Language Models for Multilingual and Multimodal Financial Application
authors: Xueqing Peng, Lingfei Qian, Yan Wang, Ruoyu Xiang, Yueru He

task_types: uncertainty quantification, multimodal classification, robustness evaluation
description: LUMA extends CIFAR 10/100 with audio and text data to create a unique multimodal dataset for learning from uncertain data. It enables controlled injection of varying types and degrees of uncertainty across modalities and includes out-of-distribution samples for benchmarking trustworthy multimodal approaches.
paper_title: LUMA: A Benchmark Dataset for Learning from Uncertain and Multimodal Data
authors: Grigor Bezirganyan, Sana Sellami, Laure Berti-Équille, Sébastien Fournier

task_types: strategic planning, tactical adaptation, real-time decision making
description: TowerMind is a tower defense game environment for evaluating LLMs' long-term planning and decision-making capabilities with low computational demands and multimodal observation space. It supports evaluation of model hallucination and provides high customizability while preserving key RTS game evaluation strengths.
paper_title: TowerMind: A Tower Defence Game Learning Environment and Benchmark for LLM as Agents
authors: Dawei Wang, Chengming Zhou, Di Zhao, Xinyuan Liu, Marci Chi Ma

task_types: drawing understanding, cross-sheet reasoning, project coordination
description: AEC-Bench evaluates agentic systems on real-world Architecture, Engineering, and Construction tasks requiring drawing understanding, cross-sheet reasoning, and construction project-level coordination. It addresses domain-specific challenges in the AEC industry with multimodal evaluation scenarios.
paper_title: AEC-Bench: A Multimodal Benchmark for Agentic Systems in Architecture, Engineering, and Construction
authors: Harsh Mankodiya, Chase Gallik, Theodoros Galanos, Andriy Mulyar

task_types: graph learning, cross-modal reasoning, structural analysis
description: OpenMAG integrates 19 datasets across 6 domains with 16 encoders supporting both static and trainable feature encoding for Multimodal-Attributed Graph learning. It implements 24 state-of-the-art models and supports 8 downstream tasks, enabling systematic assessment of necessity, data quality, effectiveness, robustness, and efficiency.
paper_title: OpenMAG: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Multimodal-Attributed Graph
authors: Chenxi Wan, Xunkai Li, Yilong Zuo, Haokun Deng, Sihan Li

description: AFBench is a large-scale benchmark for airfoil inverse design featuring 200,000 airfoils with high-quality aerodynamic and geometric labels. It introduces novel multimodal airfoil inverse design tasks including conditional generation and controllable editing, bridging academic research and industrial applications.
paper_title: AFBench: A Large-scale Benchmark for Airfoil Design
authors: Jian Liu, Jianyu Wu, Hairun Xie, Guoqing Zhang, Jing Wang

description: MUOT_3M is the first pseudo multimodal underwater object tracking benchmark comprising 3 million frames from 3,030 videos with synchronized RGB, enhanced RGB, depth, and language modalities. It addresses the scarcity of large, diverse underwater tracking datasets with comprehensive annotations including 32 tracking attributes and 677 fine-grained classes.
paper_title: MUOT_3M: A 3 Million Frame Multimodal Underwater Benchmark and the MUTrack Tracking Method
authors: Ahsan Baidar Bakht, Mohamad Alansari, Muhayy Ud Din, Muzammal Naseer, Sajid Javed

description: UniPruneBench is a unified benchmark for visual token pruning in multimodal LLMs that provides standardized protocols across six ability dimensions and ten datasets. It evaluates ten compression algorithms across three LMM families with both task accuracy and system-level metrics like runtime and latency.
paper_title: Can Visual Input Be Compressed? A Visual Token Compression Benchmark for Large Multimodal Models
authors: Tianfan Peng, Yuntao Du, Pengzhou Ji, Shijie Dong, Kailin Jiang

description: eProduct is a million-scale dataset for product recognition and visual search with 2.5 million product images, addressing super fine-grained recognition challenges in e-commerce. It includes 1.3M+ training images with titles and hierarchical labels, plus 10,000 query and 1.1M index images for visual search evaluation.
paper_title: eProduct: A Million-Scale Visual Search Benchmark to Address Product Recognition Challenges
authors: Jiangbo Yuan, An-Ti Chiang, Wen Tang, Antonio Haro

description: XMPIE is a parallel multilingual and multimodal dataset of potentially idiomatic expressions spanning 34 languages and over 10,000 items. It enables comparative analysis of idiomatic patterns across languages and modalities, with each PIE accompanied by five images representing a spectrum from idiomatic to literal meanings.
paper_title: A Parallel Cross-Lingual Benchmark for Multimodal Idiomaticity Understanding
authors: Dilara Torunoğlu-Selamet, Dogukan Arslan, Rodrigo Wilkens, Wei He, Doruk Eryiğit

task_types: multimodal biometric fusion, quality-dependent evaluation, cost-sensitive evaluation
description: This is the first benchmark for multimodal biometric fusion algorithm comparison, designed within the BioSecure DS2 evaluation campaign. It features quality-dependent and cost-sensitive evaluations for face, fingerprint, and iris biometrics, addressing realistic scenarios with device changes and system failures.
paper_title: Benchmarking Quality-Dependent and Cost-Sensitive Score-Level Multimodal Biometric Fusion Algorithms
authors: Norman Poh, Thirimachos Bourlai, Josef Kittler, Lorene Allano, Fernando Alonso-Fernandez

task_types: issue resolution, code generation, multimodal understanding
description: First multilingual, multimodal, and multi-domain GitHub issue resolution benchmark covering 4 programming languages and 8 domains. Includes multimodal information such as images in issues, addressing limitations of text-only benchmarks.
paper_title: OmniGIRL: A Multilingual and Multimodal Benchmark for GitHub Issue Resolution
authors: Lianghong Guo, Wei Tao, Runhan Jiang, Yanlin Wang, Jiachi Chen

task_types: newswriting, narrative perspective identification, information retrieval, article generation
description: Benchmark evaluating multimodal agents as journalists with real-world newswriting tasks requiring iterative planning and contextual reasoning. Tests narrative perspective identification, keyword querying, historical background retrieval, and complete article generation.
paper_title: NEWSAGENT: Benchmarking Multimodal Agents as Journalists with Real-World Newswriting Tasks
authors: Yen-Che Chien, Kuang-Da Wang, Wei-Yao Wang, Wen-Chih Peng

task_types: image generation, video generation, dialect robustness evaluation
description: DialectGen is a large-scale benchmark spanning six common English dialects to evaluate multimodal generative models' robustness to dialectal textual input. It uniquely focuses on testing whether models can effectively produce content when given prompts containing dialect variations, rather than standard English.
paper_title: DialectGen: Benchmarking and Improving Dialect Robustness in Multimodal Generation
authors: Yu Zhou, Sohyun An, Haikang Deng, Da Yin, Clark Peng

task_types: text-to-image generation, text embedding evaluation, visual text generation
description: TextInVision is a large-scale benchmark designed to evaluate diffusion models' ability to accurately embed text within generated images. It considers various text attributes and prompt complexity to assess spelling accuracy, contextual relevance, and visual coherence in text-to-image generation.
paper_title: TextInVision: Text and Prompt Complexity Driven Visual Text Generation Benchmark
authors: Forouzan Fallah, Maitreya Patel, Agneet Chatterjee, Vlad I. Morariu, Chitta Baral

task_types: workflow documentation, knowledge transfer, process improvement, workflow validation
description: WONDERBREAD is the first benchmark for evaluating multimodal foundation models on business process management (BPM) tasks beyond automation. It features documented workflow demonstrations and 6 novel BPM tasks sourced from real-world applications, focusing on workflow documentation, knowledge transfer, and process improvement rather than just end-to-end automation.
paper_title: WONDERBREAD: A Benchmark for Evaluating Multimodal Foundation Models on Business Process Management Tasks
authors: Michael Wornow, Avanika Narayan, Ben Viggiano, Ishan S. Khare, Tathagat Verma

task_types: climate question answering, knowledge synthesis, evidence grounding
description: CLINB is a climate intelligence benchmark that assesses LLMs on open-ended, grounded, multimodal question answering tasks with clear requirements for knowledge quality and evidential support. It uniquely relies on real users' questions and evaluation rubrics curated by leading climate scientists, focusing on both knowledge synthesis and verifiable attribution.
paper_title: CLINB: A Climate Intelligence Benchmark for Foundational Models
authors: Michelle Chen Huebscher, Katharine Mach, Aleksandar Stanić, Markus Leippold, Ben Gaiarin

task_types: image-to-code generation, visual perception, code synthesis
description: Omni-I2C is a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating Large Multimodal Models in converting complex, structured digital graphics into executable code. It uniquely demands synergy between high-fidelity visual perception and precise code generation, featuring authentic user-sourced cases across diverse subjects, image modalities, and programming languages.
paper_title: Omni-I2C: A Holistic Benchmark for High-Fidelity Image-to-Code Generation
authors: Jiawei Zhou, Chi Zhang, Xiang Feng, Qiming Zhang, Haibo Qiu

description: RecipeGen is the first large-scale, real-world benchmark for recipe-based Text-to-Image, Image-to-Video, and Text-to-Video generation. It uniquely provides fine-grained alignment between recipe goals, step-wise instructions, and visual content, featuring domain-specific evaluation metrics for ingredient fidelity and interaction modeling.
paper_title: RecipeGen: A Step-Aligned Multimodal Benchmark for Real-World Recipe Generation
authors: Ruoxuan Zhang, Jidong Gao, Bin Wen, Hongxia Xie, Chenming Zhang

task_types: data discovery, multimodal retrieval, compound AI system evaluation
