description: CMDBench models the complexity of enterprise data platforms for evaluating coarse-to-fine multimodal data discovery in Compound AI Systems. It uniquely adapts existing datasets to evaluate both data discovery and task execution performance across various modalities, data sources, and task difficulties in realistic enterprise settings.
paper_title: CMDBench: A Benchmark for Coarse-to-fine Multimodal Data Discovery in Compound AI Systems
authors: Yanlin Feng, Sajjadur Rahman, Aaron Feng, Vincent Chen, Eser Kandogan

task_types: structured knowledge extraction, procedural understanding, verb-argument parsing
description: This paper proposes a benchmark of structured procedural knowledge extracted from cooking videos, requiring models to produce interpretable structured knowledge in the form of verb-argument tuples. It uniquely includes 356 instructional cooking videos with 15,523 video clip/sentence-level annotations, focusing on structured procedural understanding rather than general video captioning.
paper_title: A Benchmark for Structured Procedural Knowledge Extraction from Cooking Videos
authors: Frank F. Xu, Lei Ji, Botian Shi, Junyi Du, Graham Neubig

task_types: gesture generation evaluation, motion realism assessment, speech-gesture alignment
description: This paper introduces a detailed human evaluation protocol for the BEAT2 motion-capture dataset and conducts large-scale crowdsourced evaluation to rank six recent gesture-generation models. It uniquely provides standardized evaluation for gesture generation across motion realism and speech-gesture alignment dimensions, releasing synthetic motion data and preference votes for future research.
paper_title: Towards Reliable Human Evaluations in Gesture Generation: Insights from a Community-Driven State-of-the-Art Benchmark
authors: Rajmund Nagy, Hendric Voss, Thanh Hoang-Minh, Mihail Tsakov, Teodor Nikolov

description: TimeTravel provides expert-verified samples spanning 266 distinct cultures across 10 major historical regions for AI-driven analysis of manuscripts, artworks, inscriptions, and archaeological discoveries. It uniquely focuses on historical and cultural artifact understanding, offering structured evaluation for classification, interpretation, and historical comprehension tasks.
paper_title: Time Travel: A Comprehensive Benchmark to Evaluate LMMs on Historical and Cultural Artifacts
authors: Sara Ghaboura, Ketan More, Ritesh Thawkar, Wafa Alghallabi, Omkar Thawakar

description: SVGenius provides comprehensive evaluation of SVG processing across three progressive dimensions: understanding, editing, and generation. It uniquely focuses on vector graphics with systematic complexity stratification across 24 application domains, evaluating models through 8 task categories and 18 metrics for automated graphic design applications.
paper_title: SVGenius: Benchmarking LLMs in SVG Understanding, Editing and Generation
authors: Siqi Chen, Xinyu Dong, Haolei Xu, Xingyu Wu, Fei Tang

task_types: structural awareness, feature analysis, temporal reasoning, sequence matching
description: MMTS-BENCH provides comprehensive evaluation of LLMs on temporal tasks through a hierarchical taxonomy spanning structural awareness, feature analysis, temporal reasoning, sequence matching and cross-modal alignment. It uniquely focuses on time series understanding with both real-world and synthetic data construction across multiple modalities.
paper_title: MMTS-BENCH: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Time Series Understanding and Reasoning
authors: Yao Yin, Zhenyu Xiao, Musheng Li, Yiwen Liu, Sutong Nan

modalities: visible imagery, infrared imagery, UAV data
task_types: multimodal registration, object detection, image alignment
description: ATR-UMMIM is the first benchmark specifically tailored for multimodal image registration in UAV-based applications, providing triplets of visible, infrared, and registered images with pixel-level ground truth. It uniquely covers diverse flight conditions (80m-300m altitude, 0°-75° angles) with all-day, all-year temporal variations and object-level annotations for downstream tasks.
paper_title: ATR-UMMIM: A Benchmark Dataset for UAV-Based Multimodal Image Registration under Complex Imaging Conditions
authors: Kangcheng Bin, Chen Chen, Ting Hu, Jiahao Qi, Ping Zhong

task_types: technical analysis, performance commentary, artistic evaluation
description: FSBench advances artistic sports understanding through figure skating evaluation, consisting of FSBench-Text (multiple-choice questions) and FSBench-Motion (multimodal QA pairs). It uniquely focuses on artistic sports combining technical analysis and performance commentary, addressing the gap in sports research beyond traditional ball games.
paper_title: FSBench: A Figure Skating Benchmark for Advancing Artistic Sports Understanding
authors: Rong Gao, Xin Liu, Zhuozhao Hu, Bohao Xing, Baiqiang Xia

description: Presents a reproducible, extensible benchmark for LLM-augmented multimodal movie recommendation built on MovieLens and MMTF-14K. Aligns dense item embeddings from audio, visual, and text modalities with LLM-generated synopses for thousands of movies.
paper_title: ViLLA-MMBench: A Unified Benchmark Suite for LLM-Augmented Multimodal Movie Recommendation
authors: Fatemeh Nazary, Ali Tourani, Yashar Deldjoo, Tommaso Di Noia

description: Proposes an in-the-wild multimodal plant disease recognition dataset with the largest number of disease classes and text-based descriptions for each disease. Addresses small inter-class discrepancy and large intra-class variance issues in real-world plant disease classification.
paper_title: Benchmarking In-the-wild Multimodal Disease Recognition and A Versatile Baseline
authors: Tianqi Wei, Zhi Chen, Zi Huang, Xin Yu

description: Introduces the first video benchmark for analyzing science classroom discourse with 113 NGSS-aligned clips annotated with Core Instructional Practices and sophistication levels. Evaluates pedagogical reasoning in K-12 classroom videos beyond transcript-only analysis.
paper_title: Can Multimodal LLMs See Science Instruction? Benchmarking Pedagogical Reasoning in K-12 Classroom Videos
authors: Yixuan Shen, Peng He, Honglu Liu, Jinxuan Fan, Yuyang Ji

task_types: AR scene identification, AR scene description
description: Introduces the first AR dataset specifically designed to assess VLMs' ability to analyze virtual content across a wide range of AR scene complexities. Evaluates VLM capabilities in identifying and describing AR-generated scenes for automated AR experience evaluation.
paper_title: Advancing the Understanding and Evaluation of AR-Generated Scenes: When Vision-Language Models Shine and Stumble
authors: Lin Duan, Yanming Xiu, Maria Gorlatova

description: This benchmark provides the first comprehensive evaluation of VLMs for recognizing evoked emotions from images. It assesses both correctness and robustness of emotion recognition, identifies factors affecting performance, characterizes VLM errors, and provides recommendations for future emotion research in VLMs.
paper_title: Evaluating Vision-Language Models for Emotion Recognition
authors: Sree Bhattacharyya, James Z. Wang

description: VLMInferSlow evaluates VLM efficiency robustness in realistic black-box ML-as-a-service settings without requiring model architecture access. It incorporates fine-grained efficiency modeling and uses zero-order optimization to find adversarial examples that increase computational cost by up to 128.47% with imperceptible perturbations.
paper_title: VLMInferSlow: Evaluating the Efficiency Robustness of Large Vision-Language Models as a Service
authors: Xiasi Wang, Tianliang Yao, Simin Chen, Runqi Wang, Lei YE

task_types: sketch similarity analysis, CAD generation, topology optimization, manufacturability assessment, engineering education
description: This benchmark evaluates VLMs across engineering design tasks in four areas: Conceptual Design, System-Level and Detailed Design, Manufacturing and Inspection, and Engineering Education. It includes tasks like sketch similarity analysis, CAD generation, topology optimization, and manufacturability assessment with over 1000 queries.
paper_title: From Concept to Manufacturing: Evaluating Vision-Language Models for Engineering Design
authors: Cyril Picard, Kristen M. Edwards, Anna C. Doris, Brandon Man, Giorgio Giannone

task_types: hospitality VQA, decision-oriented evaluation, informativeness assessment
description: Hospitality-VQA introduces informativeness as a formal framework to quantify how much hospitality-relevant information an image-question pair provides for consumer decision-making. It constructs a hospitality-specific VQA dataset covering various facility types with questions designed to reflect key user information needs.
paper_title: Hospitality-VQA: Decision-Oriented Informativeness Evaluation for Vision-Language Models
authors: Jeongwoo Lee, Baek Duhyeong, Eungyeol Han, Soyeon Shin, Gukin han

task_types: scene understanding, event recognition, road rage reasoning
description: This dataset introduces the road rage reasoning task with finely annotated test data to assess VLMs' capabilities in scene understanding, event recognition, and road rage reasoning. It specifically evaluates models' ability to identify driving-related triggers and engage in preventive dialogue-based interventions.
paper_title: Road Rage Reasoning with Vision-language Models (VLMs): Task Definition and Evaluation Dataset
authors: Yibing Weng, Yu Gu, Fuji Ren

description: A fine-grained benchmark for autonomous driving VLMs featuring close-form QAs across 5 domains: Traffic Knowledge Understanding, General Element Recognition, Traffic Graph Generation, Target Attribute Comprehension, and Ego Decision-Making. Progresses from static foundational knowledge to dynamic reasoning.
paper_title: Fine-Grained Evaluation of Large Vision-Language Models in Autonomous Driving
authors: Yue Li, Meng Tian, Zhenyu Lin, Jiangtong Zhu, Dechang Zhu

description: A structured evaluation framework testing whether VLMs can identify 27 specific failure modes in images generated by T2I models. Jointly evaluates text-to-image models and VLMs through hierarchical failure mode analysis.
paper_title: FineGRAIN: Evaluating Failure Modes of Text-to-Image Models with Vision Language Model Judges
authors: Kevin David Hayes, Micah Goldblum, Vikash Sehwag, Gowthami Somepalli, Ashwinee Panda

description: The first benchmark for automatic evaluation of LVLMs on self-driving corner cases. Uses hierarchical data structure and powerful LVLMs to generate high-quality annotations for complex driving scenarios.
paper_title: Automated Evaluation of Large Vision-Language Models on Self-driving Corner Cases
authors: Kai Chen, Yanze Li, Wenhua Zhang, Yanxin Liu, Pengxiang Li

task_types: visual unit testing, visual regression testing, needle-in-a-haystack tasks, glitch detection, bug report generation
description: First benchmark specifically designed for video game Quality Assurance (QA) tasks, covering visual unit testing, visual regression testing, needle-in-a-haystack tasks, glitch detection, and bug report generation. Tests VLMs on domain-specific gaming scenarios that require understanding of game development workflows and visual anomaly detection.
paper_title: VideoGameQA-Bench: Evaluating Vision-Language Models for Video Game Quality Assurance
authors: Mohammad Reza Taesiri, Abhijay Ghildyal, Saman Zadtootaghaj, Nabajeet Barman, Cor-Paul Bezemer

description: First cross-cultural evaluation instrument for generative art critique using a tri-tier evaluation framework. Tests VLMs across 165 culture-specific dimensions spanning six traditions through five levels of cultural understanding, from visual description to cultural interpretation.
paper_title: Cross-Cultural Expert-Level Art Critique Evaluation with Vision-Language Models
authors: Haorui Yu, Xuehang Wen, Fengrui Zhang, Qiufeng Yi

description: Benchmark dataset compiled through survey study with 40 low vision participants to evaluate VLMs' ability to simulate vision perception of low vision individuals. Tests whether VLMs can accurately simulate visual impairments when interpreting images through both open-ended and multiple-choice responses.
paper_title: Not There Yet: Evaluating Vision Language Models in Simulating the Visual Perception of People with Low Vision
authors: Rosiana Natalie, Wenqian Xu, Ruei-Che Chang, Rada Mihalcea, Anhong Guo

description: First Visual Question Answering benchmark focused solely on perception-based questions in traffic scenes, enriched with distance annotations. Tests small VLMs on distance-dependent traffic perception at both close (up to 20m) and long (30m+) range while excluding reasoning questions to isolate perception capabilities.
paper_title: Evaluating Small Vision-Language Models on Distance-Dependent Traffic Perception
authors: Nikos Theodoridis, Tim Brophy, Reenu Mohandas, Ganesh Sistu, Fiachra Collins

description: First Noise-free Text-Image Corruption and Evaluation pipeline for mechanistic interpretability in VLMs. Incorporates Semantic Minimal Pairs framework for image corruption and Symmetric Token Replacement for text, enabling semantically meaningful causal mediation analysis for both modalities.
paper_title: What Do VLMs NOTICE? A Mechanistic Interpretability Pipeline for Gaussian-Noise-free Text-Image Corruption and Evaluation
authors: Michal Golovanevsky, William Rudman, Vedant Palit, Ritambhara Singh, Carsten Eickhoff

description: First multimodal benchmark evaluating VLMs for strategic abilities in multi-agent environments. Comprises ten vision-grounded environments covering cooperative, competitive, and mixed-motive interactions, measuring perception, strategic reasoning, and decision-making across three dimensions.
paper_title: VS-Bench: Evaluating VLMs for Strategic Abilities in Multi-Agent Environments
authors: Zelai Xu, Zhexuan Xu, Xiangmin Yi, Huining Yuan, Mo Guang

task_types: character recognition, radical understanding, stroke analysis
description: Benchmark to evaluate LLMs' and VLMs' understanding of visual elements in Chinese characters, including radicals, composition structures, strokes, and stroke counts. Tests whether models can harness sub-character visual features in Chinese through prompting.
paper_title: The Impact of Visual Information in Chinese Characters: Evaluating Large Models' Ability to Recognize and Utilize Radicals
authors: Xiaofeng Wu, Karl Stratos, Wei Xu

description: First comprehensive evaluation of VLM cultural competence through multimodal story generation. Develops novel multimodal framework that perturbs cultural identity cues embedded in both textual prompts and visual inputs to evaluate cultural adaptation capabilities during generative tasks.
paper_title: Toward Socially Aware Vision-Language Models: Evaluating Cultural Competence Through Multimodal Story Generation
authors: Arka Mukherjee, Shreya Ghosh

description: Data-driven evaluation framework for short-form edutainment videos using VLMs to extract unsupervised audiovisual features and predict engagement. Curated YouTube Shorts dataset enables systematic analysis of how VLM-derived features relate to human engagement behavior in viral content.
paper_title: Understanding Virality: A Rubric based Vision-Language Model Framework for Short-Form Edutainment Evaluation
authors: Arnav Gupta, Gurekas Singh Sahney, Hardik Rathi, Abhishek Chandwani, Ishaan Gupta

task_types: urban perception, urban decision-making, geospatial prediction, traffic control
description: First systematic benchmark for evaluating LLMs and VLMs capabilities for diverse urban research tasks. Interactive simulator-based evaluation platform with 8 representative urban tasks in 2 categories (perception-understanding and decision-making) tested across 13 cities worldwide.
paper_title: CityBench: Evaluating the Capabilities of Large Language Models for Urban Tasks
authors: Jie Feng, Jun Zhang, Tianhui Liu, Xin Zhang, Tianjian Ouyang

description: Introduces a dataset that controls for length while varying information content to evaluate specificity versus verbosity in image descriptions. The benchmark specifically disentangles description specificity from length by defining specificity relative to a contrast set.
paper_title: When More Words Say Less: Decoupling Length and Specificity in Image Description Evaluation
authors: Rhea Kapur, Robert Hawkins, Elisa Kreiss

description: Proposes a benchmark for evaluating projection-layer generalization by adapting object detection datasets into prompting format with train/test splits having disjoint label sets. This enables precise control over seen and unseen concept separation to test generalization to unseen visual concepts.
paper_title: Prompt the Unseen: Evaluating Visual-Language Alignment Beyond Supervision
authors: Raehyuk Jung, Seungjun Yu, Hyunjung Shim

description: DCI (Densely Captioned Images) provides human-annotated dense descriptions averaging 1000+ words per image with mask-aligned descriptions. It introduces a novel evaluation task matching captions with corresponding subcrops, testing fine-grained visual-text understanding beyond typical short captions.
paper_title: A Picture is Worth More Than 77 Text Tokens: Evaluating CLIP-Style Models on Dense Captions
authors: Jack Urbanek, Florian Bordes, Pietro Astolfi, Mary Williamson, Vasu Sharma

task_types: dialogue evaluation, entity recognition, review generation, suggestion generation
description: Comprehensive evaluation system for MLLMs in art evaluation dialogues across nine critical dimensions. Features process-oriented Human-Computer Interaction design with modular agents for entity recognition, review generation, and suggestion generation.
paper_title: ArtMentor: AI-Assisted Evaluation of Artworks to Explore Multimodal Large Language Models Capabilities
authors: Chanjin Zheng, Zengyi Yu, Yilin Jiang, Mingzi Zhang, Xunuo Lu

task_types: face recognition, face analysis, bias detection, face authentication, face localization
description: Comprehensive benchmark for evaluating MLLMs on complex face understanding tasks across bias, authentication, recognition, analysis, and localization. Features 5,000 multimodal multiple-choice questions covering 14 tasks across 6 categories with a newly created FaceXAPI dataset.
paper_title: FaceXBench: Evaluating Multimodal LLMs on Face Understanding
authors: Kartik Narayan, Vibashan VS, Vishal M. Patel

task_types: creativity assessment, creative idea evaluation, creative process evaluation
description: First benchmark covering multiple dimensions of creativity evaluation from idea to process to products, with human-aligned assessment. Includes CreMIT dataset with 2.2K multimodal data, 79.2K human feedbacks, and 4.7M multi-typed instructions for creativity evaluation.
paper_title: CreBench: Human-Aligned Creativity Evaluation from Idea to Process to Product
authors: Kaiwen Xue, Chenglong Li, Zhonghong Ou, Guoxin Zhang, Kaoyan Lu

task_types: emotion statement judgment, emotion interpretation, context-based emotion judgment
description: Introduces Emotion Statement Judgment task for customized visual emotion evaluation using open-vocabulary, multifaceted approach. Features automated pipeline for constructing emotion-centric statements with minimal human effort, addressing constraints in existing emotion evaluation methods.
paper_title: Customizing Visual Emotion Evaluation for MLLMs: An Open-vocabulary, Multifaceted, and Scalable Approach
authors: Daiqing Wu, Dongbao Yang, Sicheng Zhao, Can Ma, Yu Zhou

task_types: social inference, social analysis, social information extraction, interaction understanding
description: Framework grounded in interaction theory to evaluate social understanding abilities along three dimensions: Social Inference, Holistic Social Analysis, and Directed Social Analysis. Assesses MLLMs' ability to interpret human social interactions and extract relevant social information.
paper_title: Social Caption: Evaluating Social Understanding in Multimodal Models
authors: Bhaavanaa Thumu, Leena Mathur, Youssouf Kebe, Louis-Philippe Morency

task_types: contrast sensitivity measurement, frequency tuning evaluation
description: Introduces a psychophysics-inspired method for estimating Contrast Sensitivity Functions in MLLMs by treating them as end-to-end observers. Uses noise-based stimuli filtered at specific spatial frequencies to derive psychometric functions and contrast thresholds without internal activation analysis.
paper_title: Contrast Sensitivity in Multimodal Large Language Models: A Psychophysics-Inspired Evaluation
authors: Pablo Hernández-Cámara, Alexandra Gomez-Villa, Jose Manuel Jaén-Lorites, Jorge Vila-Tomás, Valero Laparra

description: Develops quantitative framework for evaluating VLMs' capabilities in generating critiques for traditional Chinese painting using multi-dimensional evaluative features and persona-guided prompting.
paper_title: A Structured Framework for Evaluating and Enhancing Interpretive Capabilities of Multimodal LLMs in Culturally Situated Tasks
authors: Haorui Yu, Ramon Ruiz-Dolz, Qiufeng Yi

description: ICCA evaluates conversational adaptation as in-context behavior in MLLMs, assessing whether models spontaneously increase communication efficiency during interactions like humans do in reference games.
paper_title: Talk Less, Interact Better: Evaluating In-context Conversational Adaptation in Multimodal LLMs
authors: Yilun Hua, Yoav Artzi

description: THEMIS evaluates MLLMs on visual fraud reasoning in academic scenarios with over 4,000 questions from retracted-paper cases, covering five fraud types and 16 fine-grained manipulation operations.
paper_title: THEMIS: Towards Holistic Evaluation of MLLMs for Scientific Paper Fraud Forensics
authors: Tzu-Yen Ma, Bo Zhang, Zichen Tang, Junpeng Ding, Haolin Tian

task_types: financial statement auditing, company fundamental reasoning, industry trend insights, financial risk sensing, asset allocation analysis
description: First unified multimodal benchmark for high-information-density financial environments covering text, images, and videos. Constructs five core financial scenarios including Financial Statement Auditing, Company Fundamental Reasoning, Industry Trend Insights, Financial Risk Sensing, and Asset Allocation Analysis with bilingual (Chinese and English) question-answer pairs.
paper_title: UniFinEval: Towards Unified Evaluation of Financial Multimodal Models across Text, Images and Videos
authors: Zhi Yang, Lingfeng Zeng, Fangqi Lou, Qi Qi, Wei Zhang

task_types: cultural relevance, semantic equivalence, visual similarity
description: Proposes suite of automatic evaluation metrics for image transcreation across cultures, categorized into object-based, embedding-based, and VLM-based metrics. Evaluates three critical dimensions: cultural relevance, semantic equivalence, and visual similarity, with meta-evaluation across 7 countries showing strong correlation with human ratings.
paper_title: Towards Automatic Evaluation for Image Transcreation
authors: Simran Khanuja, Vivek Iyer, Claire He, Graham Neubig

description: Visual question answering benchmark designed to probe knowledge of culture-specific concepts and evaluate cultural adaptation through contextual information. Distinguishes between parametric knowledge from training and contextual knowledge provided during inference via visual and textual descriptions.
paper_title: CROPE: Evaluating In-Context Adaptation of Vision and Language Models to Culture-Specific Concepts
authors: Malvina Nikandrou, Georgios Pantazopoulos, Nikolas Vitsakis, Ioannis Konstas, Alessandro Suglia

description: A model-agnostic framework for evaluating reason-responsiveness in vision language models for automated driving. Systematically measures whether model decisions reflect genuine reason-responsive decision making rather than post-hoc rationalizations through controlled contextual variation.
paper_title: CARE Drive A Framework for Evaluating Reason-Responsiveness of Vision Language Models in Automated Driving
authors: Lucas Elbert Suryana, Farah Bierenga, Sanne van Buuren, Pepijn Kooij, Elsefien Tulleners

task_types: parameter estimation, physics reasoning, system identification
description: High-fidelity benchmark for inverse recovery and identification of physical dynamic systems from monocular video. Features real-world videos with independently measured ground-truth parameters and standardized evaluation protocol for physics parameter estimation.
paper_title: IRIS: A Real-World Benchmark for Inverse Recovery and Identification of Physical Dynamic Systems from Monocular Video
authors: Rasul Khanbayov, Mohamed Rayan Barhdadi, Erchin Serpedin, Hasan Kurban

task_types: fMRI vision decoding, mental image reconstruction
description: Benchmark dataset of human fMRI activity paired with mental images to complement Natural Scenes Dataset. Enables assessment of fMRI-to-image reconstruction models on mental imagery rather than just seen images, addressing real-world medical and BCI applications.
paper_title: NSD-Imagery: A benchmark dataset for extending fMRI vision decoding methods to mental imagery
authors: Reese Kneeland, Paul S. Scotti, Ghislain St-Yves, Jesse Breedlove, Kendrick Kay

description: Large-scale multi-domain anomaly detection benchmark comprising 380 categories across Electronics, Industry, Agrifood, Infrastructure, and Medical domains. Includes pixel-level annotations and structured text descriptions enabling multimodal anomaly detection tasks.
paper_title: ADNet: A Large-Scale and Extensible Multi-Domain Benchmark for Anomaly Detection Across 380 Real-World Categories
authors: Hai Ling, Jia Guo, Zhulin Tao, Yunkang Cao, Donglin Di

task_types: low-level control, game playing, zero-shot policy
description: Novel benchmark testing multimodal LLMs as low-level policies in Atari games, assessing zero-shot game playing capabilities without environment-specific training. Evaluates emergent capabilities for direct game environment interaction using pre-existing multimodal knowledge.
paper_title: Atari-GPT: Benchmarking Multimodal Large Language Models as Low-Level Policies in Atari Games
authors: Nicholas R. Waytowich, Devin White, MD Sunbeam, Vinicius G. Goecks

task_types: spatial perception, object understanding, scene understanding, scene reasoning, crop identification, health monitoring
description: Comprehensive agricultural remote sensing benchmark covering four task dimensions: spatial perception, object understanding, scene understanding, and scene reasoning. Integrates eight public datasets plus private farmland data with 13 task types from crop identification to environmental analysis.
paper_title: Can Large Multimodal Models Understand Agricultural Scenes? Benchmarking with AgroMind
authors: Qingmei Li, Yang Zhang, Zurong Mai, Yuhang Chen, Shuohong Lou

description: A benchmark for core psychological reasoning consisting of procedurally generated 3D animations testing intuitive psychology principles. Structured around four scenarios probing goal preferences, action efficiency, unobserved constraints, and cost-reward trade-offs inspired by cognitive development studies.
paper_title: AGENT: A Benchmark for Core Psychological Reasoning
authors: Tianmin Shu, Abhishek Bhandwaldar, Chuang Gan, Kevin A. Smith, Shari Liu

description: A benchmark challenging machines to predict plausibility of agent behavior based on underlying action causes, adopted from developmental cognitive science paradigms. Enables direct comparison between human and machine performance on infant-like reasoning about goals, preferences, and actions.
paper_title: Baby Intuitions Benchmark (BIB): Discerning the goals, preferences, and actions of others
authors: Kanishk Gandhi, Gala Stojnic, Brenden M. Lake, Moira R. Dillon

task_types: autonomous driving, physics reasoning, policy reasoning, scenario reasoning
description: AgentDrive provides 300,000 LLM-generated driving scenarios for training and evaluating autonomous agents with AgentDrive-MCQ containing 100,000 multiple-choice questions. It formalizes a factorized scenario space across seven orthogonal axes and includes both simulation-ready specifications and reasoning-oriented evaluation across five cognitive dimensions.
paper_title: AgentDrive: An Open Benchmark Dataset for Agentic AI Reasoning with LLM-Generated Scenarios in Autonomous Systems
authors: Mohamed Amine Ferrag, Abderrahmane Lakas, Merouane Debbah

task_types: travel planning, constraint coordination, visual perception
description: A realistic multimodal travel-planning benchmark with 150 real-world scenarios featuring 15+ interdependent temporal and logical constraints. Includes WorldTravel-Webscape environment with 2,000+ rendered webpages for visual constraint extraction.
paper_title: WorldTravel: A Realistic Multimodal Travel-Planning Benchmark with Tightly Coupled Constraints
authors: Zexuan Wang, Chenghao Yang, Yingqi Que, Zhenzhu Yang, Huaqing Yuan

description: PillagerBench is a novel framework for evaluating multi-agent systems in real-time competitive team-vs-team scenarios in Minecraft. It provides extensible API, multi-round testing, and rule-based built-in opponents for fair, reproducible comparisons in competitive environments.
paper_title: PillagerBench: Benchmarking LLM-Based Agents in Competitive Minecraft Team Environments
authors: Olivier Schipper, Yudi Zhang, Yali Du, Mykola Pechenizkiy, Meng Fang

description: Novel benchmark for evaluating search agents on fuzzy exploratory search with ambiguous, multifaceted queries where users seek the most relevant webpage. Comprises 663 questions across seven domains with controllable difficulty based on factual claims of web contents.
paper_title: Needle in the Web: A Benchmark for Retrieving Targeted Web Pages in the Wild
authors: Yumeng Wang, Tianyu Fan, Lingrui Xu, Chao Huang

description: Large-scale challenging dataset for deep-research scenarios with 9000 questions spanning three difficulty levels. Built from multi-hop QA datasets via autonomous pipeline, including high-quality search trajectories with reasoning chains and verifiable answers.
paper_title: DeepResearch-9K: A Challenging Benchmark Dataset of Deep-Research Agent
authors: Tongzhou Wu, Yuhao Wang, Xinyu Ma, Xiuqiang He, Shuaiqiang Wang

task_types: graphic design planning, creative workflow generation
description: Planning benchmark for graphic design covering 1,079 user queries and input images across four design types. Evaluates LLM agents' ability to generate workflows integrating explicit design constraints and implicit commonsense constraints in web environments.
paper_title: GraphicBench: A Planning Benchmark for Graphic Design with Language Agents
authors: Dayeon Ki, Tianyi Zhou, Marine Carpuat, Gang Wu, Puneet Mathur

description: High-difficulty benchmark for evaluating LLM agents on Chinese web browsing. Features 289 multi-hop questions across 11 domains, reverse-engineered from verifiable answers, addressing linguistic and infrastructural complexities of Chinese information ecosystem.
paper_title: BrowseComp-ZH: Benchmarking Web Browsing Ability of Large Language Models in Chinese
authors: Peilin Zhou, Bruce Leon, Xiang Ying, Can Zhang, Yifan Shao

description: Benchmark for long-horizon inference using multi-modal evidence in simulated household environments. Inspired by 'whodunit' stories, asks models to identify which agent caused environmental changes based on step-by-step replay with vision, language, and auditory cues.
paper_title: MARPLE: A Benchmark for Long-Horizon Inference
authors: Emily Jin, Zhuoyi Huang, Jan-Philipp Fränken, Weiyu Liu, Hannah Cha

task_types: technical drawing revision, function execution, instruction following
description: Evaluates LLM agents for technical drawing revision tasks in civil engineering with 46 customized functions/tools. Tests interpretation of intricate long-context instructions, prior knowledge leveraging, and adaptation to dynamic instruction quality.
paper_title: DrafterBench: Benchmarking Large Language Models for Tasks Automation in Civil Engineering
authors: Yinsheng Li, Zhen Dong, Yi Shao

task_types: marketing analysis, multi-tool collaboration, trajectory following
description: Real-world, trajectory-aware benchmark designed based on actual business requirements of advertising and marketing platforms. Constructed from real user marketing analysis requests with domain expert-provided reference answers and tool-call trajectories, categorizing requests into three difficulty levels for multi-round, multi-tool collaboration evaluation.
paper_title: AD-Bench: A Real-World, Trajectory-Aware Advertising Analytics Benchmark for LLM Agents
authors: Lingxiang Hu, Yiding Sun, Tianle Xia, Wenwei Li, Ming Xu

num_samples: 200 end-to-end analysis tasks across finance, healthcare, and other domains
task_types: cross-modal analysis, visual table extraction, joint reasoning
description: Novel benchmark for unified, insight-driven analysis across heterogeneous data modalities, bridging structured queryable sources and unstructured visual documents. Constructed via human-in-the-loop reverse-synthesis pipeline with three difficulty tiers evaluating visual table extraction, cross-modal alignment, and multi-step joint reasoning.
paper_title: DataCross: A Unified Benchmark and Agent Framework for Cross-Modal Heterogeneous Data Analysis
authors: Ruyi Qi, Zhou Liu, Wentao Zhang

description: DefectBench is the first multi-dimensional benchmark designed to evaluate Large Multimodal Models (LMMs) on building facade inspection tasks beyond basic semantic recognition. It assesses models across three escalating cognitive dimensions: Semantic Perception, Spatial Localization, and Generative Geometry Segmentation using a standardized hierarchical ontology unified from 12 fragmented datasets.
paper_title: Can Large Multimodal Models Inspect Buildings? A Hierarchical Benchmark for Structural Pathology Reasoning
authors: Hui Zhong, Yichun Gao, Luyan Liu, Hai Yang, Wang Wang

description: A grid-world benchmark to assess social skills of Deep RL agents, focusing on language use in complex social contexts and embodied communication in multimodal settings. Evaluates socio-cognitive abilities beyond simple vocabulary size.
paper_title: SocialAI 0.1: Towards a Benchmark to Stimulate Research on Socio-Cognitive Abilities in Deep Reinforcement Learning Agents
authors: Grgur Kovač, Rémy Portelas, Katja Hofmann, Pierre-Yves Oudeyer

description: A comprehensive benchmark with multiple grid-world environments featuring scripted social agents to assess acquisition of social skills in DRL agents. Expands beyond language to broader social competencies in evolving social worlds.
paper_title: SocialAI: Benchmarking Socio-Cognitive Abilities in Deep Reinforcement Learning Agents
authors: Grgur Kovač, Rémy Portelas, Katja Hofmann, Pierre-Yves Oudeyer

description: A benchmark for Visually Impaired Assistance using Large Models, where given an image and linguistic request from VI users, models provide step-by-step guidance grounded in physical environments. Tests environment grounding and fine-grained guidance capabilities.
paper_title: VIALM: A Survey and Benchmark of Visually Impaired Assistance with Large Models
authors: Yi Zhao, Yilin Zhang, Rong Xiang, Jing Li, Hillming Li

description: A comprehensive benchmark for generic cooperative autonomous driving with 204 sequences and 30,000 frames from multiple vehicles. Supports various tasks including detection, tracking, mapping, forecasting, and path planning with multimodal sensors.
paper_title: M3CAD: Towards Generic Cooperative Autonomous Driving Benchmark
authors: Morui Zhu, Yongqi Zhu, Yihao Zhu, Qi Chen, Deyuan Qu

description: A large-scale dataset with over one million items from driving handbooks, theory tests, and simulated road tests across various countries. Encompasses explicit driving knowledge from theory to practice, like obtaining a driver's license.
paper_title: Can LVLMs Obtain a Driver's License? A Benchmark Towards Reliable AGI for Autonomous Driving
authors: Yuhang Lu, Yichen Yao, Jiadong Tu, Jiangnan Shao, Yuexin Ma

description: The largest benchmark for anomaly detection in autonomous driving incorporating large-scale multimodal sensor data and spatial voxel ground truth. First benchmark to contain both content and temporal anomalies with sensor-independent evaluation.
paper_title: AnoVox: A Benchmark for Multimodal Anomaly Detection in Autonomous Driving
authors: Daniel Bogdoll, Iramm Hamdard, Lukas Namgyu Rößler, Felix Geisler, Muhammed Bayram

description: A benchmark for evaluating video generation models on physical reasoning using 80 real-world videos capturing physical phenomena guided by conservation laws. Uses physics-informed metrics to assess adherence to physical principles.
paper_title: Morpheus: Benchmarking Physical Reasoning of Video Generative Models with Real Physical Experiments
authors: Chenyu Zhang, Daniil Cherniavskii, Antonios Tragoudaras, Antonios Vozikis, Thijmen Nijdam

description: A benchmark for accident anticipation that addresses when, where, and what accidents might occur using LLMs. Integrates chain-based attention mechanism and three-stage processing for enhanced predictive capabilities beyond traditional dashcam models.
paper_title: When, Where, and What? A Novel Benchmark for Accident Anticipation and Localization with Large Language Models
authors: Haicheng Liao, Yongkang Li, Chengyue Wang, Yanchen Guan, KaHou Tam

description: A structured empirical benchmark for defogging methods including classical filters, modern networks, chained variants, and VLM-based approaches. Evaluates both synthetic (Foggy Cityscapes) and real-world (ACDC) conditions with downstream perception metrics.
paper_title: From Filters to VLMs: Benchmarking Defogging Methods through Object Detection and Segmentation Performance
authors: Ardalan Aryashad, Parsa Razmara, Amin Mahjoub, Seyedarmin Azizi, Mahdi Salmani

description: First fine-grained image-text dataset for spatiotemporal farmland segmentation with language-driven learning paradigm. Covers all four seasons across eight typical agricultural regions in China with rich spatiotemporal characteristics.
paper_title: A large-scale image-text dataset benchmark for farmland segmentation
authors: Chao Tao, Dandan Zhong, Weiliang Mu, Zhuofei Du, Haiyang Wu

task_types: event classification, scene understanding, safety analysis
description: Synthetic multimodal driving dataset containing crashes and near-crashes derived from real-world SHRP 2 data while preserving privacy. Features de-identified keyframes using Stable Diffusion with detailed safety event annotations and synchronized multimodal data.
paper_title: SynSHRP2: A Synthetic Multimodal Benchmark for Driving Safety-critical Events Derived from Real-world Driving Data
authors: Liang Shi, Boyu Jiang, Zhenyuan Yuan, Miguel A. Perez, Feng Guo

task_types: disaster content classification, multimodal social media analysis
description: Large annotated dataset for single disaster event (Turkey-Syria Earthquake) containing multimodal social media content for disaster response. Incorporates social context features and user-centric information for crisis content classification during emergencies.
paper_title: A social context-aware graph-based multimodal attentive learning framework for disaster content classification during emergencies: a benchmark dataset and method
authors: Shahid Shafi Dar, Mohammad Zia Ur Rehman, Karan Bais, Mohammed Abdul Haseeb, Nagendra Kumara

description: Introduces a new multi-modal visual tracking task where a pursuer UAV tracks a target adversarial UAV from another moving UAV platform. The benchmark addresses the unique challenge of dual-dynamic disturbances from both capturing and target UAVs, which is more challenging than existing Anti-UAV tasks using fixed ground cameras.
paper_title: How Far are Modern Trackers from UAV-Anti-UAV? A Million-Scale Benchmark and New Baseline
authors: Chunhui Zhang, Li Liu, Zhipeng Zhang, Yong Wang, Hao Wen

description: A unified semantic assessment benchmark for text-to-image generation that spans diverse real-world scenarios (5 main themes, 20 subthemes) and comprehensively evaluates semantic consistency over 10 primary and 27 sub-criteria. Provides both English and Chinese versions in short and long forms to test model robustness.
paper_title: UniGenBench++: A Unified Semantic Evaluation Benchmark for Text-to-Image Generation
authors: Yibin Wang, Zhimin Li, Yuhang Zang, Jiazi Bu, Yujie Zhou

task_types: shadow detection, shadow removal, shadow generation
description: A unified survey and benchmark for deep-learning-based shadow detection, removal, and generation across images and videos. Provides standardized evaluation protocols and re-trains representative methods under consistent settings to enable fair comparison across the three shadow-related tasks.
paper_title: Unveiling Deep Shadows: A Survey and Benchmark on Image and Video Shadow Detection, Removal, and Generation in the Deep Learning Era
authors: Xiaowei Hu, Zhenghao Xing, Tianyu Wang, Chi-Wing Fu, Pheng-Ann Heng

task_types: geo-localization, scene reasoning, scene understanding, object understanding
description: A comprehensive benchmark for evaluating LMMs in complex multi-view urban scenarios, containing 11.6K questions across 4 task dimensions (Geo-Localization, Scene Reasoning, Scene Understanding, Object Understanding). Addresses limitations of existing urban benchmarks by providing both region-level and role-level evaluations across 14 task types.
paper_title: UrBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Evaluating Large Multimodal Models in Multi-View Urban Scenarios
authors: Baichuan Zhou, Haote Yang, Dairong Chen, Junyan Ye, Tianyi Bai

description: A multi-image benchmark for real-world multimodal sarcasm detection, composed entirely of multi-image samples from tweets and Amazon reviews. Addresses the gap in existing datasets that focus on single-image scenarios, modeling cases where sarcasm is triggered by multi-image cues.
paper_title: MMSD3.0: A Multi-Image Benchmark for Real-World Multimodal Sarcasm Detection
authors: Haochen Zhao, Yuyao Kong, Yongxiu Xu, Gaopeng Gou, Hongbo Xu

description: Currently the largest AI-generated video detection dataset with 6.78 million videos from 11 state-of-the-art generators. Features cross-source and cross-generator design to reduce content interference and ensure diversity, enabling development of generalized detection models for distinguishing AI-generated from real videos.
paper_title: GenVidBench: A 6-Million Benchmark for AI-Generated Video Detection
authors: Zhenliang Ni, Qiangyu Yan, Mouxiao Huang, Tianning Yuan, Yehui Tang

task_types: video watermark robustness evaluation, attack resistance testing
description: First systematic benchmark for evaluating robustness of video watermarks under watermark removal and forgery attacks. Features unified dataset from three video generative models across three styles, incorporating four watermarking methods and seven aggregation strategies, evaluating 12 types of perturbations under different threat models.
paper_title: VideoMarkBench: Benchmarking Robustness of Video Watermarking
authors: Zhengyuan Jiang, Moyang Guo, Kecen Li, Yuepeng Hu, Yupu Wang

description: StarFlow introduces a framework for generating structured workflows from sketch images using VLMs. It curates a diverse dataset of workflow diagrams including synthetic, manually annotated, and real-world samples for training and evaluation.
paper_title: StarFlow: Generating Structured Workflow Outputs From Sketch Images
authors: Patrice Bechard, Chao Wang, Amirhossein Abaskohi, Juan Rodriguez, Christopher Pal

description: OTR introduces a synthesized overlay text dataset for text removal evaluation, featuring text rendered on complex backgrounds using object-aware placement and vision-language model-generated content to address limitations in existing text removal benchmarks.
paper_title: OTR: Synthesizing Overlay Text Dataset for Text Removal
authors: Jan Zdenek, Wataru Shimoda, Kota Yamaguchi

task_types: AI vs human image detection, error detection
description: DiffuSyn Bench introduces an automated benchmark construction method for evaluating LVLMs' ability to differentiate between AI-generated and human-generated images. It creates diverse text-image pairs with intentional errors using topic retrieval, narrative generation, and image generation.
paper_title: DiffuSyn Bench: Evaluating Vision-Language Models on Real-World Complexities with Diffusion-Generated Synthetic Benchmarks
authors: Haokun Zhou, Yipeng Hong

description: A multimodal dataset dedicated to Egyptian culture covering landmarks, food, and folklore with manually validated cultural authenticity. Specifically designed to evaluate cultural bias in vision-language models and provide culturally aware evaluation for Middle Eastern and African contexts.
paper_title: EgMM-Corpus: A Multimodal Vision-Language Dataset for Egyptian Culture
authors: Mohamed Gamil, Abdelrahman Elsayed, Abdelrahman Lila, Ahmed Gad, Hesham Abdelgawad

description: Establishes zero-shot benchmark for image privacy classification enabling fair comparison between VLMs and specialized models. Evaluates top open-source VLMs against established vision-only and multi-modal methods in terms of performance, efficiency, and robustness.
paper_title: Zero-shot image privacy classification with Vision-Language Models
authors: Alina Elena Baia, Alessio Xompero, Andrea Cavallaro

description: Multilingual, parallel, human-verified dataset with OCR-corrected annotations for Vision-Language Translation evaluation. Introduces Density-Aware Evaluation and DA Score as robust measures addressing metric reliability issues under varying contextual complexity.
paper_title: Rethinking Multilingual Vision-Language Translation: Dataset, Evaluation, and Adaptation
authors: Xintong Wang, Jingheng Pan, Yixiao Liu, Xiaohu Zhao, Chenyang Lyu

description: A comprehensive framework for evaluating monosemanticity at the neuron-level in visual representations of Vision-Language Models. The benchmark is derived from a large-scale user study to ensure alignment with human perception in assessing interpretability.
paper_title: Sparse Autoencoders Learn Monosemantic Features in Vision-Language Models
authors: Mateusz Pach, Shyamgopal Karthik, Quentin Bouniot, Serge Belongie, Zeynep Akata

description: A revised definition and benchmark for open-set recognition in the age of Vision-Language Models, establishing evaluation protocols for VLMs under open-set conditions. Addresses how VLMs introduce closed-set assumptions despite internet-scale training and frequently misclassify objects outside their query set.
paper_title: Open-Set Recognition in the Age of Vision-Language Models
authors: Dimity Miller, Niko Sünderhauf, Alex Kenna, Keita Mason

description: A novel benchmark for 2D, 2.5D and 3D unobserved object detection that evaluates models' ability to predict locations of nearby objects that are occluded or lie outside the image frame. Evaluated on indoor scenes from RealEstate10k and NYU Depth v2 datasets.
paper_title: Believing is Seeing: Unobserved Object Detection using Generative Models
authors: Subhransu S. Bhattacharjee, Dylan Campbell, Rahul Shome

description: A benchmark for automatically evaluating the placement of virtual objects in augmented reality using open-vocabulary vision-language models. Alleviates the need for costly user studies by providing automated evaluation of object placement validity in mixed reality environments.
paper_title: OCTO+: A Suite for Automatic Open-Vocabulary Object Placement in Mixed Reality
authors: Aditya Sharma, Luke Yoffe, Tobias Höllerer

description: Novel task and benchmark for efficiently evaluating VLMs' zero-shot performance on downstream applications without access to the downstream task dataset. Methods perform model selection and performance prediction based solely on text descriptions of desired applications.
paper_title: LOVM: Language-Only Vision Model Selection
authors: Orr Zohar, Shih-Cheng Huang, Kuan-Chieh Wang, Serena Yeung

description: Framework for systematically addressing both noisy labels and missing label detection in image classification test sets. Integrates vision-language models with machine/human curation methods to improve benchmark quality and enable more accurate model comparisons.
paper_title: When VLMs Meet Image Classification: Test Sets Renovation via Missing Label Identification
authors: Zirui Pang, Haosheng Tan, Yuhan Pu, Zhijie Deng, Zhouan Shen

description: AquaOV255 is the first large-scale underwater segmentation dataset with 255 categories and 20K+ images. UOVSBench integrates this with five additional underwater datasets to create the first underwater open-vocabulary segmentation benchmark.
paper_title: Exploring the Underwater World Segmentation without Extra Training
authors: Bingyu Li, Tao Huo, Da Zhang, Zhiyuan Zhao, Junyu Gao

task_types: game completion, real-time interaction, spatial navigation, memory management
description: A benchmark consisting of 10 popular video games from the 1990s that VLMs directly interact with in real-time. Challenges models to complete entire games with only raw visual inputs and high-level descriptions, testing perception, spatial navigation, and memory management.
paper_title: VideoGameBench: Can Vision-Language Models complete popular video games?
authors: Alex L. Zhang, Thomas L. Griffiths, Karthik R. Narasimhan, Ofir Press

task_types: personalized alignment, role-based evaluation, situated cognition
description: A benchmark with 18k instances and 20 individuals with different Role-Sets to evaluate personalized alignment of VLM assistants with situated cognition. Tests whether VLMs can adapt to diversified human backgrounds and expectations in the same situation.
paper_title: Aligning VLM Assistants with Personalized Situated Cognition
authors: Yongqi Li, Shen Zhou, Xiaohu Li, Xin Miao, Jintao Wen

task_types: deepfake attribution, zero-shot generalization, generator traceability
description: A novel and fine-grained Zero-Shot Deepfake Attribution (ZS-DFA) benchmark to evaluate attribution performance of deepfake attributors to unseen advanced generators like diffusion models. Tests generalization to cutting-edge generative models in a fine-grained manner.
paper_title: PVLM: Parsing-Aware Vision Language Model with Dynamic Contrastive Learning for Zero-Shot Deepfake Attribution
authors: Yaning Zhang, Jiahe Zhang, Chunjie Ma, Weili Guan, Tian Gan

description: PlanBench-V is a domain-specific benchmark for evaluating Vision-Language Models on urban planning map interpretation tasks. It tests specialized understanding of spatial configurations, regulatory requirements, and multi-scale analysis in planning maps, addressing the gap in existing VLM evaluations for this critical professional domain.
paper_title: PlanGPT-VL: Enhancing Urban Planning with Domain-Specific Vision-Language Models
authors: He Zhu, Junyou Su, Minxin Chen, Wen Wang, Yijie Deng

description: FishNet++ is a large-scale multimodal benchmark for marine biology with 35,133 textual descriptions, 706,426 key-point annotations for morphological studies, and 119,399 bounding boxes for detection. It addresses the significant limitation of current MLLMs in fine-grained fish species recognition, where best models achieve less than 10% accuracy.
paper_title: FishNet++: Analyzing the capabilities of Multimodal Large Language Models in marine biology
authors: Faizan Farooq Khan, Yousef Radwan, Eslam Abdelrahman, Abdulwahab Felemban, Aymen Mir

task_types: data analysis, data modeling, end-to-end data science tasks
description: DSBench is a comprehensive benchmark designed to evaluate data science agents with realistic tasks, including 466 data analysis tasks and 74 data modeling tasks sourced from Eloquence and Kaggle competitions. It offers realistic settings with long contexts, multimodal task backgrounds, reasoning with large data files and multi-table structures, and end-to-end data modeling tasks, addressing the gap between existing simplified benchmarks and real-world data science applications.
paper_title: DSBench: How Far Are Data Science Agents from Becoming Data Science Experts?
authors: Liqiang Jing, Zhehui Huang, Xiaoyang Wang, Wenlin Yao, Wenhao Yu

description: EditVal provides a standardized benchmark for quantitatively evaluating text-guided image editing methods across 13 different edit types. It includes a curated dataset with editable attributes and an automated evaluation pipeline using pre-trained vision-language models to assess editing fidelity.
paper_title: EditVal: Benchmarking Diffusion Based Text-Guided Image Editing Methods
authors: Samyadeep Basu, Mehrdad Saberi, Shweta Bhardwaj, Atoosa Malemir Chegini, Daniela Massiceti

description: GameVerse introduces a comprehensive video game benchmark with a novel reflect-and-retry paradigm for VLMs, featuring a cognitive hierarchical taxonomy spanning 15 globally popular games. It enables assessment of how VLMs internalize visual experience and improve policies through video-based reflection, moving beyond traditional fire-and-forget evaluations.
paper_title: GameVerse: Can Vision-Language Models Learn from Video-based Reflection?
authors: Kuan Zhang, Dongchen Liu, Qiyue Zhao, Jinkun Hou, Xinran Zhang

benchmark_name: Visual Anomaly Detection Benchmark for Scientific Workflows
description: Constructs a visual benchmark tailored for process anomaly detection in scientific workflows using VLM-based visual reasoning. It provides a systematic evaluation framework for different levels of supervision through progressively informative prompt configurations in robotic scientific laboratory settings.
paper_title: A VLM-based Method for Visual Anomaly Detection in Robotic Scientific Laboratories
authors: Shiwei Lin, Chenxu Wang, Xiaozhen Ding, Yi Wang, Boyuan Du

description: EvalMuse-40K provides 40K image-text pairs with fine-grained human annotations for evaluating text-to-image generation models. It employs balanced prompt sampling and data re-annotation strategies to ensure diversity and reliability, enabling comprehensive evaluation of image-text alignment metrics.
paper_title: EvalMuse-40K: A Reliable and Fine-Grained Benchmark with Comprehensive Human Annotations for Text-to-Image Generation Model Evaluation
authors: Shuhao Han, Haotian Fan, Jiachen Fu, Liang Li, Tao Li

description: The paper introduces Multi-type Anomaly (MulA), the largest-scale anomaly detection dataset encompassing 72 multi-scale defect types across 26 categories. This benchmark specifically tests industrial anomaly detection capabilities of vision-language models in manufacturing and inspection scenarios.
paper_title: ADSeeker: A Knowledge-Grounded Reasoning Framework for Industry Anomaly Detection and Reasoning
authors: Kai Zhang, Zekai Zhang, Xihe Sun, Anpeng Wang, Jingmeng Nie

description: The paper introduces MSC, a marine wildlife video dataset with grounded segmentation and clip-level captioning. This benchmark provides triplets of video, text, and segmentation masks to facilitate visual grounding and captioning specifically for marine environment understanding, addressing the complexity of underwater scenes and marine object dynamics.
paper_title: MSC: A Marine Wildlife Video Dataset with Grounded Segmentation and Clip-Level Captioning
authors: Quang-Trung Truong, Yuk-Kwan Wong, Vo Hoang Kim Tuyen Dang, Rinaldi Gotama, Duc Thanh Nguyen

description: VEU-Bench is a comprehensive benchmark for video editing understanding that categorizes video editing components across various dimensions from intra-frame features to inter-shot attributes. It encompasses 19 fine-grained tasks across three stages: recognition, reasoning, and judging of video editing elements.
paper_title: VEU-Bench: Towards Comprehensive Understanding of Video Editing
authors: Bozheng Li, Yongliang Wu, Yi Lu, Jiashuo Yu, Licheng Tang

description: MovieTection is a benchmark comprising 14,000 frames paired with detailed captions from films released before and after model training cutoffs. It's designed to assess whether copyrighted content was used in VLM training by testing recognition capabilities.
paper_title: DIS-CO: Discovering Copyrighted Content in VLMs Training Data
authors: André V. Duarte, Xuandong Zhao, Arlindo L. Oliveira, Lei Li

description: ChatBEV-QA is a novel BEV VQA benchmark containing over 137k questions designed to encompass a wide range of traffic scene understanding tasks using bird's-eye-view maps. It includes global scene understanding, vehicle-lane interactions, and vehicle-vehicle interactions for autonomous driving applications.
paper_title: ChatBEV: A Visual Language Model that Understands BEV Maps
authors: Qingyao Xu, Siheng Chen, Guang Chen, Yanfeng Wang, Ya Zhang

task_types: visual question answering, specialized domain reasoning
description: CAD-VQA is a new dataset for evaluating VLMs on CAD-related visual question answering tasks, addressing the lack of benchmarks in specialized domains. It focuses on Computer-Aided Design as an exemplar field requiring precise interpretation of complex structures.
paper_title: How to Determine the Preferred Image Distribution of a Black-Box Vision-Language Model?
authors: Saeid Asgari Taghanaki, Joseph Lambourne, Alana Mongkhounsavath

task_types: product property extraction, knowledge graph construction
description: A human-annotated e-commerce product dataset for benchmarking product property extraction in knowledge graph construction. Tests automated extraction of structured product information from raw product images using VLMs and LLMs.
paper_title: Hierarchical Knowledge Graph Construction from Images for Scalable E-Commerce
authors: Zhantao Yang, Han Zhang, Fangyi Chen, Anudeepsekhar Bolimera, Marios Savvides

description: A synthetic benchmark evaluating VLMs' ability to generate plant simulation parameters in JSON format from drone-based remote sensing images. Tests structural metadata interpretation and parameter estimation for agricultural digital twins.
paper_title: Using Vision Language Foundation Models to Generate Plant Simulation Configurations via In-Context Learning
authors: Heesup Yun, Isaac Kazuo Uyehara, Earl Ranario, Lars Lundqvist, Christine H. Diepenbrock

task_types: time series anomaly detection, point-wise anomaly detection, range-wise anomaly detection, variate-wise anomaly detection
description: VisualTimeAnomaly is a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating multimodal LLMs on time series anomaly detection. It progressively tests point-, range-, and variate-wise anomalies, extending to irregular sampling conditions to assess zero-shot TSAD capabilities.
paper_title: Can Multimodal LLMs Perform Time Series Anomaly Detection?
authors: Xiongxiao Xu, Haoran Wang, Yueqing Liang, Philip S. Yu, Yue Zhao

description: Builds MMMC, a benchmark of professionally produced, discipline-specific educational videos for evaluating educational video generation. Also introduces TeachQuiz metric and proposes Code2Video framework for code-centric educational video generation.
paper_title: Code2Video: A Code-centric Paradigm for Educational Video Generation
authors: Yanzhe Chen, Kevin Qinghong Lin, Mike Zheng Shou

task_types: open-vocabulary semantic segmentation, domain generalization, cross-domain evaluation
description: The first benchmark for Open-Vocabulary Domain Generalization in Semantic Segmentation (OVDG-SS) in autonomous driving. It addresses both unseen domains and unseen categories, covering synthetic-to-real and real-to-real generalization scenarios in urban driving contexts.
paper_title: Open-Vocabulary Domain Generalization in Urban-Scene Segmentation
authors: Dong Zhao, Qi Zang, Nan Pu, Wenjing Li, Nicu Sebe

description: First benchmark for CAD code generation with natural language prompts paired with expert-annotated scripting code for 3D objects. Tests vision-language models' ability to generate correct CAD code that produces accurate 3D designs.
paper_title: Generating CAD Code with Vision-Language Models for 3D Designs
authors: Kamel Alrashedy, Pradyumna Tambwekar, Zulfiqar Zaidi, Megan Langwasser, Wei Xu

description: First open-source dataset for zero-shot road safety assessment using VLMs with street-level images annotated according to iRAP standards. Tests VLMs' ability to classify road safety attributes for infrastructure risk assessment.
paper_title: V-RoAst: Visual Road Assessment. Can VLM be a Road Safety Assessor Using the iRAP Standard?
authors: Natchapon Jongwiriyanurak, Zichao Zeng, June Moh Goo, Xinglei Wang, Ilya Ilyankou

description: Introduces a benchmark for evaluating subject-driven image generation and manipulation models. Tests identity fidelity, prompt adherence, manipulation consistency, and background consistency in complex scenes with multiple subjects.
paper_title: OpenSubject: Leveraging Video-Derived Identity and Diversity Priors for Subject-driven Image Generation and Manipulation
authors: Yexin Liu, Manyuan Zhang, Yueze Wang, Hongyu Li, Dian Zheng

description: A VQA benchmark for assessing VLMs based on Schwartz's value dimensions that capture core human values. Tests preferences and value-oriented content understanding using social media videos.
paper_title: Value-Spectrum: Quantifying Preferences of Vision-Language Models via Value Decomposition in Social Media Contexts
authors: Jingxuan Li, Yuning Yang, Shengqi Yang, Linfan Zhang, Ying Nian Wu

description: A comprehensive benchmark for assessing VLMs' capabilities in plant stress phenotyping across 12 diverse agricultural tasks. It evaluates zero-shot and few-shot performance for specialized agricultural applications, addressing the challenge of limited annotated data in agriculture.
paper_title: Leveraging Vision Language Models for Specialized Agricultural Tasks
authors: Muhammad Arbab Arshad, Talukder Zaki Jubery, Tirtho Roy, Rim Nassiri, Asheesh K. Singh

description: AgriPath-LF16 provides a controlled evaluation of domain effects in crop disease classification with 111k images spanning 16 crops and 41 diseases. It explicitly separates laboratory and field imagery to enable systematic analysis of model performance under domain shift.
paper_title: AgriPath: A Systematic Exploration of Architectural Trade-offs for Crop Disease Classification
authors: Hamza Mooraj, George Pantazopoulos, Alessandro Suglia

description: MAC introduces the task of predicting upcoming characters in live chats using both partially typed text and visual context. It extends traditional text-only auto-completion to multimodal settings, requiring models to ground predictions in shared visual context to better capture user intent in real-time conversations.
paper_title: Router-Suggest: Dynamic Routing for Multimodal Auto-Completion in Visually-Grounded Dialogs
authors: Sandeep Mishra, Devichand Budagam, Anubhab Mandal, Bishal Santra, Pawan Goyal

description: MolVision constructs a benchmark spanning ten diverse molecular datasets, covering classification, regression and description tasks for molecular property prediction. It uniquely integrates both molecular structure as images and textual descriptions to enhance property prediction using Vision-Language Models.
paper_title: MolVision: Molecular Property Prediction with Vision Language Models
authors: Deepan Adak, Yogesh Singh Rawat, Shruti Vyas

description: LUCID is a new large-scale multimodal lunar dataset consisting of 96k high-resolution panchromatic images paired with detailed captions describing lunar terrain characteristics, and 81k question-answer pairs. It enables specialized vision-language models for lunar surface and subsurface characterization in planetary science applications.
paper_title: LLaVA-LE: Large Language-and-Vision Assistant for Lunar Exploration
authors: Gokce Inal, Pouyan Navard, Alper Yilmaz

description: FRB is a compact and well-structured dataset comprising 15 commonly used fonts with two versions: an easy version where 10 sentences are rendered in different fonts, and a hard version where each text sample consists of font names themselves, introducing a stroop effect that challenges model perception in fine-grained font recognition tasks.
paper_title: Texture or Semantics? Vision-Language Models Get Lost in Font Recognition
authors: Zhecheng Li, Guoxian Song, Yujun Cai, Zhen Xiong, Junsong Yuan

description: PtychoBench is a new multi-modal, multi-task benchmark for ptychographic analysis in advanced microscopy workflows. It systematically evaluates domain adaptation strategies for foundation models on visual artifact detection tasks with VLMs and textual parameter recommendation tasks with LLMs in data-scarce scientific regimes.
paper_title: Adapting General-Purpose Foundation Models for X-ray Ptychography in Low-Data Regimes
authors: Robinson Umeike, Neil Getty, Yin Xiangyu, Yi Jiang

task_types: visual element identification, symbolic meaning matching, cultural explanation
description: A multimodal dataset focused on traditional Chinese pun rebus art understanding. Tests VLMs on identifying salient visual elements, matching elements with symbolic meanings, and explaining cultural messages in Chinese art.
paper_title: Creating a Lens of Chinese Culture: A Multimodal Dataset for Chinese Pun Rebus Art Understanding
authors: Tuo Zhang, Tiantian Feng, Yibin Ni, Mengqin Cao, Ruying Liu

description: A high-quality anomaly detection dataset for robotic power line inspection created by domain experts from Hydro-Québec. Features high-resolution images with challenging real-world anomalies and defects with varying severity levels for visual anomaly detection in critical infrastructure applications.
paper_title: CableInspect-AD: An Expert-Annotated Anomaly Detection Dataset
authors: Akshatha Arodi, Margaux Luck, Jean-Luc Bedwani, Aldo Zaimi, Ge Li

task_types: out-of-distribution detection, image classification, semantic segmentation
description: A large-scale dataset for semantic out-of-distribution detection in image classification and semantic segmentation. Addresses limitations of existing OOD benchmarks by focusing on semantic shift challenges and providing scale comparable to modern training datasets.
paper_title: SOOD-ImageNet: a Large-Scale Dataset for Semantic Out-Of-Distribution Image Classification and Semantic Segmentation
authors: Alberto Bacchin, Davide Allegro, Stefano Ghidoni, Emanuele Menegatti

task_types: social media classification, wildfire content analysis
description: WildFireCan-MMD provides multimodal X posts from recent Canadian wildfires, annotated across twelve key themes for wildfire social media analysis. It addresses the underrepresentation of Canadian wildfire contexts in existing datasets.
paper_title: WildFireCan-MMD: A Multimodal Dataset for Classification of User-Generated Content During Wildfires in Canada
authors: Braeden Sherritt, Isar Nejadgholi, Efstratios Aivaliotis, Khaled Mslmani, Marzieh Amini

task_types: visual question answering, coral reef analysis, ecological assessment
description: CoralVQA is the first large-scale VQA dataset for coral reef analysis, containing real-world coral images from 67 genera across 3 oceans. It comprehensively assesses ecological and health-related conditions with domain-specific annotations and multidimensional questions.
paper_title: CoralVQA: A Large-Scale Visual Question Answering Dataset for Coral Reef Image Understanding
authors: Hongyong Han, Wei Wang, Gaowei Zhang, Mingjie Li, Yi Wang

modalities: video, CAN bus data, text annotations
task_types: lane keeping assist evaluation, driving performance assessment, system disengagement analysis
description: OpenLKA is the first open, large-scale dataset for Lane Keeping Assist evaluation from 62 production vehicle models with 400 hours of driving data. It combines CAN bus streams, dash-cam video, and VLM-generated scene annotations for comprehensive LKA performance assessment.
paper_title: OpenLKA: An Open Dataset of Lane Keeping Assist from Recent Car Models under Real-world Driving Conditions
authors: Yuhang Wang, Abdulaziz Alhuraish, Shengming Yuan, Hao Zhou

task_types: composed image retrieval, fashion image retrieval
description: FACap is a large-scale, automatically constructed fashion-domain composed image retrieval dataset. It leverages web-sourced fashion images and a two-stage annotation pipeline using VLM and LLM to generate accurate and detailed modification texts for fine-grained fashion understanding.
paper_title: FACap: A Large-scale Fashion Dataset for Fine-grained Composed Image Retrieval
authors: François Gardères, Shizhe Chen, Camille-Sovanneary Gauthier, Jean Ponce

modalities: video, bounding boxes, segmentation masks, species labels
description: SA-FARI is the largest open-source multi-animal tracking dataset for wild animals, comprising camera trap videos from 741 locations across 4 continents spanning 99 species. It provides exhaustive annotations with masklet identities and combines high species diversity with multi-region coverage.
paper_title: The SA-FARI Dataset: Segment Anything in Footage of Animals for Recognition and Identification
authors: Dante Francisco Wasmuht, Otto Brookes, Maximillian Schall, Pablo Palencia, Chris Beirne

task_types: exoplanet detection, circumstellar disk classification, representation learning
description: POLARIS provides polarimetric imaging data from high-contrast imagers for exoplanetary disk representation learning. It classifies reference star and circumstellar disk images using the full SPHERE/IRDIS polarized-light archive, requiring minimal manual labeling for exoplanet detection.
paper_title: POLARIS: A High-contrast Polarimetric Imaging Benchmark Dataset for Exoplanetary Disk Representation Learning
authors: Fangyi Cao, Bin Ren, Zihao Wang, Shiwei Fu, Youbin Mo

description: Focuses on activity understanding in molecular biology laboratories with fine-grained hierarchical annotations. Introduces transparent solution tracking and multimodal action recognition benchmarks for specialized scientific domain applications.
paper_title: ProBio: A Protocol-guided Multimodal Dataset for Molecular Biology Lab
authors: Jieming Cui, Ziren Gong, Baoxiong Jia, Siyuan Huang, Zilong Zheng

description: Introduces a new sentiment system designed for video recommendation scenarios, moving beyond traditional positive/negative sentiment to describe sentimental styles. Focuses on multimodal sentiment analysis for video understanding in recommendation contexts.
paper_title: A Multimodal Sentiment Dataset for Video Recommendation
authors: Hongxuan Tang, Hao Liu, Xinyan Xiao, Hua Wu

description: Framework for generating multimodal datasets with explicitly calculable mutual information between modalities. Enables systematic studies of mutual information estimators and multimodal self-supervised learning with known ground truth MI values.
paper_title: Multimodal Datasets with Controllable Mutual Information
authors: Raheem Karim Hashmani, Garrett W. Merz, Helen Qu, Mariel Pettee, Kyle Cranmer

description: Introduces Multimodal Chat Translation task combining dialogue history, visual context, and sentiment for more accurate translations. Tests translation quality enhancement through multimodal and sentiment feature incorporation.
paper_title: MSCTD: A Multimodal Sentiment Chat Translation Dataset
authors: Yunlong Liang, Fandong Meng, Jinan Xu, Yufeng Chen, Jie Zhou

description: First dataset for fake news video explanation, requiring natural language explanations of why multimodal news videos are identified as fake. Tests comprehensive understanding of correlation and consistency between visual and textual content.
paper_title: Multimodal Fake News Video Explanation: Dataset, Analysis and Evaluation
authors: Lizhi Chen, Zhong Qian, Peifeng Li, Qiaoming Zhu

description: First German multimodal sarcasm detection dataset with full-modal annotations across text, audio, and video. Tests sarcasm detection in conversational settings and evaluates human-model alignment in multimodal understanding.
paper_title: MuSaG: A Multimodal German Sarcasm Dataset with Full-Modal Annotations
authors: Aaron Scott, Maike Züfle, Jan Niehues

description: Focuses on biomedical experiment method classification using automated distant supervision from curated databases. Tests multimodal understanding in scientific domains with emphasis on grounding visual concepts with language.
paper_title: MELINDA: A Multimodal Dataset for Biomedical Experiment Method Classification
authors: Te-Lin Wu, Shikhar Singh, Sayan Paul, Gully Burns, Nanyun Peng

description: Peak-centered multimodal dataset with immediate-recall annotation paradigm for emotion recognition. Addresses temporal label noise in traditional whole-trial labeling with event-centered timestamps and intensity annotations.
paper_title: FIRMED: A Peak-Centered Multimodal Dataset with Fine-Grained Annotation for Emotion Recognition
authors: Hao Tang, Songyun Xie, Xinzhou Xie, Can Liao, Bohan Li

modalities: video, EEG, ECG, EOG, EMG, PPG, BCG
task_types: emotion recognition, affective computing, physiological signal analysis
description: MAD provides a unique multimodal affective computing benchmark by synchronously collecting diverse physiological signals (EEG, ECG, EOG, EMG, PPG, BCG) with tri-view RGB-D facial videos. It introduces a three-level emotion annotation framework spanning stimulus elicitation, subjective cognition, and behavioral expression for comprehensive emotion modeling.
paper_title: MAD: A Multimodal and Multi-perspective Affective Dataset with Hierarchical Annotations
authors: Shengwei Guo, Yunqing Qiao, Wenzhan Zhang, Bo Liu, Yong Wang

description: ComicScene154 introduces the first manually annotated dataset for scene-level narrative analysis in comics, combining text and imagery in ways distinct from purely textual or audiovisual media. It provides a unique resource for computational narrative understanding by conceptualizing comics as multimodal storytelling data.
paper_title: ComicScene154: A Scene Dataset for Comic Analysis
authors: Sandro Paval, Ivan P. Yamshchikov, Pascal Meißner

task_types: tree detection, instance segmentation, forest monitoring
description: This dataset provides the first photorealistic virtual forest environment for multimodal tree detection and instance segmentation. It addresses the domain gap between common outdoor benchmarks and real forest datasets by creating a large-scale virtual forest monitoring benchmark with multimodal transformer-based algorithms.
paper_title: M2fNet: Multi-modal Forest Monitoring Network on Large-scale Virtual Dataset
authors: Yawen Lu, Yunhan Huang, Su Sun, Tansi Zhang, Xuewen Zhang

description: MoTime provides the first comprehensive suite of multimodal time series forecasting datasets that pair temporal signals with external modalities like text, metadata, and images. It uniquely supports evaluation under both standard forecasting and cold-start scenarios where no historical data is available.
paper_title: MoTime: A Dataset Suite for Multimodal Time Series Forecasting
authors: Xin Zhou, Weiqing Wang, Francisco J. Baldán, Wray Buntine, Christoph Bergmeir

task_types: lecture captioning, visual synthesis, multimodal understanding
description: This dataset provides 180+ hours of aligned lecture slides and speech for multimodal understanding of educational content. It uniquely focuses on educational scenarios with tasks for automatic captioning and visual figure synthesis, addressing challenges in crossmodal alignment, technical language, and long-range sequences.
paper_title: Multimodal Lecture Presentations Dataset: Understanding Multimodality in Educational Slides
authors: Dong Won Lee, Chaitanya Ahuja, Paul Pu Liang, Sanika Natu, Louis-Philippe Morency

description: Real-IAD D3 is a high-precision multimodal dataset for industrial anomaly detection that uniquely incorporates an additional pseudo-3D modality generated through photometric stereo, alongside high-resolution RGB images and micrometer-level 3D point clouds. It features 20 categories with finer defects, diverse anomalies, and greater scale compared to existing datasets.
paper_title: Real-IAD D3: A Real-World 2D/Pseudo-3D/3D Dataset for Industrial Anomaly Detection
authors: Wenbing Zhu, Lidong Wang, Ziqing Zhou, Chengjie Wang, Yurui Pan

description: BioVITA is a novel visual-textual-acoustic alignment framework for biological applications featuring a large-scale training dataset with 1.3 million audio clips and 2.3 million images covering 14,133 species. It develops cross-modal retrieval benchmarks covering all possible directional retrieval across three modalities (image-to-audio, audio-to-text, text-to-image) at three taxonomic levels.
paper_title: BioVITA: Biological Dataset, Model, and Benchmark for Visual-Textual-Acoustic Alignment
authors: Risa Shinoda, Kaede Shiohara, Nakamasa Inoue, Kuniaki Saito, Hiroaki Santo

description: This paper creates a multimodal hyperbole detection dataset from Weibo (Chinese social media) that treats text and images as two modalities to explore their roles in hyperbole detection. It evaluates cross-domain performance across five different topics and serves as a benchmark for multimodal hyperbole detection research.
paper_title: Image Matters: A New Dataset and Empirical Study for Multimodal Hyperbole Detection
authors: Huixuan Zhang, Xiaojun Wan

modalities: transaction data, geo-location, dialogue embeddings, product data
description: MBD is the first industrial-scale publicly available multimodal banking dataset containing information on more than 2M corporate clients with multiple data sources: 950M bank transactions, 1B geo position events, 5M dialogue embeddings, and monthly product purchases. It introduces novel multimodal benchmarks for future purchase prediction and modality matching tasks.
paper_title: Multimodal Banking Dataset: Understanding Client Needs through Event Sequences
authors: Dzhambulat Mollaev, Alexander Kostin, Maria Postnova, Ivan Karpukhin, Ivan Kireev

description: This paper presents a novel multimodal dataset for automating detection and localisation of multi-component extended radio galaxies and their corresponding infrared hosts. It comprises 4,155 instances in 2,800 images with both radio and infrared modalities, featuring the first publicly accessible dataset with highly sensitive radio telescope images and instance-level annotations.
paper_title: A Multimodal Dataset and Benchmark for Radio Galaxy and Infrared Host Detection
authors: Nikhel Gupta, Zeeshan Hayder, Ray P. Norris, Minh Hyunh, Lars Petersson

description: ViMACSA is a new Vietnamese multimodal dataset for Aspect-Category Sentiment Analysis consisting of 4,876 text-image pairs with 14,618 fine-grained annotations for both text and image in the hotel domain. It addresses the gap in existing datasets that focus on textual annotations while neglecting fine-grained information in images.
paper_title: New Benchmark Dataset and Fine-Grained Cross-Modal Fusion Framework for Vietnamese Multimodal Aspect-Category Sentiment Analysis
authors: Quy Hoang Nguyen, Minh-Van Truong Nguyen, Kiet Van Nguyen

task_types: conversational recommendation, preference inference, satisfaction prediction
