paper_title: AccidentBench: Benchmarking Multimodal Understanding and Reasoning in Vehicle Accidents and Beyond
authors: Shangding Gu, Xiaohan Wang, Donghao Ying, Haoyu Zhao, Runing Yang

task_types: classification, object detection, segmentation, visual grounding
description: FACET evaluates fairness in computer vision across demographic attributes like gender and skin tone, testing for performance disparities in classification, detection, and segmentation tasks. It provides manually annotated person-related attributes and fine-grained classes with expert reviewers, enabling intersectional analysis of bias across multiple demographic dimensions.
paper_title: FACET: Fairness in Computer Vision Evaluation Benchmark
authors: Laura Gustafson, Chloe Rolland, Nikhila Ravi, Quentin Duval, Aaron Adcock

task_types: bias evaluation, stereotype assessment, open-ended QA
description: BBQ-V comprehensively assesses stereotype biases in Large Multimodal Models across nine categories and 50 sub-categories using real, multi-actor images. It addresses gaps in bias evaluation by providing visually grounded scenarios that challenge models to reason accurately about visual stereotypes in real-world contexts.
paper_title: BBQ-V: Benchmarking Visual Stereotype Bias in Large Multimodal Models
authors: Vishal Narnaware, Ashmal Vayani, Rohit Gupta, Sirnam Swetha, Mubarak Shah

task_types: vulnerability assessment, attack simulation, robustness evaluation
description: AgentHazard investigates vulnerabilities in mobile GUI agents through scalable attack simulation with manipulated screen content by unprivileged third parties. It includes dynamic task execution environment with 58 tasks and static dataset from 210 screenshots across 14 commercial apps to test agent robustness against misleading content.
paper_title: Hijacking JARVIS: Benchmarking Mobile GUI Agents against Unprivileged Third Parties
authors: Guohong Liu, Jialei Ye, Jiacheng Liu, Yuanchun Li, Wei Liu

description: SecureWebArena is the first holistic benchmark for evaluating security of LVLM-based web agents. It includes six simulated realistic web environments and 2,970 trajectories with a structured taxonomy of six attack vectors spanning user-level and environment-level manipulations, along with multi-layered evaluation analyzing internal reasoning, behavioral trajectory, and task outcomes.
paper_title: SecureWebArena: A Holistic Security Evaluation Benchmark for LVLM-based Web Agents
authors: Zonghao Ying, Yangguang Shao, Jianle Gan, Gan Xu, Junjie Shen

description: This benchmark introduces a comprehensive safety evaluation suite for Vision LLMs covering both out-of-distribution generalization and adversarial robustness. It presents two novel VQA datasets with variants and proposes attack strategies for misleading VLLMs, along with jailbreaking strategies targeting vision or language components.
paper_title: How Many Unicorns Are in This Image? A Safety Evaluation Benchmark for Vision LLMs
authors: Haoqin Tu, Chenhang Cui, Zijun Wang, Yiyang Zhou, Bingchen Zhao

task_types: safety evaluation, risk assessment, embodied agent safety
description: SafeMindBench is a multimodal benchmark with 5,558 samples spanning four task categories across high-risk scenarios such as sabotage, harm, privacy, and illegal behavior. It systematically evaluates safety risks in embodied LLM agents across four reasoning stages and three safety constraint types.
paper_title: SafeMind: Benchmarking and Mitigating Safety Risks in Embodied LLM Agents
authors: Ruolin Chen, Yinqian Sun, Jihang Wang, Mingyang Lv, Qian Zhang

description: SafePlan-Bench establishes a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating task-planning safety in LLM-based embodied agents. It encompasses 2,027 daily tasks distributed across 8 distinct hazard categories, revealing that agents can exhibit unsafe behaviors even without adversarial inputs or malicious intent.
paper_title: A Framework for Benchmarking and Aligning Task-Planning Safety in LLM-Based Embodied Agents
authors: Yuting Huang, Leilei Ding, Zhipeng Tang, Tianfu Wang, Xinrui Lin

description: AdvCUA is the first benchmark aligned with real-world tactics, techniques, and procedures (TTPs) in MITRE ATT&CK Enterprise Matrix, comprising 140 tasks including malicious tasks and end-to-end kill chains. It systematically evaluates computer-use agents under realistic enterprise OS security threats in multi-host environments.
paper_title: Code Agent can be an End-to-end System Hacker: Benchmarking Real-world Threats of Computer-use Agent
authors: Weidi Luo, Qiming Zhang, Tianyu Lu, Xiaogeng Liu, Bin Hu

modalities: image, infrared, audio, depth, radar, gas measurements, temperature, humidity
task_types: safety assessment, anomaly detection, multimodal fusion
description: First multimodal benchmark for safety assessment in industrial inspection scenarios using real-world data from inspection robots. Covers five industrial scenarios with pixel-level annotations and seven synchronized sensing modalities for comprehensive safety assessment.
paper_title: InspecSafe-V1: A Multimodal Benchmark for Safety Assessment in Industrial Inspection Scenarios
authors: Zeyi Liu, Shuang Liu, Jihai Min, Zhaoheng Zhang, Jun Cen

task_types: safety evaluation, risk detection, content moderation
description: Comprehensive benchmark for safety evaluation of text-to-image models with hierarchical risk taxonomy (6 primary categories, 14 subcategories). Features effective risky prompts annotated with both hierarchical labels and detailed risk reasons for fine-grained safety assessment.
paper_title: T2I-RiskyPrompt: A Benchmark for Safety Evaluation, Attack, and Defense on Text-to-Image Model
authors: Chenyu Zhang, Tairen Zhang, Lanjun Wang, Ruidong Chen, Wenhui Li

task_types: safety evaluation, multilingual assessment, cross-modal risk analysis
description: Benchmark for safety evaluation of multilingual vision-language models with harmful image-text pairs across 10 languages. Explicitly partitioned into image-dominant and text-dominant subsets to disentangle risk sources and assess cross-modal safety interactions.
paper_title: Lingua-SafetyBench: A Benchmark for Safety Evaluation of Multilingual Vision-Language Models
authors: Enyi Shi, Pengyang Shao, Yanxin Zhang, Chenhang Cui, Jiayi Lyu

description: Benchmark for assessing LMM vulnerabilities against AI-generated extremist content including photorealistic images and text. Simulates real-world events and malicious use cases using state-of-the-art image generation techniques to test safety mechanisms.
paper_title: ExtremeAIGC: Benchmarking LMM Vulnerability to AI-Generated Extremist Content
authors: Bhavik Chandna, Mariam Aboujenane, Usman Naseem

description: Multimodal, multilingual benchmark for detecting harmful and offensive humor with strict annotation distinguishing Safe from Harmful (Explicit/Implicit) categories. Tests deep reasoning across text, images, and videos in English, Arabic, and universal contexts.
paper_title: Harm or Humor: A Multimodal, Multilingual Benchmark for Overt and Covert Harmful Humor
authors: Ahmed Sharshar, Hosam Elgendy, Saad El Dine Ahmed, Yasser Rohaim, Yuxia Wang

task_types: preference assessment, safety evaluation, bias detection
description: Large-scale video preference benchmark evaluating video generation across five critical aspects: Alignment, Safety, Fineness, Coherence & Consistency, and Bias & Fairness. Incorporates 28 fine-grained criteria and introduces MJ-VIDEO reward model for precise preference assessment in video generation.
paper_title: MJ-VIDEO: Fine-Grained Benchmarking and Rewarding Video Preferences in Video Generation
authors: Haibo Tong, Zhaoyang Wang, Zhaorun Chen, Haonian Ji, Shi Qiu

benchmark_name: Unified Framework for Assessing Societal Bias
description: A unified framework for systematically evaluating gender, race, and age biases in VLMs across professions with automated pipeline to generate synthetic datasets that conceal demographic information. Covers all inference modes including image-to-text, text-to-text, text-to-image, and image-to-image.
paper_title: A Unified Framework and Dataset for Assessing Societal Bias in Vision-Language Models
authors: Ashutosh Sathe, Prachi Jain, Sunayana Sitaram

description: Benchmark for uncovering demographic biases in VLMs across gender, race, age, and skin tone with the most diverse coverage to date. Introduces interpretable GRAS Bias Score and reveals methodological insight about requiring multiple question formulations for VQA bias evaluation.
paper_title: Ask Me Again Differently: GRAS for Measuring Bias in Vision Language Models on Gender, Race, Age, and Skin Tone
authors: Shaivi Malik, Hasnat Md Abdullah, Sriparna Saha, Amit Sheth

description: Moral Dilemma Simulation benchmark grounded in Moral Foundation Theory that enables mechanistic analysis of moral reasoning in Vision-Language Models through orthogonal manipulation of visual and contextual variables. Reveals how visual inputs alter moral decision-making and bypass text-based safety mechanisms.
paper_title: Visual Distraction Undermines Moral Reasoning in Vision-Language Models
authors: Xinyi Yang, Chenheng Xu, Weijun Hong, Ce Mo, Qian Wang

description: Benchmark for assessing gender, race, and color stereotypes in LVLMs using Stereotype Content Model (SCM) based evaluation metrics. Addresses limitations of previous studies by considering the effect of color and using content-aware evaluation.
paper_title: A Stereotype Content Analysis on Color-related Social Bias in Large Vision Language Models
authors: Junhyuk Choi, Minju Kim, Yeseon Hong, Bugeun Kim

task_types: safety assessment, semantic steering evaluation, refusal detection
description: A benchmark for situational safety under semantic cues that evaluates whether multimodal safety behavior in VLMs can be steered by simple semantic interventions. Includes evaluation protocol separating behavioral refusal, grounded safety reasoning, and false refusals.
paper_title: SAVeS: Steering Safety Judgments in Vision-Language Models via Semantic Cues
authors: Carlos Hinojosa, Clemens Grange, Bernard Ghanem

description: SafetyPairs introduces a novel benchmark of counterfactual image pairs that differ only in safety-critical features while maintaining safety-irrelevant details. It contains over 3,020 images spanning 9 safety categories, enabling fine-grained evaluation of vision-language models' abilities to distinguish between subtly different images for safety assessment.
paper_title: SafetyPairs: Isolating Safety Critical Image Features with Counterfactual Image Generation
authors: Alec Helbling, Shruti Palaskar, Kundan Krishna, Polo Chau, Leon Gatys

task_types: counterfactual visual question answering, bias detection, occupation classification
description: GenderBias-VL is the first benchmark to evaluate occupation-related gender bias in LVLMs using counterfactual visual questions under individual fairness criteria. It comprises 34,581 visual question counterfactual pairs covering 177 occupations, using text-to-image diffusion models to generate occupation images and their gender counterfactuals for granular bias detection that group fairness may overlook.
paper_title: GenderBias-\emph{VL}: Benchmarking Gender Bias in Vision Language Models via Counterfactual Probing
authors: Yisong Xiao, Aishan Liu, QianJia Cheng, Zhenfei Yin, Siyuan Liang

description: DriveBench evaluates VLM reliability for autonomous driving across 17 settings including clean, corrupted, and text-only inputs. It encompasses 20,498 question-answer pairs across four mainstream driving tasks, specifically designed to test visual grounding and interpretable decision-making in safety-critical scenarios.
paper_title: Are VLMs Ready for Autonomous Driving? An Empirical Study from the Reliability, Data, and Metric Perspectives
authors: Shaoyuan Xie, Lingdong Kong, Yuhao Dong, Chonghao Sima, Wenwei Zhang

description: Creates the largest benchmark test set for assessing generalization ability of deepfake detection models, comprising 10 public large-scale deepfake datasets with 5 main evaluation metrics. It addresses the 'Penny-Wise and Pound-Foolish' issue by evaluating both deepfake detection and object classification performance.
paper_title: Penny-Wise and Pound-Foolish in Deepfake Detection
authors: Yabin Wang, Zhiwu Huang, Su Zhou, Adam Prugel-Bennett, Xiaopeng Hong

description: VPI-COCO provides a benchmark for evaluating VLM-based attribute inference attacks and protection methods, comprising 522 images with hierarchically structured privacy questions and non-private counterparts. It enables fine-grained evaluation of privacy preservation methods in terms of both privacy protection and user experience.
paper_title: Who Can See Through You? Adversarial Shielding Against VLM-Based Attribute Inference Attacks
authors: Yucheng Fan, Jiawei Chen, Yu Tian, Zhaoxia Yin

description: Constructs a benchmark dataset for evaluating LVLM copyright detection capabilities, comprising positive samples that violate IP figure copyrights and negative samples that resemble but don't infringe. It addresses the gap in comprehensive datasets for copyright infringement detection in generative AI content.
paper_title: Can Large Vision-Language Models Detect Images Copyright Infringement from GenAI?
authors: Qipan Xu, Zhenting Wang, Xiaoxiao He, Ligong Han, Ruixiang Tang

description: Contributes a new benchmark of 1,000 highly-aligned pairs for testing safety under distributional shift in vision-language models. The benchmark evaluates NSFW content detection and mitigation while assessing how safety measures affect model performance on general tasks.
paper_title: SafeR-CLIP: Mitigating NSFW Content in Vision-Language Models While Preserving Pre-Trained Knowledge
authors: Adeel Yousaf, Joseph Fioresi, James Beetham, Amrit Singh Bedi, Mubarak Shah

description: MM-SpuBench is a comprehensive, human-verified benchmark dataset consisting of image-class pairs annotated with core and spurious attributes, grounded in a taxonomy of nine distinct types of spurious correlations. It evaluates multimodal large language models' robustness to spurious biases and their tendency to exploit superficial correlations.
paper_title: MM-SpuBench: Towards Better Understanding of Spurious Biases in Multimodal LLMs
authors: Wenqian Ye, Bohan Liu, Guangtao Zheng, Di Wang, Yunsheng Ma

task_types: face anti-spoofing, attack classification, reasoning, localization
description: The benchmark introduces comprehensive datasets for face anti-spoofing tasks including coarse-grained classification, fine-grained classification, reasoning, and attack localization. It enables explainable face anti-spoofing with multimodal reasoning capabilities.
paper_title: FaceShield: Explainable Face Anti-Spoofing with Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Hongyang Wang, Yichen Shi, Zhuofu Tao, Yuhao Gao, Liepiao Zhang

description: The first large-scale Visual Question Answering dataset tailored for Face Anti-Spoofing. It covers 14 spoofing attack types and provides high-quality Chain-of-Thought VQA annotations to enhance model interpretability and reasoning in face anti-spoofing tasks.
paper_title: Harnessing Chain-of-Thought Reasoning in Multimodal Large Language Models for Face Anti-Spoofing
authors: Honglu Zhang, Zhiqin Fang, Ningning Zhao, Saihui Hou, Long Ma

description: The paper introduces the multimodal misinformation reasoning (MMR) dataset with over 8K image-text pairs containing both reasoning processes and classification labels. This benchmark specifically tests multimodal misinformation detection through adaptive multi-dimensional thinking, providing task-specific reasoning trajectories for training detectors.
paper_title: MMD-Thinker: Adaptive Multi-Dimensional Thinking for Multimodal Misinformation Detection
authors: Junjie Wu, Guohong Fu

description: The paper constructs PAPI, the largest dataset for studying private attribute profiling in personal images, comprising 2,510 images from 251 individuals with 3,012 annotated privacy attributes. This benchmark tests VLMs' ability to infer sensitive personal information including age, health, personality, and social traits from image collections.
paper_title: The Eye of Sherlock Holmes: Uncovering User Private Attribute Profiling via Vision-Language Model Agentic Framework
authors: Feiran Liu, Yuzhe Zhang, Xinyi Huang, Yinan Peng, Xinfeng Li

description: The first expert-annotated multimodal benchmark dataset of over 2,200 TikTok videos labeled by clinical psychiatrists for detecting pro-bigorexia content. Features five categories and eighteen fine-grained subcategories to identify harmful content that promotes muscle dysmorphic behaviors and cognitions, particularly targeting adolescent males.
paper_title: BigTokDetect: A Clinically-Informed Vision-Language Modeling Framework for Detecting Pro-Bigorexia Videos on TikTok
authors: Minh Duc Chu, Kshitij Pawar, Zihao He, Roxanna Sharifi, Ross Sonnenblick

task_types: safety evaluation, over-refusal detection, safe completion
description: A large-scale multimodal benchmark focused on over-refusal and safe completion in VLMs across 12 hazard categories. Tests dual-use scenarios where instructions are harmless but accompanying images contain harmful content, evaluating models' ability to achieve safe completion rather than binary refusal or dangerous generation.
paper_title: DUAL-Bench: Measuring Over-Refusal and Robustness in Vision-Language Models
authors: Kaixuan Ren, Preslav Nakov, Usman Naseem

task_types: scene understanding, visual question answering, empathetic captioning, visual grounding, multilinguality, image resilience
description: A unified evaluation framework designed to characterize human-centered alignment across realistic, socially grounded visual contexts. Contains 32,000 expert-verified image-question pairs spanning seven tasks including scene understanding, empathetic captioning, and multilingual VQA, with metrics covering fairness, ethics, inclusivity, empathy, and robustness.
paper_title: HumaniBench: A Human-Centric Framework for Large Multimodal Models Evaluation
authors: Shaina Raza, Aravind Narayanan, Vahid Reza Khazaie, Ashmal Vayani, Ahmed Y. Radwan

task_types: image captioning, visual question answering, visual grounding
description: A construction safety inspection dataset featuring 10,000 construction site images with annotations for three interconnected tasks: image captioning, safety rule violation VQA, and construction element visual grounding. Enables evaluation of VLMs for safety inspection applications.
paper_title: Are Large Pre-trained Vision Language Models Effective Construction Safety Inspectors?
authors: Xuezheng Chen, Zhengbo Zou

description: First large-scale multimodal question-answering benchmark comprising 228K examples across 18 sub-tasks for traffic safety. Encompasses diverse traffic safety queries from accidents and corner cases to common safety knowledge for autonomous driving applications.
paper_title: SafeDriveRAG: Towards Safe Autonomous Driving with Knowledge Graph-based Retrieval-Augmented Generation
authors: Hao Ye, Mengshi Qi, Zhaohong Liu, Liang Liu, Huadong Ma

description: A large-scale multimodal safety dataset comprising over 30K red-teaming instances spanning 51 diverse risk categories. Tests VLM safety vulnerabilities through adaptive red-teaming strategies covering diverse adversarial patterns.
paper_title: ARMs: Adaptive Red-Teaming Agent against Multimodal Models with Plug-and-Play Attacks
authors: Zhaorun Chen, Xun Liu, Mintong Kang, Jiawei Zhang, Minzhou Pan

task_types: two-alternative forced choice, multiple-choice socioeconomic inference, numeric salary recommendation
description: A face-only counterfactual evaluation paradigm for measuring social bias in VLMs using controlled demographic edits of real photos. Creates counterfactual variants by editing only facial attributes while keeping all other visual factors fixed to isolate demographic effects.
paper_title: Measuring Social Bias in Vision-Language Models with Face-Only Counterfactuals from Real Photos
authors: Haodong Chen, Qiang Huang, Jiaqi Zhao, Qiuping Jiang, Xiaojun Chang

description: Dynamic sandbox environment with safety detection benchmark for mobile GUI agents, featuring realistic trajectories with fine-grained annotations to detect safety concerns like system compromise and privacy leakage in mobile environments.
paper_title: OS-Sentinel: Towards Safety-Enhanced Mobile GUI Agents via Hybrid Validation in Realistic Workflows
authors: Qiushi Sun, Mukai Li, Zhoumianze Liu, Zhihui Xie, Fangzhi Xu

description: The first identity-affiliation dataset comprising seven typical scenarios for benchmarking VLMs' susceptibility to identity-affiliation leakage. It tests privacy risks where models can embed associations between facial identities and private property/social relationships from private photos.
paper_title: DP^2-VL: Private Photo Dataset Protection by Data Poisoning for Vision-Language Models
authors: Hongyi Miao, Jun Jia, Xincheng Wang, Qianli Ma, Wei Sun

description: A comprehensive benchmark for deepfake detection across diverse generative models including 3 held-in and 20 held-out datasets. Tests VLMs' ability to distinguish real from AI-generated fake images across text-to-image generation, image editing and adversarial attacks.
paper_title: AntifakePrompt: Prompt-Tuned Vision-Language Models are Fake Image Detectors
authors: You-Ming Chang, Chen Yeh, Wei-Chen Chiu, Ning Yu

task_types: contextual privacy reasoning, geolocation disclosure assessment
description: A benchmark that challenges VLMs to interpret latent social norms and contextual cues in real-world images to determine appropriate levels of location disclosure. It evaluates how well models respect contextual integrity in privacy-sensitive geolocation scenarios.
paper_title: Do Vision-Language Models Respect Contextual Integrity in Location Disclosure?
authors: Ruixin Yang, Ethan Mendes, Arthur Wang, James Hays, Sauvik Das

task_types: risk identification, emergency response, safety classification
description: VERI diagnoses overreaction bias in VLMs for emergency recognition using 200 synthetic images (100 contrastive pairs) and 50 real-world images. Each emergency scene is paired with a visually similar but safe counterpart to specifically test false positive rates in safety-critical scenarios.
paper_title: Better Safe Than Sorry? Overreaction Problem of Vision Language Models in Visual Emergency Recognition
authors: Dasol Choi, Seunghyun Lee, Youngsook Song

description: ConfusedTourist is a cultural adversarial robustness suite that tests VLMs' stability when exposed to multiple, potentially conflicting cultural cues in the same image. It evaluates whether models can maintain accuracy when faced with perturbed geographical and cultural information through image-stacking perturbations.
paper_title: Vision Language Models are Confused Tourists
authors: Patrick Amadeus Irawan, Ikhlasul Akmal Hanif, Muhammad Dehan Al Kautsar, Genta Indra Winata, Fajri Koto

task_types: misleading intent detection, misleading source attribution, creator desire inference
description: Large-scale benchmark for multimodal misinformation detection focusing on creator intent analysis. Features intent-guided simulation framework that models both desired influence and execution plans of news creators across visual and textual manipulations.
paper_title: Seeing Through Deception: Uncovering Misleading Creator Intent in Multimodal News with Vision-Language Models
authors: Jiaying Wu, Fanxiao Li, Zihang Fu, Min-Yen Kan, Bryan Hooi

description: A novel dataset of 3,600 image-text pairs designed to evaluate safety calibration across both undersafety and oversafety in VLMs. The dataset contains pairs that are visually or textually similar but differ in safety, enabling systematic assessment of safety calibration.
paper_title: VSCBench: Bridging the Gap in Vision-Language Model Safety Calibration
authors: Jiahui Geng, Qing Li, Zongxiong Chen, Yuxia Wang, Derui Zhu

task_types: deepfake detection, artifact localization, artifact explanation
description: The first dataset for explainable deepfake detection in videos with manual annotations including text descriptions and click locations for artifacts. Enables development of explainable models that can both detect and explain deepfake artifacts.
paper_title: ExDDV: A New Dataset for Explainable Deepfake Detection in Video
authors: Vlad Hondru, Eduard Hogea, Darian Onchis, Radu Tudor Ionescu

description: A bilingual Korean-English multimodal dataset for training and evaluating jailbreak detectors in financial applications. Emphasizes domain realism through finance-relevant queries paired with image-grounded threats.
paper_title: FENCE: A Financial and Multimodal Jailbreak Detection Dataset
authors: Mirae Kim, Seonghun Jeong, Youngjun Kwak

description: A comprehensive multimodal dataset pairing images and videos with established persuasion principles across commercial, subjective/behavioral, and adversarial contexts. Evaluates how Large Vision-Language Models function as persuadees and their susceptibility to multimodal persuasive content.
paper_title: MMPersuade: A Dataset and Evaluation Framework for Multimodal Persuasion
authors: Haoyi Qiu, Yilun Zhou, Pranav Narayanan Venkit, Kung-Hsiang Huang, Jiaxin Zhang

task_types: collision detection, collision prediction, autonomous driving evaluation
description: CARLA-Collide provides multimodal dataset capturing realistic collision events across diverse road networks for end-to-end autonomous driving evaluation. Real-Collide offers dashcam videos with collision detection annotations for real-world driving scenarios.
paper_title: Collision-Aware Vision-Language Learning for End-to-End Driving with Multimodal Infraction Datasets
authors: Alex Koran, Dimitrios Sinodinos, Hadi Hojjati, Takuya Nanri, Fangge Chen

description: HintsOfTruth uniquely combines real-world and synthetic multimodal content for checkworthiness detection, addressing the challenge of identifying claims that warrant fact-checking. The dataset's mix of authentic and generated image/claim pairs provides a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating detection methods against diverse misinformation types.
paper_title: HintsOfTruth: A Multimodal Checkworthiness Detection Dataset with Real and Synthetic Claims
authors: Michiel van der Meer, Pavel Korshunov, Sébastien Marcel, Lonneke van der Plas

description: BanglaAbuseMeme is the first Bengali meme dataset for abusive content detection in a low-resource setting. It addresses the gap in benchmark datasets for Bengali memes (images with Bengali text embedded) and enables training of AI models to detect and flag abusive memes that integrate images with Bengali text.
paper_title: BanglaAbuseMeme: A Dataset for Bengali Abusive Meme Classification
authors: Mithun Das, Animesh Mukherjee

task_types: hate speech detection, temporal localization, video classification
description: HateClipSeg is a large-scale multimodal dataset with both video-level and segment-level annotations, comprising over 11,714 segments labeled across five offensive categories (Hateful, Insulting, Sexual, Violence, Self-Harm) with explicit target victim labels. It proposes three novel tasks: Trimmed Hateful Video Classification, Temporal Hateful Video Localization, and Online Hateful Video Classification.
paper_title: HateClipSeg: A Segment-Level Annotated Dataset for Fine-Grained Hate Video Detection
authors: Han Wang, Zhuoran Wang, Roy Ka-Wei Lee

task_types: reliability checks, originality checks, AI detection
description: MM Health addresses health misinformation detection with a large-scale multimodal dataset including both human-generated and AI-generated content across textual and visual modalities. It provides benchmarks for reliability checks, originality checks, and fine-grained AI detection in health misinformation.
paper_title: From Generation to Detection: A Multimodal Multi-Task Dataset for Benchmarking Health Misinformation
authors: Zhihao Zhang, Yiran Zhang, Xiyue Zhou, Liting Huang, Imran Razzak

description: Collects hateful and non-hateful memes from Pinterest to evaluate out-of-sample performance and assess generalizability of Facebook's Hateful Memes Challenge dataset. Highlights gaps between synthetic examples and real-world memes.
paper_title: Memes in the Wild: Assessing the Generalizability of the Hateful Memes Challenge Dataset
authors: Hannah Rose Kirk, Yennie Jun, Paulius Rauba, Gal Wachtel, Ruining Li

task_types: spatial reasoning, mental rotation, spatial visualization
description: A multi-faceted benchmark for understanding spatial reasoning in VLMs covering spatial relations, orientation and navigation, mental rotation, and spatial visualization. Assesses performance in both synthetic and real-world images.
paper_title: Mind the Gap: Benchmarking Spatial Reasoning in Vision-Language Models
authors: Ilias Stogiannidis, Steven McDonagh, Sotirios A. Tsaftaris

description: An interactive multimodal benchmark based on sliding tile puzzles to evaluate spatial reasoning capabilities of VLMs acting as agents. Supports visual 3D, 2D, and text-based input modalities for comprehensive assessment.
paper_title: iVISPAR -- An Interactive Visual-Spatial Reasoning Benchmark for VLMs
authors: Julius Mayer, Mohamad Ballout, Serwan Jassim, Farbod Nosrat Nezami, Elia Bruni

description: NuScenes-SpatialQA is the first large-scale ground-truth-based QA benchmark specifically designed to evaluate spatial understanding and reasoning capabilities of VLMs in autonomous driving scenarios. It systematically evaluates both spatial understanding and reasoning across multiple dimensions using 3D scene graphs.
paper_title: NuScenes-SpatialQA: A Spatial Understanding and Reasoning Benchmark for Vision-Language Models in Autonomous Driving
authors: Kexin Tian, Jingrui Mao, Yunlong Zhang, Jiwan Jiang, Yang Zhou

task_types: spatial reasoning, dynamic reasoning, complex spatial logic, spatial interaction, perspective-taking
description: OmniSpatial is a comprehensive spatial reasoning benchmark grounded in cognitive psychology, covering four major categories: dynamic reasoning, complex spatial logic, spatial interaction, and perspective-taking with 50 fine-grained subcategories. It goes beyond basic spatial relations to test more advanced spatial reasoning capabilities that current VLMs struggle with.
paper_title: OmniSpatial: Towards Comprehensive Spatial Reasoning Benchmark for Vision Language Models
authors: Mengdi Jia, Zekun Qi, Shaochen Zhang, Wenyao Zhang, Xinqiang Yu

task_types: dynamic spatial reasoning, motion pattern recognition
description: DSI-Bench is a benchmark with nearly 1,000 dynamic videos and over 1,700 manually annotated questions covering nine decoupled motion patterns of observers and objects for evaluating Dynamic Spatial Intelligence. It uses spatially and temporally symmetric designs to reduce biases and systematically evaluate models' reasoning about self-motion and object motion in dynamic 3D scenarios.
paper_title: DSI-Bench: A Benchmark for Dynamic Spatial Intelligence
authors: Ziang Zhang, Zehan Wang, Guanghao Zhang, Weilong Dai, Yan Xia

task_types: UAV navigation, environmental perception, scene understanding, spatial reasoning
description: SpatialSky-Bench is a comprehensive benchmark specifically designed to evaluate the spatial intelligence capabilities of VLMs in UAV navigation scenarios. It comprises Environmental Perception and Scene Understanding categories divided into 13 subcategories including bounding boxes, color, distance, height, and landing safety analysis, revealing significant gaps in VLMs' spatial capabilities for UAV applications.
paper_title: Is your VLM Sky-Ready? A Comprehensive Spatial Intelligence Benchmark for UAV Navigation
authors: Lingfeng Zhang, Yuchen Zhang, Hongsheng Li, Haoxiang Fu, Yingbo Tang

task_types: spatial reasoning, navigation guidance, environmental understanding
description: SA-Bench (Space-Aware Benchmark) addresses limitations of current VLMs in understanding physical environments for guide dog robot applications. It focuses on spatial relationships crucial for navigation in complex environments and includes an evaluation protocol to assess VLM effectiveness in delivering walking guidance to visually impaired individuals, emphasizing virtual path to destination in 3D space.
paper_title: Space-Aware Instruction Tuning: Dataset and Benchmark for Guide Dog Robots Assisting the Visually Impaired
authors: ByungOk Han, Woo-han Yun, Beom-Su Seo, Jaehong Kim

description: SpatiaLQA evaluates spatial logical reasoning capabilities by combining understanding of spatial relationships among objects with logical dependencies between steps in multi-step tasks. It tests VLMs on real-world indoor scenes requiring both spatial comprehension and logical reasoning for decision-making.
paper_title: SpatiaLQA: A Benchmark for Evaluating Spatial Logical Reasoning in Vision-Language Models
authors: Yuechen Xie, Xiaoyan Zhang, Yicheng Shan, Hao Zhu, Rui Tang

description: MultihopSpatial evaluates multi-hop compositional spatial reasoning with 1- to 3-hop complex queries across diverse spatial perspectives. It introduces Acc@50IoU metric that simultaneously evaluates reasoning and visual grounding capabilities essential for Vision-Language-Action agent deployment.
paper_title: MultihopSpatial: Multi-hop Compositional Spatial Reasoning Benchmark for Vision-Language Model
authors: Youngwan Lee, Soojin Jang, Yoorhim Cho, Seunghwan Lee, Yong-Ju Lee

task_types: 3D assembly, physical reasoning, visual question answering
description: PhyBlock assesses physical understanding and planning through robotic 3D block assembly tasks using a four-level cognitive hierarchy. It evaluates progressive spatial reasoning and fundamental physical comprehension including object properties and spatial relationships.
paper_title: PhyBlock: A Progressive Benchmark for Physical Understanding and Planning via 3D Block Assembly
authors: Liang Ma, Jiajun Wen, Min Lin, Rongtao Xu, Xiwen Liang

task_types: spatial perception, spatial understanding, spatial planning
description: SIBench is a comprehensive spatial intelligence benchmark encompassing nearly 20 datasets across 23 task settings, categorizing spatial intelligence into basic perception, spatial understanding, and spatial planning. It reveals pronounced gaps between perception and reasoning capabilities in current VLMs.
paper_title: How Far are VLMs from Visual Spatial Intelligence? A Benchmark-Driven Perspective
authors: Songsong Yu, Yuxin Chen, Hao Ju, Lianjie Jia, Fuxi Zhang

description: CoSpace evaluates continuous space perception ability in VLMs by testing their understanding of spatially continuous images captured from a static viewpoint with shifting orientations. This benchmark uniquely focuses on the compositional characteristic of images that enables reconstruction of entire spaces from sequential viewpoints.
paper_title: CoSpace: Benchmarking Continuous Space Perception Ability for Vision-Language Models
authors: Yiqi Zhu, Ziyue Wang, Can Zhang, Peng Li, Yang Liu

benchmark_name: CHAIN (Causal Hierarchy of Actions and Interactions)
description: Shifts evaluation from passive perception to active problem-solving through interactive 3D physics-driven environments. Tests understanding of physical constraints, geometry, and causal relationships through mechanical puzzles and 3D manipulation tasks requiring multi-step planning.
paper_title: From Perception to Action: An Interactive Benchmark for Vision Reasoning
authors: Yuhao Wu, Maojia Song, Yihuai Lan, Lei Wang, Zhiqiang Hu

description: Systematic benchmark for object-centric spatial reasoning using controlled synthetic dataset. Evaluates three distinct tasks: spatial localization, spatial reasoning, and downstream retrieval, revealing trade-offs between precise localization and relational reasoning capabilities in foundation models.
paper_title: Spatial Reasoning in Foundation Models: Benchmarking Object-Centric Spatial Understanding
authors: Vahid Mirjalili, Ramin Giahi, Sriram Kollipara, Akshay Kekuda, Kehui Yao

description: Spatial-DISE is a unified benchmark based on a cognitively grounded taxonomy that categorizes spatial reasoning tasks into four fundamental quadrants: Intrinsic-Static, Intrinsic-Dynamic, Extrinsic-Static, and Extrinsic-Dynamic. It addresses data scarcity through a scalable automated pipeline generating diverse and verifiable spatial reasoning questions.
paper_title: Spatial-DISE: A Unified Benchmark for Evaluating Spatial Reasoning in Vision-Language Models
authors: Xinmiao Huang, Qisong He, Zhenglin Huang, Boxuan Wang, Zhuoyun Li

task_types: spatial reasoning, measurement, perspective-taking, spatiotemporal tracking
description: InfiniBench is a fully automated, customizable benchmark generator that synthesizes infinite varieties of 3D scenes with parameterized control over scene complexity for spatial reasoning evaluation. It uniquely translates natural language scene descriptions into photo-realistic videos with complex physically plausible 3D layouts.
paper_title: InfiniBench: Infinite Benchmarking for Visual Spatial Reasoning with Customizable Scene Complexity
authors: Haoming Wang, Qiyao Xue, Wei Gao

task_types: spatial counting, distance measurement, localization, relational reasoning
description: The first large-scale spatial intelligence dataset for sports scenarios with over 1M QA pairs. Covers spatial counting, distance measurement, localization, and relational reasoning across net sports using well-defined court geometry as metric anchors.
paper_title: Stepping VLMs onto the Court: Benchmarking Spatial Intelligence in Sports
authors: Yuchen Yang, Yuqing Shao, Duxiu Huang, Linfeng Dong, Yifei Liu

task_types: 3D visual question answering, cultural heritage analysis
description: The first 3D visual question answering dataset for ancient Greek pottery analysis with 664 3D vase models. Addresses cultural heritage domain specialization with targeted training data for vase artifact analysis.
paper_title: VaseVQA-3D: Benchmarking 3D VLMs on Ancient Greek Pottery
authors: Nonghai Zhang, Zeyu Zhang, Jiazi Wang, Yang Zhao, Hao Tang

task_types: causal discovery, causal reasoning, visual reasoning
description: CAUSAL3D integrates structured data with 3D visual representations to evaluate causal reasoning capabilities. It comprises 19 3D-scene datasets capturing diverse causal relations, views, and backgrounds, enabling systematic evaluation of causal discovery and reasoning across scenes of varying complexity.
paper_title: CAUSAL3D: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Causal Learning from Visual Data
authors: Disheng Liu, Yiran Qiao, Wuche Liu, Yiren Lu, Yunlai Zhou

task_types: spatial reasoning, deformation reasoning, forward reasoning, reverse reasoning
description: This benchmark evaluates VLM spatial deformation reasoning from 2D to 3D using unlimited evaluation problem pairs generated without data leakage. It explores both forward reasoning (operations to final state) and reverse reasoning (final state to operations) in a ladder competition format with increasing deformation steps.
paper_title: Ascending the Infinite Ladder: Benchmarking Spatial Deformation Reasoning in Vision-Language Models
authors: Jiahuan Zhang, Shunwen Bai, Tianheng Wang, Kaiwen Guo, Kai Han

task_types: spatial reasoning, depth estimation, localization, distance estimation
description: A large-scale benchmark built on nuScenes dataset specifically designed to evaluate spatial reasoning capabilities of VLMs in driving scenarios. It systematically covers six spatial categories including orientation, depth estimation, and inter-object relationships, with 41,080 training instances and 9,250 evaluation samples.
paper_title: SURDS: Benchmarking Spatial Understanding and Reasoning in Driving Scenarios with Vision Language Models
authors: Xianda Guo, Ruijun Zhang, Yiqun Duan, Yuhang He, Dujun Nie

description: A benchmark for assessing spatial awareness of image generation models with a specialized evaluation pipeline that reconstructs 3D scene geometry using multiple visual foundation models. It provides a more accurate and human-aligned metric of spatial faithfulness compared to standard VLM evaluations.
paper_title: GenSpace: Benchmarking Spatially-Aware Image Generation
authors: Zehan Wang, Jiayang Xu, Ziang Zhang, Tianyu Pang, Chao Du

task_types: top-down image understanding, rotation consistency evaluation
description: A benchmark for top-down image understanding with 2000 curated questions for each rotation, introducing RotationalEval (RE) that measures consistency across four rotated views. It uniquely addresses the rotation invariance property of top-down images and includes a reliability framework that separates genuine knowledge from chance.
paper_title: TDBench: A Benchmark for Top-Down Image Understanding with Reliability Analysis of Vision-Language Models
authors: Kaiyuan Hou, Minghui Zhao, Lilin Xu, Yuang Fan, Xiaofan Jiang

task_types: physical generative reasoning, construction planning, structural validation
description: A novel benchmark for physical generative reasoning grounded in residential timber-frame construction with over 26,000 structures spanning 13 architectural styles. It uniquely tests the capacity to synthesize artifacts satisfying geometric, structural, constructability, and code-compliance constraints with deterministic validation framework.
paper_title: How Far Are Vision-Language Models from Constructing the Real World? A Benchmark for Physical Generative Reasoning
authors: Luyu Yang, Yutong Dai, An Yan, Viraj Prabhu, Ran Xu

description: OpenBench is a benchmark specifically designed for outdoor navigation in residential areas for smart logistics, reflecting real-world challenges faced by autonomous delivery systems. It compensates for limitations of existing benchmarks that are inadequate for assessing last-mile delivery scenarios.
paper_title: OpenBench: A New Benchmark and Baseline for Semantic Navigation in Smart Logistics
authors: Junhui Wang, Dongjie Huo, Zehui Xu, Yongliang Shi, Yimin Yan

description: RoadSceneBench is a lightweight benchmark for mid-level road scene understanding that emphasizes relational understanding and structural consistency. Unlike large-scale perception datasets, it focuses on reasoning capabilities to infer road topology and dynamic scene structure.
paper_title: RoadSceneBench: A Lightweight Benchmark for Mid-Level Road Scene Understanding
authors: Xiyan Liu, Han Wang, Yuhu Wang, Junjie Cai, Zhe Cao

task_types: 3D reconstruction, symmetry detection, mental rotation, shape classification
description: GIQ evaluates 3D geometric reasoning capabilities using synthetic and real-world images of diverse polyhedra with varying complexity and symmetry. It tests monocular 3D reconstruction, symmetry detection, mental rotation, and shape classification to reveal significant gaps in geometric understanding.
paper_title: GIQ: Benchmarking 3D Geometric Reasoning of Vision Foundation Models with Simulated and Real Polyhedra
authors: Mateusz Michalkiewicz, Anekha Sokhal, Tadeusz Michalkiewicz, Piotr Pawlikowski, Mahsa Baktashmotlagh

task_types: spatial relationship recognition, object localization, spatial reasoning
description: GSR-BENCH extends the What'sUp dataset to provide comprehensive evaluation for grounded spatial reasoning, evaluating 27 different models including 3 classes of MLLMs varying in parameter sizes (7B-110B). It focuses on spatial relationship understanding between objects in images.
paper_title: GSR-BENCH: A Benchmark for Grounded Spatial Reasoning Evaluation via Multimodal LLMs
authors: Navid Rajabi, Jana Kosecka

task_types: multi-view QA, spatial reasoning, cross-view understanding
description: CityCube probes cross-view spatial reasoning capabilities of VLMs in urban environments with 5,022 multi-view QA pairs. It integrates four viewpoint dynamics across vehicles, drones, and satellites, categorized into five cognitive dimensions and three spatial relation expressions.
paper_title: CityCube: Benchmarking Cross-view Spatial Reasoning on Vision-Language Models in Urban Environments
authors: Haotian Xu, Yue Hu, Zhengqiu Zhu, Chen Gao, Ziyou Wang

modalities: point clouds, RGB-D images, navigation meshes, 3D detections, language
description: Space3D-Bench provides 1000 spatial questions about 3D scenes using multiple data modalities including point clouds, posed RGB-D images, navigation meshes, and 3D object detections. It uses an indoor spatial taxonomy inspired by geographic information systems for balanced coverage.
paper_title: Space3D-Bench: Spatial 3D Question Answering Benchmark
authors: Emilia Szymanska, Mihai Dusmanu, Jan-Willem Buurlage, Mahdi Rad, Marc Pollefeys

task_types: 3D consistency evaluation, multi-view generation assessment, robustness testing
description: A comprehensive benchmark for multi-view image generation models that evaluates 3D consistency in geometry and texture, image quality, and semantics. It introduces a novel 3D self-consistency metric comparing 3D reconstructions from disjoint generated multi-views instead of ground truth comparison.
paper_title: MVGBench: Comprehensive Benchmark for Multi-view Generation Models
authors: Xianghui Xie, Chuhang Zou, Meher Gitika Karumuri, Jan Eric Lenssen, Gerard Pons-Moll

description: SpaCE-10 evaluates compositional spatial intelligence in MLLMs by defining 10 atomic spatial capabilities combined into 8 compositional capabilities. It provides over 5k QA pairs for 811 real indoor scenes with point cloud input and multi-choice QA.
paper_title: SpaCE-10: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Multimodal Large Language Models in Compositional Spatial Intelligence
authors: Ziyang Gong, Wenhao Li, Oliver Ma, Songyuan Li, Zhaokai Wang

description: Cube Bench uses Rubik's cube scenarios to evaluate spatial and sequential reasoning in MLLMs across five skills: face reconstruction, optimal move selection, outcome prediction, multi-step plan execution, and error detection/revision. It provides a compact probe of sequential spatial reasoning.
paper_title: Cube Bench: A Benchmark for Spatial Visual Reasoning in MLLMs
authors: Dhruv Anand, Ehsan Shareghi

description: 4D-Bench is the first benchmark to evaluate multimodal large language models' understanding of 4D objects (3D objects with temporal evolution over time). It features tasks in 4D object Question Answering and 4D object captioning, requiring multi-view spatial-temporal understanding that differs from existing 2D image/video-based benchmarks.
paper_title: 4D-Bench: Benchmarking Multi-modal Large Language Models for 4D Object Understanding
authors: Wenxuan Zhu, Bing Li, Cheng Zheng, Jinjie Mai, Jun Chen

description: 360Bench is a Visual Question Answering benchmark featuring 7K-resolution 360° images across seven representative tasks. It uniquely presents problems with identical contexts but varied questions to assess MLLMs' capabilities in understanding 360° environments with geometric distortion and complex spatial relations.
paper_title: 360° Image Perception with MLLMs: A Comprehensive Benchmark and a Training-Free Method
authors: Huyen T. T. Tran, Van-Quang Nguyen, Farros Alferro, Kang-Jun Liu, Takayuki Okatani

task_types: pattern prediction, multi-step spatial reasoning, spatial relationship prediction, code generation
description: Introduces a novel dataset for evaluating multi-step spatial reasoning and mathematical constraint handling in MLLMs through origami tasks. Contains 350 instances with crease patterns, folding processes, and final shapes, proposing four evaluation tasks including pattern prediction and spatial reasoning.
paper_title: ORIGAMISPACE: Benchmarking Multimodal LLMs in Multi-Step Spatial Reasoning with Mathematical Constraints
authors: Rui Xu, Dakuan Lu, Zicheng Zhao, Xiaoyu Tan, Xintao Wang

task_types: 3D geometric reasoning, chain-of-thought reasoning, point cloud understanding
description: Presents a novel framework empowering MLLMs with explicit Chain-of-Thought reasoning for 3D point cloud understanding. Constructs Point-Reason-Instruct benchmark with ~86k instruction-tuning samples featuring hierarchical CoT annotations to address geometric hallucinations in 3D reasoning.
paper_title: PointCoT: A Multi-modal Benchmark for Explicit 3D Geometric Reasoning
authors: Dongxu Zhang, Yiding Sun, Pengcheng Li, Yumou Liu, Hongqiang Lin

task_types: object understanding, scene understanding, spatial relationship understanding, spatiotemporal reasoning
description: Presents a versatile 4D spatial intelligence benchmark with ~40,000 question-answer pairs covering 18 well-defined tasks across six cognitive categories. Provides comprehensive assessment of 4D spatial reasoning abilities including object understanding, scene understanding, and spatiotemporal relationships.
paper_title: Spatial4D-Bench: A Versatile 4D Spatial Intelligence Benchmark
authors: Pan Wang, Yang Liu, Guile Wu, Eduardo R. Corral-Soto, Chengjie Huang

task_types: aerial spatial reasoning, 3D spatial understanding, distance reasoning
description: Presents a novel benchmark for evaluating MLLMs' spatial reasoning abilities from aerial perspective in open environments. Comprises 73k QA pairs spanning 7 general spatial reasoning tasks supporting both visual and point cloud modalities, generated from real-world and simulated aerial scenes.
paper_title: Open3D-VQA: A Benchmark for Comprehensive Spatial Reasoning with Multimodal Large Language Model in Open Space
authors: Weichen Zhang, Zile Zhou, Xin Zeng, Xuchen Liu, Jianjie Fang

task_types: spatial cognition, planning, causal inference, symbolic reasoning
description: SpatialBench provides a hierarchical spatial cognition framework that decomposes spatial intelligence into five progressively complex levels from basic observation to high-level planning. It constructs a large-scale, fine-grained benchmark covering 15 tasks aligned with these cognitive levels and introduces capability-oriented metrics for assessing overall spatial reasoning ability.
paper_title: SpatialBench: Benchmarking Multimodal Large Language Models for Spatial Cognition
authors: Peiran Xu, Sudong Wang, Yao Zhu, Jianing Li, Gege Qi

task_types: causal reasoning, spatial reasoning, collision prediction, trajectory prediction
description: CausalSpatial is a diagnostic benchmark evaluating whether models can anticipate consequences of object motions across four tasks: Collision, Compatibility, Occlusion, and Trajectory. It focuses on object-centric causal spatial reasoning and the ability to answer 'what-if' questions in 3D scenes.
paper_title: CausalSpatial: A Benchmark for Object-Centric Causal Spatial Reasoning
authors: Wenxin Ma, Chenlong Wang, Ruisheng Yuan, Hao Chen, Nanru Dai

task_types: spatial understanding, road marking recognition, traffic reasoning
description: RoadBench is a systematic benchmark that comprehensively evaluates MLLMs' fine-grained spatial understanding and reasoning capabilities using BEV and FPV image inputs focused on road markings and urban traffic systems. It comprises six tasks with 9,121 strictly manually verified test cases forming a systematic evaluation framework.
paper_title: RoadBench: Benchmarking MLLMs on Fine-Grained Spatial Understanding and Reasoning under Urban Road Scenarios
authors: Jun Zhang, Jie Feng, Long Chen, Junhui Wang, Zhicheng Liu

description: SPR-128K is a comprehensive spatial plausibility reasoning dataset with over 128k samples that evaluates spatial plausibility reasoning ability under four aspects. It addresses the lack of data and weak spatial plausibility reasoning ability in MLLMs through comprehensive evaluation of spatial reasoning capabilities.
paper_title: SPR-128K: A New Benchmark for Spatial Plausibility Reasoning with Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Zhiyuan Hu, Zheng Sun, Yi Wei, Long Yu

task_types: perspective perception, perspective reasoning, spatial reasoning
description: First benchmark specifically designed to evaluate MLLMs' understanding of perspective through 10 tasks across three dimensions: Perspective Perception, Reasoning, and Robustness. Tests capabilities like vanishing point perception, perspective type reasoning, and spatial consistency under perturbations.
paper_title: MMPerspective: Do MLLMs Understand Perspective? A Comprehensive Benchmark for Perspective Perception, Reasoning, and Robustness
authors: Yolo Y. Tang, Pinxin Liu, Zhangyun Tan, Mingqian Feng, Rui Mao

description: The first real open-world autonomous driving benchmark for 3D object detection with corner case discovery. Built using MLLM-based annotation pipeline, it unifies corner case objects across five autonomous driving datasets in a comprehensive evaluation framework.
paper_title: OpenAD: Open-World Autonomous Driving Benchmark for 3D Object Detection
authors: Zhongyu Xia, Jishuo Li, Zhiwei Lin, Xinhao Wang, Yongtao Wang

description: The first natural understanding benchmark for multi-dimensional intelligence and numerical reasoning in 3D environments. Features multi-scale annotations and automated annotation pipeline NUMINA-Flow, addressing the gap in fine-grained numerical reasoning task annotations for 3D spatial measurements.
paper_title: NUMINA: A Natural Understanding Benchmark for Multi-dimensional Intelligence and Numerical Reasoning Abilities
authors: Changyu Zeng, Yifan Wang, Zimu Wang, Wei Wang, Zhengni Yang

task_types: spatial navigation, object interaction, multi-step reasoning, exploration
description: MM-Escape introduces an extensible benchmark for multimodal reasoning using EscapeCraft, a customizable 3D room escape environment. It uniquely emphasizes intermediate model behaviors and reasoning processes alongside final task completion, enabling analysis of exploration strategies and failure modes in spatial environments.
paper_title: EscapeCraft: A 3D Room Escape Environment for Benchmarking Complex Multimodal Reasoning Ability
authors: Ziyue Wang, Yurui Dong, Fuwen Luo, Minyuan Ruan, Zhili Cheng

task_types: spatial planning, architecture design, instruction following
description: MineAnyBuild evaluates spatial planning ability through executable architecture building in Minecraft, requiring generation of building plans from multimodal instructions. It uniquely assesses spatial understanding, reasoning, creativity, and commonsense in open-world construction tasks with infinitely expandable data collection.
paper_title: MineAnyBuild: Benchmarking Spatial Planning for Open-world AI Agents
authors: Ziming Wei, Bingqian Lin, Zijian Jiao, Yunshuang Nie, Liang Ma

description: A scalable 3D benchmark with large-scale instruction-tuning dataset spanning object-level to scene-level spatial and semantic scales. Covers 10 diverse multi-modal tasks with rigorous pipeline for automatically constructing 3D instruction-tuning datasets, addressing both perception and planning tasks.
paper_title: 3DBench: A Scalable 3D Benchmark and Instruction-Tuning Dataset
authors: Junjie Zhang, Tianci Hu, Xiaoshui Huang, Yongshun Gong, Dan Zeng

description: MARBLE presents two challenging multimodal spatial reasoning tasks (M-Portal and M-Cube) that require step-by-step planning under spatial, visual, and physical constraints. The benchmark is designed to test complex multimodal reasoning capabilities where current MLLMs achieve near-random performance, highlighting significant limitations in spatial planning abilities.
paper_title: MARBLE: A Hard Benchmark for Multimodal Spatial Reasoning and Planning
authors: Yulun Jiang, Yekun Chai, Maria Brbić, Michael Moor

task_types: visual question answering, omnidirectional reasoning, panoramic understanding
description: OmniVQA is the first dataset and benchmark for omnidirectional visual question answering using 360° field of view images. It evaluates MLLMs' capability to comprehend and reason about panoramic scenes, addressing challenges in object localization, feature extraction, and hallucination suppression within omnidirectional contexts.
paper_title: Towards Omnidirectional Reasoning with 360-R1: A Dataset, Benchmark, and GRPO-based Method
authors: Xinshen Zhang, Zhen Ye, Xu Zheng

task_types: geometric reasoning, perspective-taking, occlusion handling, mental rotation, spatial reasoning
description: Spatial-CAPTCHA-Bench evaluates fundamental differences in spatial reasoning between humans and MLLMs through dynamic questions requiring geometric reasoning, perspective-taking, occlusion handling, and mental rotation. It demonstrates that humans vastly outperform state-of-the-art MLLMs with the best model achieving only 31.0% accuracy.
paper_title: Spatial CAPTCHA: Generatively Benchmarking Spatial Reasoning for Human-Machine Differentiation
authors: Arina Kharlamova, Bowei He, Chen Ma, Xue Liu

description: Fully human-annotated benchmark for video-based spatial intelligence operationalizing a four-level framework (Perception, Planning, Prediction, Cross-Video Reasoning). Tests geometric reasoning, motion grounding, and long-horizon prediction with expert-validated explanatory rationales.
paper_title: MMSI-Video-Bench: A Holistic Benchmark for Video-Based Spatial Intelligence
authors: Jingli Lin, Runsen Xu, Shaohao Zhu, Sihan Yang, Peizhou Cao

task_types: spatial visualization, mental rotation, spatial reasoning
description: SpatialViz-Bench is a comprehensive multi-modal benchmark for spatial visualization with 12 tasks across 4 sub-abilities, comprising 1,180 programmatically generated problems. It uniquely focuses on the cognitively-grounded ability to imagine and manipulate visual images mentally, addressing spatial skills insufficiently evaluated in existing benchmarks.
paper_title: SpatialViz-Bench: A Cognitively-Grounded Benchmark for Diagnosing Spatial Visualization in MLLMs
authors: Siting Wang, Minnan Pei, Luoyang Sun, Cheng Deng, Yuchen Li

description: 3DSRBench is the first comprehensive 3D spatial reasoning benchmark with 2,772 manually annotated visual question-answer pairs across 12 question types. It includes paired images with common and uncommon viewpoints to study robustness of 3D spatial reasoning with respect to camera 3D viewpoints.
paper_title: 3DSRBench: A Comprehensive 3D Spatial Reasoning Benchmark
authors: Wufei Ma, Haoyu Chen, Guofeng Zhang, Yu-Cheng Chou, Jieneng Chen

description: FE-3DGQA addresses 3D grounded visual question answering with diverse free-form question-answer pairs and completely grounded bounding box annotations. It labels objects with different semantic types including answer-grounded and contextual objects to enable explainable 3D VQA with reduced language priors and co-reference ambiguity.
paper_title: Towards Explainable 3D Grounded Visual Question Answering: A New Benchmark and Strong Baseline
authors: Lichen Zhao, Daigang Cai, Jing Zhang, Lu Sheng, Dong Xu

description: FRIEDA tests complex open-ended cartographic reasoning in LVLMs across three spatial relation categories: topological, metric, and directional. All questions require multi-step inference with many requiring cross-map grounding and reasoning, sourced from real map images across various domains and geographical areas to evaluate spatial intelligence.
paper_title: FRIEDA: Benchmarking Multi-Step Cartographic Reasoning in Vision-Language Models
authors: Jiyoon Pyo, Yuankun Jiao, Dongwon Jung, Zekun Li, Leeje Jang

task_types: multi-object recognition, 2D location, 3D location, 3D orientation, 6D spatial reasoning
description: Scalable synthetic dataset for 6D spatial reasoning with cascading evaluation structure across 4 key capabilities and 7 question types. Introduces Relative Performance Dropping Rate metric and uncovers prediction biases across different attributes.
paper_title: Spatial457: A Diagnostic Benchmark for 6D Spatial Reasoning of Large Multimodal Models
authors: Xingrui Wang, Wufei Ma, Tiezheng Zhang, Celso M de Melo, Jieneng Chen

description: PathEval evaluates VLMs as plan evaluators in complex path-planning scenarios, requiring models to abstract optimal path traits from scenario descriptions, demonstrate precise low-level perception on paths, and integrate information to decide the better path. It reveals critical bottlenecks in VLMs' vision components for perceiving low-level path details.
paper_title: Evaluating Vision-Language Models as Evaluators in Path Planning
authors: Mohamed Aghzal, Xiang Yue, Erion Plaku, Ziyu Yao

task_types: spatial reasoning, frame of reference evaluation
description: COMFORT (COnsistent Multilingual Frame Of Reference Test) systematically assesses spatial reasoning capabilities of VLMs under ambiguous spatial expressions. It evaluates how models handle different frames of reference and tests cross-cultural diversity in spatial reasoning, revealing poor robustness and English dominance in multilingual settings.
paper_title: Do Vision-Language Models Represent Space and How? Evaluating Spatial Frame of Reference Under Ambiguities
authors: Zheyuan Zhang, Fengyuan Hu, Jayjun Lee, Freda Shi, Parisa Kordjamshidi

description: SPHERE provides hierarchical evaluation of spatial reasoning across increasing complexity levels, from fundamental skills to multi-skill integration. It systematically probes distance/proximity reasoning, egocentric/allocentric perspectives, and spatial logic application in physical contexts.
paper_title: SPHERE: Unveiling Spatial Blind Spots in Vision-Language Models Through Hierarchical Evaluation
authors: Wenyu Zhang, Wei En Ng, Lixin Ma, Yuwen Wang, Junqi Zhao

description: The first comprehensive benchmark for multi-viewpoint spatial localization evaluation across five task types. Uses automated 3D annotation pipeline to test cross-viewpoint understanding and spatial reasoning from different entity perspectives.
paper_title: ViewSpatial-Bench: Evaluating Multi-perspective Spatial Localization in Vision-Language Models
authors: Dingming Li, Hongxing Li, Zixuan Wang, Yuchen Yan, Hang Zhang

task_types: inverse rendering, 3D scene reconstruction, tool use
description: Benchmark challenging VLMs to demonstrate scene understanding through agentic inverse rendering - actively using programming and rendering tools to recreate the underlying 3D structure of input images. Tests understanding-by-creating approach rather than passive recognition, measuring geometric accuracy, spatial relations, and appearance attributes.
paper_title: IR3D-Bench: Evaluating Vision-Language Model Scene Understanding as Agentic Inverse Rendering
authors: Parker Liu, Chenxin Li, Zhengxin Li, Yipeng Wu, Wuyang Li

description: Introduces CAPTURe task for counting objects arranged in patterns by inferring how patterns continue behind occluders. Tests spatial understanding and world model formation through both real (CAPTURe-real) and synthetic (CAPTURe-synthetic) datasets.
paper_title: CAPTURe: Evaluating Spatial Reasoning in Vision Language Models via Occluded Object Counting
authors: Atin Pothiraj, Elias Stengel-Eskin, Jaemin Cho, Mohit Bansal

task_types: spatial reasoning, geometric reasoning, topological reasoning
description: VQA benchmark for spatial reasoning on constrained manifolds with 1,000 ranking questions spanning geometric and topological reasoning. Built from complex real-world 3D structures requiring compositional spatial operations like mental rotation and occlusion reasoning.
paper_title: Thinking in Structures: Evaluating Spatial Intelligence through Reasoning on Constrained Manifolds
authors: Chen Yang, Guanxin Lin, Youquan He, Peiyao Chen, Guanghe Liu

task_types: scene understanding, spatial reasoning, perspective taking
description: Introduces a novel visual perspective taking evaluation using controlled scenes with humanoid minifigures and objects, systematically varying spatial configurations and viewpoints. The benchmark specifically tests three levels of visual cognition: scene understanding, spatial reasoning, and visual perspective taking with 7 diagnostic questions per task.
paper_title: Beyond Recognition: Evaluating Visual Perspective Taking in Vision Language Models
authors: Gracjan Góral, Alicja Ziarko, Piotr Miłoś, Michał Nauman, Maciej Wołczyk

description: MentalBlackboard evaluates spatial visualization through Paper Folding and Hole Punching tests with two core tasks: prediction and planning. It uniquely tests mental ability to imagine, transform, and manipulate spatial characteristics of objects through mathematical transformations.
paper_title: MentalBlackboard: Evaluating Spatial Visualization via Mathematical Transformations
authors: Nilay Yilmaz, Maitreya Patel, Naga Sai Abhiram Kusumba, Yixuan He, Yezhou Yang

description: Evaluation framework for assessing alignment between fine-grained instructions and generated 3D scenes using diverse grounding tools. Tests realistic spatial layouts and object attributes in embodied environments with explicit scene component grounding.
paper_title: LEGO-Eval: Towards Fine-Grained Evaluation on Synthesizing 3D Embodied Environments with Tool Augmentation
authors: Gyeom Hwangbo, Hyungjoo Chae, Minseok Kang, Hyeonjong Ju, Soohyun Oh

task_types: counting, attribute identification, relative distance, relative direction, object manipulation, camera pose estimation
description: All-Angles Bench evaluates multi-view understanding in MLLMs across 90 real-world scenes with 2,100+ human-annotated question-answer pairs. It tests geometric correspondence and cross-view alignment through six tasks including counting, attribute identification, relative distance/direction, object manipulation, and camera pose estimation.
paper_title: Seeing from Another Perspective: Evaluating Multi-View Understanding in MLLMs
authors: Chun-Hsiao Yeh, Chenyu Wang, Shengbang Tong, Ta-Ying Cheng, Ruoyu Wang

description: SpatialScore provides the most comprehensive benchmark for multimodal spatial intelligence with approximately 5K manually verified samples spanning 30 distinct tasks. It covers multiple visual data types, input modalities, and question-answering formats to holistically assess spatial understanding capabilities in MLLMs.
paper_title: SpatialScore: Towards Comprehensive Evaluation for Spatial Intelligence
authors: Haoning Wu, Xiao Huang, Yaohui Chen, Ya Zhang, Yanfeng Wang

description: RotBench evaluates MLLMs' ability to accurately identify image orientation across 0°, 90°, 180°, and 270° rotations. It uniquely tests spatial reasoning capabilities for detecting rotational cues and contextualizing spatial relationships regardless of image orientation using manually-filtered lifestyle, portrait, and landscape images.
paper_title: RotBench: Evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models on Identifying Image Rotation
authors: Tianyi Niu, Jaemin Cho, Elias Stengel-Eskin, Mohit Bansal

task_types: spatial perception, spatial relation, spatial orientation, mental rotation, spatial visualization
description: Introduces a psychometric framework defining five Basic Spatial Abilities (BSAs) in VLMs: Spatial Perception, Spatial Relation, Spatial Orientation, Mental Rotation, and Spatial Visualization. Uses nine validated psychometric experiments to evaluate spatial intelligence hierarchies in VLMs, revealing systematic gaps compared to humans and providing a diagnostic toolkit for spatial intelligence evaluation.
paper_title: Defining and Evaluating Visual Language Models' Basic Spatial Abilities: A Perspective from Psychometrics
authors: Wenrui Xu, Dalin Lyu, Weihang Wang, Jie Feng, Chen Gao

task_types: spatial reasoning, view-taking, dynamic scene understanding
description: Benchmark dataset for Spatial Intelligence Thorough Evaluation in multi-choice visual QA format, assessing spatial intelligence across diverse visual modalities (single-image, multi-image, video) and SI factors from figural to environmental scales. Combines bottom-up survey of 31 datasets with top-down cognitive science approach.
paper_title: SITE: towards Spatial Intelligence Thorough Evaluation
authors: Wenqi Wang, Reuben Tan, Pengyue Zhu, Jianwei Yang, Zhengyuan Yang

task_types: spatial reasoning, 2D-to-3D planning, visual question answering
description: Evaluates spatial reasoning and 2D-to-3D planning through origami-inspired folding tasks, including both regular and impossible fold patterns. Introduces novel diagnostic metrics for viewpoint consistency and impossible fold detection to measure cross-view consistency and physical feasibility.
paper_title: GamiBench: Evaluating Spatial Reasoning and 2D-to-3D Planning Capabilities of MLLMs with Origami Folding Tasks
authors: Ryan Spencer, Roey Yaari, Ritvik Vemavarapu, Joyce Yang, Steven Ngo

task_types: outline prediction, code generation, geometric reasoning
description: A geometry-grounded benchmark using the Tangram game with exact, machine-verifiable coordinate specifications through the Tangram Construction Expression framework. Tests compositional spatial reasoning through outline prediction and inverse geometric assembly problems with rigorous mathematical formulation.
paper_title: TangramPuzzle: Evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models with Compositional Spatial Reasoning
authors: Daixian Liu, Jiayi Kuang, Yinghui Li, Yangning Li, Di Yin

description: Benchmark for abstractive spatiotemporal reasoning from videos using controllable, scenario-driven synthetic egocentric video dataset. Features five abstractive reasoning tasks with extractive counterparts spanning object-, room-, and floor-plan-level scenarios.
paper_title: Reasoning over Video: Evaluating How MLLMs Extract, Integrate, and Reconstruct Spatiotemporal Evidence
authors: Seunghwan Bang, Hwanjun Song

task_types: compositional reasoning, spatio-temporal reasoning, video question answering
description: Benchmark for compositional spatio-temporal reasoning with 192M unbalanced and 3.9M balanced question-answer pairs. Tests generalization to novel compositions, indirect references, and more compositional steps while minimizing linguistic biases.
paper_title: AGQA: A Benchmark for Compositional Spatio-Temporal Reasoning
authors: Madeleine Grunde-McLaughlin, Ranjay Krishna, Maneesh Agrawala

task_types: direction reasoning, spatial reasoning, compass reasoning
description: Benchmark specifically designed to evaluate compass direction reasoning capabilities in multimodal language models. Tests both spatial directions (up, down, left, right) and compass directions (north, south, east, west) using three types of images.
paper_title: Do Multimodal Language Models Really Understand Direction? A Benchmark for Compass Direction Reasoning
authors: Hang Yin, Zhifeng Lin, Xin Liu, Bin Sun, Kan Li

task_types: 3D visual grounding, spatial reasoning, part-level grounding, space-level grounding
description: Holistic 3D visual grounding benchmark spanning four grounding levels: human-activity areas, unoccupied space, individual objects, and fine-grained parts. Tests comprehensive spatial reasoning and fine-grained perception beyond traditional object-level semantics in 3D scenes.
paper_title: From Objects to Anywhere: A Holistic Benchmark for Multi-level Visual Grounding in 3D Scenes
authors: Tianxu Wang, Zhuofan Zhang, Ziyu Zhu, Yue Fan, Jing Xiong

description: The first physics-aligned interactive benchmark for language-driven engineering construction. Features customizable framework, extendable task design spanning static and dynamic mechanics across difficulty tiers, and 3D Spatial Geometric Computation Library for construction based on language instructions.
paper_title: BuildArena: A Physics-Aligned Interactive Benchmark of LLMs for Engineering Construction
authors: Tian Xia, Tianrun Gao, Wenhao Deng, Long Wei, Xiaowei Qian

task_types: spatial planning, energy collection, commonsense spatial reasoning
description: GRASP evaluates commonsense spatial reasoning through grid-based environments where agents solve energy collection problems with spatial planning requirements. It directly evaluates plans produced by LLMs rather than text-based spatial descriptions, testing spatial reasoning with planning constraints.
paper_title: GRASP: A Grid-Based Benchmark for Evaluating Commonsense Spatial Reasoning
authors: Zhisheng Tang, Mayank Kejriwal

task_types: spatial reasoning, vector-based math, building operations
description: Adapts the Minecraft builder task into an LLM benchmark for evaluating spatial reasoning abilities. Provides a comprehensive synthetic benchmark with distinct tasks comprising common building operations to test builder agents over spatial reasoning and vector-based math.
paper_title: A LLM Benchmark based on the Minecraft Builder Dialog Agent Task
authors: Chris Madge, Massimo Poesio

description: A comprehensive benchmark for object orientation perception with four dimensions: frontal alignment, rotational transformations, relative directional relationships, and canonical orientation. Features 33,656 questions across 67 object categories with coarse-to-granular design.
paper_title: Seeing Isn't Orienting: A Cognitively Grounded Benchmark Reveals Systematic Orientation Failures in MLLMs Supplementary
authors: Nazia Tasnim, Keanu Nichols, Yuting Yan, Nicholas Ikechukwu, Elva Zou

description: A cognitively grounded hierarchical benchmark for object orientation understanding with four dimensions evaluated at coarse and granular levels. Composed from 13,652 images with 33,656 multiple-choice questions covering 67 object categories.
paper_title: Seeing Isn't Orienting: A Cognitively Grounded Benchmark Reveals Systematic Orientation Failures in MLLMs Supplementary
authors: Nazia Tasnim, Keanu Nichols, Yuting Yang, Nicholas Ikechukwu, Elva Zou

description: A multimodal dataset collected in off-road environments containing annotations for 13,556 LiDAR scans and 6,235 images. Addresses the gap in off-road autonomous navigation datasets, presenting unique challenges related to class imbalance and environmental topography compared to urban datasets.
paper_title: RELLIS-3D Dataset: Data, Benchmarks and Analysis
authors: Peng Jiang, Philip Osteen, Maggie Wigness, Srikanth Saripalli

description: MSQA is a large-scale multi-modal situated reasoning dataset with 251K question-answering pairs across 9 categories in 3D scenes. It introduces interleaved multi-modal input (text, image, point cloud) and includes the MSNN benchmark for situated navigation reasoning.
paper_title: Multi-modal Situated Reasoning in 3D Scenes
authors: Xiongkun Linghu, Jiangyong Huang, Xuesong Niu, Xiaojian Ma, Baoxiong Jia

description: Novel benchmark covering diverse aspects of spatial reasoning including relationship understanding, navigation, and counting. Reveals counter-intuitive findings that VLMs often underperform compared to LLM counterparts despite additional visual input.
paper_title: Is A Picture Worth A Thousand Words? Delving Into Spatial Reasoning for Vision Language Models
authors: Jiayu Wang, Yifei Ming, Zhenmei Shi, Vibhav Vineet, Xin Wang

description: Largest open-source dataset for spatial reasoning with 12 million QA pairs spanning single-view and multi-view settings, supporting 19 instruction formats. Introduces novel rotation angle prediction task for multi-view reasoning not explored in prior work.
paper_title: InternSpatial: A Comprehensive Dataset for Spatial Reasoning in Vision-Language Models
authors: Nianchen Deng, Lixin Gu, Shenglong Ye, Yinan He, Zhe Chen

description: A spatial evaluation benchmark categorizing spatial understanding into absolute spatial positioning (left, right queries) and 3D spatial understanding (movement and rotation). Uses entirely synthetic data to enable low-cost test sample generation while preventing dataset contamination.
paper_title: LRR-Bench: Left, Right or Rotate? Vision-Language models Still Struggle With Spatial Understanding Tasks
authors: Fei Kong, Jinhao Duan, Kaidi Xu, Zhenhua Guo, Xiaofeng Zhu

description: RevQA is a question-answering benchmark designed to evaluate the spatial reasoning abilities of multimodal large language models (MLLMs). It specifically tests models' robustness to complex spatial reasoning under adversarial settings, complementing the REVISION framework for generating spatially accurate synthetic images.
paper_title: REVISION: Rendering Tools Enable Spatial Fidelity in Vision-Language Models
authors: Agneet Chatterjee, Yiran Luo, Tejas Gokhale, Yezhou Yang, Chitta Baral

description: VSP evaluates visual spatial planning capabilities in VLMs by breaking down tasks into perception and reasoning sub-components. It measures models' ability to comprehend spatial arrangements and devise action plans, revealing fundamental deficiencies in visual perception and reasoning bottlenecks that explain poor performance in spatial planning tasks.
paper_title: VSP: Assessing the dual challenges of perception and reasoning in spatial planning tasks for VLMs
authors: Qiucheng Wu, Handong Zhao, Michael Saxon, Trung Bui, William Yang Wang

description: This synthetic benchmark probes two complementary skills in VLMs: situational awareness (recognizing harmful vs benign interactions) and spatial awareness (tracking who does what to whom, reasoning about positions and motion). It tests through minimal video pairs across three challenges: violence distinction, assailant role binding, and trajectory alignment.
paper_title: Assessing Situational and Spatial Awareness of VLMs with Synthetically Generated Video
authors: Pascal Benschop, Justin Dauwels, Jan van Gemert

description: GeoMeter is a suite of benchmark datasets encompassing 2D and 3D scenarios specifically designed to evaluate geometric understanding in VLMs, focusing on depth and height perception capabilities. It rigorously tests aspects that are fundamental to intelligence and practical applications in visual perception.
paper_title: Understanding Depth and Height Perception in Large Visual-Language Models
authors: Shehreen Azad, Yash Jain, Rishit Garg, Yogesh S Rawat, Vibhav Vineet

description: MAPG-Bench is specifically designed to evaluate metric-semantic goal grounding, addressing a gap in existing language grounding evaluations. It focuses on complex metric-semantic language queries that require reasoning about both semantic references and spatial/metric constraints in 3D space.
paper_title: Meanings and Measurements: Multi-Agent Probabilistic Grounding for Vision-Language Navigation
authors: Swagat Padhan, Lakshya Jain, Bhavya Minesh Shah, Omkar Patil, Thao Nguyen

description: SpatialBench comprehensively evaluates VLMs' spatial understanding capabilities at different levels, focusing on depth-related reasoning. Combined with the SpatialQA dataset, it provides multi-level depth-related questions to assess and improve spatial understanding in vision-language models.
paper_title: SpatialBot: Precise Spatial Understanding with Vision Language Models
authors: Wenxiao Cai, Iaroslav Ponomarenko, Jianhao Yuan, Xiaoqi Li, Wankou Yang

task_types: human behavior analysis, 3D scene reasoning, commonsense reasoning
description: HIS-Bench evaluates Human-In-Scene understanding by requiring agents to comprehend human states and behaviors within 3D scenes, reason about surrounding environments, and answer human-related questions. It systematically evaluates across a spectrum from basic perception to commonsense reasoning and planning.
paper_title: HIS-GPT: Towards 3D Human-In-Scene Multimodal Understanding
authors: Jiahe Zhao, Ruibing Hou, Zejie Tian, Hong Chang, Shiguang Shan

task_types: spatial referring expression understanding, object localization
