description: HumanVBench comprises 16 carefully designed tasks exploring human-centric video understanding across two primary dimensions: inner emotion and outer manifestations. It spans static/dynamic, basic/complex, and single-modal/cross-modal aspects with automated pipelines for video annotation.
paper_title: HumanVBench: Exploring Human-Centric Video Understanding Capabilities of MLLMs with Synthetic Benchmark Data
authors: Ting Zhou, Daoyuan Chen, Qirui Jiao, Bolin Ding, Yaliang Li

description: MT-Video-Bench is a holistic video understanding benchmark that evaluates MLLMs in multi-turn dialogues rather than single-turn Q&A. It assesses 6 core competencies focusing on perceptivity and interactivity across 1,000 meticulously curated multi-turn dialogues from diverse domains, aligned with real-world applications like interactive sports analysis.
paper_title: MT-Video-Bench: A Holistic Video Understanding Benchmark for Evaluating Multimodal LLMs in Multi-Turn Dialogues
authors: Yaning Pan, Qianqian Xie, Guohui Zhang, Zekun Wang, Yongqian Wen

task_types: fine-grained perception, procedural understanding, scientific reasoning
description: ExpVid is the first benchmark designed to systematically evaluate MLLMs on scientific experiment videos. It features a three-level task hierarchy mirroring the scientific process: fine-grained perception, procedural understanding, and scientific reasoning that connects experiments to published conclusions.
paper_title: ExpVid: A Benchmark for Experiment Video Understanding & Reasoning
authors: Yicheng Xu, Yue Wu, Jiashuo Yu, Ziang Yan, Tianxiang Jiang

task_types: interactive video understanding, livestream analysis, multimodal reasoning
description: Proposes the first omnimodal benchmark for interactive livestream video understanding featuring 24 diverse tasks. Incorporates audio, speech, and real-time comments modalities to evaluate perceptual, reasoning, and livestream-specific challenges through standardized semi-automatic annotation workflow.
paper_title: LiViBench: An Omnimodal Benchmark for Interactive Livestream Video Understanding
authors: Xiaodong Wang, Langling Huang, Zhirong Wu, Xu Zhao, Teng Xu

task_types: motion understanding, temporal dynamics comprehension, video motion analysis
description: Introduces a comprehensive benchmark for fine-grained video motion understanding comprising 1,776 videos with structured manual annotations. Features both close-ended (8,184 QA pairs) and open-ended evaluation tasks spanning six distinct motion comprehension sub-tasks.
paper_title: FAVOR-Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Fine-Grained Video Motion Understanding
authors: Chongjun Tu, Lin Zhang, Pengtao Chen, Peng Ye, Xianfang Zeng

task_types: accident scene understanding, safety-critical reasoning, temporal-spatial analysis
description: Presents a large-scale vision-language benchmark for evaluating MLLMs in high-risk traffic scenarios involving vulnerable road users. Comprises 1K real-world dashcam accident videos with 6K multiple-choice QA pairs across six safety-critical categories and 1K dense scene descriptions.
paper_title: VRU-Accident: A Vision-Language Benchmark for Video Question Answering and Dense Captioning for Accident Scene Understanding
authors: Younggun Kim, Ahmed S. Abdelrahman, Mohamed Abdel-Aty

description: A multimodal retrieval-augmented generation benchmark constructed from 599 videos covering chart-heavy reports, news broadcasts, and software tutorials. What makes it unique is its focus on fine-grained multimodal information retrieval and reasoning over long temporal video spans requiring high-density format understanding.
paper_title: CFVBench: A Comprehensive Video Benchmark for Fine-grained Multimodal Retrieval-Augmented Generation
authors: Kaiwen Wei, Xiao Liu, Jie Zhang, Zijian Wang, Ruida Liu

description: ViRectify is a comprehensive benchmark to evaluate MLLMs' fine-grained correction capability in video reasoning scenarios. It challenges models to perform step-wise error identification and generate rationales with key video evidence grounding across dynamic perception, scientific reasoning, and embodied decision-making domains.
paper_title: ViRectify: A Challenging Benchmark for Video Reasoning Correction with Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Xusen Hei, Jiali Chen, Jinyu Yang, Mengchen Zhao, Yi Cai

task_types: cross-video reasoning, spatial-temporal reasoning, multi-video analysis
description: CrossVid is the first benchmark designed to comprehensively evaluate MLLMs' spatial-temporal reasoning ability in cross-video contexts. It provides 5,331 videos with 9,015 question-answering pairs across four high-level dimensions and ten specific tasks, requiring simultaneous understanding of multiple videos.
paper_title: CrossVid: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Evaluating Cross-Video Reasoning in Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Jingyao Li, Jingyun Wang, Molin Tan, Haochen Wang, Cilin Yan

description: The first benchmark tailored for compositional text-to-video generation evaluation. Encompasses diverse compositionality aspects including attribute binding, spatial relationships, motion binding, object interactions, and generative numeracy with specialized MLLM-based evaluation metrics.
paper_title: T2V-CompBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Compositional Text-to-video Generation
authors: Kaiyue Sun, Kaiyi Huang, Xian Liu, Yue Wu, Zihan Xu

task_types: stock recommendation analysis, conviction assessment, financial discourse
description: Introduces a multimodal dataset with expert annotations for benchmarking MLLMs and LLMs in financial discourse analysis, focusing on stock recommendations from social media influencers. The benchmark evaluates models' ability to analyze multimodal signals including tone, delivery style, and facial expressions beyond text-based financial analysis to assess investment conviction.
paper_title: VideoConviction: A Multimodal Benchmark for Human Conviction and Stock Market Recommendations
authors: Michael Galarnyk, Veer Kejriwal, Agam Shah, Yash Bhardwaj, Nicholas Meyer

task_types: cross-video reasoning, object association, event association, spatiotemporal reasoning
description: First diagnostic benchmark for assessing cross-video relational reasoning across multiple videos, spanning three hierarchical tiers: cross-video object association, cross-video event association, and cross-video complex reasoning. Built from five domain-diverse video clusters, it challenges models to analyze and integrate spatiotemporal patterns from dynamic visual streams.
paper_title: CVBench: Benchmarking Cross-Video Synergies for Complex Multimodal Reasoning
authors: Nannan Zhu, Yonghao Dong, Teng Wang, Xueqian Li, Shengjun Deng

task_types: object state change, action understanding, temporal reasoning
description: Benchmark specifically designed to assess object state change (OSC) performance in text-to-video generation models, constructed from instructional cooking data. Systematically organizes action-object interactions into regular, novel, and compositional scenarios to evaluate both in-distribution performance and generalization in understanding transformations of object states induced by actions.
paper_title: OSCBench: Benchmarking Object State Change in Text-to-Video Generation
authors: Xianjing Han, Bin Zhu, Shiqi Hu, Franklin Mingzhe Li, Patrick Carrington

task_types: image-grounded video perception, image-grounded video reasoning
description: First comprehensive benchmark for evaluating Image-Grounded Video Perception and Reasoning, consisting of 967 videos paired with meticulously annotated image-text queries across 13 tasks (7 perception and 6 reasoning tasks) and 5 categories. Addresses the significant role of image context in video comprehension, an area largely overlooked by existing evaluation frameworks.
paper_title: IV-Bench: A Benchmark for Image-Grounded Video Perception and Reasoning in Multimodal LLMs
authors: David Ma, Yuanxing Zhang, Jincheng Ren, Jarvis Guo, Yifan Yao

task_types: semantic understanding, spatial understanding, attribute binding, reasoning
description: Novel benchmark for evaluating Image-to-Video generation models with focus on semantic understanding and reasoning across four dimensions: spatial understanding, attribute binding, category understanding, and reasoning. Uses instance-level and feedback-based reasoning pipelines with MLLM evaluation methods to assess beyond video quality and temporal consistency.
paper_title: UI2V-Bench: An Understanding-based Image-to-video Generation Benchmark
authors: Ailing Zhang, Lina Lei, Dehong Kong, Zhixin Wang, Jiaqi Xu

num_samples: 900 text questions + 1,701 video clips with 12,048 QA pairs
task_types: multiple-choice QA, sports recognition, foul detection, rule application, strategy understanding
description: SPORTU provides comprehensive sports understanding evaluation through two components: text-based rule comprehension and video-based multi-level reasoning from recognition to complex foul detection. It uniquely combines sports rule understanding with visual reasoning across 7 sports with slow-motion video analysis.
paper_title: SPORTU: A Comprehensive Sports Understanding Benchmark for Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Haotian Xia, Zhengbang Yang, Junbo Zou, Rhys Tracy, Yuqing Wang

num_samples: 2,501 questions from 429 news groups with 1,405 videos
task_types: multi-source reasoning, cross-video understanding, perspective comparison
description: VNU-Bench is the first benchmark for multi-source, cross-video understanding in news domain. It uniquely requires comparing perspectives from different sources, aligning multimodal evidence across sources, and synthesizing multi-source information rather than single-source intra-video reasoning.
paper_title: VNU-Bench: A Benchmarking Dataset for Multi-Source Multimodal News Video Understanding
authors: Zibo Liu, Muyang Li, Zhe Jiang, Shigang Chen

task_types: age estimation, emotion recognition, social relationship prediction, intention prediction
description: HumanVideo-MME provides comprehensive evaluation of MLLMs in human-centric video understanding across 13 tasks from basic attribute perception to advanced cognitive reasoning. It spans 50 distinct visual scenarios and covers videos from short-term (10 seconds) to long-term (up to 30 minutes) with diverse question formats.
paper_title: HumanVideo-MME: Benchmarking MLLMs for Human-Centric Video Understanding
authors: Yuxuan Cai, Jiangning Zhang, Zhenye Gan, Qingdong He, Xiaobin Hu

description: A benchmark for multimodal deep reasoning in videos requiring long-range, multi-frame evidence analysis and reasoning over hidden information rather than direct perception. Features manually annotated tasks with extensive real-world user understanding and carefully designed distractor strategies to reduce model shortcuts.
paper_title: MMR-V: What's Left Unsaid? A Benchmark for Multimodal Deep Reasoning in Videos
authors: Kejian Zhu, Zhuoran Jin, Hongbang Yuan, Jiachun Li, Shangqing Tu

description: A modern benchmark suite specifically for instruction-guided video editing assessment with diverse database spanning seven semantic dimensions and video lengths from 32 to 1,024 frames. Establishes three-dimensional evaluation protocol covering video quality, instruction compliance, and video fidelity.
paper_title: IVEBench: Modern Benchmark Suite for Instruction-Guided Video Editing Assessment
authors: Yinan Chen, Jiangning Zhang, Teng Hu, Yuxiang Zeng, Zhucun Xue

description: SeriesBench evaluates narrative-driven drama series understanding across 28 specialized tasks requiring deep narrative comprehension. Unlike existing video benchmarks focused on standalone videos and visual elements, it assesses complex continuous narratives and introduces a novel long-span narrative annotation method for understanding plot structures and character relationships.
paper_title: SeriesBench: A Benchmark for Narrative-Driven Drama Series Understanding
authors: Chenkai Zhang, Yiming Lei, Zeming Liu, Haitao Leng, Shaoguo Liu

description: MVBench addresses temporal understanding in dynamic video tasks through 20 challenging video tasks that cannot be solved with single frames. It introduces a novel static-to-dynamic method to systematically generate video tasks requiring temporal skills from perception to cognition, using automatic conversion of video annotations into multiple-choice QA.
paper_title: MVBench: A Comprehensive Multi-modal Video Understanding Benchmark
authors: Kunchang Li, Yali Wang, Yinan He, Yizhuo Li, Yi Wang

task_types: trustworthiness evaluation, spatiotemporal analysis, cross-modal assessment
description: Trust-videoLLMs is the first comprehensive benchmark evaluating trustworthiness in videoLLMs across five critical dimensions: truthfulness, robustness, safety, fairness, and privacy. It comprises 30 tasks with adapted, synthetic, and annotated videos to assess spatiotemporal risks and cross-modal impact.
paper_title: Benchmarking the Trustworthiness in Multimodal LLMs for Video Understanding
authors: Youze Wang, Zijun Chen, Ruoyu Chen, Shishen Gu, Wenbo Hu

task_types: fake news detection, perception evaluation, understanding assessment, reasoning analysis
description: POVFNDB is a process-oriented benchmark comprising 10 tasks designed to systematically evaluate MLLMs' perception, understanding, and reasoning capabilities in video fake news detection. It contains 36,240 human-annotated QA pairs spanning 15 distinct evaluation dimensions for the entire detection process.
paper_title: Perception, Understanding and Reasoning, A Multimodal Benchmark for Video Fake News Detection
authors: Cui Yakun, Peng Qi, Fushuo Huo, Hang Du, Weijie Shi

description: Multi-modal Untrimmed Video Retrieval benchmark supporting video-centric multi-modal queries through text descriptions, video tags, and mask prompts. Focuses on multi-level visual correspondence across six levels (copy, event, scene, instance, action, others) for long-video platform applications.
paper_title: MUVR: A Multi-Modal Untrimmed Video Retrieval Benchmark with Multi-Level Visual Correspondence
authors: Yue Feng, Jinwei Hu, Qijia Lu, Jiawei Niu, Li Tan

description: First comprehensive full-spectrum Multi-Modal Evaluation benchmark for MLLMs in video analysis spanning 6 primary visual domains with 30 subfields. Features diversity in video duration (11 seconds to 1 hour), breadth in data modalities (video, subtitles, audio), and rigorous manual annotation quality.
paper_title: Video-MME: The First-Ever Comprehensive Evaluation Benchmark of Multi-modal LLMs in Video Analysis
authors: Chaoyou Fu, Yuhan Dai, Yongdong Luo, Lei Li, Shuhuai Ren

task_types: counterfactual reasoning, video understanding, structured reasoning
description: COVER is a multidimensional multimodal benchmark for counterfactual video reasoning that systematically evaluates MLLMs across abstract-concrete and perception-cognition dimensions. It uniquely decomposes complex queries into structured sub-questions, enabling fine-grained analysis of reasoning capabilities in dynamic video environments.
paper_title: Reasoning is All You Need for Video Generalization: A Counterfactual Benchmark with Sub-question Evaluation
authors: Qiji Zhou, Yifan Gong, Guangsheng Bao, Hongjie Qiu, Jinqiang Li

task_types: video-conditioned QA, cross-frame analysis, web retrieval, multi-hop reasoning
description: VideoDR is the first video deep research benchmark for video-conditioned open-domain question answering that requires cross-frame visual anchor extraction, interactive web retrieval, and multi-hop reasoning over joint video-web evidence. It uniquely combines video analysis with open-web search capabilities for comprehensive video understanding.
paper_title: Watching, Reasoning, and Searching: A Video Deep Research Benchmark on Open Web for Agentic Video Reasoning
authors: Chengwen Liu, Xiaomin Yu, Zhuoyue Chang, Zhe Huang, Shuo Zhang

task_types: video understanding, knowledge-based QA, decision making
description: Video-Bench provides comprehensive evaluation across three distinct levels: Video-exclusive Understanding, Prior Knowledge-based QA, and Comprehension and Decision-making. It comprises 10 meticulously crafted tasks and includes an automatic toolkit for processing model outputs, targeting the ultimate goal of achieving artificial general intelligence in video understanding.
paper_title: Video-Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark and Toolkit for Evaluating Video-based Large Language Models
authors: Munan Ning, Bin Zhu, Yujia Xie, Bin Lin, Jiaxi Cui

description: SCBench introduces sports video commentary generation as a novel benchmarking task, featuring intricate visual information and sequential events. It includes SCORES, a six-dimensional metric specifically designed for sports commentary, and CommentarySet with 5,775 annotated video clips, addressing limitations in current benchmarks that use simple videos.
paper_title: SCBench: A Sports Commentary Benchmark for Video LLMs
authors: Kuangzhi Ge, Lingjun Chen, Kevin Zhang, Yulin Luo, Tianyu Shi

description: InstructionBench challenges models' advanced temporal reasoning within instructional videos characterized by strict step-by-step flow. It contains 5k questions across 700+ videos with both coarse-grained event-level and fine-grained object-level reasoning, filtering out questions answerable by common-sense knowledge to focus on visual perception and analysis.
paper_title: InstructionBench: An Instructional Video Understanding Benchmark
authors: Haiwan Wei, Yitian Yuan, Xiaohan Lan, Wei Ke, Lin Ma

description: MVP (Minimal Video Pairs) mitigates shortcut solutions through minimal-change pairs - visually similar videos with identical questions but opposing answers. This design ensures models must provide correct answers for both examples in the pair, as models relying solely on visual or textual biases would achieve below random performance.
paper_title: A Shortcut-aware Video-QA Benchmark for Physical Understanding via Minimal Video Pairs
authors: Benno Krojer, Mojtaba Komeili, Candace Ross, Quentin Garrido, Koustuv Sinha

description: Video-TT (Video Thinking Test) assesses if video LLMs can interpret real-world videos as effectively as humans, comprising 1,000 YouTube Shorts videos each with one open-ended question and four adversarial questions. It evaluates both correctness in visual content interpretation and robustness against natural adversarial questions probing visual and narrative complexity.
paper_title: Towards Video Thinking Test: A Holistic Benchmark for Advanced Video Reasoning and Understanding
authors: Yuanhan Zhang, Yunice Chew, Yuhao Dong, Aria Leo, Bo Hu

description: CL-VISTA addresses limitations of existing continual learning benchmarks by curating 8 diverse tasks spanning perception, understanding, and reasoning that induce substantial distribution shifts. It establishes a comprehensive evaluation framework across 6 protocols and 3 dimensions: performance, computational efficiency, and memory footprint, specifically tailored for Video-LLMs.
paper_title: CL-VISTA: Benchmarking Continual Learning in Video Large Language Models
authors: Haiyang Guo, Yichen Shi, Fei Zhu, Wenzhuo Liu, Hongbo Zhao

description: HERBench makes multi-evidence integration unavoidable by requiring each question to need at least three non-overlapping cues from distinct video segments. It introduces Minimum Required Frame-Set (MRFS) to measure evidential demand and contains 26,806 five-way multiple-choice questions across 12 compositional tasks, addressing single-cue shortcuts in existing benchmarks.
paper_title: HERBench: A Benchmark for Multi-Evidence Integration in Video Question Answering
authors: Dan Ben-Ami, Gabriele Serussi, Kobi Cohen, Chaim Baskin

description: VELOCITI studies Video-LLM compositional reasoning by disentangling and assessing comprehension of agents, actions, and their associations across multiple events. It adopts StrictVLE setup requiring correct classification rather than ranking, and focuses on short video compositional reasoning.
paper_title: VELOCITI: Benchmarking Video-Language Compositional Reasoning with Strict Entailment
authors: Darshana Saravanan, Varun Gupta, Darshan Singh, Zeeshan Khan, Vineet Gandhi

description: Shot2Story provides detailed shot-level captions, comprehensive video summaries and QA pairs for multi-shot video understanding. It includes captions for both visual signals and human narrations, designed to test single-shot captioning, multi-shot summarization, and multi-shot QA tasks.
paper_title: Shot2Story: A New Benchmark for Comprehensive Understanding of Multi-shot Videos
authors: Mingfei Han, Linjie Yang, Xiaojun Chang, Lina Yao, Heng Wang

task_types: risk assessment, temporal reasoning, anticipatory reasoning
description: A benchmark for anticipatory reasoning from early risk cues in videos, where models must identify and reason from the earliest visual signals that indicate potential safety concerns before accidents occur. Unlike existing datasets that provide access to full video sequences including the accident itself, RiskCueBench focuses on early risk signal clips to better reflect real-world conditions.
paper_title: RiskCueBench: Benchmarking Anticipatory Reasoning from Early Risk Cues in Video-Language Models
authors: Sha Luo, Yogesh Prabhu, Timothy Ossowski, Kaiping Chen, Junjie Hu

task_types: action spotting, camera shot segmentation, replay grounding
description: A large-scale corpus with 300k manual annotations for soccer broadcast video understanding, extending beyond basic tasks to include action spotting, camera shot segmentation with boundary detection, and a novel replay grounding task. The benchmark focuses on holistic understanding of broadcast videos requiring generic reasoning about video editing content.
paper_title: SoccerNet-v2: A Dataset and Benchmarks for Holistic Understanding of Broadcast Soccer Videos
authors: Adrien Deliège, Anthony Cioppa, Silvio Giancola, Meisam J. Seikavandi, Jacob V. Dueholm

task_types: understanding tasks, reasoning tasks, anomaly detection, interaction understanding
description: A versatile video QA benchmark with 25,000 questions from 3,400 videos spanning 14 categories and various durations. Encompasses 19 types of understanding tasks (anomaly detection, interaction understanding) and 8 reasoning tasks (logical reasoning, causal reasoning), constructed using an automatic framework with GPT-4o and advanced analysis tools.
paper_title: VideoVista: A Versatile Benchmark for Video Understanding and Reasoning
authors: Yunxin Li, Xinyu Chen, Baotian Hu, Longyue Wang, Haoyuan Shi

description: A corruption robustness benchmark for spatial-temporal models that considers temporal corruptions beyond spatial corruptions in images. The first systematic study to establish corruption robustness benchmarks specifically for video understanding, evaluating CNN-based and Transformer-based spatial-temporal models against various temporal and spatial corruptions.
paper_title: Benchmarking the Robustness of Spatial-Temporal Models Against Corruptions
authors: Chenyu Yi, Siyuan Yang, Haoliang Li, Yap-peng Tan, Alex Kot

description: A benchmark for multimodal and long-form procedural video understanding in novel domains using International Space Station spacewalk recordings. Features temporally segmented and labeled tasks with two main tasks: step recognition and video question answering, designed to test generalization to novel domains and utilization of long temporal context.
paper_title: Spacewalk-18: A Benchmark for Multimodal and Long-form Procedural Video Understanding in Novel Domains
authors: Zitian Tang, Rohan Myer Krishnan, Zhiqiu Yu, Chen Sun

description: A comprehensive benchmark suite with two components: Video Task Adaption Benchmark (VidTAB) for evaluating task adaptability under few-shot conditions, and Video Embedding Benchmark (VidEB) for assessing representation power. Addresses limitations of existing benchmarks regarding diversity, evaluation costs, and saturated performance metrics.
paper_title: VideoEval: Comprehensive Benchmark Suite for Low-Cost Evaluation of Video Foundation Model
authors: Xinhao Li, Zhenpeng Huang, Jing Wang, Kunchang Li, Limin Wang

description: A benchmark for detailed omnimodal captioning of short-form user-generated videos (TikTok), featuring 1000 videos with balanced integration of audio and visual modalities. Uses a structured three-stage human-in-the-loop annotation pipeline covering audio-only, visual-only, and joint audio-visual semantics, with 4000 QA pairs probing both unimodal and cross-modal understanding.
paper_title: UGC-VideoCaptioner: An Omni UGC Video Detail Caption Model and New Benchmarks
authors: Peiran Wu, Yunze Liu, Zhengdong Zhu, Enmin Zhou, Junxiao Shen

description: A fine-grained Video Corpus Moment Retrieval benchmark requiring localization of best-matched moments from corpus with partially matched candidates. Introduces an automatic video-text annotation pipeline using LLMs and LMMs with Statics and Dynamics Enhanced Captioning modules, plus a Fine-Granularity Aware Noise Evaluator to filter inaccurate annotations.
paper_title: VERIFIED: A Video Corpus Moment Retrieval Benchmark for Fine-Grained Video Understanding
authors: Houlun Chen, Xin Wang, Hong Chen, Zeyang Zhang, Wei Feng

task_types: agentic perception, multi-agent reasoning, temporal grounding
description: A framework for evaluating agentic-centric perception and reasoning through 3D gameplay videos with dense annotations at 1.22 labels/second. Features time-synced concurrent captions structured around Self, Other Agents, and World, with 2.4K diagnostic QA pairs organized into three cognitive complexity levels and a structured distractor taxonomy for fine-grained hallucination analysis.
paper_title: GameplayQA: A Benchmarking Framework for Decision-Dense POV-Synced Multi-Video Understanding of 3D Virtual Agents
authors: Yunzhe Wang, Runhui Xu, Kexin Zheng, Tianyi Zhang, Jayavibhav Niranjan Kogundi

description: First comprehensive benchmark to evaluate proactive interaction capabilities in video large language models, where systems autonomously determine timing of multi-turn responses during video playback. Introduces PAUC metric that accounts for temporal dynamics of model responses.
paper_title: ProactiveVideoQA: A Comprehensive Benchmark Evaluating Proactive Interactions in Video Large Language Models
authors: Yueqian Wang, Xiaojun Meng, Yifan Wang, Huishuai Zhang, Dongyan Zhao

description: Massive benchmark designed to evaluate capabilities of LMMs in understanding Multi-Discipline Lectures. Focuses on cognitive challenges requiring both perception and reasoning in lecture comprehension across multiple academic disciplines.
paper_title: Video-MMLU: A Massive Multi-Discipline Lecture Understanding Benchmark
authors: Enxin Song, Wenhao Chai, Weili Xu, Jianwen Xie, Yuxuan Liu

task_types: open-ended video QA, multimodal reasoning, visual-dialogue integration
description: MovieRecapsQA is the first reference-free open-ended VideoQA benchmark using movie recap videos, generating questions that require multimodal reasoning with necessary 'facts' for evaluation. It enables evaluation across different input settings and categorizes questions by required modality (visual, dialogue, or both).
paper_title: MovieRecapsQA: A Multimodal Open-Ended Video Question-Answering Benchmark
authors: Shaden Shaar, Bradon Thymes, Sirawut Chaixanien, Claire Cardie, Bharath Hariharan

task_types: text-to-video retrieval, video-to-text retrieval, video captioning
description: MSVD-Indonesian introduces the first public Indonesian video-text dataset by translating English MSVD captions into Indonesian. It uniquely addresses the gap in multimodal research for Indonesian language, enabling evaluation of cross-lingual transfer learning approaches for video-text tasks in under-resourced languages.
paper_title: MSVD-Indonesian: A Benchmark for Multimodal Video-Text Tasks in Indonesian
authors: Willy Fitra Hendria

task_types: scientific video reasoning, domain-specific understanding, spatiotemporal perception
description: SciVideoBench assesses advanced video reasoning in scientific contexts using carefully crafted multiple-choice questions from cutting-edge scientific experimental videos spanning 25+ specialized academic subjects. It uniquely demands sophisticated domain-specific knowledge, precise spatiotemporal perception, and intricate logical reasoning rather than simple perception tasks.
paper_title: SciVideoBench: Benchmarking Scientific Video Reasoning in Large Multimodal Models
authors: Andong Deng, Taojiannan Yang, Shoubin Yu, Lincoln Spencer, Mohit Bansal

description: Introduces a benchmark that systematically evaluates MLLM robustness against five tampering techniques (rotation, masking, substitution, repetition, dropping) with ~3.4K original videos expanded into over ~17K tampered clips. Covers 19 distinct video manipulation tasks based on real-world scenarios.
paper_title: MVTamperBench: Evaluating Robustness of Vision-Language Models
authors: Amit Agarwal, Srikant Panda, Angeline Charles, Bhargava Kumar, Hitesh Patel

description: VIBE provides annotation-free evaluation of video-to-text summaries using grounding (visual content alignment) and utility (task informativeness) metrics. It uniquely evaluates VLM outputs for decision-making efficiency across traffic monitoring, research screening, and video understanding tasks.
paper_title: VIBE: Annotation-Free Video-to-Text Information Bottleneck Evaluation for TL;DR
authors: Shenghui Chen, Po-han Li, Sandeep Chinchali, Ufuk Topcu

task_types: video summarization evaluation, information loss measurement
description: ViSIL introduces an information-theoretic framework that quantifies video information not captured by multimodal summaries through VLM inference. It enables unified evaluation across disparate summary formats (text vs. keyframes) and establishes Pareto-optimal frontiers for information-speed trade-offs.
paper_title: ViSIL: Unified Evaluation of Information Loss in Multimodal Video Captioning
authors: Po-han Li, Shenghui Chen, Ufuk Topcu, Sandeep Chinchali

task_types: phonological sign-form prediction, transparency, graded iconicity ratings
description: Novel video-based benchmark that adapts psycholinguistic measures to evaluate VLMs on sign language form-meaning mapping through three tasks: phonological sign-form prediction, transparency (inferring meaning from visual form), and graded iconicity ratings. Tests VLMs' ability to recover essential mappings from dynamic human motion rather than static context.
paper_title: The Visual Iconicity Challenge: Evaluating Vision-Language Models on Sign Language Form-Meaning Mapping
authors: Onur Keleş, Aslı Özyürek, Gerardo Ortega, Kadir Gökgöz, Esam Ghaleb

task_types: action recognition, character recognition, world model evaluation
description: VLM-based evaluator for video world model rollouts featuring evaluation protocol targeting action recognition and character recognition across binary, multiple-choice, and open-ended formats. Enables fine-grained, temporally grounded assessment of world model performance.
paper_title: Adapting Vision-Language Models for Evaluating World Models
authors: Mariya Hendriksen, Tabish Rashid, David Bignell, Raluca Georgescu, Abdelhak Lemkhenter

task_types: video quality assessment, object detection, tracking, segmentation
description: Diagnostic evaluation framework for AI-generated driving videos with taxonomy of failure modes including visual artifacts, implausible motion, and traffic violations. Tests suitability of generated videos for autonomous driving model training and evaluation.
paper_title: Are AI-Generated Driving Videos Ready for Autonomous Driving? A Diagnostic Evaluation Framework
authors: Xinhao Xiang, Abhijeet Rastogi, Jiawei Zhang

task_types: visual knowledge understanding, intuitive physics, intention recognition, world knowledge reasoning
description: Comprehensive benchmark evaluating visual knowledge understanding through 1,680 questions in 1,249 videos covering 8 core types spanning world-centric (intuitive physics) and human-centric (subjective intentions) visual knowledge. Bridges perception and reasoning by assessing high-level vision-grounded semantics.
paper_title: VKnowU: Evaluating Visual Knowledge Understanding in Multimodal LLMs
authors: Tianxiang Jiang, Sheng Xia, Yicheng Xu, Linquan Wu, Xiangyu Zeng

task_types: robustness evaluation, counterfactual video understanding, OOD detection
description: First benchmark for evaluating MLLMs on dynamic out-of-distribution counterfactual video test sets by editing Style, Object, Background and their compositions. Tests robustness of video understanding capabilities when faced with manipulated video content.
paper_title: RO-Bench: Large-scale robustness evaluation of MLLMs with text-driven counterfactual videos
authors: Zixi Yang, Jiapeng Li, Muxi Diao, Yinuo Jing, Kongming Liang

task_types: coherence validation, error awareness, error type detection, reasoning evaluation
description: VF-Eval introduces four tasks to evaluate MLLMs' ability to generate feedback on AI-generated content videos: coherence validation, error awareness, error type detection, and reasoning evaluation. It specifically focuses on assessing MLLMs' interpretation capabilities for synthetic AIGC videos rather than natural videos.
paper_title: VF-Eval: Evaluating Multimodal LLMs for Generating Feedback on AIGC Videos
authors: Tingyu Song, Tongyan Hu, Guo Gan, Yilun Zhao

task_types: narrative understanding, entity tracking, temporal reasoning, compositional reasoning
description: NarrativeTrack is the first benchmark to evaluate narrative understanding in MLLMs through fine-grained entity-centric reasoning. It uses a Compositional Reasoning Progression (CRP) framework that progressively increases narrative complexity across entity existence, changes, and ambiguity, testing temporal persistence to contextual evolution and fine-grained perceptual reasoning.
paper_title: NarrativeTrack: Evaluating Entity-Centric Reasoning for Narrative Understanding
authors: Hyeonjeong Ha, Jinjin Ge, Bo Feng, Kaixin Ma, Gargi Chakraborty

task_types: video evaluation, preference annotation, quality assessment
description: UVE-Bench evaluates the performance of MLLMs as unified evaluators for AI-generated videos, collecting videos from state-of-the-art video generative models with pairwise human preference annotations across 15 evaluation aspects. It investigates the feasibility of using MLLMs for comprehensive automatic metrics of AI-generated video content.
paper_title: UVE: Are MLLMs Unified Evaluators for AI-Generated Videos?
authors: Yuanxin Liu, Rui Zhu, Shuhuai Ren, Jiacong Wang, Haoyuan Guo

task_types: multi-video understanding, cross-video reasoning, temporal analysis
description: Introduces the first comprehensive benchmark for evaluating Multi-Video Understanding in MLLMs with 1,824 question-answer pairs spanning 4,959 videos. Assesses eight core competencies across fundamental perception and high-order reasoning tasks aligned with real-world applications like sports analytics and autonomous driving.
paper_title: MVU-Eval: Towards Multi-Video Understanding Evaluation for Multimodal LLMs
authors: Tianhao Peng, Haochen Wang, Yuanxing Zhang, Zekun Wang, Zili Wang

task_types: explanation, counterfactual reasoning, future prediction, domain-specific understanding
description: MMWorld evaluates MLLMs as "world models" through multi-discipline, multi-faceted video understanding. It uniquely covers various disciplines requiring domain expertise and tests multi-faceted reasoning including explanation, counterfactual thinking, and future prediction across 69 subdisciplines.
paper_title: MMWorld: Towards Multi-discipline Multi-faceted World Model Evaluation in Videos
authors: Xuehai He, Weixi Feng, Kaizhi Zheng, Yujie Lu, Wanrong Zhu

task_types: text recognition, text comprehension, text reasoning
description: Comprehensive video OCR benchmark with 10 task categories comprising 25 individual tasks across 44 diverse scenarios. Features 1,464 videos with varying resolutions and 2,000 manually annotated QA pairs, extending beyond text recognition to incorporate deeper comprehension and reasoning of textual content within videos.
paper_title: MME-VideoOCR: Evaluating OCR-Based Capabilities of Multimodal LLMs in Video Scenarios
authors: Yang Shi, Huanqian Wang, Wulin Xie, Huanyao Zhang, Lijie Zhao

description: Scalable synthetic evaluator framework that decouples video content from queries by inserting unrelated visual 'needles' into original videos. VNBench includes tasks such as retrieval, ordering, and counting to evaluate temporal perception, chronological ordering, and spatio-temporal coherence across different video lengths.
paper_title: Needle In A Video Haystack: A Scalable Synthetic Evaluator for Video MLLMs
authors: Zijia Zhao, Haoyu Lu, Yuqi Huo, Yifan Du, Tongtian Yue

description: Fine-grained video caption benchmark using Monte Carlo Tree Search to construct diverse descriptive sentences representing video content. Enables comprehensive evaluation of MLLMs on video captioning through iterative enhancement of video details like actions, objects, and environment.
paper_title: Evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models on Video Captioning via Monte Carlo Tree Search
authors: Linhao Yu, Xinguang Ji, Yahui Liu, Fanheng Kong, Chenxi Sun

description: A diagnostic suite for systematically evaluating existing video understanding evaluations and distilling spatio-temporal challenges. Analyzes that 54% of existing benchmark samples are solvable without visual input or temporal context.
paper_title: Video-Oasis: Rethinking Evaluation of Video Understanding
authors: Geuntaek Lim, Minho Shim, Sungjune Park, Jaeyun Lee, Inwoong Lee

description: A new benchmark for evaluating video description models featuring videos from diverse sources and varying complexity. Includes an automatic method specifically designed to assess the quality of fine-grained video descriptions.
paper_title: Tarsier: Recipes for Training and Evaluating Large Video Description Models
authors: Jiawei Wang, Liping Yuan, Yuchen Zhang, Haomiao Sun

description: Novel fill-in-the-blanks evaluation framework for video understanding that tests model understanding by requiring prediction of masked noun phrases in video captions. Avoids weaknesses of multiple-choice QA and open-ended captioning evaluation.
paper_title: FIBER: Fill-in-the-Blanks as a Challenging Video Understanding Evaluation Framework
authors: Santiago Castro, Ruoyao Wang, Pingxuan Huang, Ian Stewart, Oana Ignat

description: First comprehensive benchmark for evaluating multimodal reward models in video domain covering perception, knowledge, reasoning, and safety. Features 1,563 annotated preference triplets with 1,482 unique videos and 1,559 distinct questions.
paper_title: VideoRewardBench: Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Reward Models for Video Understanding
authors: Zhihong Zhang, Xiaojian Huang, Jin Xu, Zhuodong Luo, Xinzhi Wang

description: First comprehensive benchmark for factuality evaluation in video contexts requiring integration of external knowledge. Features multi-hop fact-seeking questions with per-hop sub-QAs and short-form definitive answers requiring temporal grounding.
paper_title: Video SimpleQA: Towards Factuality Evaluation in Large Video Language Models
authors: Meng Cao, Pengfei Hu, Yingyao Wang, Jihao Gu, Haoran Tang

description: Evaluates Video-LMMs on complex real-world video reasoning and robustness across 11 diverse video dimensions. Addresses limitations of existing benchmarks that focus only on general video comprehension and neglects reasoning capabilities in complex scenarios.
paper_title: How Good is my Video LMM? Complex Video Reasoning and Robustness Evaluation Suite for Video-LMMs
authors: Muhammad Uzair Khattak, Muhammad Ferjad Naeem, Jameel Hassan, Muzammal Naseer, Federico Tombari

description: Arena-style benchmark with automated evaluation framework using user simulation to generate open-ended, adaptive questions for video analysis. Features modified ELO Rating System and fault-driven evolution strategy for increasing question complexity.
paper_title: VideoAutoArena: An Automated Arena for Evaluating Large Multimodal Models in Video Analysis through User Simulation
authors: Ziyang Luo, Haoning Wu, Dongxu Li, Jing Ma, Mohan Kankanhalli

description: Manually annotated benchmark for complex, long-horizon, perception-centric video reasoning where no single moment is sufficient. Requires multiple temporally separated pieces of visual evidence and compositional constraints under conjunctive and sequential logic.
paper_title: PerceptionComp: A Video Benchmark for Complex Perception-Centric Reasoning
authors: Shaoxuan Li, Zhixuan Zhao, Hanze Deng, Zirun Ma, Shulin Tian

task_types: video segmentation, temporal reasoning, reasoning segmentation
description: First benchmark for temporally-constrained video reasoning segmentation, where models must infer when target objects become contextually relevant based on text queries with temporal reasoning. Features automated benchmark construction method for scalability.
paper_title: Temporally-Constrained Video Reasoning Segmentation and Automated Benchmark Construction
authors: Yiqing Shen, Chenjia Li, Chenxiao Fan, Mathias Unberath

task_types: video question answering, hand-object interaction, video segmentation
description: Fine-grained video QA benchmark for hand-object interaction dynamics covering both manipulation and effect aspects. Features six question types and includes segmentation masks for object/part-level reasoning evaluation.
paper_title: HanDyVQA: A Video QA Benchmark for Fine-Grained Hand-Object Interaction Dynamics
authors: Masatoshi Tateno, Gido Kato, Hirokatsu Kataoka, Yoichi Sato, Takuma Yagi

description: First dedicated benchmark for video language continual learning across video-text tasks. Features ten-minute-long videos with corresponding language queries to evaluate models' ability to continuously adapt while retaining prior knowledge.
paper_title: ViLCo-Bench: VIdeo Language COntinual learning Benchmark
authors: Tianqi Tang, Shohreh Deldari, Hao Xue, Celso De Melo, Flora D. Salim

description: Benchmarking dataset with longer videos across six categories and dense concept annotations for video summarization. Introduces novel recipe using pareto optimality to automatically generate multiple reference summaries and comprehensive evaluation framework.
paper_title: Realistic Video Summarization through VISIOCITY: A New Benchmark and Evaluation Framework
authors: Vishal Kaushal, Suraj Kothawade, Rishabh Iyer, Ganesh Ramakrishnan

description: Long video dataset for single object tracking with 50 HD videos covering over 400 minutes (676K frames). More than 20x larger in average duration per sequence compared to existing tracking datasets, enabling assessment of long-term tracking performance.
paper_title: Long-Term Visual Object Tracking Benchmark
authors: Abhinav Moudgil, Vineet Gandhi

description: Benchmark for long-form text-to-video generation featuring multi-scene prompts with hierarchical metadata. Introduces LoCoT2V-Eval framework covering perceptual quality, text-video alignment, temporal quality, and Human Expectation Realization Degree (HERD).
paper_title: LoCoT2V-Bench: Benchmarking Long-Form and Complex Text-to-Video Generation
authors: Xiangqing Zheng, Chengyue Wu, Kehai Chen, Min Zhang

description: Real-world, large-scale dataset for multi-target multi-camera tracking with 16 multi-modal cameras in campus and factory environments. Features spatially aligned RGB and thermal cameras across various time, weather, and season conditions.
paper_title: MTMMC: A Large-Scale Real-World Multi-Modal Camera Tracking Benchmark
authors: Sanghyun Woo, Kwanyong Park, Inkyu Shin, Myungchul Kim, In So Kweon

description: Benchmarking dataset with longer videos across six categories and dense concept annotations for realistic video summarization. Proposes evaluation framework for better quantitative assessment closer to human judgment than single measures.
paper_title: How Good is a Video Summary? A New Benchmarking Dataset and Evaluation Framework Towards Realistic Video Summarization
authors: Vishal Kaushal, Suraj Kothawade, Anshul Tomar, Rishabh Iyer, Ganesh Ramakrishnan

description: Expert-validated benchmark for confusion recognition and localization in educational videos. Features multi-stage filtering pipeline with model-assisted screening, researcher curation, and expert validation for higher-quality annotations.
paper_title: ConfusionBench: An Expert-Validated Benchmark for Confusion Recognition and Localization in Educational Videos
authors: Lu Dong, Xiao Wang, Mark Frank, Srirangaraj Setlur, Venu Govindaraju

description: Story-oriented benchmark for assessing text-to-video models' story-completion capabilities with prompts representing short stories of 2-4 consecutive events. Uses advanced vision-language models with unanimous voting for reliable event completion verification.
paper_title: Is Your World Simulator a Good Story Presenter? A Consecutive Events-Based Benchmark for Future Long Video Generation
authors: Yiping Wang, Xuehai He, Kuan Wang, Luyao Ma, Jianwei Yang

description: RealVideoQuest evaluates text-to-video models' ability to answer real-world, visually grounded queries with video responses. It identifies actual user queries requiring visual demonstrations and builds high-quality query-video pairs through multistage retrieval, addressing the gap in visual response generation for complex user intents.
paper_title: Respond Beyond Language: A Benchmark for Video Generation in Response to Realistic User Intents
authors: Shuting Wang, Yunqi Liu, Zixin Yang, Ning Hu, Zhicheng Dou

description: RVTBench introduces Reasoning Visual Tasks (RVTs) that extend beyond traditional video reasoning segmentation to accommodate multiple output formats. It uses a novel automated construction pipeline leveraging digital twin representations and contains 3,896 queries across four RVT types, three reasoning categories, and four difficulty levels from 200 video sequences.
paper_title: RVTBench: A Benchmark for Visual Reasoning Tasks
authors: Yiqing Shen, Chenjia Li, Chenxiao Fan, Mathias Unberath

task_types: generative visual reasoning, multi-step planning, pattern extrapolation
description: Gen-ViRe is a framework for evaluating Chain-of-Frames (CoF) reasoning in video generation models. It decomposes reasoning into six cognitive dimensions and 24 subtasks, providing the first quantitative assessment of video models as reasoners through multi-source data curation and hybrid VLM-assisted evaluation.
paper_title: Can World Simulators Reason? Gen-ViRe: A Generative Visual Reasoning Benchmark
authors: Xinxin Liu, Zhaopan Xu, Ming Li, Kai Wang, Yong Jae Lee

task_types: chain-of-frame reasoning, spatial reasoning, temporal reasoning, physical reasoning
description: MME-CoF is a compact benchmark that enables in-depth assessment of Chain-of-Frame (CoF) reasoning in video models. It evaluates reasoning behavior across 12 dimensions including spatial, geometric, physical, temporal, and embodied logic, systematically characterizing both strengths and failure modes of video models as zero-shot reasoners.
paper_title: Are Video Models Ready as Zero-Shot Reasoners? An Empirical Study with the MME-CoF Benchmark
authors: Ziyu Guo, Xinyan Chen, Renrui Zhang, Ruichuan An, Yu Qi

task_types: video retrieval, moment localization, memory-based search
description: A comprehensive system for evaluating real-world video memory search with 1,440 samples spanning 20 diverse categories and four duration groups from real-world open-web videos. Uses hierarchical description framework with Global Impression, Key Moment, Temporal Context, and Auditory Memory to mimic realistic multi-dimensional search cues.
paper_title: Beyond Closed-Pool Video Retrieval: A Benchmark and Agent Framework for Real-World Video Search and Moment Localization
authors: Tao Yu, Yujia Yang, Haopeng Jin, Junhao Gong, Xinlong Chen

description: ViLPAct is a vision-language benchmark for human activity planning where embodied AI agents reason and forecast future actions based on video clips and textual intents. It extends Charades dataset with crowdsourced intents and focuses on compositional generalization challenges.
paper_title: ViLPAct: A Benchmark for Compositional Generalization on Multimodal Human Activities
authors: Terry Yue Zhuo, Yaqing Liao, Yuecheng Lei, Lizhen Qu, Gerard de Melo

description: First comprehensive benchmark for video anomaly detection in smart home scenarios using multi-modal large language models. Features novel anomaly taxonomy with seven categories including Wildlife, Senior Care, and Baby Monitoring with detailed annotations.
paper_title: SmartHome-Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Video Anomaly Detection in Smart Homes Using Multi-Modal Large Language Models
authors: Xinyi Zhao, Congjing Zhang, Pei Guo, Wei Li, Lin Chen

description: An enhanced comprehensive benchmark suite that evaluates video generation quality across 16 hierarchical dimensions, supporting both text-to-video and image-to-video evaluation. Introduces trustworthiness evaluation and includes human preference annotations to validate alignment with human perception.
paper_title: VBench++: Comprehensive and Versatile Benchmark Suite for Video Generative Models
authors: Ziqi Huang, Fan Zhang, Xiaojie Xu, Yinan He, Jiashuo Yu

description: A comprehensive benchmark suite for video generation that dissects video quality into 16 specific, hierarchical dimensions with tailored prompts and evaluation methods. Provides human preference annotations to validate alignment with human perception and offers insights into current models' strengths and weaknesses.
paper_title: VBench: Comprehensive Benchmark Suite for Video Generative Models
authors: Ziqi Huang, Yinan He, Jiashuo Yu, Fan Zhang, Chenyang Si

description: A large-scale benchmark dataset created with five SOTA video generation models based on hand-crafted instructions, systematically evaluating video generation across nine critical quality dimensions. Accompanied by AIGVE-Tool, a unified framework providing structured evaluation pipeline with five-category taxonomy.
paper_title: AIGVE-Tool: AI-Generated Video Evaluation Toolkit with Multifaceted Benchmark
authors: Xinhao Xiang, Xiao Liu, Zizhong Li, Zhuosheng Liu, Jiawei Zhang

description: A comprehensive video generation benchmark featuring a rich prompt suite and extensive evaluation dimensions, representing the first systematic attempt to leverage MLLMs across all dimensions relevant to video generation assessment. Achieves superior alignment with human preferences through few-shot scoring and chain-of-query techniques.
paper_title: Video-Bench: Human-Aligned Video Generation Benchmark
authors: Hui Han, Siyuan Li, Jiaqi Chen, Yiwen Yuan, Yuling Wu

task_types: character consistency evaluation, face consistency assessment
description: A framework specifically for evaluating and comparing character consistency in AI-generated videos, addressing the major challenge of maintaining coherent appearance and attributes across video sequences. Provides standardized metrics to highlight gaps in existing character consistency solutions.
paper_title: Face Consistency Benchmark for GenAI Video
authors: Michal Podstawski, Malgorzata Kudelska, Haohong Wang

task_types: action execution, story continuation, story generation
description: Provides a multi-task benchmark for continuous story visualization with three tasks of increasing difficulty: action execution, story continuation, and story generation. Features comprehensive human annotations on existing datasets and establishes guidelines for human evaluation of video stories.
paper_title: StoryBench: A Multifaceted Benchmark for Continuous Story Visualization
authors: Emanuele Bugliarello, Hernan Moraldo, Ruben Villegas, Mohammad Babaeizadeh, Mohammad Taghi Saffar

task_types: video generation evaluation, physics assessment, commonsense reasoning
description: Advances video generation evaluation beyond superficial faithfulness to intrinsic faithfulness, assessing five key dimensions: Human Fidelity, Controllability, Creativity, Physics, and Commonsense. Integrates both generalist VLMs/LLMs and specialist methods like anomaly detection for comprehensive evaluation.
paper_title: VBench-2.0: Advancing Video Generation Benchmark Suite for Intrinsic Faithfulness
authors: Dian Zheng, Ziqi Huang, Hongbo Liu, Kai Zou, Yinan He

task_types: identity preservation, lip synchronization, video quality assessment
description: Establishes a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating talking-head video generation with standardized dataset preprocessing and evaluation metrics for identity preservation, lip synchronization, video quality, and natural motion. Provides reproducible evaluation framework to replace subjective human evaluations.
paper_title: What comprises a good talking-head video generation?: A Survey and Benchmark
authors: Lele Chen, Guofeng Cui, Ziyi Kou, Haitian Zheng, Chenliang Xu

description: Introduces fine-grained evaluation of text-to-video generation through multi-aspect categorization based on major content, attributes to control, and prompt complexity. Also temporal-aware with categories tailored for video generation, enabling comprehensive analysis of T2V model performance across different prompt types.
paper_title: FETV: A Benchmark for Fine-Grained Evaluation of Open-Domain Text-to-Video Generation
authors: Yuanxin Liu, Lei Li, Shuhuai Ren, Rundong Gao, Shicheng Li

description: First unified benchmark for world generation, decomposing world generation into next-scene generation tasks with explicit camera trajectory-based layout specifications. Evaluates diverse approaches from 3D/4D scene generation to video generation models through controllability, quality, and dynamics metrics.
paper_title: WorldScore: A Unified Evaluation Benchmark for World Generation
authors: Haoyi Duan, Hong-Xing Yu, Sirui Chen, Li Fei-Fei, Jiajun Wu

description: Pioneering benchmark for Video Connecting task that generates smooth intermediate content between given start and end clips. Features 1,579 high-quality videos across 15 main categories and 72 subcategories, evaluating three core aspects: Video Quality Score, Start-End Consistency Score, and Transition Smoothness Score.
paper_title: VC-Bench: Pioneering the Video Connecting Benchmark with a Dataset and Evaluation Metrics
authors: Zhiyu Yin, Zhipeng Liu, Kehai Chen, Lemao Liu, Jin Liu

description: Comprehensive evaluation framework with 700 diverse prompts for text-to-video generation based on real-world user data analysis. Evaluates video generative models on visual qualities, content qualities, motion qualities, and text-video alignment using 17 objective metrics with human alignment scoring.
paper_title: EvalCrafter: Benchmarking and Evaluating Large Video Generation Models
authors: Yaofang Liu, Xiaodong Cun, Xuebo Liu, Xintao Wang, Yong Zhang

description: First comprehensive Video Motion Benchmark with perception-aligned motion metrics identifying five dimensions based on human perception. Features Meta-Guided Motion Prompt Generation creating diverse prompts across six key dynamic scene dimensions, achieving 35.3% improvement in correlation with human evaluation.
paper_title: VMBench: A Benchmark for Perception-Aligned Video Motion Generation
authors: Xinran Ling, Chen Zhu, Meiqi Wu, Hangyu Li, Xiaokun Feng

description: T2VPhysBench is the first physics-based benchmark that systematically evaluates whether text-to-video systems obey twelve core physical laws including Newtonian mechanics and conservation principles. It employs rigorous human evaluation protocols to assess physical consistency in generated videos.
paper_title: T2VPhysBench: A First-Principles Benchmark for Physical Consistency in Text-to-Video Generation
authors: Xuyang Guo, Jiayan Huo, Zhenmei Shi, Zhao Song, Jiahao Zhang

task_types: structural reasoning, spatial reasoning, symbolic reasoning, action planning
description: TiViBench is a hierarchical benchmark designed to evaluate reasoning capabilities of image-to-video generation models across four dimensions: structural reasoning, spatial pattern reasoning, symbolic reasoning, and action planning. It spans 24 diverse task scenarios across 3 difficulty levels.
paper_title: TiViBench: Benchmarking Think-in-Video Reasoning for Video Generative Models
authors: Harold Haodong Chen, Disen Lan, Wen-Jie Shu, Qingyang Liu, Zihan Wang

description: TDVE-DB is a large-scale benchmark dataset for text-driven video editing consisting of 3,857 edited videos generated from 12 diverse models across 8 editing categories. It includes 173,565 human subjective ratings along three dimensions: edited video quality, editing alignment, and structural consistency.
paper_title: TDVE-Assessor: Benchmarking and Evaluating the Quality of Text-Driven Video Editing with LMMs
authors: Juntong Wang, Jiarui Wang, Huiyu Duan, Guangtao Zhai, Xiongkuo Min

description: GenVideo is the first AI-generated video detection dataset featuring over one million AI-generated and real videos with rich diversity of generated content and methodologies. It covers a broad spectrum of video categories and generation techniques for developing fake video detectors.
paper_title: DeMamba: AI-Generated Video Detection on Million-Scale GenVideo Benchmark
authors: Haoxing Chen, Yan Hong, Zizheng Huang, Zhuoer Xu, Zhangxuan Gu

description: BrokenVideos is a benchmark dataset of 3,254 AI-generated videos with meticulously annotated pixel-level masks highlighting regions of visual corruption. It addresses the gap in comprehensive benchmarks for artifact localization in AI-generated videos with fine-grained spatial annotations.
paper_title: BrokenVideos: A Benchmark Dataset for Fine-Grained Artifact Localization in AI-Generated Videos
authors: Jiahao Lin, Weixuan Peng, Bojia Zi, Yifeng Gao, Xianbiao Qi

description: This work constructs a video dataset using advanced diffusion-based video generation algorithms with various semantic contents for AI-generated video detection. It includes typical video lossy operations over network transmission to generate degraded samples for robustness evaluation.
paper_title: Exposing AI-generated Videos: A Benchmark Dataset and a Local-and-Global Temporal Defect Based Detection Method
authors: Peisong He, Leyao Zhu, Jiaxing Li, Shiqi Wang, Haoliang Li

description: AIGVE-60K is a comprehensive dataset for AI-Generated Video Evaluation featuring 3,050 extensive prompts across 20 fine-grained task dimensions with 120K mean-opinion scores and 60K question-answering pairs. It provides bidirectional benchmarking for both text-to-video generation and video-to-text interpretation capabilities.
paper_title: LOVE: Benchmarking and Evaluating Text-to-Video Generation and Video-to-Text Interpretation
authors: Jiarui Wang, Huiyu Duan, Ziheng Jia, Yu Zhao, Woo Yi Yang

description: The Open-HyperMotionX Dataset and HyperMotionX Bench provide high-quality human pose annotations and curated video clips for evaluating pose-guided human image animation models under complex human motion conditions. It addresses limitations in existing benchmarks for complex human body motions.
paper_title: HyperMotionX: The Dataset and Benchmark with DiT-Based Pose-Guided Human Image Animation of Complex Motions
authors: Shuolin Xu, Siming Zheng, Ziyi Wang, HC Yu, Jinwei Chen

task_types: sequential narrative generation, narrative coherence evaluation
description: SeqBench is a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating sequential narrative coherence in text-to-video generation. It includes a carefully designed dataset of 320 prompts spanning various narrative complexities, with 2,560 human-annotated videos generated from 8 state-of-the-art T2V models.
paper_title: SeqBench: Benchmarking Sequential Narrative Generation in Text-to-Video Models
authors: Zhengxu Tang, Zizheng Wang, Luning Wang, Zitao Shuai, Chenhao Zhang

description: Celeb-DF++ is a new large-scale and challenging video DeepFake benchmark covering three forgery scenarios: Face-swap, Face-reenactment, and Talking-face. It contains high-quality forged videos generated using 22 various recent DeepFake methods with diverse architectures and generation pipelines.
paper_title: Celeb-DF++: A Large-scale Challenging Video DeepFake Benchmark for Generalizable Forensics
authors: Yuezun Li, Delong Zhu, Xinjie Cui, Siwei Lyu

description: SVTA is the first large-scale dataset for cross-modal anomaly retrieval, featuring 41,315 videos (1.36M frames) with paired captions covering 30 normal activities and 68 anomalous events. It leverages generative models to overcome data availability challenges in video anomaly retrieval.
paper_title: Towards Scalable Video Anomaly Retrieval: A Synthetic Video-Text Benchmark
authors: Shuyu Yang, Yilun Wang, Yaxiong Wang, Li Zhu, Zhedong Zheng

description: LingoQA introduces a novel dataset for visual question answering in autonomous driving with 28K unique short video scenarios and 419K annotations. It includes a specialized truthfulness classifier (Lingo-Judge) achieving 0.95 Spearman correlation with human evaluations.
paper_title: LingoQA: Visual Question Answering for Autonomous Driving
authors: Ana-Maria Marcu, Long Chen, Jan Hünermann, Alice Karnsund, Benoit Hanotte

description: Introduces VidQAP which poses video question answering as a fill-in-the-phrase task using semantic roles derived from video descriptions. Novel approach to reduce language bias by retrieving videos with different answers for the same question.
paper_title: Video Question Answering with Phrases via Semantic Roles
authors: Arka Sadhu, Kan Chen, Ram Nevatia

description: A benchmark designed to evaluate physical commonsense reasoning in text-to-video generation models, focusing on tool use, material properties, and procedural interactions. It uses a three-stage evaluation pipeline with grounded physics questions to assess whether generated videos violate basic physical laws.
paper_title: Can Your Model Separate Yolks with a Water Bottle? Benchmarking Physical Commonsense Understanding in Video Generation Models
authors: Enes Sanli, Baris Sarper Tezcan, Aykut Erdem, Erkut Erdem

description: Structured evaluation framework for professional video generation with four hierarchical taxonomies (Setup, Event, Lighting, Camera) defining 76 fine-grained control nodes. Includes 20K videos annotated by 80+ film professionals across 10+ models for cinematic control evaluation.
paper_title: Stable Cinemetrics : Structured Taxonomy and Evaluation for Professional Video Generation
authors: Agneet Chatterjee, Rahim Entezari, Maksym Zhuravinskyi, Maksim Lapin, Reshinth Adithyan

task_types: video reasoning, robustness evaluation, embodied AI assessment
description: PVRBench is a new benchmark that injects real-world perturbations (weather, occlusion, camera motion) into embodied video datasets to assess both accuracy and reasoning quality under realistic disturbances. It addresses the gap between clean, controlled evaluation settings and real-world robustness by evaluating vision-language models under conditions that cause substantial understanding and reasoning degradation.
paper_title: Are Video Reasoning Models Ready to Go Outside?
authors: Yangfan He, Changgyu Boo, Jaehong Yoon

task_types: multi-object tracking, query-driven tracking, spatiotemporal reasoning
description: Constructs a large-scale benchmark with grounded queries and sequence-level splits for query-driven multi-object tracking that formulates tracking as spatiotemporal reasoning conditioned on natural language queries. Enables evaluation of language-guided tracking with temporal coherence and identity consistency.
paper_title: QTrack: Query-Driven Reasoning for Multi-modal MOT
authors: Tajamul Ashraf, Tavaheed Tariq, Sonia Yadav, Abrar Ul Riyaz, Wasif Tak

description: Introduces a descriptive caption dataset and proposes comprehensive evaluation methods specifically for evaluating Vision Language Models in human activity recognition (HAR) for healthcare settings. Addresses the challenge of evaluating dynamic and non-deterministic VLM outputs in medical HAR applications.
paper_title: Vision Language Models for Dynamic Human Activity Recognition in Healthcare Settings
authors: Abderrazek Abid, Thanh-Cong Ho, Fakhri Karray

description: InsTrack introduces instruction tracking, where trackers receive implicit tracking instructions requiring automatic reasoning about what to track in video frames. It contains over one thousand instruction-video pairs for evaluating complex reasoning-based tracking without explicit box/mask templates.
paper_title: Tracking with Human-Intent Reasoning
authors: Jiawen Zhu, Zhi-Qi Cheng, Jun-Yan He, Chenyang Li, Bin Luo

description: A multilingual benchmark dataset for real-time video commentary generation featuring Japanese and English datasets of racing and fighting games. The benchmark evaluates both semantic relevance and timing alignment of generated commentary, supporting research on pause-aware generation and real-time multimodal content creation.
paper_title: Real-Time Generation of Game Video Commentary with Multimodal LLMs: Pause-Aware Decoding Approaches
authors: Anum Afzal, Yuki Saito, Hiroya Takamura, Katsuhito Sudoh, Shinnosuke Takamichi

task_types: video editing, highlight detection, content summarization
description: DramaAD is a novel benchmark dataset comprising over 800 short drama episodes and 500 professionally edited advertisement clips for video editing evaluation. It supports research in automatic video editing from long-form content to engaging short clips, focusing on multimodal narrative understanding and coherence.
paper_title: From Long Videos to Engaging Clips: A Human-Inspired Video Editing Framework with Multimodal Narrative Understanding
authors: Xiangfeng Wang, Xiao Li, Yadong Wei, Xueyu Song, Yang Song

description: The paper introduces HRVideoBench, the first comprehensive high-resolution video understanding benchmark. This benchmark specifically tests MLLMs' ability to process and comprehend high-resolution videos, complementing existing benchmarks that focus primarily on long-duration videos.
paper_title: VISTA: Enhancing Long-Duration and High-Resolution Video Understanding by Video Spatiotemporal Augmentation
authors: Weiming Ren, Huan Yang, Jie Min, Cong Wei, Wenhu Chen

description: The paper introduces RivaBench, the first reasoning-intensive video understanding benchmark featuring over 4,000 high-quality, expert-curated question-answer pairs. This benchmark specifically tests complex reasoning abilities across challenging scenarios like standup comedy, academic presentations, and synthetic video detection.
paper_title: video-SALMONN-o1: Reasoning-enhanced Audio-visual Large Language Model
authors: Guangzhi Sun, Yudong Yang, Jimin Zhuang, Changli Tang, Yixuan Li

task_types: event detection, temporal grounding, event reasoning, multi-event understanding
description: Introduces the first large-scale benchmark for fine-grained event-level video understanding with 12 tasks across 8 domains. Unlike existing video-level QA benchmarks, E.T. Bench evaluates models on detailed event grounding, temporal localization, and multi-event reasoning within videos.
paper_title: E.T. Bench: Towards Open-Ended Event-Level Video-Language Understanding
authors: Ye Liu, Zongyang Ma, Zhongang Qi, Yang Wu, Ying Shan

task_types: degraded video understanding, quality-aware video analysis
description: First benchmark specifically designed to evaluate how RTC-induced video degradation (compression artifacts, quality loss) affects MLLM accuracy in real-time communication scenarios. Addresses the unique challenge of AI video assistants operating under network constraints.
paper_title: Artic: AI-oriented Real-time Communication for MLLM Video Assistant
authors: Jiangkai Wu, Zhiyuan Ren, Junquan Zhong, Liming Liu, Xinggong Zhang

task_types: degraded video understanding, streaming quality assessment
description: First benchmark evaluating how video streaming quality degradation affects MLLM accuracy in AI Video Chat scenarios. Specifically designed to understand the impact of ultra-low bitrate transmission on AI video understanding capabilities.
paper_title: Chat with AI: The Surprising Turn of Real-time Video Communication from Human to AI
authors: Jiangkai Wu, Zhiyuan Ren, Liming Liu, Xinggong Zhang

task_types: semantic role labeling, event detection, entity recognition, temporal reasoning
description: Large-scale video understanding benchmark for visual semantic role labeling in videos with 29K 10-second movie clips annotated with verbs and semantic roles every 2 seconds. Introduces the novel task of VidSRL (Visual Semantic Role Labeling) with entity co-referencing and event-event relations.
paper_title: Visual Semantic Role Labeling for Video Understanding
authors: Arka Sadhu, Tanmay Gupta, Mark Yatskar, Ram Nevatia, Aniruddha Kembhavi

description: CoF-Data is a large dataset of diverse questions, answers, and corresponding frame-grounded reasoning traces about both natural and synthetic videos. It uniquely provides chain-of-frames reasoning where reasoning steps are explicitly grounded in and refer to relevant video frames.
paper_title: Chain-of-Frames: Advancing Video Understanding in Multimodal LLMs via Frame-Aware Reasoning
authors: Sara Ghazanfari, Francesco Croce, Nicolas Flammarion, Prashanth Krishnamurthy, Farshad Khorrami

description: PhysGame is the first benchmark to evaluate physical commonsense violations in gameplay videos, testing models' understanding of physics laws across four fundamental domains (mechanics, kinematics, optics, material properties). The benchmark uniquely leverages gameplay video glitches that defy physics commonsense to assess Video LLMs' physical reasoning capabilities.
paper_title: PhysGame: Uncovering Physical Commonsense Violations in Gameplay Videos
authors: Meng Cao, Haoran Tang, Haoze Zhao, Hangyu Guo, Jiaheng Liu

task_types: video understanding, planning tasks, multiple-choice questions
description: SEED-Bench-R1 is designed to systematically evaluate post-training methods for MLLMs in video understanding with intricate real-world videos and complex everyday planning tasks. It features a three-level hierarchy evaluation (in-distribution, cross-environment, and cross-environment-task scenarios) to assess generalization capabilities.
paper_title: Exploring the Effect of Reinforcement Learning on Video Understanding: Insights from SEED-Bench-R1
authors: Yi Chen, Yuying Ge, Rui Wang, Yixiao Ge, Lu Qiu

description: SynFlash is a synthetic benchmark specifically designed to evaluate short-lived anomaly events in videos with controllable sub-second anomaly patterns and frame-level ground truth. This benchmark addresses the challenge of evaluating models on brief but critical events that existing samplers often miss.
paper_title: Shot-Aware Frame Sampling for Video Understanding
authors: Mengyu Zhao, Di Fu, Yongyu Xie, Jiaxing Zhang, Zhigang Yuan

task_types: video question answering, referring object segmentation
description: VideoInfer is a manually curated object-centric video instruction dataset featuring question-answering pairs that require reasoning. The benchmark focuses on object-centric, multiround interactions with videos using both textual and visual prompts.
paper_title: Object-centric Video Question Answering with Visual Grounding and Referring
authors: Haochen Wang, Qirui Chen, Cilin Yan, Jiayin Cai, Xiaolong Jiang

description: OVQA (Open-vocabulary Video Question Answering) is a new benchmark designed to measure the generalizability of VideoQA models by considering rare and unseen answers rather than just frequent closed-vocabulary answers. It tests model performance on out-of-vocabulary answers to evaluate true generalization capabilities.
paper_title: Open-vocabulary Video Question Answering: A New Benchmark for Evaluating the Generalizability of Video Question Answering Models
authors: Dohwan Ko, Ji Soo Lee, Miso Choi, Jaewon Chu, Jihwan Park

description: ActionAtlas v1.0 is a multiple-choice video question answering benchmark featuring short videos across various sports with 934 videos showcasing 580 unique actions across 56 sports. It focuses on intricate movements and tests models' capability to discern subtle differences between similar-looking actions within specialized domains.
paper_title: ActionAtlas: A VideoQA Benchmark for Domain-specialized Action Recognition
authors: Mohammadreza Salehi, Jae Sung Park, Tanush Yadav, Aditya Kusupati, Ranjay Krishna

task_types: video narrative grounding, video question answering
description: Introduces a new annotation protocol where annotators speak and move their mouse simultaneously on videos, grounding each spoken word with visual traces. This enables capturing complex events with multiple actors and objects, creating benchmarks for video narrative grounding and video question answering tasks.
paper_title: Connecting Vision and Language with Video Localized Narratives
authors: Paul Voigtlaender, Soravit Changpinyo, Jordi Pont-Tuset, Radu Soricut, Vittorio Ferrari

task_types: compositional video question answering, compositional consistency evaluation
description: Develops a question decomposition engine that breaks compositional questions into directed acyclic graphs of sub-questions, with each parent question being a composition of its children. This enables evaluation of compositional consistency in video QA models through novel metrics.
paper_title: Measuring Compositional Consistency for Video Question Answering
authors: Mona Gandhi, Mustafa Omer Gul, Eva Prakash, Madeleine Grunde-McLaughlin, Ranjay Krishna

description: First large-scale Video Question Answering benchmark specifically engineered for all-waterway environments and Autonomous Surface Vessels. Encompasses six waterway categories with multifaceted variables like lighting and weather, using a five-tier hierarchical cognitive framework for maritime navigation.
paper_title: WaterVideoQA: ASV-Centric Perception and Rule-Compliant Reasoning via Multi-Modal Agents
authors: Runwei Guan, Shaofeng Liang, Ningwei Ouyang, Weichen Fei, Shanliang Yao

description: Introduces two new sports VideoQA benchmarks derived from FineGym and FineDiving datasets. These benchmarks enable comprehensive evaluation of sports video understanding with domain-specific challenges, complemented by a multimodal sports knowledge scene graph spanning nine sports.
paper_title: FineQuest: Adaptive Knowledge-Assisted Sports Video Understanding via Agent-of-Thoughts Reasoning
authors: Haodong Chen, Haojian Huang, XinXiang Yin, Dian Shao

task_types: video question answering, temporal localization, spatial relationship reasoning
description: Multi-Object Multi-Actor Question Answering dataset designed to address temporal and spatial granularity gaps in existing VideoQA datasets. Features ground truth scene graphs and temporal interval annotations for fine-grained video understanding with emphasis on temporal localization and spatial relationship reasoning.
paper_title: Towards Fine-Grained Video Question Answering
authors: Wei Dai, Alan Luo, Zane Durante, Debadutta Dash, Arnold Milstein

task_types: text-to-video retrieval, video question answering, video captioning
description: Video-And-Language Understanding Evaluation benchmark that assembles 11 datasets across 3 tasks: text-to-video retrieval, video question answering, and video captioning. Covers broad range of video genres, lengths, data volumes, and difficulty levels, promoting models that leverage both video frames and subtitles.
paper_title: VALUE: A Multi-Task Benchmark for Video-and-Language Understanding Evaluation
authors: Linjie Li, Jie Lei, Zhe Gan, Licheng Yu, Yen-Chun Chen

description: First fully annotated and large-scale VideoQA dataset derived from ActivityNet. Consists of complex web videos with comprehensive annotations, addressing limitations of previous small-scale and automatically generated VideoQA datasets for practical applicability.
paper_title: ActivityNet-QA: A Dataset for Understanding Complex Web Videos via Question Answering
authors: Zhou Yu, Dejing Xu, Jun Yu, Ting Yu, Zhou Zhao

description: Introduces two challenging benchmarks for Video QA and Retrieval collected from diverse video content over multimodalities. These benchmarks test understanding of complex social dynamics with multi-character interactions from large-scale video datasets.
paper_title: HERO: Hierarchical Encoder for Video+Language Omni-representation Pre-training
authors: Linjie Li, Yen-Chun Chen, Yu Cheng, Zhe Gan, Licheng Yu

description: Video question answering dataset for multimodal counterfactual reasoning covering diverse real-world scenarios. Features both first and third-person viewpoints with questions spanning physical, social, and temporal reasoning dimensions for comprehensive counterfactual ability evaluation.
paper_title: ACQUIRED: A Dataset for Answering Counterfactual Questions In Real-Life Videos
authors: Te-Lin Wu, Zi-Yi Dou, Qingyuan Hu, Yu Hou, Nischal Reddy Chandra

num_samples: 8 hours of traffic footage, 25,000+ QA pairs
task_types: traffic video question answering, incident detection
description: Curated dataset for traffic monitoring VideoQA tasks featuring real-world traffic footage from diverse intersections. Covers spatiotemporal dynamics, vehicle interactions, incident detection, and other critical traffic attributes for intelligent transportation systems.
paper_title: InterAct-Video: Reasoning-Rich Video QA for Urban Traffic
authors: Joseph Raj Vishal, Divesh Basina, Rutuja Patil, Manas Srinivas Gowda, Katha Naik

num_samples: 58K video and question pairs from 10K videos
task_types: descriptive questions, counterfactual questions, causal questions
description: Video question answering dataset requiring causal reasoning about physical forces and object interactions. Introduces causal questions based on Force Dynamics Theory involving understanding of cause, enable, and prevent relationships between objects in 20 different virtual environments.
paper_title: CRAFT: A Benchmark for Causal Reasoning About Forces and inTeractions
authors: Tayfun Ates, M. Samil Atesoglu, Cagatay Yigit, Ilker Kesen, Mert Kobas

task_types: camera motion classification, motion-augmented captioning, video question answering, video-text retrieval
description: A large-scale dataset and benchmark designed to assess camera motion understanding in videos. Features a taxonomy of camera motion primitives designed with cinematographers and expert annotations through multi-stage quality control.
paper_title: Towards Understanding Camera Motions in Any Video
authors: Zhiqiu Lin, Siyuan Cen, Daniel Jiang, Jay Karhade, Hewei Wang

description: A video captioning benchmark built from carefully designed questions by annotators that reflect human-consistent understanding of video. Addresses the gap between existing benchmarks and human cognition in video understanding.
paper_title: LFS: Learnable Frame Selector for Event-Aware and Temporally Diverse Video Captioning
authors: Lianying Chao, Linfeng Yin, Peiyu Ren, Yifan Jiang, Qiaoyu Ren

task_types: video question answering, nonverbal social reasoning
