description: A video question-answering benchmark focusing on nonverbal social reasoning capabilities using mime videos. Comprises 806 carefully annotated question-answer pairs designed to probe understanding of nonverbal social communication through gesture and movement.
paper_title: MimeQA: Towards Socially-Intelligent Nonverbal Foundation Models
authors: Hengzhi Li, Megan Tjandrasuwita, Yi R. Fung, Armando Solar-Lezama, Paul Pu Liang

description: A large-scale benchmark for fine-grained compositional reasoning over untrimmed ActivityNet videos. Features 1.4 billion unbalanced and 13.4 million balanced QA pairs automatically generated from annotated spatio-temporal scene graphs with fine-grained taxonomies.
paper_title: ANetQA: A Large-scale Benchmark for Fine-grained Compositional Reasoning over Untrimmed Videos
authors: Zhou Yu, Lixiang Zheng, Zhou Zhao, Fei Wu, Jianping Fan

description: VRR-QA focuses on Visual Relational Reasoning Beyond Explicit Cues, requiring models to infer hidden relationships and contextual cues that are only implied across frames in creative and cinematic videos. The benchmark comprises 1K meticulously expert-annotated QA pairs from movies and creative content covering 15 genres across 7 decades.
paper_title: VRR-QA: Visual Relational Reasoning in Videos Beyond Explicit Cues
authors: Sirnam Swetha, Rohit Gupta, Parth Parag Kulkarni, David G Shatwell, Jeffrey A Chan Santiago

task_types: video question answering, temporal grounding, spatial grounding
description: VideoZeroBench is a hierarchical benchmark for challenging long-video question answering that rigorously verifies spatio-temporal evidence. It features 500 manually annotated questions across 13 domains, paired with temporal intervals and spatial bounding boxes as evidence, using a five-level evaluation protocol.
paper_title: VideoZeroBench: Probing the Limits of Video MLLMs with Spatio-Temporal Evidence Verification
authors: Jiahao Meng, Tan Yue, Qi Xu, Haochen Wang, Zhongwei Ren

description: EgoExoAsk comprises 27,666 QA pairs generated from Ego-Exo4D's expert commentary annotation, designed to evaluate video question generation models' ability to elicit unseen knowledge from human experts through question-to-answer retrieval simulation.
paper_title: Evaluating the Capability of Video Question Generation for Expert Knowledge Elicitation
authors: Huaying Zhang, Atsushi Hashimoto, Tosho Hirasawa

task_types: timestamp localization, video description, counter-intuitive reasoning, creativity scoring
description: FunQA is a challenging video QA dataset specifically designed to evaluate video reasoning based on counter-intuitive and surprising videos. It covers three types of surprising videos: HumorQA, CreativeQA, and MagicQA, with tasks assessing counter-intuitive timestamp localization, detailed video description, reasoning around counter-intuitiveness, and higher-level tasks like creativity scoring.
paper_title: FunQA: Towards Surprising Video Comprehension
authors: Binzhu Xie, Sicheng Zhang, Zitang Zhou, Bo Li, Yuanhan Zhang

task_types: person recognition, behavior analysis, summarization, cross-video reasoning
description: CrossVideoQA is a comprehensive benchmark dataset specifically designed for person-centric cross-video analysis. Unlike traditional single-video QA, this benchmark focuses on establishing meaningful connections across multiple video streams through person-anchored reasoning, enabling sophisticated reasoning across distributed visual information in cross-video scenarios.
paper_title: VideoForest: Person-Anchored Hierarchical Reasoning for Cross-Video Question Answering
authors: Yiran Meng, Junhong Ye, Wei Zhou, Guanghui Yue, Xudong Mao

description: VideoEspresso features video QA pairs with essential spatial details and temporal coherence, plus multimodal Chain-of-Thought annotations for intermediate reasoning steps. It includes 14 evaluation tasks and emphasizes fine-grained video reasoning via core frame selection.
paper_title: VideoEspresso: A Large-Scale Chain-of-Thought Dataset for Fine-Grained Video Reasoning via Core Frame Selection
authors: Songhao Han, Wei Huang, Hairong Shi, Le Zhuo, Xiu Su

task_types: video reasoning, spatio-temporal grounding, video question answering
description: A comprehensive evaluation suite for challenging video understanding tasks focusing on the ability to reason about "what", "where", "when", and "how" of a video. It addresses critical data gaps in detailed video understanding and provides spatio-temporally grounded video captions.
paper_title: PerceptionLM: Open-Access Data and Models for Detailed Visual Understanding
authors: Jang Hyun Cho, Andrea Madotto, Effrosyni Mavroudi, Triantafyllos Afouras, Tushar Nagarajan

task_types: theory of mind reasoning, belief reasoning, desire reasoning, intention reasoning
description: The first benchmark to explore cognitive reasoning capabilities of VideoQA models in the context of Theory of Mind. It offers tasks at two difficulty levels, assessing Belief, Desire and Intention (BDI) reasoning in both simple and complex scenarios, inspired by cognitive development of children's ToM.
paper_title: BDIQA: A New Dataset for Video Question Answering to Explore Cognitive Reasoning through Theory of Mind
authors: Yuanyuan Mao, Xin Lin, Qin Ni, Liang He

task_types: real-time video QA, temporal grounding, live-streaming comprehension
description: A video-text training dataset designed to adapt VideoLLMs to video-text duet interaction format, characterized by continuous video playback with text messages inserted at any position. It introduces the Multi-Answer Grounded Video Question Answering (MAGQA) task to benchmark real-time response ability.
paper_title: VideoLLM Knows When to Speak: Enhancing Time-Sensitive Video Comprehension with Video-Text Duet Interaction Format
authors: Yueqian Wang, Xiaojun Meng, Yuxuan Wang, Jianxin Liang, Jiansheng Wei

task_types: timestamped video captioning, video summarization, video question answering, temporal grounding, video reasoning
description: A benchmark for structured comprehension of real-world user-generated short videos from platforms like WeChat Channel and TikTok. It evaluates multi-granularity timestamped video captioning, summarization, open-ended QA, temporal grounding, and video reasoning with complex visual elements and high information density.
paper_title: ARC-Hunyuan-Video-7B: Structured Video Comprehension of Real-World Shorts
authors: Yuying Ge, Yixiao Ge, Chen Li, Teng Wang, Junfu Pu

description: The first event-inclusive, human-annotated multimodal benchmark that covers diverse real-world scenarios. EventBench uniquely incorporates event camera data and provides a comprehensive evaluation framework for event-guided video understanding.
paper_title: EventSTU: Event-Guided Efficient Spatio-Temporal Understanding for Video Large Language Models
authors: Wenhao Xu, Xin Dong, Yue Li, Haoyuan Shi, Zhiwei Xiong

description: Personal video instance retrieval benchmark for evaluating VLMs' ability to find specific named object instances (e.g., 'My dog Biscuit') in videos, addressing personalized search beyond category-level queries.
paper_title: Meta-Personalizing Vision-Language Models to Find Named Instances in Video
authors: Chun-Hsiao Yeh, Bryan Russell, Josef Sivic, Fabian Caba Heilbron, Simon Jenni

benchmark_name: Biryani Preparation Video Dataset and QA Benchmark
task_types: question answering, procedural understanding, video comparison
description: First large-scale curated dataset of biryani preparation videos comprising 120 high-quality YouTube recordings across 12 distinct regional styles. Introduces a comprehensive question-answer benchmark spanning multiple reasoning levels to evaluate procedural understanding in VLMs for culturally grounded cooking videos.
paper_title: How Does India Cook Biryani?
authors: Shubham Goel, Farzana S, C V Rishi, Aditya Arun, C V Jawahar

task_types: reasoning-aware video editing, video-to-video generation, physical plausibility reasoning
description: Introduces RVE-Bench for Reason-Informed Video Editing with two subsets: Reasoning-Aware Video Editing (RAVE) and In-Context Video-to-Video Generation (ICVG). Also proposes ReViSE self-reflective learning framework for reasoning-aware video editing.
paper_title: Towards Reason-Informed Video Editing in Unified Models with Self-Reflective Learning
authors: Xinyu Liu, Hangjie Yuan, Yujie Wei, Jiazheng Xing, Yujin Han

task_types: video anomaly detection, abnormal event localization, video understanding
description: A cross-domain evaluation benchmark based on XD-Violence dataset specifically designed for video anomaly detection and understanding. It includes an instruction-following dataset for fine-tuning video-anomaly-aware MLLMs to test models' ability to summarize and localize abnormal events.
paper_title: Aligning Effective Tokens with Video Anomaly in Large Language Models
authors: Yingxian Chen, Jiahui Liu, Ruidi Fan, Yanwei Li, Chirui Chang

task_types: video anomaly prediction, video anomaly detection, anomaly analysis
description: First large-scale benchmark designed for VLM-based online video anomaly prediction, detection, and analysis (VAPDA). Introduces a novel event-level anomaly prediction task for proactive forecasting in real-time streaming video scenarios.
paper_title: AssistPDA: An Online Video Surveillance Assistant for Video Anomaly Prediction, Detection, and Analysis
authors: Zhiwei Yang, Chen Gao, Jing Liu, Peng Wu, Guansong Pang

task_types: anomaly recognition, temporal grounding, anomaly detection, anomaly anticipation
description: CueBench is the first benchmark devoted to context-aware video anomalies within a unified evaluation framework. It establishes an event-centric hierarchical taxonomy with 14 conditional and 18 absolute anomaly events across 174 scenes and 198 attributes, testing recognition, temporal grounding, detection, and anticipation.
paper_title: CueBench: Advancing Unified Understanding of Context-Aware Video Anomalies in Real-World
authors: Yating Yu, Congqi Cao, Zhaoying Wang, Weihua Meng, Jie Li

task_types: video object segmentation, object captioning, referring expression segmentation
description: SAV-Caption is created by annotating an existing large-scale segmentation dataset (SAV) with pseudo object captions using VLMs. It enables training and evaluation for the novel task of video object captioning combined with segmentation from various prompts (text, box, mask).
paper_title: VoCap: Video Object Captioning and Segmentation from Any Prompt
authors: Jasper Uijlings, Xingyi Zhou, Xiuye Gu, Arsha Nagrani, Anurag Arnab

task_types: abductive reasoning, defeasible reasoning, unexpected event understanding
description: Evaluates VLMs' ability to reason about unexpected events through abductive and defeasible tasks. Focuses on atypical events in videos requiring models to extend beyond pattern recognition.
paper_title: Black Swan: Abductive and Defeasible Video Reasoning in Unpredictable Events
authors: Aditya Chinchure, Sahithya Ravi, Raymond Ng, Vered Shwartz, Boyang Li

description: A benchmark for physics-grounded anomaly detection that evaluates VLMs' ability to understand kinematic constraints and detect irregular motions. It focuses on causal understanding of dynamics rather than appearance-centric correlations for identifying physical anomalies.
paper_title: Multi-turn Physics-informed Vision-language Model for Physics-grounded Anomaly Detection
authors: Yao Gu, Xiaohao Xu, Yingna Wu

task_types: visual entity tracking, temporal reasoning, object tracking
description: Synthetic diagnostic testbed featuring visually identical objects that require tracking exclusively through spatiotemporal continuity. Designed to expose VLMs' fundamental limitations in entity tracking without visual shortcuts.
paper_title: Can Vision-Language Models Solve the Shell Game?
authors: Tiedong Liu, Wee Sun Lee

task_types: turning point classification, turning point detection
description: A meticulously curated dataset focusing on detecting turning points in casual conversations with precise timestamps, descriptions, and visual-textual evidence. It provides multi-modal annotation for critical moments like emotional outbursts or decision changes.
paper_title: MTP: A Dataset for Multi-Modal Turning Points in Casual Conversations
authors: Gia-Bao Dinh Ho, Chang Wei Tan, Zahra Zamanzadeh Darban, Mahsa Salehi, Gholamreza Haffari

description: A dataset for evaluating VLMs' understanding of sequential tasks with hierarchical structure from basic single-step to complex multi-step tasks. Features videos with step-by-step descriptions across virtual home, Minecraft, and real-world environments.
paper_title: ViSTa Dataset: Do vision-language models understand sequential tasks?
authors: Evžen Wybitul, Evan Ryan Gunter, Mikhail Seleznyov, David Lindner

description: A dataset for text-driven saliency detection in 360-degree videos with 16,000 triplets of ERP frames, textual descriptions, and ground-truth saliency maps. Enables customized text-driven saliency detection in immersive video content.
paper_title: TSalV360: A Method and Dataset for Text-driven Saliency Detection in 360-Degrees Videos
authors: Ioannis Kontostathis, Evlampios Apostolidis, Vasileios Mezaris

description: A pedestrian-centric traffic video dataset with fine-grained spatial-temporal understanding featuring dual perspectives from vehicle ego and fixed overhead cameras. Includes comprehensive textual descriptions and unique 3D Gaze data for synchronized 2D/3D analysis.
paper_title: WTS: A Pedestrian-Centric Traffic Video Dataset for Fine-grained Spatial-Temporal Understanding
authors: Quan Kong, Yuki Kawana, Rajat Saini, Ashutosh Kumar, Jingjing Pan

description: Designed for evaluating domain generalization in video understanding across 11 distinct domains with three types of domain shifts. Tests model robustness to distribution changes while maintaining semantic similarity across domains.
paper_title: VUDG: A Dataset for Video Understanding Domain Generalization
authors: Ziyi Wang, Zhi Gao, Boxuan Yu, Zirui Dai, Yuxiang Song

description: Tests video models on recognizing letters drawn in the air by humans, requiring understanding of complex articulated motions and long-range temporal integration. Reveals significant gaps between human and model performance on motion understanding.
paper_title: AirLetters: An Open Video Dataset of Characters Drawn in the Air
authors: Rishit Dagli, Guillaume Berger, Joanna Materzynska, Ingo Bax, Roland Memisevic

description: Proposes hierarchical difficulty levels for video story understanding based on memory capacity, logical complexity, and DIKW pyramid. Addresses limitations in existing video Q&A datasets that lack story-level understanding and variance in difficulty.
paper_title: Constructing Hierarchical Q&A Datasets for Video Story Understanding
authors: Yu-Jung Heo, Kyoung-Woon On, Seongho Choi, Jaeseo Lim, Jinah Kim

task_types: action recognition, tactical analysis, decision evaluation
description: Features multi-level semantic annotation hierarchy for badminton video understanding with Foundational Actions, Tactical Semantics, and Decision Evaluation. Tests fine-grained spatio-temporal reasoning and tactical comprehension in high-speed sports.
paper_title: FineBadminton: A Multi-Level Dataset for Fine-Grained Badminton Video Understanding
authors: Xusheng He, Wei Liu, Shanshan Ma, Qian Liu, Chenghao Ma

description: Provides intent-grounded video understanding with three hierarchical description layers (factual, abstract, intent) progressing from surface to depth. Tests deep-cognitive video comprehension through intent expressions requiring contextual integration.
paper_title: VideoMind: An Omni-Modal Video Dataset with Intent Grounding for Deep-Cognitive Video Understanding
authors: Baoyao Yang, Wanyun Li, Dixin Chen, Junxiang Chen, Wenbin Yao

task_types: video captioning, sentence localization, question answering
description: Large-scale pre-training dataset with automatically extracted video-sentence pairs from web pages. Designed for generic video understanding and vision-language pre-training with applications to video captioning and question answering.
paper_title: Auto-captions on GIF: A Large-scale Video-sentence Dataset for Vision-language Pre-training
authors: Yingwei Pan, Yehao Li, Jianjie Luo, Jun Xu, Ting Yao

description: ProMQA is the first dataset to evaluate multimodal procedural activity understanding through question-answering on cooking videos paired with instruction manuals. It uses a cost-effective human-LLM collaborative annotation approach and focuses on application-oriented evaluation scenarios rather than traditional classification tasks.
paper_title: ProMQA: Question Answering Dataset for Multimodal Procedural Activity Understanding
authors: Kimihiro Hasegawa, Wiradee Imrattanatrai, Zhi-Qi Cheng, Masaki Asada, Susan Holm

task_types: question answering, procedural activity understanding, assembly tasks
description: ProMQA-Assembly extends procedural QA evaluation to assembly tasks, providing multimodal QA pairs on human assembly activities with instruction manuals. It uniquely integrates fine-grained action labels and instruction task graphs to diversify question types and facilitate systematic evaluation of assembly assistants.
paper_title: ProMQA-Assembly: Multimodal Procedural QA Dataset on Assembly
authors: Kimihiro Hasegawa, Wiradee Imrattanatrai, Masaki Asada, Susan Holm, Yuran Wang

task_types: video summarization, text summarization, multimodal summarization, thumbnail generation
description: MMSum provides human-validated summaries for both video and textual content with comprehensive categorization spanning 17 principal categories and 170 subcategories. It addresses limitations of existing MSMO datasets through superior human instruction, proper categorization, and benchmark tests for video, text, and multimodal summarization.
paper_title: MMSum: A Dataset for Multimodal Summarization and Thumbnail Generation of Videos
authors: Jielin Qiu, Jiacheng Zhu, William Han, Aditesh Kumar, Karthik Mittal

task_types: scene segmentation, topic segmentation, dialogue generation
description: VSTAR introduces video-grounded dialogue understanding that specifically addresses scene and topic transitions, which existing benchmarks neglect. Built from 395 TV series, it provides benchmarks for scene segmentation, topic segmentation, and dialogue generation that incorporate intrinsic multimodal dialogue attributes.
paper_title: VSTAR: A Video-grounded Dialogue Dataset for Situated Semantic Understanding with Scene and Topic Transitions
authors: Yuxuan Wang, Zilong Zheng, Xueliang Zhao, Jinpeng Li, Yueqian Wang

task_types: drone detection, drone tracking, trajectory forecasting
description: FRED provides the first multimodal dataset specifically designed for drone detection, tracking, and trajectory forecasting, combining RGB video and event streams. It uniquely features 7+ hours of densely annotated drone trajectories with challenging scenarios like rain and adverse lighting conditions, addressing limitations of traditional RGB cameras for fast-moving objects.
paper_title: FRED: The Florence RGB-Event Drone Dataset
authors: Gabriele Magrini, Niccolò Marini, Federico Becattini, Lorenzo Berlincioni, Niccolò Biondi

task_types: video-text understanding, anomaly detection, multimodal tasks
description: First multimodal surveillance video dataset with fine-grained event content and timing annotations on real-world surveillance footage. Enables surveillance video-and-language understanding tasks which go beyond traditional anomaly detection to semantic understanding.
paper_title: Towards Surveillance Video-and-Language Understanding: New Dataset, Baselines, and Challenges
authors: Tongtong Yuan, Xuange Zhang, Kun Liu, Bo Liu, Chen Chen

description: Video-language story dataset containing naturalistic storytelling videos of movie and TV series summaries totaling 869 hours, designed to capture multimodal story events with abundant mental-state descriptions and cross-domain semantic gaps that challenge existing models in story understanding.
paper_title: Synopses of Movie Narratives: a Video-Language Dataset for Story Understanding
authors: Yidan Sun, Qin Chao, Yangfeng Ji, Boyang Li

description: VISTA is the first video-to-text summarization dataset specifically designed for scientific domains, containing recorded AI conference presentations paired with their paper abstracts. It enables evaluation of models' ability to transform scientific presentation videos into structured textual summaries.
paper_title: What Is That Talk About? A Video-to-Text Summarization Dataset for Scientific Presentations
authors: Dongqi Liu, Chenxi Whitehouse, Xi Yu, Louis Mahon, Rohit Saxena

task_types: cross-modal video retrieval, text reading comprehension
description: TextVR introduces a large-scale cross-modal video retrieval dataset with text reading comprehension, containing 42.2k sentence queries for 10.5k videos across 8 scenario domains. It requires unified cross-modal models to recognize and comprehend texts, relate them to visual context, and determine vital text semantic information for video retrieval tasks.
paper_title: A Large Cross-Modal Video Retrieval Dataset with Reading Comprehension
authors: Weijia Wu, Yuzhong Zhao, Zhuang Li, Jiahong Li, Hong Zhou

description: EmoVid introduces the first multimodal, emotion-annotated video dataset specifically designed for creative media including cartoon animations, movie clips, and animated stickers. Each video is annotated with emotion labels, visual attributes, and text captions, establishing a new benchmark for affective video computing and emotion-conditioned video generation.
paper_title: EmoVid: A Multimodal Emotion Video Dataset for Emotion-Centric Video Understanding and Generation
authors: Zongyang Qiu, Bingyuan Wang, Xingbei Chen, Yingqing He, Zeyu Wang

task_types: video chain-of-thought reasoning, video question answering
description: VideoCoT introduces a video Chain-of-Thought dataset with an active annotation tool combining machine and human expertise under active learning paradigm. It contributes three datasets (VideoCoT, TopicQA, TopicCoT) specifically for video reasoning, addressing the lack of CoT datasets in video domains and improving MLLM reasoning capabilities on videos.
paper_title: VideoCoT: A Video Chain-of-Thought Dataset with Active Annotation Tool
authors: Yan Wang, Yawen Zeng, Jingsheng Zheng, Xiaofen Xing, Jin Xu

task_types: video question answering, video question generation, spatio-temporal reasoning
description: UDVideoQA introduces a traffic video question answering dataset capturing unscripted real-world urban dynamics from 16 hours of intersection footage. It features 28K question-answer pairs with hierarchical reasoning levels from basic understanding to counterfactual inference, plus complementary video question generation benchmarks, addressing multi-object spatio-temporal reasoning in urban environments.
paper_title: UDVideoQA: A Traffic Video Question Answering Dataset for Multi-Object Spatio-Temporal Reasoning in Urban Dynamics
authors: Joseph Raj Vishal, Nagasiri Poluri, Katha Naik, Rutuja Patil, Kashyap Hegde Kota

description: MVQA-68K introduces a novel multi-dimensional video quality assessment dataset with over 68,000 videos annotated across seven quality dimensions including aesthetics, camera movement, dynamic degree, texture detail, composition, visual quality, and factual consistency. It provides detailed chain-of-thought reasoning annotations to facilitate interpretability.
paper_title: MVQA-68K: A Multi-dimensional and Causally-annotated Dataset with Quality Interpretability for Video Assessment
authors: Yanyun Pu, Kehan Li, Zeyi Huang, Zhijie Zhong, Kaixiang Yang

description: A systematic benchmark designed to evaluate VLMs for multimodal graph learning where multimodal entities are connected through explicit relational graphs. Tests three paradigms for integrating VLMs with graph reasoning across diverse domains.
paper_title: GraphVLM: Benchmarking Vision Language Models for Multimodal Graph Learning
authors: Jiajin Liu, Dongzhe Fan, Chuanhao Ji, Daochen Zha, Qiaoyu Tan

description: An abstract pattern recognition benchmark that pairs 318 human-written descriptions of visual patterns from 40 writing systems with three visual presentation styles. Evaluates abstract pattern recognition requiring spatial reference and compositionality understanding.
paper_title: GlyphPattern: An Abstract Pattern Recognition Benchmark for Vision-Language Models
authors: Zixuan Wu, Yoolim Kim, Carolyn Jane Anderson

description: The first open-source benchmark for comparing VLM-grounded symbolic approaches with direct VLM planning methods. Features increasingly challenging tasks in Blocksworld and simulated household robotics environments.
paper_title: ViPlan: A Benchmark for Visual Planning with Symbolic Predicates and Vision-Language Models
authors: Matteo Merler, Nicola Dainese, Minttu Alakuijala, Giovanni Bonetta, Pietro Ferrazzi

description: A novel benchmark framework designed to systematically evaluate VLMs' understanding and reasoning about fundamental physical principles through challenging simulated environments. Tests complex physical reasoning and predictive control capabilities.
paper_title: DeepPHY: Benchmarking Agentic VLMs on Physical Reasoning
authors: Xinrun Xu, Pi Bu, Ye Wang, Börje F. Karlsson, Ziming Wang

description: This is the first benchmark for evaluating VLMs on foundational Visual Network Analysis tasks, testing their ability to perform graph theoretic tasks like identifying nodes of maximal degree, signed triad balance, and component counting on rendered graphs. It reveals significant limitations in VLMs' graph analysis capabilities.
paper_title: Multimodal LLMs Struggle with Basic Visual Network Analysis: a VNA Benchmark
authors: Evan M. Williams, Kathleen M. Carley

task_types: visual question answering, tool usage, web exploration
description: OWLViz presents a challenging benchmark for Open World Visual question answering that requires integrating visual understanding, web exploration, and specialized tool usage. It reveals significant performance gaps between human capabilities (69.2%) and state-of-the-art VLMs (26.6%).
paper_title: OWLViz: An Open-World Benchmark for Visual Question Answering
authors: Thuy Nguyen, Dang Nguyen, Hoang Nguyen, Thuan Luong, Long Hoang Dang

description: This benchmark evaluates VLMs' Theory of Mind capabilities using an open-ended question framework to assess their ability to infer human intentions, beliefs, and mental states from visual scenarios. It uniquely focuses on complex social scenarios like bullying or cheating.
paper_title: How Well Can Vison-Language Models Understand Humans' Intention? An Open-ended Theory of Mind Question Evaluation Benchmark
authors: Ximing Wen, Mallika Mainali, Anik Sen

description: VLM-RobustBench is a comprehensive robustness benchmark spanning 49 augmentation types across noise, blur, weather, digital, and geometric perturbations evaluated under graded severities and binary transforms. It reveals that VLMs are semantically strong but spatially fragile, with spatial perturbations often causing more damage than visually severe corruptions.
paper_title: VLM-RobustBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Robustness of Vision-Language Models
authors: Rohit Saxena, Alessandro Suglia, Pasquale Minervini

description: AgroCoT is a VQA dataset that integrates Chain-of-Thought reasoning specifically designed to evaluate the reasoning capabilities of VLMs in agricultural contexts. It focuses on testing logical reasoning and problem-solving skills needed for complex agricultural applications like precision farming, crop monitoring, and pest detection, particularly in zero-shot scenarios.
paper_title: AgroCoT: A Chain-of-Thought Benchmark for Evaluating Reasoning in Vision-Language Models for Agriculture
authors: Yibin Wen, Qingmei Li, Zi Ye, Jiarui Zhang, Zurong Mai

description: COREVQA (Crowd Observations and Reasoning Entailment) is a benchmark of synthetically generated true/false statement pairs with images from CrowdHuman dataset, specifically designed to test visual entailment reasoning on challenging crowded scenes. It addresses the gap in evaluating VLMs' ability to accept or refute hypotheses based on visual evidence in complex crowded environments.
paper_title: COREVQA: A Crowd Observation and Reasoning Entailment Visual Question Answering Benchmark
authors: Ishant Chintapatla, Kazuma Choji, Naaisha Agarwal, Andrew Lin, Hannah You

task_types: atypicality classification, statement retrieval, object recognition, persuasive reasoning
description: This work introduces three novel tasks (Multi-label Atypicality Classification, Atypicality Statement Retrieval, and Atypical Object Recognition) to benchmark VLMs' understanding of atypicality in persuasive images, particularly advertisements. It tests VLMs' ability to comprehend rhetorical and persuasive visual media that employ atypical imagery using surprising object juxtapositions to convey shared properties.
paper_title: Benchmarking VLMs' Reasoning About Persuasive Atypical Images
authors: Sina Malakouti, Aysan Aghazadeh, Ashmit Khandelwal, Adriana Kovashka

task_types: error detection, error classification, reasoning evaluation
description: MMErroR is a multi-modal benchmark where each sample embeds a single coherent reasoning error across 24 subdomains and six top-level domains. Unlike existing benchmarks focusing on answer correctness, it uniquely targets process-level, error-centric evaluation requiring models to detect incorrect reasoning and classify error types.
paper_title: MMErroR: A Benchmark for Erroneous Reasoning in Vision-Language Models
authors: Yang Shi, Yifeng Xie, Minzhe Guo, Liangsi Lu, Mingxuan Huang

task_types: mutual exclusivity evaluation, spatial reasoning, word learning
description: MEBench evaluates mutual exclusivity bias, a cognitive phenomenon observed in children during word learning, incorporating spatial reasoning to create challenging evaluation settings. It uniquely tests whether VLMs exhibit human-like cognitive biases in word-object associations and spatial understanding.
paper_title: MEBench: A Novel Benchmark for Understanding Mutual Exclusivity Bias in Vision-Language Models
authors: Anh Thai, Stefan Stojanov, Zixuan Huang, Bikram Boote, James M. Rehg

task_types: object state identification, image retrieval, state change identification
description: STATUS Bench is the first benchmark for rigorously evaluating object state understanding in VLMs through three simultaneous tasks: object state identification, image retrieval, and state change identification. It uniquely focuses on subtle variations in object states (positional and functional) with a rigorous consistency evaluation scheme.
paper_title: STATUS Bench: A Rigorous Benchmark for Evaluating Object State Understanding in Vision-Language Models
authors: Mahiro Ukai, Shuhei Kurita, Nakamasa Inoue

task_types: visual puzzles, complex visual reasoning, multi-step reasoning
description: PARROT-360V features challenging visual puzzles designed to test VLMs on complex visual reasoning tasks that require deep reasoning and integration of multiple data modalities. It uniquely focuses on complex, multi-step reasoning tasks akin to human problem-solving, revealing significant performance gaps in state-of-the-art models.
paper_title: Beyond Visual Understanding: Introducing PARROT-360V for Vision Language Model Benchmarking
authors: Harsha Vardhan Khurdula, Basem Rizk, Indus Khaitan, Janit Anjaria, Aviral Srivastava

task_types: reasoning segmentation, weather robustness evaluation, visual reasoning
description: WeatherReasonSeg evaluates VLM performance in reasoning-based segmentation under adverse weather conditions across five reasoning dimensions. It uniquely combines controllable synthetic weather with real-world adverse-weather data and mask-guided LLM prompting to assess robustness of reasoning segmentation capabilities.
paper_title: WeatherReasonSeg: A Benchmark for Weather-Aware Reasoning Segmentation in Visual Language Models
authors: Wanjun Du, Zifeng Yuan, Tingting Chen, Fucai Ke, Beibei Lin

description: CompareBench evaluates visual comparison reasoning across four tasks: quantity, temporal, geometric, and spatial comparisons. It provides controlled and diagnostic evaluation to reveal systematic blind spots in VLMs for comparison tasks that are trivial for humans.
paper_title: CompareBench: A Benchmark for Visual Comparison Reasoning in Vision-Language Models
authors: Jie Cai, Kangning Yang, Lan Fu, Jiaming Ding, Jinlong Li

description: MeasureBench evaluates visual measurement reading from various types of measurement instruments using both real-world and synthesized images. It includes an extensible pipeline for procedural gauge generation and highlights fundamental limitations in fine-grained spatial grounding.
paper_title: Do Vision-Language Models Measure Up? Benchmarking Visual Measurement Reading with MeasureBench
authors: Fenfen Lin, Yesheng Liu, Haiyu Xu, Chen Yue, Zheqi He

description: VRIQ assesses visual reasoning ability through abstract puzzle-style and natural-image reasoning tasks with diagnostic probes targeting perception and reasoning. It reveals that most failures arise from perception limitations rather than reasoning deficits in current VLMs.
paper_title: VRIQ: Benchmarking and Analyzing Visual-Reasoning IQ of VLMs
authors: Tina Khezresmaeilzadeh, Jike Zhong, Konstantinos Psounis

description: ColorBench comprehensively assesses VLMs' color understanding including perception, reasoning, and robustness across diverse test scenarios grounded in real applications. It evaluates how models perceive colors, infer meanings from color cues, and maintain consistency under color transformations.
paper_title: ColorBench: Can VLMs See and Understand the Colorful World? A Comprehensive Benchmark for Color Perception, Reasoning, and Robustness
authors: Yijun Liang, Ming Li, Chenrui Fan, Ziyue Li, Dang Nguyen

description: YearGuessr exposes popularity bias in VLMs through a large-scale ordinal regression benchmark for construction year prediction. It introduces popularity-aware interval accuracy metrics and reveals that VLMs rely heavily on memorization of famous buildings rather than generalizable understanding.
paper_title: Beyond Memorization: A Multi-Modal Ordinal Regression Benchmark to Expose Popularity Bias in Vision-Language Models
authors: Li-Zhong Szu-Tu, Ting-Lin Wu, Chia-Jui Chang, He Syu, Yu-Lun Liu

benchmark_name: VISLA (Variance and Invariance to Semantic and Lexical Alterations)
task_types: semantic equivalence, image-to-text retrieval, text-to-text retrieval
description: VISLA presents a 3-way semantic equivalence task with triplets of sentences and images to evaluate both VLMs and unimodal language models. It uniquely unifies image-to-text and text-to-text retrieval tasks to assess semantic and lexical understanding variations.
paper_title: VISLA Benchmark: Evaluating Embedding Sensitivity to Semantic and Lexical Alterations
authors: Sri Harsha Dumpala, Aman Jaiswal, Chandramouli Sastry, Evangelos Milios, Sageev Oore

description: Creates synthetic datasets requiring minimal world knowledge to isolate pure visual reasoning capabilities from confounding factors. Tests VLMs through systematic analysis across varying reasoning steps, comparing visual embeddings versus textual scene descriptions and evaluating chain-of-thought prompting effectiveness.
paper_title: Zero-Shot Visual Reasoning by Vision-Language Models: Benchmarking and Analysis
authors: Aishik Nagar, Shantanu Jaiswal, Cheston Tan

task_types: visual social inference, object localization, behavioral cue analysis
description: Novel benchmark for visual social inference using sports domain to evaluate VLMs' ability to localize removed objects from behavioral cues. Tests understanding of gaze direction, pose, and orientation while providing scalable pipeline for generating additional test items across soccer, basketball, and volleyball.
paper_title: Spot The Ball: A Benchmark for Visual Social Inference
authors: Neha Balamurugan, Sarah Wu, Adam Chun, Gabe Gaw, Cristobal Eyzaguirre

task_types: cultural reasoning, visual question answering, segmentation, multiple-choice
description: Novel two-stage cultural reasoning benchmark requiring VLMs to select correct visual options then segment cultural artifacts as evidence. Features 1,065 images capturing 138 cultural artifacts from seven Southeast Asia countries with systematic organization of visual options by country origin.
paper_title: Seeing Culture: A Benchmark for Visual Reasoning and Grounding
authors: Burak Satar, Zhixin Ma, Patrick A. Irawan, Wilfried A. Mulyawan, Jing Jiang

task_types: object counting, attention analysis, cross-modal binding
description: Systematic synthetic benchmark characterizing VLM counting performance across controlled perturbations including object properties, background variations, and prompt specificity. Provides diagnostic framework for analyzing enumeration behavior and cross-modal binding failure modes with attention analysis.
paper_title: Can Vision-Language Models Count? A Synthetic Benchmark and Analysis of Attention-Based Interventions
authors: Saurav Sengupta, Nazanin Moradinasab, Jiebei Liu, Donald E. Brown

task_types: visual question answering, figure-ground segregation, chromatic perception, spatial reasoning
description: Large-scale benchmark based on Ishihara-style chromatic camouflaged images extending classic dot plates with multiple geometries and chromatic variations. Covers nine VQA tasks including recognition, counting, comparison, and spatial reasoning under figure-ground segregation challenges.
paper_title: ChromouVQA: Benchmarking Vision-Language Models under Chromatic Camouflaged Images
authors: Yunfei Zhang, Yizhuo He, Yuanxun Shao, Zhengtao Yao, Haoyan Xu

task_types: multi-step visual reasoning, chain-of-questions, planning and following
description: V-REX is a benchmark for challenging visual reasoning tasks requiring native multi-step exploration through Chain-of-Questions (CoQ). It disentangles VLMs' planning and following capabilities while covering rich application scenarios across diverse domains with reliable quantitative analysis of intermediate steps.
paper_title: V-REX: Benchmarking Exploratory Visual Reasoning via Chain-of-Questions
authors: Chenrui Fan, Yijun Liang, Shweta Bhardwaj, Kwesi Cobbina, Ming Li

description: A diagnostic benchmark for ordinal number understanding with 39,000 QA pairs. Tests N-th object identification with controlled difficulty along three axes: ordinal magnitude (up to 300), arrangement complexity, and object count.
paper_title: OrdinalBench: A Benchmark Dataset for Diagnosing Generalization Limits in Ordinal Number Understanding of Vision-Language Models
authors: Yusuke Tozaki, Hisashi Miyamori

description: A multilingual benchmark for visual word puzzles with 1,343 puzzles requiring discovery of implicit visual cues and mapping to non-literal concepts. Spans English, Persian, Arabic, and cross-lingual puzzles with conceptually dense scenes.
paper_title: Eye-Q: A Multilingual Benchmark for Visual Word Puzzle Solving and Image-to-Phrase Reasoning
authors: Ali Najar, Alireza Mirrokni, Arshia Izadyari, Sadegh Mohammadian, Amir Homayoon Sharifizade

description: A benchmark that systematically varies object foregrounds and backgrounds to disentangle effects of viewpoint variation, scale shifts, and object confusions from background correlations. Designed to work with the CCI interpretability method for comprehensive CLIP evaluation.
paper_title: Concept Regions Matter: Benchmarking CLIP with a New Cluster-Importance Approach
authors: Aishwarya Agarwal, Srikrishna Karanam, Vineet Gandhi

description: A novel benchmark for cross-modal failure mode profiling that systematically compares image-to-text and text-to-image tasks. Adapts MMVP benchmark questions into explicit and implicit prompts across nine visual reasoning categories.
paper_title: AMVICC: A Novel Benchmark for Cross-Modal Failure Mode Profiling for VLMs and IGMs
authors: Aahana Basappa, Pranay Goel, Anusri Karra, Anish Karra, Asa Gilmore

task_types: object detection, intent prediction, risk assessment, action suggestion
description: DRAMA-X provides fine-grained evaluation of intent prediction and risk reasoning for vulnerable road users in driving scenarios. It features 5,686 accident-prone frames with nine-class directional intent taxonomy, binary risk scores, and expert-generated action suggestions, enabling structured evaluation of safety-critical autonomous driving decisions.
paper_title: DRAMA-X: A Fine-grained Intent Prediction and Risk Reasoning Benchmark For Driving
authors: Mihir Godbole, Xiangbo Gao, Zhengzhong Tu

description: MVV (Mapillary Vistas Validation for Traffic Signs) provides fine-grained traffic sign recognition by decomposing composite signs into granular, semantically meaningful categories. It includes pixel-level instance masks with expert annotations, addressing the limitation of coarse-grained labels in autonomous driving datasets.
paper_title: Mapillary Vistas Validation for Fine-Grained Traffic Signs: A Benchmark Revealing Vision-Language Model Limitations
authors: Sparsh Garg, Abhishek Aich

description: PairTally evaluates fine-grained visual counting where each image contains two object categories requiring models to distinguish based on subtle differences in shape, size, color, or semantics. It includes both inter-category and intra-category settings for rigorous evaluation of selective counting capabilities.
paper_title: Can Current AI Models Count What We Mean, Not What They See? A Benchmark and Systematic Evaluation
authors: Gia Khanh Nguyen, Yifeng Huang, Minh Hoai

task_types: assembly instruction understanding, cross-modal alignment, visual reasoning
description: IKEA-Bench evaluates cross-depiction assembly instruction alignment with 1,623 questions across 6 task types on 29 IKEA furniture products. It systematically assesses the depiction gap between assembly diagrams and video frames, revealing challenges in visual encoding for cross-modal understanding.
paper_title: Benchmarking and Mechanistic Analysis of Vision-Language Models for Cross-Depiction Assembly Instruction Alignment
authors: Zhuchenyang Liu, Yao Zhang, Yu Xiao

task_types: visual question answering, driving perception, prediction, planning
description: AutoDrive-QA converts open-ended driving QA datasets into structured multiple-choice questions with distractors grounded in five realistic error categories. It enables reproducible evaluation of VLMs across perception, prediction, and planning tasks in complex urban driving scenes, addressing ambiguity in existing evaluation methods.
paper_title: AutoDrive-QA: A Multiple-Choice Benchmark for Vision-Language Evaluation in Urban Autonomous Driving
authors: Boshra Khalili, Andrew W. Smyth

description: A benchmark focusing on photography-related visual reasoning tasks that require MLLMs to identify numerical camera settings from visual information. It uniquely tests understanding of underlying physics (illumination, blur) and their interplay with camera parameters, representing a challenging capability for practical photography applications.
paper_title: CameraBench: Benchmarking Visual Reasoning in MLLMs via Photography
authors: I-Sheng Fang, Jun-Cheng Chen

description: A comprehensive fine-grained evaluation benchmark comprising 1.01 million questions and 0.33 million images, systematically examining LVLMs from both human-oriented and machine-oriented perspectives. It uniquely focuses on semantic recognition and fine-grained feature representation capabilities fundamental to computer vision.
paper_title: Benchmarking Large Vision-Language Models on Fine-Grained Image Tasks: A Comprehensive Evaluation
authors: Hong-Tao Yu, Xiu-Shen Wei, Yuxin Peng, Serge Belongie

description: COLA is a text-to-image retrieval benchmark with about 1.2k composed queries of 168 objects and 197 attributes on 30K images. It tests compositional reasoning by requiring models to retrieve images with correct attribute-object configurations while avoiding distractors.
paper_title: COLA: A Benchmark for Compositional Text-to-image Retrieval
authors: Arijit Ray, Filip Radenovic, Abhimanyu Dubey, Bryan A. Plummer, Ranjay Krishna

task_types: graphic reasoning, spatial reasoning, relational reasoning, abstract reasoning
description: ReasonBench focuses on structured graphic reasoning tasks from real-world intelligence tests, covering location, attribute, quantity, and multi-element reasoning dimensions. It provides comprehensive evaluation of spatial, relational, and abstract reasoning capabilities in complex graphics.
paper_title: Oedipus and the Sphinx: Benchmarking and Improving Visual Language Models for Complex Graphic Reasoning
authors: Jianyi Zhang, Xu Ji, Ziyin Zhou, Yuchen Zhou, Shubo Shi

task_types: causal structure inference, intervention target prediction, counterfactual prediction
description: CausalVLBench introduces the first comprehensive causal reasoning benchmark for multi-modal in-context learning, encompassing causal structure inference, intervention target prediction, and counterfactual prediction. It evaluates LVLMs' ability to perform visual causal reasoning across three representation learning datasets.
paper_title: CausalVLBench: Benchmarking Visual Causal Reasoning in Large Vision-Language Models
authors: Aneesh Komanduri, Karuna Bhaila, Xintao Wu

description: QuantiPhy is the first benchmark designed to quantitatively measure VLMs' physical reasoning ability using over 3.3K video-text instances with numerical ground truth. It evaluates estimation of object size, velocity, and acceleration at given timestamps, moving beyond qualitative assessments to numerical accuracy.
paper_title: QuantiPhy: A Quantitative Benchmark Evaluating Physical Reasoning Abilities of Vision-Language Models
authors: Li Puyin, Tiange Xiang, Ella Mao, Shirley Wei, Xinye Chen

description: This benchmark evaluates infant-scale vision-language models on fine-grained attribute discrimination (color, size, texture) across 67 object classes using synthetic rendering. It decouples attribute values from object identity and reveals dissociations between visual and linguistic attribute information processing.
paper_title: Benchmarking Attribute Discrimination in Infant-Scale Vision-Language Models
authors: Patrick Batsell, Tsutsui Satoshi, Bihan Wen

description: VPBench is a larger visually prompted benchmark with 16 visual marker variants that addresses the fragility of existing visual prompting benchmarks. It tests how models handle visual prompts with coordinate markers and reveals that model performance is surprisingly sensitive to seemingly irrelevant visual marker details like color and size.
paper_title: Visually Prompted Benchmarks Are Surprisingly Fragile
authors: Haiwen Feng, Long Lian, Lisa Dunlap, Jiahao Shu, XuDong Wang

description: IllusionBench+ is a comprehensive visual illusion dataset featuring 1,051 images across classical cognitive illusions and real-world scene illusions. It includes trap illusions that resemble classical patterns but differ in reality, highlighting hallucination issues in VLMs.
paper_title: IllusionBench+: A Large-scale and Comprehensive Benchmark for Visual Illusion Understanding in Vision-Language Models
authors: Yiming Zhang, Zicheng Zhang, Xinyi Wei, Xiaohong Liu, Guangtao Zhai

task_types: image description, visual question answering, cognitive reasoning
description: This benchmark evaluates high-level cognitive abilities of LVLMs using images with rich semantics inspired by the Cookie Theft task from human cognitive tests. It defines eight reasoning capabilities and comprises image description and visual question answering tasks to test cognitive abilities.
paper_title: A Cognitive Evaluation Benchmark of Image Reasoning and Description for Large Vision-Language Models
authors: Xiujie Song, Mengyue Wu, Kenny Q. Zhu, Chunhao Zhang, Yanyi Chen

task_types: rebus puzzle solving, visual wordplay, multi-step reasoning
description: This benchmark presents 1,333 diverse English Rebus Puzzles across 18 categories requiring image recognition, cognitive skills, commonsense reasoning, and image-based wordplay. It challenges VLMs with creative picture-symbol-letter combinations representing words or phrases.
paper_title: $\left|\,\circlearrowright\,\boxed{\text{BUS}}\,\right|$: A Large and Diverse Multimodal Benchmark for evaluating the ability of Vision-Language Models to understand Rebus Puzzles
authors: Trishanu Das, Abhilash Nandy, Khush Bajaj, Deepiha S

task_types: code generation, symbolic visual representation, visual reasoning
description: VCode reframes multimodal understanding as code generation where models must produce SVG code that preserves symbolic meaning from images across three domains (MM-Vet, MMMU, CV-Bench). It introduces CodeVQA evaluation protocol where policy models answer questions over rendered SVGs to assess symbolic fidelity.
paper_title: VCode: a Multimodal Coding Benchmark with SVG as Symbolic Visual Representation
authors: Kevin Qinghong Lin, Yuhao Zheng, Hangyu Ran, Dantong Zhu, Dongxing Mao

task_types: multiple-choice questions, cognitive reasoning, pattern recognition
description: NTSEBench evaluates cognitive multi-modal reasoning and problem-solving skills using real-world NTSE examination questions. It features 2728 multiple-choice questions with 4,642 images across 26 question types, testing intelligence and critical thinking beyond rote learning.
paper_title: NTSEBENCH: Cognitive Reasoning Benchmark for Vision Language Models
authors: Pranshu Pandya, Vatsal Gupta, Agney S Talwarr, Tushar Kataria, Dan Roth

description: Provides a comprehensive benchmark with 22 tasks for examining fundamental graph understanding and reasoning capacities of LVLMs. Focuses on evaluating performance on relational and structurally complex visual graph information.
paper_title: Benchmarking and Improving Large Vision-Language Models for Fundamental Visual Graph Understanding and Reasoning
authors: Yingjie Zhu, Xuefeng Bai, Kehai Chen, Yang Xiang, Jun Yu

description: Novel Chain of Multi-modal Thought benchmark requiring both multi-modal input and multi-modal reasoning output, unlike traditional text-only output paradigms. Tests visual operations including creation, deletion, update, and selection to mimic human-like reasoning with integrated visual operations.
paper_title: CoMT: A Novel Benchmark for Chain of Multi-modal Thought on Large Vision-Language Models
authors: Zihui Cheng, Qiguang Chen, Jin Zhang, Hao Fei, Xiaocheng Feng

description: Large-scale benchmark for multi-turn, multi-image visual reasoning with minimal language guidance, testing progressive context-dependent reasoning across sequential interdependent tasks. Generated using multi-agent pipeline to ensure high visual diversity and controlled language bias across three domains.
paper_title: VisChainBench: A Benchmark for Multi-Turn, Multi-Image Visual Reasoning Beyond Language Priors
authors: Wenbo Lyu, Yingjun Du, Jinglin Zhao, Xianton Zhen, Ling Shao

description: Fine-grained Recognition Open World benchmark designed for detailed evaluation of LVLMs' fine-grained recognition capabilities using GPT-4o evaluation. Includes mosaic data combining multiple short-answer responses and open-world data from real-world questions.
paper_title: Towards Fine-Grained Recognition with Large Visual Language Models: Benchmark and Optimization Strategies
authors: Cong Pang, Hongtao Yu, Zixuan Chen, Lewei Lu, Xin Lou

description: First systematic benchmark for evaluating LVLMs' understanding of amodal completion reports using Basic Formal Ontology classification. Tests models' ability to interpret descriptions of perceptual experiences where objects are partially hidden or occluded.
paper_title: Bridging Perception and Language: A Systematic Benchmark for LVLMs' Understanding of Amodal Completion Reports
authors: Amane Watahiki, Tomoki Doi, Taiga Shinozaki, Satoshi Nishida, Takuya Niikawa

task_types: visual transformation reasoning, spatial reasoning, procedural reasoning, quantitative reasoning
description: First comprehensive benchmark for visual transformation reasoning in real-world human-object interaction scenarios. Evaluates three essential reasoning dimensions (spatial, procedural, quantitative) across 12 manipulation tasks using first-person manipulation videos.
paper_title: VisualTrans: A Benchmark for Real-World Visual Transformation Reasoning
authors: Yuheng Ji, Yipu Wang, Yuyang Liu, Xiaoshuai Hao, Yue Liu

description: First benchmark for spurious-correlation few-shot classification collected from real-world scenarios. Addresses understudied SC-FSC problem where classifiers rely on non-causal features correlated with labels in base classes but relationships don't hold during deployment.
paper_title: MetaCoCo: A New Few-Shot Classification Benchmark with Spurious Correlation
authors: Min Zhang, Haoxuan Li, Fei Wu, Kun Kuang

description: FewSTAB is an automatic benchmarking framework that assesses robustness of few-shot classifiers against spurious bias using attribute-based sample selection strategies. It creates evaluation tasks with biased attributes automatically using pre-trained vision-language models, eliminating manual dataset curation and enabling varied degrees of robustness evaluation.
paper_title: Benchmarking Spurious Bias in Few-Shot Image Classifiers
authors: Guangtao Zheng, Wenqian Ye, Aidong Zhang

description: Dysca is a dynamic and scalable benchmark using synthesized images to avoid data leakage, evaluating LVLMs across 51 image styles and 20 subtasks under 4 scenarios (Clean, Corruption, Print Attacking, Adversarial Attacking). It uses Stable Diffusion with rule-based generation for novel images, questions, and answers, providing scalable evaluation infrastructure.
paper_title: Dysca: A Dynamic and Scalable Benchmark for Evaluating Perception Ability of LVLMs
authors: Jie Zhang, Zhongqi Wang, Mengqi Lei, Zheng Yuan, Bei Yan

description: MVI-Bench is the first comprehensive benchmark for evaluating robustness to Misleading Visual Inputs in LVLMs, organized around three hierarchical levels: Visual Concept, Visual Attribute, and Visual Relationship. It includes 1,248 expertly annotated VQA instances across six categories with novel MVI-Sensitivity metric for granular robustness evaluation.
paper_title: MVI-Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Evaluating Robustness to Misleading Visual Inputs in LVLMs
authors: Huiyi Chen, Jiawei Peng, Dehai Min, Changchang Sun, Kaijie Chen

task_types: logical reasoning, multiple-choice QA, open-ended QA
description: LogicVista evaluates integrated logical reasoning capabilities of MLLMs in visual contexts across 5 logical reasoning tasks encompassing 9 different capabilities. Each question includes human-written reasoning annotations, enabling both open-ended and multiple-choice evaluation for systematic assessment of logical cognition abilities.
paper_title: LogicVista: Multimodal LLM Logical Reasoning Benchmark in Visual Contexts
authors: Yijia Xiao, Edward Sun, Tianyu Liu, Wei Wang

task_types: analogical reasoning, compositional reasoning, symbolic rule extraction
description: CARV (Compositional Analogical Reasoning in Vision) extends analogical reasoning from single pairs to multiple pairs, requiring MLLMs to extract symbolic rules and compose new transformations. It's the first diagnostic benchmark testing compositional analogical reasoning, a critical component of higher-order intelligence.
paper_title: CARV: A Diagnostic Benchmark for Compositional Analogical Reasoning in Multimodal LLMs
authors: Yongkang Du, Xiaohan Zou, Minhao Cheng, Lu Lin

task_types: logical reasoning, inductive reasoning, deductive reasoning, abductive reasoning
description: MME-Reasoning is a comprehensive benchmark evaluating logical reasoning ability of MLLMs, covering all three types of reasoning (inductive, deductive, and abductive). Each question effectively evaluates reasoning ability rather than perceptual skills or knowledge breadth.
paper_title: MME-Reasoning: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Logical Reasoning in MLLMs
authors: Jiakang Yuan, Tianshuo Peng, Yilei Jiang, Yiting Lu, Renrui Zhang

task_types: comparative reasoning, relative comparison, visual attribute comparison
description: MLLM-CompBench evaluates comparative reasoning capability of MLLMs through around 40K image pairs covering eight dimensions: visual attribute, existence, state, emotion, temporality, spatiality, quantity, and quality across diverse visual domains.
paper_title: MLLM-CompBench: A Comparative Reasoning Benchmark for Multimodal LLMs
authors: Jihyung Kil, Zheda Mai, Justin Lee, Zihe Wang, Kerrie Cheng

description: VisuLogic provides 1,000 human-verified visual reasoning problems across six categories including quantitative shifts, spatial relations, and attribute comparisons. It focuses on genuine vision-centric reasoning without allowing language-based shortcuts.
paper_title: VisuLogic: A Benchmark for Evaluating Visual Reasoning in Multi-modal Large Language Models
authors: Weiye Xu, Jiahao Wang, Weiyun Wang, Zhe Chen, Wengang Zhou

description: This benchmark evaluates MLLM visual perception across seven psychology-inspired subtasks in 2D and 3D with 1,758 images and 2,612 questions. It features controllable complexity and reveals that MLLMs achieve below 50% accuracy compared to 96.49% human performance.
paper_title: Do You See Me : A Multidimensional Benchmark for Evaluating Visual Perception in Multimodal LLMs
authors: Aditya Kanade, Tanuja Ganu

description: CODIS assesses the ability of models to use context provided in free-form text to enhance visual comprehension. It evaluates context-dependent visual understanding where images need to be interpreted within a broader textual context.
paper_title: CODIS: Benchmarking Context-Dependent Visual Comprehension for Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Fuwen Luo, Chi Chen, Zihao Wan, Zhaolu Kang, Qidong Yan

description: II-Bench (Image Implication understanding Benchmark) aims to evaluate MLLMs' higher-order perception of images, focusing on the model's ability to understand image implications. It addresses a gap in exploring higher-order perceptual capabilities beyond basic visual understanding, revealing significant performance gaps between MLLMs and humans.
paper_title: II-Bench: An Image Implication Understanding Benchmark for Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Ziqiang Liu, Feiteng Fang, Xi Feng, Xinrun Du, Chenhao Zhang

task_types: execution, perception reasoning, learning, memory, planning
description: KidGym is a comprehensive 2D grid-based benchmark inspired by Wechsler Intelligence Scales for assessing five essential capabilities of MLLMs: Execution, Perception Reasoning, Learning, Memory and Planning. It comprises 12 unique tasks designed to gauge MLLMs' adaptability and developmental potential, mirroring stages of children's cognitive growth with diverse scenarios and randomly generated layouts.
paper_title: Children's Intelligence Tests Pose Challenges for MLLMs? KidGym: A 2D Grid-Based Reasoning Benchmark for MLLMs
authors: Hengwei Ye, Yuanting Guan, Yuxuan Ge, Tianying Zhu, Zhenhan Guan

description: This benchmark evaluates eight state-of-the-art generative MLLMs across three datasets on their ability to detect and explain six types of figurative meaning in internet memes. It includes human evaluation of explanations generated by MLLMs, assessing whether the reasoning supports predicted labels and remains faithful to original meme content.
paper_title: I Came, I Saw, I Explained: Benchmarking Multimodal LLMs on Figurative Meaning in Memes
authors: Shijia Zhou, Saif M. Mohammad, Barbara Plank, Diego Frassinelli

description: Hyperphantasia is a synthetic benchmark designed to evaluate the mental visualization abilities of MLLMs through four carefully constructed puzzles. Each puzzle is procedurally generated at three difficulty levels, enabling controlled analysis of model performance in internally constructing visual patterns to support problem solving, addressing a gap in current benchmarks that assess only passive visual perception.
paper_title: Hyperphantasia: A Benchmark for Evaluating the Mental Visualization Capabilities of Multimodal LLMs
authors: Mohammad Shahab Sepehri, Berk Tinaz, Zalan Fabian, Mahdi Soltanolkotabi

description: AssoCiAm is a benchmark designed to evaluate associative ability while circumventing ambiguity through a hybrid computational method. It decomposes ambiguity into internal and external types and addresses the inherent ambiguity in association tasks that arises from divergent nature of associations, providing more accurate and reliable evaluations of creative thinking in MLLMs.
paper_title: AssoCiAm: A Benchmark for Evaluating Association Thinking while Circumventing Ambiguity
authors: Yifan Liu, Wenkuan Zhao, Shanshan Zhong, Jinghui Qin, Mingfu Liang

task_types: error detection, chain-of-thought reasoning, visual puzzle solving
description: PRISM-Bench introduces a diagnostic task where models must identify the first incorrect step in a chain-of-thought containing exactly one error when solving visual puzzles. This enables fine-grained assessment of logical consistency, error detection, and multi-step symbolic, geometric, and analogical reasoning.
paper_title: PRISM-Bench: A Benchmark of Puzzle-Based Visual Tasks with CoT Error Detection
authors: Yusu Qian, Cheng Wan, Chao Jia, Yinfei Yang, Qingyu Zhao

description: SketchJudge is a novel benchmark for evaluating MLLMs as graders of hand-drawn STEM diagrams. It encompasses 1,015 hand-drawn student responses across geometry, physics, charts, and flowcharts, requiring complex structural, semantic, and metacognitive reasoning for visual grading tasks.
paper_title: SketchJudge: A Diagnostic Benchmark for Grading Hand-drawn Diagrams with Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Yuhang Su, Mei Wang, Yaoyao Zhong, Guozhang Li, Shixing Li

task_types: inconsistency detection, semantic reasoning, layout analysis
description: Proposes the Multimodal Inconsistency Reasoning benchmark to assess MLLMs' ability to detect and reason about semantic mismatches in layout-rich content. Comprises 534 challenging samples with synthetically injected errors across five reasoning-heavy categories including factual contradiction and identity misattribution.
paper_title: Multimodal Inconsistency Reasoning (MMIR): A New Benchmark for Multimodal Reasoning Models
authors: Qianqi Yan, Yue Fan, Hongquan Li, Shan Jiang, Yang Zhao

task_types: tool recognition, tool understanding, tool creation
description: Presents the first benchmark dedicated to evaluating physical tool understanding in MLLMs. Structured as VQA dataset with over 1,000 image-text pairs across three difficulty levels: tool recognition, tool understanding, and tool creation, assessing capabilities in physical tool comprehension.
paper_title: PhysToolBench: Benchmarking Physical Tool Understanding for MLLMs
authors: Zixin Zhang, Kanghao Chen, Xingwang Lin, Lutao Jiang, Xu Zheng

task_types: abstract visual reasoning, cultural knowledge reasoning, linguistic expression understanding
description: Introduces a novel dataset for evaluating abstract visual reasoning in MLLMs using non-photographic, stylized images. Comprises 3,551 images spanning four categories (Public Figures, Popular Culture, Linguistic Expressions, Literary Works) requiring integration of symbolic, cultural, and linguistic knowledge.
paper_title: VisualQuest: A Benchmark for Abstract Visual Reasoning in MLLMs
authors: Kelaiti Xiao, Liang Yang, Dongyu Zhang, Paerhati Tulajiang, Hongfei Lin

description: VERIFY is explicitly designed to isolate and rigorously evaluate the visual reasoning capabilities of MLLMs by compelling models to reason primarily from visual information with minimal textual context. Each problem is accompanied by human-annotated reasoning paths, making it the first to provide in-depth evaluation of model decision-making processes.
paper_title: VERIFY: A Benchmark of Visual Explanation and Reasoning for Investigating Multimodal Reasoning Fidelity
authors: Jing Bi, Junjia Guo, Susan Liang, Guangyu Sun, Luchuan Song

description: MuCR is a multimodal causal reasoning benchmark that leverages synthetic siamese images and text pairs to challenge MLLMs' causal inference abilities. It includes tailored metrics from multiple perspectives: image-level match, phrase-level understanding, and sentence-level explanation to assess cross-modal causal reasoning.
paper_title: Multimodal Causal Reasoning Benchmark: Challenging Vision Large Language Models to Discern Causal Links Across Modalities
authors: Zhiyuan Li, Heng Wang, Dongnan Liu, Chaoyi Zhang, Ao Ma

task_types: visual illusion detection, anomaly detection, perceptual reasoning
description: VIA-Bench is designed to probe MLLM performance on visual illusions and anomalies with six core categories: color illusions, motion illusions, gestalt illusions, geometric and spatial illusions, general visual illusions, and visual anomalies. It constructs over 1K high-quality question-answer pairs requiring nuanced visual reasoning.
paper_title: Seeing Is Believing? A Benchmark for Multimodal Large Language Models on Visual Illusions and Anomalies
authors: Wenjin Hou, Wei Liu, Han Hu, Xiaoxiao Sun, Serena Yeung-Levy

description: First VQA benchmark specifically designed for occlusion perception evaluation. Uses a novel layered synthesis approach on SA-1B to create semantically coherent occlusion scenarios across five tailored tasks, addressing a critical gap in spatial understanding evaluation.
paper_title: Beyond the Visible: Benchmarking Occlusion Perception in Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Zhaochen Liu, Kaiwen Gao, Shuyi Liang, Bin Xiao, Limeng Qiao

task_types: mental rotation, spatial relation inference, figure-ground discrimination
description: Digitizes 20 vision-centric subtests from FRCT cognitive psychology assessment spanning four domains of human visual cognition. Features algorithms to automatically construct unlimited test cases with controllable difficulty, focusing on foundational visual capabilities like mental rotation and spatial relation inference.
paper_title: Human Cognitive Benchmarks Reveal Foundational Visual Gaps in MLLMs
authors: Jen-Tse Huang, Dasen Dai, Jen-Yuan Huang, Youliang Yuan, Xiaoyuan Liu

description: Benchmark for multi-hop reasoning over ultra-high-resolution images (hundreds of megapixels to gigapixels). Comprises Humanistic and Natural Scenes categories with questions organized into three difficulty levels, evaluating reasoning capabilities under extreme visual information complexity.
paper_title: UR-Bench: A Benchmark for Multi-Hop Reasoning over Ultra-High-Resolution Images
authors: Siqi Li, Xinyu Cai, Jianbiao Mei, Nianchen Deng, Pinlong Cai

description: A novel multimodal benchmark for assessing deception in complex social interactions with synchronized video and text. Features verifiable ground-truth labels for every statement and tests models' ability to 'read the room' and detect lies in multi-party social scenarios.
paper_title: Can MLLMs Read the Room? A Multimodal Benchmark for Assessing Deception in Multi-Party Social Interactions
authors: Caixin Kang, Yifei Huang, Liangyang Ouyang, Mingfang Zhang, Ruicong Liu

description: An MLLM-annotated benchmark for Most Important Person localization and group context understanding in 'in-the-wild' social situations. Addresses the subjective and contextual complexity of identifying the most important person in social event setups with diverse causality aspects.
paper_title: MIP-GAF: A MLLM-annotated Benchmark for Most Important Person Localization and Group Context Understanding
authors: Surbhi Madan, Shreya Ghosh, Lownish Rai Sookha, M. A. Ganaie, Ramanathan Subramanian

description: A holistic evaluation benchmark for human-centric scene understanding spanning 4 primary visual domains with 15 secondary domains and 43 sub-fields. Evaluates human-based activities progressively from granular perception to higher-dimensional reasoning across eight dimensions with multi-person and multi-image understanding.
paper_title: Human-MME: A Holistic Evaluation Benchmark for Human-Centric Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Yuansen Liu, Haiming Tang, Jinlong Peng, Jiangning Zhang, Xiaozhong Ji

description: A comprehensive multimodal Theory of Mind benchmark using realistic narrative-rich short films. Features over 2,300 multiple-choice questions across seven ToM categories with long video context windows and realistic social interactions for deeper character mental state understanding.
paper_title: MOMENTS: A Comprehensive Multimodal Benchmark for Theory of Mind
authors: Emilio Villa-Cueva, S M Masrur Ahmed, Rendi Chevi, Jan Christian Blaise Cruz, Kareem Elzeky

task_types: geometric reasoning, visual construction, chain-of-thought reasoning
description: First benchmark for geometric reasoning requiring dynamic manipulation of visual aids with textual construction steps aligned to ground-truth visual updates. Enables evaluation of strategic construction knowledge for visual-text interleaved reasoning.
paper_title: Thinking with Constructions: A Benchmark and Policy Optimization for Visual-Text Interleaved Geometric Reasoning
authors: Haokun Zhao, Wanshi Xu, Haidong Yuan, Songjun Cao, Long Ma

task_types: object detection, visual grounding, out-of-distribution generalization
description: Large-scale OOD dataset with object-level annotations encompassing 14 natural distributional shifts and over 1,196K labeled bounding boxes. Introduces two novel benchmarks O(OD)2 for evaluating OOD generalization capabilities of object detectors and OODG for assessing OOD generalization of grounding abilities in MLLMs under controlled distribution shifts.
paper_title: COUNTS: Benchmarking Object Detectors and Multimodal Large Language Models under Distribution Shifts
authors: Jiansheng Li, Xingxuan Zhang, Hao Zou, Yige Guo, Renzhe Xu

task_types: compositional reasoning, conditional reasoning, visual workflow
description: Benchmark for visually grounded deep compositional reasoning organized as multi-layer reasoning chains, where every layer contains non-trivial compositional conditions built from multiple objects, attributes, or relations. Uses agentic synthesis pipeline with Verifiable Programmatic Intermediate Representation (VPIR) to ensure mechanically verifiable conditions across natural images, data charts, and GUI trajectories.
paper_title: MM-CondChain: A Programmatically Verified Benchmark for Visually Grounded Deep Compositional Reasoning
authors: Haozhan Shen, Shilin Yan, Hongwei Xue, Shuaiqi Lu, Xiaojun Tang

task_types: visual reasoning tracing, object localization, intermediate reasoning
description: Human-annotated benchmark for evaluating Visual Reasoning Tracer (VRT) task, requiring models to not only localize target objects but explicitly predict intermediate objects forming the reasoning path. Includes new metric for assessing reasoning trace quality and focuses on making the reasoning processes of MLLMs more transparent and traceable.
paper_title: Visual Reasoning Tracer: Object-Level Grounded Reasoning Benchmark
authors: Haobo Yuan, Yueyi Sun, Yanwei Li, Tao Zhang, Xueqing Deng

task_types: visual reasoning, perceptual reasoning, image-grounded reasoning
description: Vision-centric reasoning benchmark grounded in challenging perceptual tasks requiring models to reason from visual content alone. Integrates seven types of visual challenges, natural adversarial image pairs enforcing reliance on visual content, and annotated reasoning chains for fine-grained evaluation of reasoning processes rather than final answers alone.
paper_title: BLINK-Twice: You see, but do you observe? A Reasoning Benchmark on Visual Perception
authors: Junyan Ye, Dongzhi Jiang, Jun He, Baichuan Zhou, Zilong Huang

description: Bridges gap between human and machine assessment of graph similarity through three interconnected experiments using 1,881 node-link diagrams. Benchmarks 16 computational measures against human judgments and evaluates MLLMs as perceptual proxies, though the primary contribution appears to be evaluation methodology rather than a standalone benchmark.
paper_title: Seeing Graphs Like Humans: Benchmarking Computational Measures and MLLMs for Similarity Assessment
authors: Seokweon Jung, Jeongmin Rhee, Seoyoung Doh, Hyeon Jeon, Ghulam Jilani Quadri

description: MUCAR specifically evaluates multimodal ambiguity resolution across multilingual and cross-modal scenarios. It includes both multilingual datasets where visual context resolves textual ambiguity and dual-ambiguity datasets pairing ambiguous images with ambiguous text requiring mutual disambiguation.
paper_title: MUCAR: Benchmarking Multilingual Cross-Modal Ambiguity Resolution for Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Xiaolong Wang, Zhaolu Kang, Wangyuxuan Zhai, Xinyue Lou, Yunghwei Lai

task_types: tool-based reasoning, image manipulation, chain-of-thought reasoning
description: TIR-Bench evaluates agentic thinking-with-images across 13 diverse tasks requiring novel tool use for image processing and manipulation in chain-of-thought reasoning. It goes beyond basic operations to test complex, dynamic, and tool-dependent reasoning capabilities.
paper_title: TIR-Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Agentic Thinking-with-Images Reasoning
authors: Ming Li, Jike Zhong, Shitian Zhao, Haoquan Zhang, Shaoheng Lin

task_types: reasoning by analogy, pattern completion, spatial matching, counting, scaling
description: VRB evaluates MLLMs on authentic visual problems from primary school classrooms in Zambia and India. It uniquely uses unedited, minimal-text images to test spatial reasoning capabilities like analogy, pattern completion, and spatial matching, revealing distinct performance ceilings in dynamic spatial operations.
paper_title: Visual Reasoning Benchmark: Evaluating Multimodal LLMs on Classroom-Authentic Visual Problems from Primary Education
authors: Mohamed Huti, Alasdair Mackintosh, Amy Waldock, Dominic Andrews, Maxime Lelièvre

description: REBUS evaluates multimodal large language models on rebus puzzles, requiring combination of image recognition and string manipulation with hypothesis testing, multi-step reasoning, and understanding of human cognition. It covers 333 original examples of image-based wordplay across 13 categories like movies, composers, and major cities.
paper_title: REBUS: A Robust Evaluation Benchmark of Understanding Symbols
authors: Andrew Gritsevskiy, Arjun Panickssery, Aaron Kirtland, Derik Kauffman, Hans Gundlach

description: PhysicsMind evaluates physical reasoning and prediction in both simulation and real environments across three canonical physics principles: Center of Mass, Lever Equilibrium, and Newton's First Law. It uniquely combines VQA tasks for reasoning and Video Generation tasks for physics-consistent motion prediction.
paper_title: PhysicsMind: Sim and Real Mechanics Benchmarking for Physical Reasoning and Prediction in Foundational VLMs and World Models
authors: Chak-Wing Mak, Guanyu Zhu, Boyi Zhang, Hongji Li, Xiaowei Chi

description: A dynamic VQA dataset featuring three types of complex questions requiring adaptive retrieval strategies: rapidly changing answers, multi-modal knowledge requirements, and multi-hop reasoning. Designed to evaluate multimodal retrieval augmented generation systems beyond standard two-step retrieval scenarios.
paper_title: Benchmarking Multimodal Retrieval Augmented Generation with Dynamic VQA Dataset and Self-adaptive Planning Agent
authors: Yangning Li, Yinghui Li, Xinyu Wang, Yong Jiang, Zhen Zhang

benchmark_name: Ukrainian Visual Word Sense Disambiguation Benchmark
description: The first Visual Word Sense Disambiguation benchmark in Ukrainian, requiring identification of appropriate word representations from sets of ten images with minimal context. Enables cross-language model performance comparisons and reveals performance gaps between Ukrainian and English for this task.
paper_title: Ukrainian Visual Word Sense Disambiguation Benchmark
authors: Yurii Laba, Yaryna Mohytych, Ivanna Rohulia, Halyna Kyryleyza, Hanna Dydyk-Meush

task_types: visual reasoning, content creation, scene text recognition
description: A multimodal benchmark evaluating cognitive capabilities of MLLMs in text-rich visual scenes through visual reasoning and content-creation tasks. Incorporates perception tasks to ensure consistent comparison of both cognitive and perceptual capabilities, with automatic evaluation pipeline for content-creation fairness.
paper_title: MCTBench: Multimodal Cognition towards Text-Rich Visual Scenes Benchmark
authors: Bin Shan, Xiang Fei, Wei Shi, An-Lan Wang, Guozhi Tang

task_types: optical image generation, optical phenomenon understanding
description: The first benchmark systematically evaluating MLLMs on geometric optics through two tasks: generating optically authentic imagery and understanding optical phenomena. Tests fine-grained physical principles in geometric optics scenarios with focus on optical authenticity and instruction fidelity.
paper_title: GOBench: Benchmarking Geometric Optics Generation and Understanding of MLLMs
authors: Xiaorong Zhu, Ziheng Jia, Jiarui Wang, Xiangyu Zhao, Haodong Duan

description: MMRel focuses specifically on inter-object relation understanding in multimodal large language models, featuring large-scale data spanning three domains and around 400 relations. It uniquely includes adversarial cases with highly unusual relations to evaluate relation hallucination, providing manually verified high-quality labels for exceptional annotation accuracy.
paper_title: MMRel: Benchmarking Relation Understanding in Multi-Modal Large Language Models
authors: Jiahao Nie, Gongjie Zhang, Wenbin An, Yun Xing, Yap-Peng Tan

description: OmniDiff addresses Image Difference Captioning by providing 324 diverse scenarios spanning real-world and 3D synthetic environments with fine-grained human annotations averaging 60 words. It covers 12 distinct change types and requires both precise visual change localization and coherent semantic expression, going beyond simplistic descriptions in prior benchmarks.
paper_title: OmniDiff: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Fine-grained Image Difference Captioning
authors: Yuan Liu, Saihui Hou, Saijie Hou, Jiabao Du, Shibei Meng

description: SVIP introduces a benchmark for training and testing Chain-of-Thought reward models in multimodal settings by automatically generating step-level training samples through visual program code analysis. It evaluates multimodal CoT reward models across visual tasks and introduces the TriAtt-CoT multi-head attention mechanism for step-level evaluation.
paper_title: Benchmarking Multimodal CoT Reward Model Stepwise by Visual Program
authors: Minghe Gao, Xuqi Liu, Zhongqi Yue, Yang Wu, Shuang Chen

description: MME-CC organizes 11 representative reasoning tasks into three fundamental categories of visual information: spatial, geometric, and knowledge-based reasoning, providing fine-grained analyses of MLLMs' cognitive capacity.
