It focuses on vision-centric cognitive behaviors and identifies common error patterns including orientation mistakes and fragile cross-view identity persistence.
paper_title: MME-CC: A Challenging Multi-Modal Evaluation Benchmark of Cognitive Capacity
authors: Kaiyuan Zhang, Chenghao Yang, Zhoufutu Wen, Sihang Yuan, Qiuyue Wang

task_types: multi-modal reasoning, symbolic reasoning, multi-hop reasoning, reasoning trace evaluation
description: MMLU-Reason is a high-difficulty dataset of 1,083 questions spanning six diverse reasoning types with symbolic depth and multi-hop demands. It features a modular Reasoning Trace Evaluation Pipeline (RTEP) for assessing reasoning quality beyond accuracy through metrics like relevance, consistency, and structured error annotations.
paper_title: MMLU-Reason: Benchmarking Multi-Task Multi-modal Language Understanding and Reasoning
authors: Guiyao Tie, Xueyang Zhou, Tianhe Gu, Ruihang Zhang, Chaoran Hu

task_types: multimodal chain of thought, multi-image VQA, imaginary captioning, hallucination detection, fine-grained retrieval
description: JourneyBench is a comprehensive human-annotated benchmark of generated images designed to assess fine-grained multimodal reasoning in unusual imaginary scenarios. It features five tasks including complementary multimodal chain of thought, multi-image VQA, and imaginary image captioning, explicitly requiring reasoning where language bias is insufficient.
paper_title: JourneyBench: A Challenging One-Stop Vision-Language Understanding Benchmark of Generated Images
authors: Zhecan Wang, Junzhang Liu, Chia-Wei Tang, Hani Alomari, Anushka Sivakumar

description: Two-tier benchmark using Unity simulations to evaluate language grounding and intuitive physics understanding in video-based MLLMs. Tests object permanence and continuity principles while assessing models' ability to relate textual descriptions with visual information.
paper_title: GRASP: A novel benchmark for evaluating language GRounding And Situated Physics understanding in multimodal language models
authors: Serwan Jassim, Mario Holubar, Annika Richter, Cornelius Wolff, Xenia Ohmer

description: CRYSTAL is a diagnostic benchmark with 6,372 instances that evaluates multimodal reasoning through verifiable intermediate steps rather than just final answers. It introduces novel metrics (Match F1 and Ordered Match F1) to assess step-level precision, recall, and reasoning order, revealing systematic failures invisible to answer accuracy alone.
paper_title: Beyond Final Answers: CRYSTAL Benchmark for Transparent Multimodal Reasoning Evaluation
authors: Wayner Barrios, SouYoung Jin

description: Visual CoT introduces a large-scale dataset with 438k question-answer pairs annotated with intermediate bounding boxes highlighting key regions essential for answering questions. About 98k pairs include detailed reasoning steps, addressing challenges with interpretability and complex visual inputs, especially high-resolution images or small interested regions.
paper_title: Visual CoT: Advancing Multi-Modal Language Models with a Comprehensive Dataset and Benchmark for Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
authors: Hao Shao, Shengju Qian, Han Xiao, Guanglu Song, Zhuofan Zong

description: VideoCogQA uses synthetic videos generated via programmatic engine to provide fine-grained control over visual elements, temporal dynamics, and task difficulty. It focuses on evaluating cognitive abilities involving symbolic and abstract perception independent of prior visual scene knowledge.
paper_title: VideoCogQA: A Controllable Benchmark for Evaluating Cognitive Abilities in Video-Language Models
authors: Chenglin Li, Qianglong Chen, Zhi Li, Feng Tao, Yin Zhang

description: DTVLT is a multi-modal diverse text benchmark for visual language tracking that utilizes LLMs to generate varied semantic annotations with different text lengths and granularities. It establishes a novel benchmark based on five prominent VLT and SOT benchmarks with three sub-tasks: short-term tracking, long-term tracking, and global instance tracking.
paper_title: DTVLT: A Multi-modal Diverse Text Benchmark for Visual Language Tracking Based on LLM
authors: Xuchen Li, Shiyu Hu, Xiaokun Feng, Dailing Zhang, Meiqi Wu

description: A knowledge-based visual question answering benchmark where image content alone is insufficient to answer questions, encouraging methods that rely on external knowledge resources. Contains more than 14,000 questions requiring external knowledge, addressing the limitation of existing VQA benchmarks focused on simple counting and object detection.
paper_title: OK-VQA: A Visual Question Answering Benchmark Requiring External Knowledge
authors: Kenneth Marino, Mohammad Rastegari, Ali Farhadi, Roozbeh Mottaghi

description: A continual learning benchmark for cross-domain visual question answering, evaluating how vision-language models can adapt to new domains without forgetting previously acquired knowledge. Conducts extensive experiments across 4 VLPMs, 4 CL approaches, and 5 VQA datasets from different domains, addressing the gap in continual learning research for multimodal tasks.
paper_title: CL-CrossVQA: A Continual Learning Benchmark for Cross-Domain Visual Question Answering
authors: Yao Zhang, Haokun Chen, Ahmed Frikha, Yezi Yang, Denis Krompass

description: ReasonVQA is a VQA dataset automatically integrated with structured encyclopedic knowledge that generates complex, multi-hop questions requiring reasoning across multiple steps. The dataset surpasses the largest existing knowledge-requiring datasets by more than an order of magnitude and poses significant challenges to state-of-the-art VQA models.
paper_title: ReasonVQA: A Multi-hop Reasoning Benchmark with Structural Knowledge for Visual Question Answering
authors: Duong T. Tran, Trung-Kien Tran, Manfred Hauswirth, Danh Le Phuoc

description: VQA-GEN is the first multi-modal benchmark dataset for distribution shift in VQA, generated through a shift-induced pipeline that creates joint visual and textual domain shifts. Unlike existing datasets that focus on unilateral textual shifts, VQA-GEN addresses the multi-modal nature of VQA with comprehensive shifts across both domains.
paper_title: VQA-GEN: A Visual Question Answering Benchmark for Domain Generalization
authors: Suraj Jyothi Unni, Raha Moraffah, Huan Liu

description: This is the first large-scale benchmark specifically designed to test visual robustness of VQA models against realistic corruption effects like image blur. It comprises 213,000 augmented images and introduces several robustness evaluation metrics that can be unified for various use cases.
paper_title: Visual Robustness Benchmark for Visual Question Answering (VQA)
authors: Md Farhan Ishmam, Ishmam Tashdeed, Talukder Asir Saadat, Md Hamjajul Ashmafee, Abu Raihan Mostofa Kamal

description: DVE introduces a new task allowing modification of entailment relationships between image premises and text hypotheses based on additional updates. This concept, well-established in NLP, remains unexplored in visual entailment and enables models to refine initial interpretations for improved accuracy.
paper_title: Defeasible Visual Entailment: Benchmark, Evaluator, and Reward-Driven Optimization
authors: Yue Zhang, Liqiang Jing, Vibhav Gogate

description: Adversarial VQA is collected through an adversarial human-and-model-in-the-loop procedure where non-expert annotators successfully attack SOTA VQA models. It reveals the fragility of current models and demonstrates that large-scale pre-trained models perform far worse on this benchmark than on standard VQA v2.
paper_title: Adversarial VQA: A New Benchmark for Evaluating the Robustness of VQA Models
authors: Linjie Li, Jie Lei, Zhe Gan, Jingjing Liu

description: WinoGAViL is an online game-based dynamic evaluation benchmark inspired by Codenames, where a spymaster gives textual cues related to visual candidates. It collects 3.5K instances that are intuitive for humans (>90% Jaccard index) but challenging for AI models, requiring diverse reasoning skills including general knowledge and abstraction.
paper_title: WinoGAViL: Gamified Association Benchmark to Challenge Vision-and-Language Models
authors: Yonatan Bitton, Nitzan Bitton Guetta, Ron Yosef, Yuval Elovici, Mohit Bansal

description: This benchmark curates novel program synthesis tasks based on real-world XLogoOnline visual programming environment, requiring a combination of spatial planning, basic programming, and logical reasoning. It includes a large-scale synthetic training dataset with over 80,000 tasks and demonstrates the challenge for current SOTA models.
paper_title: Program Synthesis Benchmark for Visual Programming in XLogoOnline Environment
authors: Chao Wen, Jacqueline Staub, Adish Singla

description: Super-CLEVR is a virtual benchmark that isolates different factors in VQA domain shifts including visual complexity, question redundancy, concept distribution, and concept compositionality. This allows researchers to study the effects of each domain shift factor independently in a controlled manner.
paper_title: Super-CLEVR: A Virtual Benchmark to Diagnose Domain Robustness in Visual Reasoning
authors: Zhuowan Li, Xingrui Wang, Elias Stengel-Eskin, Adam Kortylewski, Wufei Ma

description: Illusory VQA introduces four specialized datasets (IllusionMNIST, IllusionFashionMNIST, IllusionAnimals, IllusionChar) designed to evaluate multimodal models' performance on visual illusions. It reveals the disparity between human and model perception of illusions and tests robustness to visual deception.
paper_title: Illusory VQA: Benchmarking and Enhancing Multimodal Models on Visual Illusions
authors: Mohammadmostafa Rostamkhani, Baktash Ansari, Hoorieh Sabzevari, Farzan Rahmani, Sauleh Eetemadi

task_types: image captioning, cross-modal matching, visual question answering, explanation generation
description: WHOOPS! features purposefully commonsense-defying images created by designers using image generation tools to test visual commonsense reasoning. It includes a challenging explanation generation task where models must identify and explain why images are unusual or violate expectations.
paper_title: Breaking Common Sense: WHOOPS! A Vision-and-Language Benchmark of Synthetic and Compositional Images
authors: Nitzan Bitton-Guetta, Yonatan Bitton, Jack Hessel, Ludwig Schmidt, Yuval Elovici

description: iVQA introduces the inverse problem of VQA where models must generate questions corresponding to given image-answer pairs. It serves as both a challenging benchmark for visuo-linguistic understanding and a diagnostic tool to expose VQA models' belief sets through reinforcement learning.
paper_title: Inverse Visual Question Answering: A New Benchmark and VQA Diagnosis Tool
authors: Feng Liu, Tao Xiang, Timothy M. Hospedales, Wankou Yang, Changyin Sun

description: This benchmark addresses limitations of VQA-CP v2 by considering varying types of shortcuts through multiple OOD test sets with different distribution shifts. It provides more rigorous evaluation of shortcut learning in VQA and standardizes OOD evaluation procedures.
paper_title: Language Prior Is Not the Only Shortcut: A Benchmark for Shortcut Learning in VQA
authors: Qingyi Si, Fandong Meng, Mingyu Zheng, Zheng Lin, Yuanxin Liu

description: IconQA introduces a new challenge for visual question answering on abstract diagram/icon images, requiring diverse cognitive reasoning skills including geometric, commonsense, and arithmetic reasoning. It provides three sub-tasks (multi-image-choice, multi-text-choice, filling-in-the-blank) and is complemented by Icon645 dataset with 645,687 colored icons for semantic representation learning.
paper_title: IconQA: A New Benchmark for Abstract Diagram Understanding and Visual Language Reasoning
authors: Pan Lu, Liang Qiu, Jiaqi Chen, Tony Xia, Yizhou Zhao

description: Pix2Fact evaluates expert-level perception and knowledge-intensive multi-hop reasoning with high-resolution 4K+ images across 8 daily-life scenarios. Each question requires detailed visual grounding, multi-hop reasoning, and integration of external knowledge, crafted by PhD-level annotators to test advanced visual comprehension capabilities.
paper_title: From Pixels to Facts (Pix2Fact): Benchmarking Multi-Hop Reasoning for Fine-Grained Visual Fact Checking
authors: Yifan Jiang, Cong Zhang, Bofei Zhang, Yifan Yang, Bingzhang Wang

description: Tangram evaluates geometric element recognition in Large Multimodal Models using 1,080 diverse geometric diagrams from educational materials. Unlike existing benchmarks emphasizing higher-level cognition, Tangram focuses on understanding geometric elements through a 'simple yet challenging' counting task, revealing significant gaps in basic perception abilities.
paper_title: Tangram: Benchmark for Evaluating Geometric Element Recognition in Large Multimodal Models
authors: Chao Zhang, Jiamin Tang, Jing Xiao

description: HVQR evaluates explainable high-order visual question reasoning with questions containing relationship triplets requiring multistep reasoning. It provides explicit evaluation on multistep reasoning processes constructed with image scene graphs and commonsense knowledge bases, with each relationship triplet appearing only once to prevent overfitting.
paper_title: Explainable High-order Visual Question Reasoning: A New Benchmark and Knowledge-routed Network
authors: Qingxing Cao, Bailin Li, Xiaodan Liang, Liang Lin

description: First benchmark assessing discipline-informed knowledge and reasoning in image editing across 10 academic domains. Features multi-dimensional evaluation protocol for discipline reasoning, visual consistency, and logical readability.
paper_title: GRADE: Benchmarking Discipline-Informed Reasoning in Image Editing
authors: Mingxin Liu, Ziqian Fan, Zhaokai Wang, Leyao Gu, Zirun Zhu

description: MM-IQ is a comprehensive evaluation framework for measuring abstraction and reasoning capabilities in multimodal models through IQ testing methodology. The benchmark comprises 2,710 meticulously curated test items spanning 8 distinct reasoning paradigms, designed to assess fundamental human-like cognitive capabilities.
paper_title: MM-IQ: Benchmarking Human-Like Abstraction and Reasoning in Multimodal Models
authors: Huanqia Cai, Yijun Yang, Winston Hu

task_types: understanding-generation consistency, reasoning-centric text-to-image generation, multi-step reasoning editing
description: Comprehensive benchmark evaluating unified models across understanding-generation consistency, reasoning-centric text-to-image generation, and multi-step reasoning in editing. Features task-specific evaluation pipelines for fine-grained and interpretable evaluation.
paper_title: GIR-Bench: Versatile Benchmark for Generating Images with Reasoning
authors: Hongxiang Li, Yaowei Li, Bin Lin, Yuwei Niu, Yuhang Yang

task_types: humor understanding, panel sequencing, narrative reasoning
description: PixelHumor evaluates LMMs' ability to interpret multimodal humor and recognize narrative sequences in multi-panel comics. It addresses critical limitations in current models' integration of visual and textual cues for coherent narrative and humor understanding, representing a core aspect of social intelligence.
paper_title: Humor in Pixels: Benchmarking Large Multimodal Models Understanding of Online Comics
authors: Yuriel Ryan, Rui Yang Tan, Kenny Tsu Wei Choo, Roy Ka-Wei Lee

description: EDIR leverages image editing to achieve precise control over modification types and content, enabling fine-grained CIR evaluation across five main categories and fifteen subcategories. It addresses evaluation gaps in current CIR benchmarks that feature limited query categories and fail to capture diverse real-world scenarios.
paper_title: Rethinking Composed Image Retrieval Evaluation: A Fine-Grained Benchmark from Image Editing
authors: Tingyu Song, Yanzhao Zhang, Mingxin Li, Zhuoning Guo, Dingkun Long

task_types: step correctness evaluation, answer aggregation, reasoning process search
description: MPBench is a comprehensive, multi-task, multimodal benchmark for assessing process-level reward models in reasoning scenarios. It employs three evaluation paradigms targeting different roles of PRMs: step correctness, answer aggregation, and reasoning process search, going beyond existing text-based error detection benchmarks.
paper_title: MPBench: A Comprehensive Multimodal Reasoning Benchmark for Process Errors Identification
authors: Zhaopan Xu, Pengfei Zhou, Jiaxin Ai, Wangbo Zhao, Kai Wang

task_types: fine-grained image analysis, visual aspect identification
description: Benchmark for evaluating multimodal models' fine-grained image analysis capabilities across seven visual aspects: main object, additional objects, background, detail, dominant colors, style, and viewpoint. Uses diverse generated images to assess comprehensive image understanding.
paper_title: Benchmarking Multimodal Models for Fine-Grained Image Analysis: A Comparative Study Across Diverse Visual Features
authors: Evgenii Evstafev

task_types: state transition, dynamic process, temporal sequence, scientific simulation
description: First evaluation benchmark dedicated to reasoning over intermediate pathways in image editing. Covers state transition, dynamic process, temporal sequence, and scientific simulation with assessment criteria for logical coherence and visual naturalness.
paper_title: InEdit-Bench: Benchmarking Intermediate Logical Pathways for Intelligent Image Editing Models
authors: Zhiqiang Sheng, Xumeng Han, Zhiwei Zhang, Zenghui Xiong, Yifan Ding

task_types: geometric pattern interpretation, code generation, visual programming
description: Benchmark evaluating LMMs' ability to interpret geometric patterns and generate precise code outputs inspired by turtle geometry. Assesses integration of visual understanding and code generation capabilities with patterned shapes having algorithmic logic.
paper_title: TurtleBench: A Visual Programming Benchmark in Turtle Geometry
authors: Sina Rismanchian, Yasaman Razeghi, Sameer Singh, Shayan Doroudi

task_types: reasoning-based image editing, multi-object interaction editing, game-world scenario editing
description: Unified benchmark for reasoning-based image editing evaluation covering real- and game-world scenarios across 8 primary dimensions. Introduces multimodal dual-reference evaluation with both textual and ground-truth image references.
paper_title: UniREditBench: A Unified Reasoning-based Image Editing Benchmark
authors: Feng Han, Yibin Wang, Chenglin Li, Zheming Liang, Dianyi Wang

task_types: path planning, spatial reasoning, constraint adherence
description: This paper presents a comprehensive benchmark for assessing multimodal LLMs in robotic path planning tasks using 2D grid environments. It specifically compares text-only versus text-plus-visual inputs across varying model sizes and grid complexities to evaluate spatial reasoning and constraint adherence capabilities.
paper_title: Assessing the Value of Visual Input: A Benchmark of Multimodal Large Language Models for Robotic Path Planning
authors: Jacinto Colan, Ana Davila, Yasuhisa Hasegawa

task_types: semantic segmentation, image generation evaluation, pixel-precision assessment
description: PixelArena proposes using semantic segmentation tasks to objectively examine fine-grained generative intelligence of omni-modal models with pixel precision. It uniquely focuses on evaluating visual intelligence through objective metrics rather than subjective aesthetics, testing models' ability to generate semantic masks with high fidelity.
paper_title: PixelArena: A benchmark for Pixel-Precision Visual Intelligence
authors: Feng Liang, Sizhe Cheng, Chenqi Yi, Yong Wang

task_types: visual chain-of-thought, intermediate image generation, spatial reasoning
description: MIRA evaluates models in scenarios where generating intermediate visual images is essential for successful reasoning, mimicking how humans 'draw to think'. It uniquely focuses on Visual Chain-of-Thought reasoning with tasks requiring sketches, structural diagrams, or path drawings as intermediate steps for complex spatial and structural reasoning.
paper_title: When Visualizing is the First Step to Reasoning: MIRA, a Benchmark for Visual Chain-of-Thought
authors: Yiyang Zhou, Haoqin Tu, Zijun Wang, Zeyu Wang, Niklas Muennighoff

task_types: infraction identification, penalty prediction, tactic explanation
description: Introduces the first multi-sports large-scale benchmark with 4,789 images and 2,052 videos designed to evaluate MLLMs on fundamental reasoning for sports intelligence. Features progressive hierarchy of questions and 6,841 Chain of Thought annotations for complex penalty prediction and tactic explanation.
paper_title: SportR: A Benchmark for Multimodal Large Language Model Reasoning in Sports
authors: Haotian Xia, Haonan Ge, Junbo Zou, Hyun Woo Choi, Xuebin Zhang

description: Introduces a dataset of 1184 puzzles derived from puzzle competitions that probes models' ability to perform implicit knowledge synthesis and multi-step deductive reasoning. Challenges models to discover hidden connections between seemingly unrelated pieces of information.
paper_title: EnigmaEval: A Benchmark of Long Multimodal Reasoning Challenges
authors: Clinton J. Wang, Dean Lee, Cristina Menghini, Johannes Mols, Jack Doughty

description: Proposes an automated framework for generating color illusion images, resulting in a dataset of 19,000 realistic illusion images. Studies perception of color illusions by vision-language models to evaluate perceptual biases similar to human vision.
paper_title: Evaluating Model Perception of Color Illusions in Photorealistic Scenes
authors: Lingjun Mao, Zineng Tang, Alane Suhr

task_types: object property recognition, spatial relation understanding
description: CIVET provides a systematic evaluation framework via controlled stimuli to assess VLMs' understanding of object properties and relations. It uses exhaustive sets of controlled stimuli free from annotation noise and dataset biases, revealing that VLMs can only recognize limited basic object properties and struggle with spatial relations.
paper_title: CIVET: Systematic Evaluation of Understanding in VLMs
authors: Massimo Rizzoli, Simone Alghisi, Olha Khomyn, Gabriel Roccabruna, Seyed Mahed Mousavi

description: ConMe introduces a novel data generation pipeline where VLMs converse with each other to collaboratively expose their weaknesses in compositional reasoning. It autonomously generates, evaluates, and selects challenging compositional reasoning questions, causing up to 33% performance decrease compared to preceding benchmarks.
paper_title: ConMe: Rethinking Evaluation of Compositional Reasoning for Modern VLMs
authors: Irene Huang, Wei Lin, M. Jehanzeb Mirza, Jacob A. Hansen, Sivan Doveh

task_types: abstract shape recognition, visual perception, shape detection
description: IllusionBench challenges VLMs to decipher shape information when shapes are represented by arrangements of visual elements in scenes. It quantifies limitations in shape perception that are easily detectable by humans but challenging for current VLMs, revealing important gaps in visual perception systems.
paper_title: Hidden in Plain Sight: Evaluating Abstract Shape Recognition in Vision-Language Models
authors: Arshia Hemmat, Adam Davies, Tom A. Lamb, Jianhao Yuan, Philip Torr

task_types: comparative perception, saccadic search, smooth visual search, nonlocal reasoning
description: This benchmark isolates three distinct forms of nonlocal vision: comparative perception (holding two images in working memory), saccadic search (evidence-driven jumps to locate targets), and smooth visual search (following continuous contours). It reveals that flagship VLMs barely exceed random accuracy on tasks trivial for humans.
paper_title: VLMs have Tunnel Vision: Evaluating Nonlocal Visual Reasoning in Leading VLMs
authors: Shmuel Berman, Jia Deng

description: DARE is a carefully curated multiple-choice VQA benchmark that evaluates VLM performance across five diverse categories while incorporating four robustness-oriented evaluations. It uniquely tests model robustness through variations in prompts, answer option subsets, output format, and number of correct answers.
paper_title: DARE: Diverse Visual Question Answering with Robustness Evaluation
authors: Hannah Sterz, Jonas Pfeiffer, Ivan Vulić

description: This benchmark introduces a reference-free metric for evaluating open-vocabulary scene graph generation capabilities of VLMs in relation prediction. It addresses the limitation of existing SGG benchmarks' limited vocabulary and provides synthetic data generation for improved evaluation.
paper_title: Measuring Image-Relation Alignment: Reference-Free Evaluation of VLMs and Synthetic Pre-training for Open-Vocabulary Scene Graph Generation
authors: Maëlic Neau, Zoe Falomir, Cédric Buche, Akihiro Sugimoto

description: A large-scale benchmark evaluating VLMs as internal world models across 23 fine-grained dimensions and 6 simulated environments. Assesses Perception (visual, spatial, temporal, quantitative, motion) and Prediction (mechanistic simulation, transitive inference, compositional inference) capabilities.
paper_title: Do Vision-Language Models Have Internal World Models? Towards an Atomic Evaluation
authors: Qiyue Gao, Xinyu Pi, Kevin Liu, Junrong Chen, Ruolan Yang

description: A synthetic benchmark for visual lateral thinking using text and icon rebus puzzles. Formulates lateral thinking as multiple-choice QA with over 1,000 puzzles testing creative problem-solving rather than traditional vertical reasoning.
paper_title: COLUMBUS: Evaluating COgnitive Lateral Understanding through Multiple-choice reBUSes
authors: Koen Kraaijveld, Yifan Jiang, Kaixin Ma, Filip Ilievski

task_types: visual reasoning, evidence grounding, multi-step reasoning
description: Process-verifiable benchmark designed to evaluate faithful visual reasoning. Comprises 200 multi-scenario high-resolution images with expert-annotated visual evidence, categorizing tasks by difficulty into perception and reasoning dimensions where reasoning requires utilizing localized visual details with prior knowledge.
paper_title: Beyond Accuracy: Evaluating Grounded Visual Evidence in Thinking with Images
authors: Xuchen Li, Xuzhao Li, Renjie Pi, Shiyu Hu, Jian Zhao

task_types: compositional generalization, concept binding, zero-shot learning
description: Benchmark evaluating compositional generalization in VLMs and diffusion models, testing ability to bind objects with attributes and relations in zero-shot learning and generalized zero-shot learning settings. Tests fundamental semantic composition abilities through concept binding tasks.
paper_title: Evaluating Compositional Generalisation in VLMs and Diffusion Models
authors: Beth Pearson, Bilal Boulbarss, Michael Wray, Martha Lewis

description: Two manually curated datasets for testing visual perspective-taking (VPT) skills - the ability to understand another person's viewpoint. Tests whether VLMs can anticipate actions based on what others can see, a capability humans develop in early childhood.
paper_title: Seeing Through Their Eyes: Evaluating Visual Perspective Taking in Vision Language Models
authors: Gracjan Góral, Alicja Ziarko, Michal Nauman, Maciej Wołczyk

description: Semi-automated benchmark with 10,000 human-verified VQA samples featuring vision-centric design where each question pairs with two images yielding different answers. Prevents blind solutions and requires diverse visio-linguistic skills including attribute bindings and advanced reasoning.
paper_title: NaturalBench: Evaluating Vision-Language Models on Natural Adversarial Samples
authors: Baiqi Li, Zhiqiu Lin, Wenxuan Peng, Jean de Dieu Nyandwi, Daniel Jiang

task_types: perceptual reasoning, rule-based inference, compositional reasoning
description: Benchmark designed to study visual reasoning in naturalistic settings without contextual language supervision across three complexity levels. Tests perceptual completion, rule-based inference, and compositional reasoning across more than 19,000 controlled task images.
paper_title: VisRes Bench: On Evaluating the Visual Reasoning Capabilities of VLMs
authors: Brigitta Malagurski Törtei, Yasser Dahou, Ngoc Dung Huynh, Wamiq Reyaz Para, Phúc H. Lê Khac

task_types: hypothesis generation, hypothesis selection, abductive reasoning
description: Novel benchmark inspired by the Dixit game that evaluates multimodal abductive reasoning through hypothesis generation and selection. Features both dynamic multi-agent environment (DixitArena) and static QA format (DixitBench) to assess creative storytelling and discriminative understanding under imperfect information.
paper_title: DixitWorld: Evaluating Multimodal Abductive Reasoning in Vision-Language Models with Multi-Agent Dixit Gameplay
authors: Yunxiang Mo, Tianshi Zheng, Qing Zong, Jiayu Liu, Baixuan Xu

task_types: structured reasoning, knowledge graph understanding, relational topology comprehension
description: Novel benchmark grounded in Multi-Modal Map for STRuctured understanding that evaluates MLLMs' ability to comprehend world knowledge with structured abstractions. Uses multi-modal knowledge graphs to synthesize images with subgraph architectures and multi-modal entities.
paper_title: Abstractive Visual Understanding of Multi-modal Structured Knowledge: A New Perspective for MLLM Evaluation
authors: Yichi Zhang, Zhuo Chen, Lingbing Guo, Yajing Xu, Min Zhang

task_types: deductive reasoning, abductive reasoning, analogical reasoning, multi-step reasoning
description: Manually curated benchmark focused on complex reasoning tasks with three key categories: deductive, abductive, and analogical reasoning. Features open-ended multi-step elaborate reasoning evaluation that incorporates intermediate reasoning steps, resembling human assessment methods.
paper_title: InfiMM-Eval: Complex Open-Ended Reasoning Evaluation For Multi-Modal Large Language Models
authors: Xiaotian Han, Quanzeng You, Yongfei Liu, Wentao Chen, Huangjie Zheng

description: ROME tests vision-language models' ability to interpret counter-intuitive scenarios that defy common sense knowledge. It contains images with unconventional situations across color, shape, material, size and positional relations to evaluate reasoning beyond typical visual common sense.
paper_title: ROME: Evaluating Pre-trained Vision-Language Models on Reasoning beyond Visual Common Sense
authors: Kankan Zhou, Eason Lai, Wei Bin Au Yeong, Kyriakos Mouratidis, Jing Jiang

description: ActiView evaluates active perception in MLLMs by restricting their perceptual field and requiring them to actively zoom or shift their view based on reasoning to answer questions successfully. This tests a specialized form of VQA that challenges models to actively adjust their perception rather than passively processing full images.
paper_title: ActiView: Evaluating Active Perception Ability for Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Ziyue Wang, Chi Chen, Fuwen Luo, Yurui Dong, Yuanchi Zhang

task_types: analogical reasoning, perceptual understanding, relational reasoning
description: VOILA is a large-scale, open-ended, dynamic benchmark that evaluates MLLMs' perceptual understanding and abstract relational reasoning through analogical mapping in the visual domain. Models must generate an image that completes an analogy between two given image pairs without relying on predefined choices, testing high-level relational reasoning capabilities.
paper_title: VOILA: Evaluation of MLLMs For Perceptual Understanding and Analogical Reasoning
authors: Nilay Yilmaz, Maitreya Patel, Yiran Lawrence Luo, Tejas Gokhale, Chitta Baral

task_types: forgery detection, visual reasoning, anomaly detection, image analysis
description: REVEAL frames forgery detection as a prompt-driven visual reasoning task using vision-language models. It incorporates two approaches: holistic scene-level evaluation (analyzing physics, semantics, perspective, realism) and region-wise anomaly detection, providing reasoning and localization for visual forgery detection across different domains.
paper_title: REVEAL -- Reasoning and Evaluation of Visual Evidence through Aligned Language
authors: Ipsita Praharaj, Yukta Butala, Badrikanath Praharaj, Yash Butala

description: Introduces a vision-language benchmark inspired by the ARC challenge that requires multi-image, multi-step reasoning and self-correction. Features procedural generation, full verifiability, and scalability with progressive difficulty levels for structured evaluation.
paper_title: EasyARC: Evaluating Vision Language Models on True Visual Reasoning
authors: Mert Unsal, Aylin Akkus

task_types: visual question answering, step-by-step reasoning, visual grounding
description: Introduces a comprehensive benchmark with ~5,500 weakly supervised and 900 human-curated examples to evaluate grounded multimodal cognition, assessing answer accuracy, reasoning quality, and visual evidence alignment. Features new metrics like MagiScore and StepSense for comprehensive grounded visual reasoning evaluation.
paper_title: MagiC: Evaluating Multimodal Cognition Toward Grounded Visual Reasoning
authors: Chengfei Wu, Ronald Seoh, Bingxuan Li, Liqiang Zhang, Fengrong Han

task_types: geometric perception, spatial understanding, shape recognition
description: Introduces a novel benchmark designed to assess geometric perception abilities of MLLMs, focusing on understanding geometric shapes, structures, and spatial relationships. Addresses the gap in evaluating foundational geometric perception capabilities that support higher-level semantic tasks.
paper_title: GePBench: Evaluating Fundamental Geometric Perception for Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Shangyu Xing, Changhao Xiang, Yuteng Han, Yifan Yue, Zhen Wu

description: Code-Vision evaluates MLLMs' ability to generate correct programs based on flowchart inputs that visually represent algorithms or processes. It uniquely tests logical understanding and code generation capabilities across programming, algorithmic, and mathematical domains using visual flowcharts.
paper_title: Code-Vision: Evaluating Multimodal LLMs Logic Understanding and Code Generation Capabilities
authors: Hanbin Wang, Xiaoxuan Zhou, Zhipeng Xu, Keyuan Cheng, Yuxin Zuo

description: ROME is a contamination-free evaluation benchmark for vision language models specifically designed to test reasoning from visual clues. It provides a preliminary evaluation framework for large reasoning models on automatically verifiable textual and visual questions.
paper_title: FlagEval Findings Report: A Preliminary Evaluation of Large Reasoning Models on Automatically Verifiable Textual and Visual Questions
authors: Bowen Qin, Chen Yue, Fang Yin, Hui Wang, JG Yao

description: MARVEL is a multidimensional abstract visual reasoning benchmark that expands beyond existing limited AVR benchmarks. It uniquely includes six core knowledge patterns, geometric and abstract shapes, and five different task configurations with a hierarchical evaluation framework testing both perception and reasoning.
paper_title: MARVEL: Multidimensional Abstraction and Reasoning through Visual Evaluation and Learning
authors: Yifan Jiang, Jiarui Zhang, Kexuan Sun, Zhivar Sourati, Kian Ahrabian

task_types: satirical image detection, satirical understanding, satirical completion
description: YesBut evaluates satirical comprehension in vision-language models through three challenging tasks: detection, understanding, and completion of satirical images. It uniquely focuses on satirical image analysis with conflicting scenarios that are funny or ironic, testing advanced humor understanding capabilities.
paper_title: YesBut: A High-Quality Annotated Multimodal Dataset for evaluating Satire Comprehension capability of Vision-Language Models
authors: Abhilash Nandy, Yash Agarwal, Ashish Patwa, Millon Madhur Das, Aman Bansal

task_types: tool-use reasoning, image manipulation, visual-textual integration
description: VisualToolBench shifts from "think about images" to "think with images" paradigm, evaluating MLLMs' ability to dynamically transform visual content and integrate it with tools. It uniquely tests active image manipulation including cropping, editing, and enhancement across five diverse domains with detailed rubrics.
paper_title: Beyond Seeing: Evaluating Multimodal LLMs on Tool-Enabled Image Perception, Transformation, and Reasoning
authors: Xingang Guo, Utkarsh Tyagi, Advait Gosai, Paula Vergara, Jayeon Park

task_types: visual difference detection, unique description, self-retrieval
description: D3 (Detect, Describe, Discriminate) evaluates MLLMs' understanding of specific visual concepts through their ability to uniquely describe extremely similar image pairs differing only in targeted visual concepts. It uniquely uses self-retrieval evaluation across six visual patterns to test fine-grained visual difference detection.
paper_title: Detect, Describe, Discriminate: Moving Beyond VQA for MLLM Evaluation
authors: Manu Gaur, Darshan Singh S, Makarand Tapaswi

description: Introduces evaluation of norm reasoning competence in MLLMs using 30 text-based and 30 image-based stories, comparing model responses against human performance on norm-related questions.
paper_title: Social Norm Reasoning in Multimodal Language Models: An Evaluation
authors: Oishik Chowdhury, Anushka Debnath, Bastin Tony Roy Savarimuthu

description: MultiStAR introduces multi-stage Abstract Visual Reasoning evaluation based on RAVEN, assessing reasoning across varying complexity levels with novel MSEval metric that considers intermediate step correctness.
paper_title: Beyond Perception: Evaluating Abstract Visual Reasoning through Multi-Stage Task
authors: Yanbei Jiang, Yihao Ding, Chao Lei, Jiayang Ao, Jey Han Lau

description: V-MAGE introduces game-based evaluation framework with five video games and 30+ scenarios for assessing MLLMs' visual reasoning in interactive, continuous-space environments using dynamic Elo-based ranking.
paper_title: V-MAGE: A Game Evaluation Framework for Assessing Vision-Centric Capabilities in Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Xiangxi Zheng, Linjie Li, Zhengyuan Yang, Ping Yu, Alex Jinpeng Wang

description: NPHardEval4V introduces multimodal benchmark grounded in four classical NP-hard problems (Knapsack, Set Cover, TSP, Vertex Cover) to assess combinatorial reasoning under visual-linguistic constraints.
paper_title: NPHardEval4V: Dynamic Evaluation of Large Vision-Language Models with Effects of Vision
authors: Xiang Li, Wenyue Hua, Kaijie Zhu, Lingyao Li, Haoyang Ling

description: Multi-level benchmark with 3.4K contemporary images and 60K+ questions covering eight tasks and 12 daily scenarios across three progressive tiers (perception, understanding, reasoning). Each image equipped with rich annotations for all tasks, supporting evaluation from basic perception to compositional reasoning with 53% images published after Jan 2025.
paper_title: LENS: Multi-level Evaluation of Multimodal Reasoning with Large Language Models
authors: Ruilin Yao, Bo Zhang, Jirui Huang, Xinwei Long, Yifang Zhang

description: Evaluates reasoning width capability of MLLMs - the capacity for broad trial-and-error search and multi-constrained optimization rather than just sequential reasoning depth. Uses fine-grained tree-of-thought evaluation protocol to jointly quantify reasoning width and depth across heterogeneous domains.
paper_title: Think 360°: Evaluating the Width-centric Reasoning Capability of MLLMs Beyond Depth
authors: Mingrui Chen, Hexiong Yang, Haogeng Liu, Huaibo Huang, Ran He

task_types: visual question answering, logic evaluation, aesthetic assessment
description: First benchmark using VQA for evaluating logic correctness of academic illustrations. Features four levels of questions from logic diagrams to assess visual-logical consistency between generated illustrations and research papers, while using VLMs for aesthetic assessment.
paper_title: AIBench: Evaluating Visual-Logical Consistency in Academic Illustration Generation
authors: Zhaohe Liao, Kaixun Jiang, Zhihang Liu, Yujie Wei, Junqiu Yu

description: A causal reasoning benchmark with explicit causal graphs detailing human-object interactions across all four levels of causality: discovery, association, intervention, and counterfactual. Goes beyond traditional commonsense causality by including formal causal graphs.
paper_title: CELLO: Causal Evaluation of Large Vision-Language Models
authors: Meiqi Chen, Bo Peng, Yan Zhang, Chaochao Lu

task_types: object discovery, visual question answering, compositional reasoning
description: Addresses limitations in object-centric learning evaluation by using instruction-tuned VLMs as evaluators for complex reasoning tasks and introducing unified metrics that jointly assess localization and representation usefulness. Goes beyond simple object discovery to measure compositional generalization.
paper_title: Evaluating Object-Centric Models beyond Object Discovery
authors: Krishnakant Singh, Simone Schaub-Meyer, Stefan Roth

description: Comprehensive benchmark for evaluating LVLMs' ability to understand Video Visual Prompts in human-model interaction scenarios. Features 980 videos and 1172 QA pairs with manually annotated visual prompt frames across three main tasks and twelve categories.
paper_title: V2P-Bench: Evaluating Video-Language Understanding with Visual Prompts for Better Human-Model Interaction
authors: Yiming Zhao, Yu Zeng, Yukun Qi, YaoYang Liu, Xikun Bao

description: Video QA benchmark focused on causal reasoning in the physical world through five question types: counterfactual, hypothetical, anticipation, planning and descriptive. Grounded in real-world scenarios while requiring deep causal understanding rather than surface perception.
paper_title: CausalVQA: A Physically Grounded Causal Reasoning Benchmark for Video Models
authors: Aaron Foss, Chloe Evans, Sasha Mitts, Koustuv Sinha, Ammar Rizvi

task_types: chain-of-thought reasoning, math, science, OCR, logic, space-time
description: Introduces MME-CoT, a specialized benchmark for evaluating Chain-of-Thought reasoning performance of LMMs across six domains. The benchmark incorporates three novel metrics assessing reasoning quality, robustness, and efficiency at a fine-grained level.
paper_title: MME-CoT: Benchmarking Chain-of-Thought in Large Multimodal Models for Reasoning Quality, Robustness, and Efficiency
authors: Dongzhi Jiang, Renrui Zhang, Ziyu Guo, Yanwei Li, Yu Qi

task_types: open-ended reasoning, creative reasoning, step-by-step reasoning, multimodal puzzle solving
description: Large-scale benchmark of puzzlehunt-style problems for assessing step-by-step, open-ended, and creative multimodal reasoning. Features problems lacking well-defined structures, requiring discovery of underlying problem patterns from multimodal evidence and iterative reasoning.
paper_title: PuzzleWorld: A Benchmark for Multimodal, Open-Ended Reasoning in Puzzlehunts
authors: Hengzhi Li, Brendon Jiang, Alexander Naehu, Regan Song, Justin Zhang

task_types: chain-of-thought reasoning, visual grounding verification, logical coherence assessment
description: Diagnostic benchmark for probing visual Chain-of-Thought reasoning in multimodal models, focusing on verification rather than generation. Tests both visual consistency (evidence anchoring) and logical coherence (causal validity) through adversarial distractors.
paper_title: MM-CoT:A Benchmark for Probing Visual Chain-of-Thought Reasoning in Multimodal Models
authors: Jusheng Zhang, Kaitong Cai, Xiaoyang Guo, Sidi Liu, Qinhan Lv

task_types: spatial navigation, abstract pattern completion, causal simulation, logical constraint satisfaction, graph theory, topology
description: TACIT Benchmark is a programmatic visual reasoning benchmark comprising 10 tasks across 6 reasoning domains with dual-track evaluation (generative and discriminative). It features deterministic verification through computer-vision pipelines and structurally plausible near-miss distractors that violate exactly one constraint, requiring fine-grained visual reasoning rather than exploiting superficial cues.
paper_title: TACIT Benchmark: A Programmatic Visual Reasoning Benchmark for Generative and Discriminative Models
authors: Daniel Nobrega Medeiros

description: SHOP-VRB is a visual reasoning benchmark focused on small household object perception for robotics applications. It includes object properties, functionality, natural language descriptions, and question-answer pairs for visual reasoning queries with scene semantic representations, specifically targeting object grasping and manipulation tasks.
paper_title: SHOP-VRB: A Visual Reasoning Benchmark for Object Perception
authors: Michal Nazarczuk, Krystian Mikolajczyk

task_types: part-based reasoning, spatial relationships, physical reasoning
description: PTR introduces part-based visual reasoning with around 70k RGBD synthetic images featuring ground truth object and part level annotations. It focuses on finer-grained concepts, richer geometry relations, and more complex physics through part-whole hierarchies, paired with 700k machine-generated questions covering conceptual, relational, and physical reasoning.
paper_title: PTR: A Benchmark for Part-based Conceptual, Relational, and Physical Reasoning
authors: Yining Hong, Li Yi, Joshua B. Tenenbaum, Antonio Torralba, Chuang Gan

description: CVR is a novel visual reasoning benchmark inspired by fluidic intelligence tests that creates compositions of abstract rules at scale. It measures sample efficiency, generalization, transfer across task rules, and the ability to leverage compositionality, designed to drive progress towards more data-efficient learning algorithms.
paper_title: A Benchmark for Compositional Visual Reasoning
authors: Aimen Zerroug, Mohit Vaishnav, Julien Colin, Sebastian Musslick, Thomas Serre

description: HumanEval-V is a rigorous benchmark of human-annotated coding tasks spanning six task types that evaluates diverse visual reasoning capabilities. It features carefully crafted diagrams paired with function signatures and test cases, employing novel code generation tasks to assess models' diagram comprehension in coding contexts.
paper_title: HumanEval-V: Benchmarking High-Level Visual Reasoning with Complex Diagrams in Coding Tasks
authors: Fengji Zhang, Linquan Wu, Huiyu Bai, Guancheng Lin, Xiao Li

description: ZeroBench is a lightweight visual reasoning benchmark designed to be entirely impossible for contemporary frontier LMMs. It consists of 100 manually curated questions and 334 subquestions, specifically created to remain challenging and relevant longer than existing benchmarks by being beyond current model capabilities.
paper_title: ZeroBench: An Impossible Visual Benchmark for Contemporary Large Multimodal Models
authors: Jonathan Roberts, Mohammad Reza Taesiri, Ansh Sharma, Akash Gupta, Samuel Roberts

task_types: few-shot learning, compositional reasoning, human-object interaction
description: Bongard-HOI is a visual reasoning benchmark focusing on compositional learning of human-object interactions from natural images. It features few-shot concept learning with hard negatives where positive and negative images only disagree on action labels, and multiple test sets with varying degrees of HOI concept overlap between training and test.
paper_title: Bongard-HOI: Benchmarking Few-Shot Visual Reasoning for Human-Object Interactions
authors: Huaizu Jiang, Xiaojian Ma, Weili Nie, Zhiding Yu, Yuke Zhu

description: MMCode is the first multi-modal coding dataset for evaluating algorithmic problem-solving skills in visually rich contexts. It contains 3,548 questions and 6,620 images collected from real-world programming challenges across 10 code competition websites, presenting significant challenges for visual reasoning and code generation.
paper_title: MMCode: Benchmarking Multimodal Large Language Models for Code Generation with Visually Rich Programming Problems
authors: Kaixin Li, Yuchen Tian, Qisheng Hu, Ziyang Luo, Zhiyong Huang

description: BenchLMM assesses the robustness of Large Multimodal Models against three different styles: artistic image style, imaging sensor style, and application style, with five sub-styles each. It provides a comprehensive evaluation of LMMs' cross-style visual capabilities and introduces a training-free method for improvement.
paper_title: BenchLMM: Benchmarking Cross-style Visual Capability of Large Multimodal Models
authors: Rizhao Cai, Zirui Song, Dayan Guan, Zhenhao Chen, Xing Luo

description: A-I-RAVEN introduces 10 generalization regimes that systematically test generalization of abstract rules applied to held-out attributes at various complexity levels. It features compositionality, variety of figure configurations, and requires less computational resources compared to PGM while maintaining systematic assessment capabilities.
paper_title: A-I-RAVEN and I-RAVEN-Mesh: Two New Benchmarks for Abstract Visual Reasoning
authors: Mikołaj Małkiński, Jacek Mańdziuk

task_types: concept learning, visual reasoning, analogy making
description: Bongard-LOGO uses program-guided generation to produce human-interpretable visual cognition problems in LOGO language. It captures three core properties of human cognition: context-dependent perception, analogy-making perception, and perception with few samples but infinite vocabulary, providing a new benchmark for human-level concept learning and reasoning.
paper_title: Bongard-LOGO: A New Benchmark for Human-Level Concept Learning and Reasoning
authors: Weili Nie, Zhiding Yu, Lei Mao, Ankit B. Patel, Yuke Zhu

description: A benchmark for hierarchical physical reasoning that tests individual physical reasoning capabilities through generated tasks in Angry Birds. It proposes a hierarchy of physical reasoning capabilities with increasing complexity, enabling granular evaluation of agents' physical understanding.
paper_title: Hi-Phy: A Benchmark for Hierarchical Physical Reasoning
authors: Cheng Xue, Vimukthini Pinto, Chathura Gamage, Peng Zhang, Jochen Renz

description: A benchmark containing simple classical mechanics puzzles in a 2D physical environment for physical reasoning. Designed to encourage development of sample-efficient learning algorithms that generalize well across physics-based puzzles.
paper_title: PHYRE: A New Benchmark for Physical Reasoning
authors: Anton Bakhtin, Laurens van der Maaten, Justin Johnson, Laura Gustafson, Ross Girshick

task_types: abstract reasoning, visual reasoning, sequential reasoning
description: A dataset of around 10,000 temporally ordered, object-level action trajectories capturing how humans iteratively solve ARC tasks. Reveals intermediate reasoning steps through human reasoning trajectories rather than static input-output supervision.
paper_title: ARCTraj: A Dataset and Benchmark of Human Reasoning Trajectories for Abstract Problem Solving
authors: Sejin Kim, Hayan Choi, Seokki Lee, Sundong Kim

description: PicWorld is the first comprehensive benchmark that assesses implicit world knowledge and physical causal reasoning of text-to-image models. It introduces evidence-grounded evaluation through PW-Agent, a multi-agent evaluator that hierarchically assesses images on their physical realism and logical consistency by decomposing prompts into verifiable visual evidence.
paper_title: Beyond Words and Pixels: A Benchmark for Implicit World Knowledge Reasoning in Generative Models
authors: Tianyang Han, Junhao Su, Junjie Hu, Peizhen Yang, Hengyu Shi

description: A difficult multiple-choice benchmark for fine-grained object classification (e.g., distinction between animal species) designed to evaluate LVLMs on tasks requiring detailed visual discrimination. Uses multiple-choice format to avoid ambiguous answers and mines negative labels with CLIP to retain classification difficulty.
paper_title: African or European Swallow? Benchmarking Large Vision-Language Models for Fine-Grained Object Classification
authors: Gregor Geigle, Radu Timofte, Goran Glavaš

description: Specialized benchmark evaluating counting capabilities of text-to-video models through rigorous human evaluation. Reveals fundamental limitations where existing models consistently fail to generate videos with accurate object counts of 9 or fewer, covering diverse generators and exploring factors affecting counting performance.
paper_title: Can You Count to Nine? A Human Evaluation Benchmark for Counting Limits in Modern Text-to-Video Models
authors: Xuyang Guo, Zekai Huang, Jiayan Huo, Yingyu Liang, Zhenmei Shi

task_types: on-screen text fidelity evaluation, temporal text consistency
description: First human-evaluation benchmark dedicated to evaluating on-screen text fidelity and temporal consistency in text-to-video models. Features complex text strings integrated with dynamic scene changes to test models' ability to maintain detailed textual instructions across frames, revealing critical gaps in current generators.
paper_title: T2VTextBench: A Human Evaluation Benchmark for Textual Control in Video Generation Models
authors: Xuyang Guo, Jiayan Huo, Zhenmei Shi, Zhao Song, Jiahao Zhang

description: CAPTCHA-X is the first real-world CAPTCHA benchmark with reasoning, covering seven categories of CAPTCHAs with step-by-step action solutions and grounding annotations. It defines five reasoning-oriented metrics for comprehensive evaluation of spatial reasoning capabilities.
paper_title: Reasoning under Vision: Understanding Visual-Spatial Cognition in Vision-Language Models for CAPTCHA
authors: Python Song, Luke Tenyi Chang, Yun-Yun Tsai, Penghui Li, Junfeng Yang

description: A novel video-based question answering benchmark for evaluating vision-language models' understanding of mimed actions. Constructed with motion capture data and includes variations with perturbations to character, background, and viewpoint to test recognition robustness.
paper_title: Can Vision Language Models Understand Mimed Actions?
authors: Hyundong Cho, Spencer Lin, Tejas Srinivasan, Michael Saxon, Deuksin Kwon

task_types: process reward modeling, visual reasoning evaluation
description: The first comprehensive benchmark for evaluating Process Reward Models under the thinking with images paradigm. Features manually annotated reasoning trajectories spanning 4 categories and 16 subcategories with 7 fine-grained error types for assessing visual reasoning processes.
paper_title: What, Whether and How? Unveiling Process Reward Models for Thinking with Images Reasoning
authors: Yujin Zhou, Pengcheng Wen, Jiale Chen, Boqin Yin, Han Zhu

description: SyntActically DE-biased benchmark that addresses syntactical bias in current benchmarks for evaluating compositional reasoning in Generative Vision-Language Models. Introduces SyntaxBias Score and adds challenging tasks to evaluate robustness against linguistic bias.
paper_title: An Examination of the Compositionality of Large Generative Vision-Language Models
authors: Teli Ma, Rong Li, Junwei Liang

description: Evaluates VLMs on subtle comparative reasoning across ten difference types (Attribute, State, Emotion, Temporal, Spatial, Existence, Quantity, Quality, Viewpoint, Action) between visually similar images. Spans diverse domains including industrial, aerial, and medical imagery, addressing gaps in existing benchmarks that focus on large, salient differences.
paper_title: VLM-SubtleBench: How Far Are VLMs from Human-Level Subtle Comparative Reasoning?
authors: Minkyu Kim, Sangheon Lee, Dongmin Park

description: Assesses LVLMs' capability to detect visually salient features that are readily apparent to humans, focusing on low-level features like color, intensity, and orientation. Includes three novel tasks: Odd-One-Out Detection, Referring Odd-One-Out, and Visual Referring Odd-One-Out.
paper_title: Vision-Language Models Can't See the Obvious
authors: Yasser Dahou, Ngoc Dung Huynh, Phuc H. Le-Khac, Wamiq Reyaz Para, Ankit Singh

description: Introduces the Atomic Visual Skills Dataset for evaluating VLMs on fundamental, indivisible visual perception skills in basic 2D Euclidean geometry. Focuses on atomic rather than composite visual perception tasks that are trivial for adult humans but challenging for VLMs.
paper_title: Decomposing Complex Visual Comprehension into Atomic Visual Skills for Vision Language Models
authors: Hyunsik Chae, Seungwoo Yoon, Jaden Park, Chloe Yewon Chun, Yongin Cho

description: Built with graph-based automatic pipeline for generating complex cross-modal reasoning tasks requiring multi-hop reasoning combining textual context with visual cues. Spans diverse domains including natural images, videos, and text-rich sources with manually verified test set.
paper_title: CRIT: Graph-Based Automatic Data Synthesis to Enhance Cross-Modal Multi-Hop Reasoning
authors: Junyoung Sung, Seungwoo Lyu, Minjun Kim, Sumin An, Arsha Nagrani

description: Evaluates VLMs' robustness in visually adversarial scenarios inspired by Ishihara color blindness tests. Features 500 Ishihara-like images with numbers 0-99 in varying color combinations to test numerical recognition in complex visual patterns.
paper_title: ColorBlindnessEval: Can Vision-Language Models Pass Color Blindness Tests?
authors: Zijian Ling, Han Zhang, Yazhuo Zhou, Jiahao Cui

task_types: scene text understanding, object reasoning, corruption robustness
description: Introduces two benchmarks to systematically evaluate VLM robustness to 19 corruption types from ImageNet-C. TextVQA-C tests scene text understanding while GQA-C evaluates object-based reasoning under various corruptions.
paper_title: Analysing the Robustness of Vision-Language-Models to Common Corruptions
authors: Muhammad Usama, Syeda Aishah Asim, Syed Bilal Ali, Syed Talal Wasim, Umair Bin Mansoor

task_types: symmetry detection, geometric transformations, spatial reasoning, chart interpretation, sequence prediction
description: A synthetic environment that procedurally generates puzzles using motifs, tiles, charts, icons, and geometric primitives with verifiable ground-truth solutions. Covers 25 task types spanning symmetry detection, geometric transformations, spatial reasoning, chart interpretation, and sequence prediction.
paper_title: SPHINX: A Synthetic Environment for Visual Perception and Reasoning
authors: Md Tanvirul Alam, Saksham Aggarwal, Justin Yang Chae, Nidhi Rastogi

task_types: instruction following, object detection, state detection, assembly reasoning
description: A hybrid benchmark combining real-world LEGO assembly logic with programmatically generated multimodal scenes. Captures stepwise visual states and procedural instructions for controlled evaluation of instruction-following, object detection, and state detection in assembly tasks.
paper_title: LEGO Co-builder: Exploring Fine-Grained Vision-Language Modeling for Multimodal LEGO Assembly Assistants
authors: Haochen Huang, Jiahuan Pei, Mohammad Aliannejadi, Xin Sun, Moonisa Ahsan

description: MATE introduces the novel task of cross-modal entity linking, featuring 5.5k evaluation instances of visual scenes aligned with textual representations. It evaluates VLMs' ability to retrieve attributes of objects across modalities using a question-answering format, revealing significant challenges as scene complexity increases.
paper_title: Vision-Language Models Struggle to Align Entities across Modalities
authors: Iñigo Alonso, Gorka Azkune, Ander Salaberria, Jeremy Barnes, Oier Lopez de Lacalle

description: This benchmark introduces the task of navigational sign understanding, focusing on parsing locations and associated directions from signs. It features a test set capturing signs with varying complexity across diverse public spaces from hospitals to shopping malls, specifically designed to advance robot navigation capabilities.
paper_title: Sign Language: Towards Sign Understanding for Robot Autonomy
authors: Ayush Agrawal, Joel Loo, Nicky Zimmerman, David Hsu

description: InsightVision is the first comprehensive, multi-level Chinese-based benchmark for evaluating implicit visual semantics understanding in large vision language models. It systematically categorizes evaluation into four subtasks: surface-level content understanding, symbolic meaning interpretation, background knowledge comprehension, and implicit meaning comprehension.
paper_title: InsightVision: A Comprehensive, Multi-Level Chinese-based Benchmark for Evaluating Implicit Visual Semantics in Large Vision Language Models
authors: Xiaofei Yin, Yijie Hong, Ya Guo, Yi Tu, Weiqiang Wang

task_types: similarity assessment, pair comparison, controllability evaluation
description: PairBench is a framework for evaluating Vision-Language Models as customizable similarity tools using widely available image datasets. It introduces four key metrics for reliable comparison: alignment with human annotations, consistency across pair ordering, distribution smoothness, and controllability through prompting, addressing the fundamental capability of visual comparison that remains insufficiently assessed.
paper_title: PairBench: Are Vision-Language Models Reliable at Comparing What They See?
authors: Aarash Feizi, Sai Rajeswar, Adriana Romero-Soriano, Reihaneh Rabbany, Valentina Zantedeschi

task_types: sketch evaluation, element-level visual question answering, abstraction assessment
description: CommonSketch is the first semantically annotated sketch dataset comprising 23,100 human-drawn sketches across 300 classes, each paired with captions and element-level annotations. It supports SEA (Sketch Evaluation metric for Abstraction efficiency), a reference-free metric that assesses how economically a sketch represents class-defining visual elements while preserving semantic recognizability using commonsense knowledge.
paper_title: SEA: Evaluating Sketch Abstraction Efficiency via Element-level Commonsense Visual Question Answering
authors: Jiho Park, Sieun Choi, Jaeyoon Seo, Minho Sohn, Yeana Kim

task_types: crossword puzzle solving, multimodal reasoning, constrained problem solving
description: CrossWordBench evaluates reasoning capabilities of LLMs and LVLMs through crossword puzzles, requiring multimodal adherence to semantic constraints from text-based clues and intersectional constraints from visual grid structures. It features controllable puzzle generation in two formats (text and image), adjustable difficulty through prefill ratio control, and different evaluation strategies from direct solving to interactive modes.
paper_title: CrossWordBench: Evaluating the Reasoning Capabilities of LLMs and LVLMs with Controllable Puzzle Generation
authors: Jixuan Leng, Chengsong Huang, Langlin Huang, Bill Yuchen Lin, William W. Cohen

task_types: resolution-sensitive visual reasoning, aspect ratio evaluation, extreme visual condition assessment
description: RC-Bench is a novel benchmark specifically designed to systematically evaluate VLM capabilities under extreme visual conditions, with emphasis on resolution and aspect ratio variations. It addresses the 'Resolution Dilemma' by providing comprehensive evaluation of VLMs under varied visual conditions, which existing benchmarks neglect by not considering resolution as a critical factor.
paper_title: Native Visual Understanding: Resolving Resolution Dilemmas in Vision-Language Models
authors: Junbo Niu, Yuanhong Zheng, Ziyang Miao, Hejun Dong, Chunjiang Ge

description: CAVE is the first benchmark of real-world visual anomalies that supports open-ended tasks including anomaly description, explanation, and justification. It provides fine-grained annotations for visual grounding and categorizes anomalies based on cognitive science principles for how humans identify and resolve anomalies.
paper_title: CAVE: Detecting and Explaining Commonsense Anomalies in Visual Environments
authors: Rishika Bhagwatkar, Syrielle Montariol, Angelika Romanou, Beatriz Borges, Irina Rish

description: Auto-Comp generates scalable compositional reasoning benchmarks with controllable complexity, featuring paired Minimal and Contextual captions for controlled A/B testing. The novel 'Confusion Benchmark' reveals VLM susceptibility to low-entropy distractors, extending beyond known bag-of-words limitations in compositional reasoning.
paper_title: Auto-Comp: An Automated Pipeline for Scalable Compositional Probing of Contrastive Vision-Language Models
authors: Cristian Sbrolli, Matteo Matteucci, Toshihiko Yamasaki

description: Creates a large-scale synthetic dataset built on 250,000 ImageNet21k images with structured thinking tokens and corresponding answers for training VLMs with explicit reasoning capabilities. The dataset captures step-by-step reasoning processes and provides evaluation benchmarks for multimodal reasoning mechanisms.
paper_title: ImageNet-Think-250K: A Large-Scale Synthetic Dataset for Multimodal Reasoning for Vision Language Models
authors: Krishna Teja Chitty-Venkata, Murali Emani

task_types: retrieval with negation, multiple choice questions with negated captions
description: Evaluates vision-language models' understanding of negation across 18 task variations and 79k examples spanning image, video, and medical datasets. The benchmark consists of Retrieval with Negation and Multiple Choice Questions with Negated Captions to assess negation comprehension in diverse multimodal settings.
paper_title: Vision-Language Models Do Not Understand Negation
authors: Kumail Alhamoud, Shaden Alshammari, Yonglong Tian, Guohao Li, Philip Torr

task_types: geometric feature recognition, plane geometry problem solving
description: Proposes a benchmark that evaluates recognition of visual geometric features including primitives (dots, lines) and relations (orthogonality) for solving plane geometry problems. Tests vision encoders' ability to detect geometric features and generalize across different diagram domains.
paper_title: GeoDANO: Geometric VLM with Domain Agnostic Vision Encoder
authors: Seunghyuk Cho, Zhenyue Qin, Yang Liu, Youngbin Choi, Seungbeom Lee

task_types: visual question answering, spurious correlation detection
description: Develops a benchmark comprised of 124 distinct types of spurious correlations extracted from real-world datasets, each containing 1 realistic and 10 synthetic VQA samples for evaluating Large Vision Language Models' ability to generalize beyond seen spurious correlations. Tests models' resistance to shortcuts and attention to overall image context.
paper_title: Escaping the SpuriVerse: Can Large Vision-Language Models Generalize Beyond Seen Spurious Correlations?
authors: Yiwei Yang, Chung Peng Lee, Shangbin Feng, Dora Zhao, Bingbing Wen

description: Introduces a new benchmark dataset consisting of RGB-D videos of real objects captured from multiple viewpoints, annotated with precise mass measurements for evaluating VLMs' ability to infer physical properties required for manipulation. Tests physical quantity estimation under realistic sensing conditions.
paper_title: PhysQuantAgent: An Inference Pipeline of Mass Estimation for Vision-Language Models
authors: Hisayuki Yokomizo, Taiki Miyanishi, Yan Gang, Shuhei Kurita, Nakamasa Inoue

description: UniG2U-Bench systematically evaluates generation-to-understanding capabilities by categorizing evaluation into 7 regimes and 30 subtasks requiring varying degrees of visual transformations. It uniquely explores when and how generation facilitates understanding in multimodal models.
paper_title: UniG2U-Bench: Do Unified Models Advance Multimodal Understanding?
authors: Zimo Wen, Boxiu Li, Wanbo Zhang, Junxiang Lei, Xiaoyu Chen

task_types: multi-graph reasoning, graph parsing, cross-modal reasoning
description: The paper introduces the first comprehensive benchmark for multi-graph joint reasoning using VLMs, covering four graph types (knowledge graphs, flowcharts, mind maps, route maps) with both homogeneous and heterogeneous groupings. It uniquely evaluates multi-graph understanding rather than single-graph reasoning.
paper_title: Graph-to-Vision: Multi-graph Understanding and Reasoning using Vision-Language Models
authors: Ruizhou Li, Haiyun Jiang

description: Compun evaluates VLMs' understanding of compound nouns through text-to-image retrieval tasks with 400 unique compound nouns. It challenges models to select correct images showing compound nouns among distractor images showing constituent nouns, testing compositional understanding.
paper_title: Do Vision-Language Models Understand Compound Nouns?
authors: Sonal Kumar, Sreyan Ghosh, S Sakshi, Utkarsh Tyagi, Dinesh Manocha

task_types: object-level editing, instruction following, visual transformation
description: VOCEdits is a benchmark dataset based on PASCAL VOC 2012, augmented with instructional edit prompts, ground-truth transformations, and precise object masks for evaluating object-level image editing. It specifically tests models' ability to perform localized shape and layout transformations while preserving visual coherence.
paper_title: POEM: Precise Object-level Editing via MLLM control
authors: Marco Schouten, Mehmet Onurcan Kaya, Serge Belongie, Dim P. Papadopoulos

description: The benchmark evaluates visual correspondence and matching abilities of MLLMs across eight aspects. It is built from 15 open-source datasets and Internet videos to comprehensively test visual matching capabilities that are essential but rarely studied in current MLLMs.
paper_title: Are They the Same? Exploring Visual Correspondence Shortcomings of Multimodal LLMs
authors: Yikang Zhou, Tao Zhang, Shilin Xu, Shihao Chen, Qianyu Zhou

task_types: physics reasoning, motion understanding, spatial interaction reasoning, video question answering
description: MASS-Bench is a comprehensive benchmark consisting of 4,350 real-world and AIGC videos with 8,361 free-form video question-answering pairs focused on physics-related comprehension tasks. It features detailed annotations including visual detections, sub-segment grounding, and full-sequence 3D motion tracking of entities to evaluate physics-driven reasoning involving motion dynamics and spatial interactions.
paper_title: MASS: Motion-Aware Spatial-Temporal Grounding for Physics Reasoning and Comprehension in Vision-Language Models
authors: Xiyang Wu, Zongxia Li, Jihui Jin, Guangyao Shi, Gouthaman KV

task_types: sequential reasoning, visual storytelling, logical ordering
description: A benchmark for evaluating AI reasoning capabilities based on human verification tasks requiring sequential reasoning and visual storytelling. FLIP challenges models to identify logically coherent orderings of 4 images, emphasizing common sense and multimodal reasoning.
paper_title: FLIP Reasoning Challenge
authors: Andreas Plesner, Turlan Kuzhagaliyev, Roger Wattenhofer

task_types: multimodal reasoning, step-by-step reasoning, cognitive function analysis
description: Decomposes complex multimodal questions into step-by-step subquestions designed to simulate human reasoning through a chain-of-thought paradigm. Each subquestion is associated with specific receptive or cognitive functions such as high-level visual reception and inference, enabling systematic analysis of VLM attention mechanisms.
paper_title: Investigating The Functional Roles of Attention Heads in Vision Language Models: Evidence for Reasoning Modules
authors: Yanbei Jiang, Xueqi Ma, Shu Liu, Sarah Monazam Erfani, Tongliang Liu

task_types: spatial reasoning, multi-step planning, interactive gaming
description: The first INteractive Game-based Vision Planning benchmark specifically designed to evaluate spatial imagination and multi-step reasoning abilities of MLLMs. Features 6 distinct games with 300 levels each having 6 unique configurations, providing focused evaluation of multi-step planning based on spatial relationships in images.
paper_title: ING-VP: MLLMs cannot Play Easy Vision-based Games Yet
authors: Haoran Zhang, Hangyu Guo, Shuyue Guo, Meng Cao, Wenhao Huang

task_types: reasoning process evaluation, intermodal relationship analysis, multimodal reasoning
description: Constructs a benchmark with 374 images and 390 reasoning instances to evaluate reasoning processes using tree structure-based metrics. Each instance includes reliable visual-textual clues as leaf nodes, with three types of intermodal relationships to investigate how cross-modal interactions influence multimodal reasoning processes.
paper_title: RPTS: Tree-Structured Reasoning Process Scoring for Faithful Multimodal Evaluation
authors: Haofeng Wang, Yu Zhang

description: SVAT challenges state-of-the-art VLMs to learn new visuospatial tasks in-context through visual demonstrations. The benchmark tests whether VLMs can learn novel spatial reasoning concepts purely from visual examples or are limited to adapting output formats.
paper_title: Can Vision Language Models Learn from Visual Demonstrations of Ambiguous Spatial Reasoning?
authors: Bowen Zhao, Leo Parker Dirac, Paulina Varshavskaya

description: VIBE introduces a new task for evaluating Visual Social-Pragmatic Inference in VLMs, testing their ability to understand human social behavior and emotions from visual cues. The benchmark addresses a previously overlooked limitation in VLMs' social reasoning capabilities.
paper_title: VIBE: Can a VLM Read the Room?
authors: Tania Chakraborty, Eylon Caplan, Dan Goldwasser

task_types: hidden content detection, optical illusion recognition
description: A benchmark of 112 images with hidden text, objects, and optical illusions that tests VLMs' ability to detect hidden content through perceptual adjustments. Reveals critical limitations in current VLMs which achieve near-zero accuracy (0-5.36%) on detecting hidden visual content.
paper_title: SemVink: Advancing VLMs' Semantic Understanding of Optical Illusions via Visual Global Thinking
authors: Sifan Li, Yujun Cai, Yiwei Wang

description: A benchmark featuring deliberately out-of-distribution images and Q&A pairs to test VLMs' reliance on visual language priors versus true visual reasoning. Each question has three potential answers with corresponding images, where one can be resolved by text priors alone and two require visual reasoning.
paper_title: Probing Visual Language Priors in VLMs
authors: Tiange Luo, Ang Cao, Gunhee Lee, Justin Johnson, Honglak Lee

task_types: visual perception evaluation, color sensitivity, shape sensitivity
description: A dataset designed as an eye examination process to investigate how VLMs perceive images, focusing on key elements of visual recognition from primitive color and shape to semantic levels. Tests VLMs' sensitivities to different visual elements and readiness for examination.
paper_title: VLM's Eye Examination: Instruct and Inspect Visual Competency of Vision Language Models
authors: Nam Hyeon-Woo, Moon Ye-Bin, Wonseok Choi, Lee Hyun, Tae-Hyun Oh

task_types: object size understanding, position recognition, existence detection, counting
description: A benchmark designed to diagnose VLMs' comprehension of fine-grained visual-linguistic concepts including object size, position, existence, and count. Uses a progressive pipeline to synthesize images that vary in specific attributes while maintaining consistency in other aspects.
paper_title: Synthesize, Diagnose, and Optimize: Towards Fine-Grained Vision-Language Understanding
authors: Wujian Peng, Sicheng Xie, Zuyao You, Shiyi Lan, Zuxuan Wu

task_types: character recognition, pose detection, narrative reasoning, storyline ordering
description: First fine-grained and comprehensive benchmark for VLM-based comic understanding, spanning tasks from foundational recognition to high-level narrative construction. Features dense annotations for characters, poses, and depth in stylized visual narratives.
paper_title: Zooming into Comics: Region-Aware RL Improves Fine-Grained Comic Understanding in Vision-Language Models
authors: Yule Chen, Yufan Ren, Sabine Süsstrunk

description: Evaluates VLMs in error-prone, vision-based sequential planning tasks across 4 domains: maze navigation, block rearrangement, image reconstruction, and object reorganization. Assesses two key abilities: Error Detection (identifying non-optimal action) and Step Completion (correcting and completing action sequences).
paper_title: CoSPlan: Corrective Sequential Planning via Scene Graph Incremental Updates
authors: Shresth Grover, Priyank Pathak, Akash Kumar, Vibhav Vineet, Yogesh S Rawat

description: GameQA is a dataset of 30 games and 158 tasks with controllable difficulty gradation, constructed using Code2Logic approach that adapts game code to synthesize game reasoning task data. It provides rich visual elements and mechanics from video games for training VLMs' general reasoning abilities.
paper_title: Game-RL: Synthesizing Multimodal Verifiable Game Data to Boost VLMs' General Reasoning
authors: Jingqi Tong, Jixin Tang, Hangcheng Li, Yurong Mou, Ming Zhang

description: The first systematic evaluation benchmark for Visual CoT robustness under visual perturbations, spanning 12 image corruption types across 4 Visual Question Answering datasets. It enables comprehensive comparison between VLMs that use Visual CoT and those that do not under various noise conditions.
paper_title: Visual CoT Makes VLMs Smarter but More Fragile
authors: Chunxue Xu, Yiwei Wang, Yujun Cai, Bryan Hooi, Songze Li

task_types: object description, visual prompt QA, referring segmentation
description: Comprehensive pixel understanding benchmark with manual verification that includes three tasks: detailed object description, visual prompt-based question answering, and visual-text referring segmentation. Tests fine-grained pixel-level understanding capabilities of MLLMs.
paper_title: Pixel-SAIL: Single Transformer For Pixel-Grounded Understanding
authors: Tao Zhang, Xiangtai Li, Zilong Huang, Yanwei Li, Weixian Lei

description: A comprehensive benchmark evaluating logical reasoning capabilities of VLMs across 9 logical categories and 4 scenarios (images, videos, anomaly detection, medical diagnostics). Tests fundamental logical structures like causality and conditionality that VLMs often fail on.
paper_title: Logic Unseen: Revealing the Logical Blindspots of Vision-Language Models
authors: Yuchen Zhou, Jiayu Tang, Shuo Yang, Xiaoyan Xiao, Yuqin Dai

description: An image-text counting benchmark that evaluates VLMs' understanding of object counting. Tests compositional concepts and quantitative understanding by requiring models to discriminate between correct and incorrect object counts in captions.
paper_title: Teaching CLIP to Count to Ten
authors: Roni Paiss, Ariel Ephrat, Omer Tov, Shiran Zada, Inbar Mosseri

task_types: chain-of-thought reasoning, visual reasoning, reasoning consistency
description: CURE benchmark measures both zero-shot reasoning performance and chain-of-thought reasoning consistency in vision-language models. Built using an LLM-Human-in-the-Loop pipeline to generate high-quality reasoning chains and consistency evaluation metrics for visual reasoning tasks.
paper_title: Measuring and Improving Chain-of-Thought Reasoning in Vision-Language Models
authors: Yangyi Chen, Karan Sikka, Michael Cogswell, Heng Ji, Ajay Divakaran

task_types: visual reflection, region-based verification, multimodal reasoning
